title: build-binaries: parse_gaia_{hip,tyc}_xmatch treats malformed angular_distance as best match
description:
`scripts/build-binaries.py:499` and `:516` parse the Gaia HIP/Tycho xmatch TSVs and pick the nearest match per HIP/Tyc by `angular_distance`. Both lines use:

    ang = safe_float(r.get("angular_distance") or "") or 0.0

A row with a missing or malformed `angular_distance` is coerced to `0.0` — which is the most-preferred value. The row wins the tie-break and displaces any real match.

These are the foundational HIP→Gaia and Tyc→Gaia cross-walks the entire resolution chain (Stages 2-7 of stellata-dch) depends on. A swap here corrupts every downstream lookup keyed by that classical identifier.

## Acceptance

1. Replace `or 0.0` with `or float("inf")` (corrupted = worst-possible) OR skip the row entirely: `if ang is None: continue`. Pick whichever is clearer at the call site.
2. Unit test: feed a fixture with two rows for the same HIP — one with `angular_distance=""` (malformed), one with `angular_distance="0.5"`; assert the 0.5 row wins.

## Smoke

Re-run `build-binaries.py` on committed inputs; cardinality of `hip_to_gaia` / `tyc_to_gaia` unchanged (no malformed rows in current TSVs, so this is a future-proofing fix).

title: refresh_lib: atomic TSV write + auto-include lib mtime in is_up_to_date
description:
Two coupled defects in `scripts/refresh_lib.py` surfaced in the retrospective on PRs 90-97. Both expose every refresh script's output to silent corruption / staleness.

**H1 — `write_tsv` is not atomic.** `write_tsv` (refresh_lib.py:362) opens the output directly. Any mid-write failure (disk full, KeyboardInterrupt, an unexpected `float(None)` on a malformed cell, OOM on a large batch) leaves a partially-written TSV whose mtime is newer than the script source. The next run sees it as up-to-date and downstream silently consumes the broken file. Affects every refresh script + `build-binaries.py` (which depends on these TSVs).

**H2 — `is_up_to_date` does not track `refresh_lib.py` itself.** Every refresh script passes `[Path(__file__)]` (plus inputs) as sources, but not `Path(refresh_lib.__file__)`. If we fix a bug in `coerce_masked`, change rounding in `write_tsv`, or extend `_dtype_matches`, none of the existing outputs are invalidated. Currently requires `--force` everywhere, with no enforcement.

## Acceptance

1. `write_tsv` writes to `output.with_suffix(output.suffix + ".tmp")` then `os.replace()` to swap in atomically (POSIX guarantee). Test: kill mid-write fixture (write 3 rows then raise from the row generator); assert the original file is either absent or fully intact, never half-written.
2. `is_up_to_date` auto-includes `refresh_lib`'s own `__file__` in the sources iterable (or a `make_is_up_to_date(extra)` wrapper does so). Test: touch `refresh_lib`'s source forward, assert `is_up_to_date` returns False without callers having to list it explicitly.
3. All 7 refresh scripts re-run `--force` end-to-end; resulting TSVs byte-identical to before.

## Smoke

Force-rebuild one TSV (e.g. `gaia_dr3_hip_xmatch.tsv`) via `--force`; diff against the previous committed version → expected: byte-identical.

title: Phase 3 — build-catalog.ts: Bailer-Jones distance override for stars with Gaia DR3 source_id
description:
# Why

Wire the B-J distance posteriors (from dch.46) into `scripts/build-catalog.ts` so every AT-HYG star with a Gaia DR3 source_id picks up its B-J distance in place of the naive 1/π value. See dch.46 for the underlying motivation (parallax inversion fails catastrophically for low-S/N parallaxes; a handful of luminous distant stars currently render outside the galactic-disc wireframe).

The integration is principled and self-targeting: B-J's photogeometric posterior dominates the prior at high parallax S/N (so well-measured nearby stars don't move), and collapses onto the Galactic-density prior at low S/N (so 14-kpc parallax-inversion artifacts are pulled back to plausible distances). Per [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] — no per-star carve-outs, no famous-star tier; uniform across the catalog.

# Scope (one commit/PR)

In `scripts/build-catalog.ts`:

1. Load `data/bailer-jones-dr3.tsv` alongside existing AT-HYG load. Build `Map<gaia_source_id, distance_pc>` keyed on source_id with `r_med_photogeo` (preferred) || `r_med_geo` (fallback when photogeo missing for a row).
2. For each AT-HYG row with non-empty `gaia` AND a B-J entry:
   - Override `dist` with the B-J value.
   - Recompute `x0`, `y0`, `z0` from the original `ra`/`dec` and the new `dist` (standard ICRS spherical → Cartesian, same convention AT-HYG uses).
   - Recompute `absmag` from the original `mag` and the new `dist` (`mag − 5·log10(dist/10)`) so disc/glow sizing in `star-physics.ts` stays consistent with placement. **This is the one footnote that's easy to miss** — without it, stars get placed at the new distance but lit for the old one.
   - Set `dist_src = "BJ"` (or extend existing enum; pick a value that distinguishes from `G_R3`).
3. Stars with no `gaia` column OR no B-J entry retain AT-HYG's values unchanged. No NaN risk.
4. Log override count + percentage at build time (per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] tier-2 statistics output).
5. Update `build-catalog-expected.json` (created in earlier phases per the dch architecture) with the new statistical bounds.
6. Pure helpers (ICRS spherical→Cartesian, absmag formula) lift to module scope or a `*-pure.ts` sibling so they are testable, per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]].

**No binary format change.** `dist`, `x0/y0/z0`, `absmag` are existing fields. The v6 binary format from dch.33 is unaffected; both beads can land independently within Phase 3.

# Acceptance criteria

- Build runs to completion; `public/catalog.bin` regenerates.
- Log line shows ≥ 90% override coverage (close to B-J's coverage of AT-HYG.gaia).
- **Three** of the four catastrophic outliers see `dist` reduce by ≥ 25% relative to current AT-HYG value:
  - HIP 22365 (9963 → 6245 pc, 37% drop)
  - HIP 25733 (14327 → 5466 pc, 62% drop)
  - HIP 38430 (12658 → 6215 pc, 51% drop)
  - HIP 46144 (9190 → 7515 pc, **18% drop only** — flag=10022 vs the others' 10033, lower posterior S/N).
  Pin each numerically with `toBe(...)` on the B-J map value in tier-A vitest. HIP 46144 gets its own assertion at the 15-20% band rather than the 25% threshold.
- HIP 23785 (well-measured F-dwarf at 93 pc): `|Δdist| / dist < 5%` — no degradation for good measurements.
- Internal consistency post-override: `sqrt(x0² + y0² + z0²) ≈ dist` to float32 precision; `mag − 5·log10(dist/10) ≈ absmag`.
- Pure helpers tested as `*-pure.ts` siblings; constants imported, not redefined.

# Smoke

After build:
- `npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts` — confirm row count unchanged, header layout unchanged.
- Run dev server, focus or warp to HIP 25733 — confirm it pulls back to a plausible disc-residing position.
- Confirm HIP 23785 visually unchanged.

# Blocked by

dch.46 — refresh-bailer-jones.py producing `data/bailer-jones-dr3.tsv`.

# Connects

[[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] — Bayesian distances for every catalog star with a Gaia ID, uniformly.
[[stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep]] — `dist_src` enum gains a new value; sweep SCIENCE.md + docs at commit time.
[[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] — numeric headlines (override count, the four named outliers' new distances) pinned with `toBe(...)`.
Sibling Phase 3 beads dch.34 (GCVS cross-match) and dch.35 (CCDM cross-match) are independent — same phase but separate `build-catalog.ts` change points.
acceptance_criteria:
- Build completes; public/catalog.bin regenerates without errors.
- Override coverage ≥ 90% logged at build time.
- HIP 22365 / 25733 / 38430 / 46144 distances drop ≥ 25% (tier-A vitest with toBe).
- HIP 23785 distance unchanged within 5% (regression guard for well-measured stars).
- x0²+y0²+z0² ≈ dist² and absmag = mag − 5·log10(dist/10) hold post-override.
- Pure helpers extracted to *-pure.ts; tests import constants, never redefine.
close_reason: Merged as PR #98 (commit cac044b). B-J override fires for 99.5% of Gaia-bearing rows; +15 stars rescued from prior >50kpc filter. Documented transient LMC regression — dch.8l0 is the lockstep partner queued next.

title: Phase 1 — scripts/refresh-bailer-jones.py + commit data/bailer-jones-dr3.tsv
description:
# Why

AT-HYG's `dist` is computed by naive 1/parallax (no Bayesian prior, no Lindegren zero-point correction). This catastrophically inverts low-S/N small parallaxes: ~20% of Gaia DR3 stars have negative measured parallaxes (no defined 1/π), and stars with parallax comparable to its own error get distances biased systematically long. Result in Stellata: a small population of distant luminous stars (OB supergiants etc.) renders outside the galactic-disc wireframe at 10-15 kpc when their true distances are more like 3-6 kpc. Concrete examples: HIP 25733 (cat: 14.3 kpc, gal R 22 kpc), HIP 22365, HIP 38430, HIP 46144 all sit visibly outside the 15 kpc midplane ring.

Bailer-Jones et al. (2021, AJ 161, 147; VizieR I/352) publishes Bayesian distance posteriors for every Gaia DR3 source by combining the Gaussian parallax likelihood with a direction-dependent Galactic-density prior. Two flavours per star:

- `r_med_geo` (geometric) — prior is direction-dependent Galactic 3D density model.
- `r_med_photogeo` (photogeometric) — adds BP/RP + apparent G via a CMD prior. Most informative.

The method handles negative parallaxes naturally (posterior collapses onto prior), self-targets the noisy-parallax regime (high-S/N stars are near-no-op since the likelihood dominates), and applies the Lindegren parallax zero-point correction upstream. This is the principled fix for the "outside the disc" outlier class — see [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] for the bar.

# Scope (one commit/PR)

1. New script `scripts/refresh-bailer-jones.py`. Reuses the shared TAP client from dch.21 (`scripts/refresh_lib.py`), configured CDS-only (see "Backend" below). Reads AT-HYG.gaia source_ids from `data/athyg_33_classic_ids.csv` and queries VizieR TAP (`I/352/gedr3dis`) with a batched `WHERE "Source" IN (...)` clause. Empirical batch sweet spot on CDS: 5000 source_ids per batch (~80 s per batch, superlinear above that).
2. Output schema (TSV): `source_id`, `r_med_geo`, `r_lo_geo`, `r_hi_geo`, `r_med_photogeo`, `r_lo_photogeo`, `r_hi_photogeo`, `flag`. The script renames VizieR's case-sensitive columns (`Source`, `rgeo`/`b_rgeo`/`B_rgeo`, `rpgeo`/`b_rpgeo`/`B_rpgeo`, `Flag`) to the paper's names on write — output is self-documenting against the Bailer-Jones 2021 reference.
3. Commit `data/bailer-jones-dr3.tsv` (LFS — expected ~15-20 MB for ~300k rows × 8 cols).
4. Per [[frozen-external-data]]: script is manual-run, NOT in `npm run build`. Idempotency via `refresh_lib.is_up_to_date`. Total refresh runtime ~85 minutes against CDS.
5. Document in the same `scripts/README.md` (or equivalent) used by sibling Phase 1 refresh scripts (dch.22-25, dch.29).

# Backend (not ESA→CDS)

Earlier draft of this bead said "Script supports the same TAP-fallback (ESA → CDS) behaviour as the dch.21 shared client." That is WRONG — I/352/gedr3dis is a VizieR-only table; the ESA Gaia archive returns a 500 with no response context and refresh_lib does not classify it as transient, so the default ESA→CDS fallback never triggers. The script passes `backends=[cds_backend]` to `TapClient` explicitly. The refresh_lib API supports this directly (custom `backends=` arg) — no library change needed.

# Coverage expectation

≥ 90% of AT-HYG.gaia source_ids should resolve to a B-J row. The ~10% gap is mostly very bright HIP stars Gaia couldn't measure (same coverage cliff Gaia's own astrometry hits in dch.29). The build-catalog integration (next bead) falls back to AT-HYG's value for these — no NaN risk. Empirical probe on the first 5000 AT-HYG source_ids returned 4935 rows (98.7%) — well above the ≥90% bar.

# Blocks

build-catalog.ts B-J distance override (next bead, depends on this output file).

# Connects

[[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] — Bayesian-distance fix is exactly the "systematic data engineering, not famous-star carve-outs" approach the principle calls for.
[[frozen-external-data]] — committed-file + manual-refresh pattern.
[[stellata-bd-operations]] — refresh-script naming + manual cadence matches Phase 1 sibling beads.
acceptance_criteria:
- Script runs end-to-end against VizieR TAP; produces non-empty TSV with the documented schema.
- ≥ 90% of AT-HYG.gaia source_ids resolve to a B-J row.
- Spot-check HIP 25733 (Gaia source_id present in AT-HYG): r_med_photogeo < AT-HYG dist (14327 pc) by ≥ 25%.
- Spot-check HIP 23785 (well-measured nearby F-dwarf): |r_med_photogeo − AT-HYG dist| / dist < 5%.
- Output file is committed and LFS-tracked.
- Script supports the same TAP-fallback (ESA → CDS) behaviour as the dch.21 shared client.

title: Kinematic distance refinement for LMC supergiants in AT-HYG
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic stellata-dch — this bead is Phase 3 Bead AA: LMC kinematic distance refinement layer).

# Goal

Replace Gaia DR3 parallax-inversion distances with the LMC eclipsing-binary distance (49.59 kpc, Pietrzyński 2019) for AT-HYG stars that pass a kinematic + sky-cone filter identifying them as LMC members. Affects ~60 stars currently misrendered as a "line of stars between MW and LMC in the intergalactic void".

# Why

Diagnosed during stellata-38m PR (LG wireframes) review. In the LMC sky direction (RA 4-6 h, Dec −65 to −75°) AT-HYG carries:

- 2551 stars at < 5 kpc — bonafide MW foreground (correct).
- **31 stars at 5-30 kpc** — appears as "intergalactic stars".
- 18 stars at 30-60 kpc — LMC main body.
- 11 stars at > 60 kpc, some pushed to 196 kpc.

All 60 stars beyond 5 kpc show:
- Proper motion ~(+1.9, +0.3) mas/yr — matches LMC bulk motion (van der Marel 2002) to within scatter.
- HDE numbers in 268xxx-271xxx range — the Henry Draper Extension's LMC concentration.
- B-supergiant spectral types where present (e.g. HD 268749 "B 7 IAB" at apparent 13 kpc).

They're all really at the LMC distance. Gaia DR3 parallax at d=50 kpc is 0.02 mas with per-source uncertainty 0.02-0.05 mas — SNR ≈ 1, so parallax-inversion smears distances over an order of magnitude. Real-world LMC publications use eclipsing binaries / Cepheids; AT-HYG's distance-inversion path is the wrong tool at this scale.

Specific to LMC because SMC and Sgr supergiants are too faint to enter AT-HYG's brightness cut (G ~10-12.5). LMC's brightest B-supergiants happen to land just inside.

# Ordering with Phase 3 Bead Z (Bailer-Jones distance override)

**This bead runs AFTER stellata-dch.47 (Bead Z, Bailer-Jones distance override).** B-J first fixes catastrophic 1/π inversion across the bulk catalog; then this bead overrides LMC-cone stars (~60 entries) to Pietrzyński 2019's 49.59 kpc. Order matters: if B-J ran second, it would clobber the kinematic value because LMC stars have Gaia source_ids and would be in the B-J data file.

**Why B-J alone doesn't fix this**: B-J's prior is a smooth Galactic-density model; the LMC isn't in the prior. B-J posteriors for LMC stars peak somewhere intermediate (5-20 kpc), not at 49.59 kpc. The kinematic sky-cone + PM filter is the principled "solve the class" fix per stellata-data-fidelity-principle. Soft awareness link to dch.47 is wired via `bd dep add stellata-8l0 stellata-dch.47 --type=related`.

Without this bead, Bead Z (dch.47) alone introduces a transient LMC regression vs today's behaviour — the two beads ship in lockstep. Priority bumped from P2 → P1 to reflect this coupling.

# Approach

In `scripts/build-catalog.ts`, after the B-J distance override (Bead Z) and before the `MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000` cutoff (`scripts/build-catalog.ts:47`):

1. Test sky position: is the star inside an LMC cone (centre RA 78.76°, Dec −69.19°, half-angle ~15°)?
2. Test proper motion: is `(pmra, pmdec)` within `±0.5 mas/yr` of `(+1.85, +0.20)`?
3. If both pass, override the distance to the LMC's eclipsing-binary distance (49.59 kpc, Pietrzyński 2019). Recompute x0/y0/z0 from (ra, dec, distance_override). Recompute absmag from (mag, distance_override).
4. Tag the row's `dist_src` as `LMC_KIN`, joining the enum `{HIP, GJ, T, N, G_R3, BJ, LMC_KIN}` so downstream consumers (e.g. Bead AB / dch.45's CCDM optical-double filter) can identify kinematically-refined entries.

Parameters (cone angle, PM tolerance, target distance) live as named constants in `catalog-pure.ts` so they're testable + tunable + extensible to future populations (SMC and Sgr will graduate into this regime when DR4 lands or AT-HYG goes deeper).

# Cutoff role (clarified)

The `MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000` cutoff (`scripts/build-catalog.ts:47`) stays in place AFTER this bead's override is applied. Its role becomes a **bounded-scope safety gate**, NOT a primary include/exclude filter:

- Stars refined to 49.59 kpc by this bead survive the cutoff (just inside).
- Stars still > 50 kpc post-refinement (no kinematic match in any modelled population) are dropped as unmodelled extragalactic — out of scope for v1.
- The cutoff value is tuned exactly to the modelled scope. When SMC/Sgr/M31 refinement layers land, bump the cutoff accordingly.

Documentation update lives in dch.42 (SCIENCE.md rewrite) — the SCIENCE.md:98-99 cutoff prose gets reframed from "safety net for catalog noise" to "bounded-scope final gate after refinement layers."

# Acceptance

- `npm run build:catalog` runs cleanly.
- A new test in `catalog-pure.test.ts` exercises the kinematic filter:
  - LMC-direction + LMC-PM star at parallax-inversion distance 13 kpc → distance overridden to 49590 pc.
  - LMC-direction + NON-LMC-PM star (e.g. high-velocity halo star) → distance unchanged.
  - NON-LMC-direction + LMC-like PM (e.g. Solar Neighbourhood star with similar PM) → distance unchanged.
  - SMC-direction + SMC-PM star (if any) → distance unchanged (no SMC handler yet).
- Ordering test: a synthetic LMC-cone + LMC-PM star with a B-J entry confirms LMC_KIN wins over BJ (this bead runs after Bead Z).
- `dist_src = "LMC_KIN"` and absmag recomputed (`mag − 5·log10(dist/10)`) on every overridden row.
- Verify post-build: query the catalog for stars in the LMC cone at non-LMC distance → expect 0 (or only halo K-giants that pass the PM cut should fail it; ~0 expected in this region).
- Visual smoke (stellata-38m PR's LMC wireframe should look cleaner): no "smear" of stars stretched between 5-200 kpc in the LMC direction; instead a concentrated 50 kpc shell.
- Tier-A vitest under Bead R covers 2-3 LMC B-supergiants from the HDE 268xxx range (added to corpus in Bead Q / dch.37).

# Why this approach (vs alternatives)

- **Filter (drop the rows)**: throws information away. We KNOW these are LMC stars; deletion is worse than refinement.
- **Accept (do nothing)**: faithful to Gaia DR3 but the visual is wrong and stellata-data-fidelity-principle prefers fixed-at-build to user-confusing-at-runtime.
- **Hand-curated overrides per star**: solves the visible exemplars, not the class — fails when AT-HYG / DR4 grows. Wrong per stellata-data-fidelity-principle.
- **Rely on B-J alone**: B-J's Galactic-density prior doesn't cover the LMC; posteriors land somewhere intermediate (5-20 kpc), not at 49.59 kpc. B-J handles the bulk-catalog catastrophic-inversion problem; LMC kinematic refinement is the LMC-specific second layer.

Kinematic + sky-cone filter is the "solve the class" approach: scales to future catalogs, applies to SMC / Sgr / M31 when their populations enter the brightness regime, and uses well-established LMC kinematics from the literature.

# References

- van der Marel & Kallivayalil 2014, ApJ 781, 121 — LMC bulk PM
- Pietrzyński et al. 2019, Nature 567, 200 — LMC distance (eclipsing binaries; 49.594 ± 0.55 kpc)
- Gaia Collaboration et al. 2021 — DR3 parallax uncertainty floor
- Bailer-Jones et al. 2021, AJ 161, 147 — Bayesian distance posteriors (the layer this bead runs on top of)

# Out of scope

- SMC / Sgr / other Magellanic-system or Local Group populations — they're not in AT-HYG depth yet. Same approach extends when they appear (file a sibling kinematic-override bead following this bead's pattern; bump the cutoff as each new modelled population is added).
- Magellanic Stream stars — too faint for AT-HYG.
- Hypervelocity stars and runaways genuinely at intermediate distances — assume they fail the LMC-PM test (different velocities). The kinematic filter must be tight enough to leave them alone.
close_reason: LMC kinematic distance refinement landed in PR #99 (squash commit d3af8f5, v2.1.0). 54 / 4727 LMC-cone stars snapped to Pietrzyński 2019's 49.594 kpc. Also folded in the dch.47 dist_src gate fix (HIP / GJ / N / OTHER rows no longer over-eagerly B-J-overridden). Six follow-up beads filed and wired: dch.54 (Dec PM widen), dch.55 (SMC sibling), dch.56 (LMC structural-disc fallback), dch.57 (SIMBAD + self-consistency QA), dch.58 (OGLE Cepheid cross-match), epic mju (Magellanic stellar-population overlay).

title: Mid-Fly recentre + pin-guard relax for navigate-mode warp arrivals
description:
Smoke-discovered regression on PR #66. The cubic-Hermite log-distance Fly profile exposes a latent Float32 precision fault in navigate-mode warp arrivals: while the camera is in the source-star's local frame and gets within ~ULP(|B|) of the destination, lookAt(B) per frame jitters as B − camera.position loses precision. For a 200pc warp ending at 1 AU, ULP(|B|) ≈ 2.4e-5 pc ≈ 5 AU, and the cubic-Hermite log-d profile spends ~last 19% of Fly inside that chaos zone (vs ~0.0001s under the old piecewise-quadratic, which the slam hid).

Two visible symptoms, single root cause:
1. **Sol off-screen on arrival, jumps in at finishWarp.** lookAt jitter occasionally lands the destination outside the NDC frustum; finishWarp's setFocus → recenterOrigin → target.snap(0,0,0) drops the destination at local (0,0,0), the pin engages, snap to centre.
2. **Discrete-stage jitter on approach.** The lookAt quaternion stepping across Float32 representations of B.

Same class as stellata-fqw (observe→observe Phase 3), already fixed there by pulling recenterFocusToStar forward into Phase 3 start. This bead extends the same convention to navigate-mode Fly.

# Fix

1. Add `shiftArrivalWaypoints(state, dx, dy, dz)` to camera-motion.ts. Shifts ArrivalState's pStart / pEnd / target.center in place. d0 / dEnd / dir are translation-invariant, no recompute. Sibling to shiftWarpWaypoints in warp-pure.ts.

2. Add `recenteredToDest: boolean` to WarpState (init false in startWarp).

3. updateWarp Fly branch: after tickArrival, if `!state.recenteredToDest && state.destKind === 'star'` and the camera is past the trajectory midpoint, call recenterFocusToStar(destIdx), shiftWarpWaypoints + shiftArrivalWaypoints with the returned delta, set recenteredToDest = true. lookAt(B) from then on becomes lookAt(0,0,0) — numerically clean.

4. Relax isPinEngaged: `!warpState` → `!warpState || warpState.recenteredToDest`. After mid-Fly recentre, focusedStar = destination, camera is doing lookAt(B) per frame (= lookAt(local origin)), and controls.target.lengthSq() < threshold (target was snapped by setFocus's recentre). All three pin preconditions are restored. The shader-pin then bypasses any residual Float32 projection-chain noise via uPinFocusToCenter = projectionMatrix * vec4(0, 0, -dPc, 1).

5. finishWarp navigate-arrival branch: if state.recenteredToDest, focusedStar already === destIdx so setFocus(destIdx) would short-circuit (line 1436-1438) and never emit 'focus' / 'state'. Detect early-recentre and emit them explicitly.

# Scope decisions

- Trigger: "camera past trajectory midpoint" — projection of (camera − pStart) onto (pEnd − pStart) > 0.5 · |pEnd − pStart|. Matches alex's stated convention ("source star behind camera"). Fires roughly at u_eased = 0.5, comfortably before the chaos zone (which starts at u_eased ≈ 0.66 for the 200pc → 1 AU case).
- Star arrivals only. Cloud arrivals don't recentre origin (setFocusedCloud doesn't call recenterOrigin), so the same fault exists for cloud warps but needs its own infrastructure. File as follow-up.

# Acceptance

- Manual smoke (alex): Betelgeuse focus from 200 pc — no off-screen / no discrete-stage jitter. Warp Sol → Betelgeuse — same. Cloud focus — unchanged (out of scope).
- Unit: shiftArrivalWaypoints translation test + invariance of d0/dEnd/dir.

# Closes on merge

stellata-2br PR #66 (umbrella).

title: Phase 6 — CLAUDE.md module roster + Local Commands + docs/build-and-data.md + RELEASING.md sweep
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Final documentation update tying everything together. Depends on stellata-dch.42 (SCIENCE.md) + stellata-dch.43 (docs/cross-match.md).

# Per memory defer-doc-updates AND stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep

This is the place to catch every stale reference to the OLD pipeline (build-binaries.py shape, refresh-gaia-binaries.py, gaia_dr3_binaries.tsv, position-based filtering language) and update to the new state.

# Deliverables

- CLAUDE.md updates:
  - Module roster — add: scripts/refresh_lib.py, scripts/refresh-gaia-hip-xmatch.py, scripts/refresh-gaia-tyc-xmatch.py, scripts/refresh-gaia-astrometry.py, scripts/refresh-gaia-nss.py, scripts/refresh-hipparcos2.py, scripts/refresh-simbad-sample.py, **scripts/refresh-bailer-jones.py**, **scripts/refresh-gaia-apsis.py**, scripts/validate-simbad-sample.py, scripts/known-stars.tsv, scripts/requirements-refresh.txt.
  - Module roster — remove or update: scripts/refresh-gaia-binaries.py (deleted), data/gaia_dr3_binaries.tsv (deleted), data/multiples-overrides.tsv (kept but shrunken).
  - Local Commands — add: npm run refresh:gaia-hip, refresh:gaia-tyc, refresh:gaia-astrometry, refresh:gaia-nss, refresh:hip2, refresh:simbad, **refresh:bailer-jones**, **refresh:gaia-apsis**, validate:simbad.
  - Documentation index — add docs/cross-match.md row with reading-time + scope.

- docs/build-and-data.md updates:

title: Phase 6 — docs/cross-match.md: developer walk-through of source-ID resolution
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Authoring of a new developer-facing doc that walks through the catalogue cross-match logic in detail. Complements SCIENCE.md (which is for the astronomer audience): this doc is for the engineer who needs to extend the pipeline, refresh data when DR4 lands, or debug a resolution failure for a specific star. Depends on Phase 2 + Phase 3 beads landed.

# Per memory defer-doc-updates

Authored AFTER the code lands. Reading the final implementation, then documenting it.

# Deliverables

- docs/cross-match.md (new, ~10-minute read) — sections:

  ## When to read this doc
  - You're refreshing external catalogs (e.g. Gaia DR4 landed); see also: docs/build-and-data.md.
  - You're adding a known-star to the validation corpus.
  - You're debugging why a specific star doesn't render correctly.

  ## The 5-tier resolution chain
  - Each tier explained: what it does, when it's used, what failure mode it handles.
  - Worked examples: Sirius A (orb6_hip), Proxima (athyg_gaia_native), a faint Tycho-only star (position_pm), an unmatchable star (unresolved).

  ## Astrometry routing
  - gaia_5p / gaia_nss_systemic / hip2_long_baseline: when each fires, why each is correct in its regime.
  - The bright-binary-contamination problem and how the cascade handles it.

  ## Orbital element selection
  - NSS > ORB6 > ORB6_spectroscopic > none priority.
  - Conversion from NSS / ORB6 published units to canonical v5 schema.

  ## Multi-layer distance-refinement stack (NEW section, drives Beads Y + Z + AA)
  - **Layer 1 — AT-HYG 1/π baseline**: today's behaviour. Catastrophically wrong for low-S/N parallaxes.
  - **Layer 2 — Bailer-Jones (2021) Bayesian override** (Bead Z / dch.47): `r_med_photogeo` ‖ `r_med_geo`. Direction-dependent Galactic prior + CMD prior. Self-targets noisy regime; near-no-op for high-S/N stars. Applies Lindegren et al. (2021) zero-point upstream. **Known limitation**: B-J's Galactic-density prior doesn't cover the LMC; LMC supergiants get pulled to intermediate-wrong distances.
  - **Layer 3 — LMC kinematic + sky-cone override** (Bead AA / stellata-8l0): sky-cone (LMC centre, half-angle) + PM gate identifies LMC members; distance overridden to Pietrzyński 2019's 49.59 kpc. `dist_src = "LMC_KIN"`.
  - **Ordering constraint** (CRITICAL): B-J runs first (Bead Z), LMC kinematic runs second (Bead AA). If reversed, B-J clobbers the kinematic override because LMC stars have Gaia source_ids. Encoded as a `--type=related` link in bd.
  - **Layer 4 — `MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000` bounded-scope safety gate** (`scripts/build-catalog.ts:47`): applied LAST. Drops anything still beyond LMC as unmodelled extragalactic. Cutoff is a function of what populations are modelled — NOT a primary include/exclude filter.
  - **Extending to SMC/Sgr/M31**: when those populations enter AT-HYG depth (DR4 or AT-HYG deepening), file a sibling kinematic-override bead following Bead AA's pattern. Bump the cutoff as each new modelled population is added.
  - **Stars not covered**: rows without a Gaia source_id retain AT-HYG values (1/π); flagged with the original `dist_src` (`HIP`, `GJ`, etc.).

  ## Astrophysical parameters (Apsis) per star (NEW section, drives Beads BB + CC — `dch.48` + `dch.49`)
  - **What Apsis is**: Gaia DR3 publishes two parallel astrophysical-parameter pipelines per source_id — `gspphot` (photometric fit to BP/RP + parallax) and `gspspec` (spectroscopic fit to RVS spectra). Each emits Teff / logg / [M/H]; gspphot additionally emits A0 line-of-sight extinction at 547.7 nm.
  - **Coverage**: empirical probe (`stellata-zsr.1`, 1000-star sample, seed 20260517) — gspphot ∪ gspspec covers 84.8% of AT-HYG.gaia for (Teff, logg). Status-quo spectral-class parsing covers 29.3%. Net uplift: 2.87×.
  - **Routing in build-catalog.ts** (lives in `stellata-dch.49`): prefer `teff_gspphot` (broader coverage, ~80%) → fall back to `teff_gspspec` (~5% incremental) → fall back to spectral-class T_TABLE (~15% gap, faint stars without high-S/N BP/RP). Per-row `teff_src` field records the chosen source.
  - **Downstream consumers** (each filed as its own follow-on bead):
    - Tier 1 colour LUT (`stellata-zsr.3`) — re-keys Teff source from Ballesteros(B-V) → Apsis-direct where available.
    - Phase 5 Bead T (`stellata-dch.40`) — direct logg improves giant / subgiant mass-ratio estimates.
    - Phase 5 Bead U (`stellata-dch.41`) — direct Teff feeds limb-darkening coefficient selection.
    - Edenhofer dust-map cross-check — uses `azero_gspphot` as an Apsis-side prior on line-of-sight extinction; filed when desired.
  - **DR4 refresh path**: `scripts/refresh-gaia-apsis.py` re-keys onto DR4 source_ids when the next data release lands. Cross-walk follows the same pattern as Bailer-Jones / astrometry (DR3↔DR4 source-id mapping during the transition window).
  - **Stars not covered**: the ~15% gap (faint Tycho-only stars, hot O/B without reliable GSP-Phot fit) falls back to spectral-class T_TABLE. Coverage degradation by magnitude is logged at build time for visibility.

  ## Optical-pair filter
  - 4-tier cascade explained.
  - The asymmetric-Gaia gate worked example (Sirius A-C).

  ## Adding to the known-stars corpus
  - Step-by-step: pick a star, cite expected values, add the row, run tests.

  ## Refreshing data when DR4 / new AT-HYG lands
  - Step-by-step: which refresh scripts to run, in which order, expected runtimes, smoke-checks per refresh.
  - **Bailer-Jones DR4 cadence**: B-J publishes a DR4-keyed catalog after each Gaia release. Refresh `data/bailer-jones-dr3.tsv` → `data/bailer-jones-dr4.tsv` in lockstep with the AT-HYG DR4 swap; Gaia provides DR3↔DR4 source-id cross-walks for the transition window.

  ## Debug recipes
  - 'Star X renders at wrong distance' — grep multiples.tsv for the system, check resolve_via, check astrometry_via, check the source files. For distance specifically: check `dist_src` value (`G_R3` raw / `BJ` refined / `LMC_KIN` LMC-overridden / `HIP` or `GJ` AT-HYG fallback / `N` dropped).
  - 'New WDS pair appears as spurious optical' — check WDS Notes flag, both components' Gaia coverage, asymm-gate eligibility.

# Acceptance

- A fresh contributor reads docs/cross-match.md cold and successfully picks up a Phase-2-equivalent debug task (e.g. 'figure out why 70 Oph is rendering at distance X instead of Y').
- A fresh contributor can also pick up a Phase-3 distance-refinement debug task (e.g. 'figure out why this LMC supergiant is at 13 kpc instead of 49.59 kpc') using only this doc.
- A fresh contributor can pick up an Apsis-routing debug task (e.g. 'figure out why this star uses spectral-class T_TABLE instead of teff_gspphot') using the Apsis section's routing prose + the per-row `teff_src` field.
- All code references in the doc (file:line) are accurate at commit time.
- Links to memories (stellata-data-fidelity-principle, frozen-external-data) embedded.

# Smoke

- Read it cold; verify it makes sense.
- A second reviewer reads it cold; ditto.

# Files to touch

- docs/cross-match.md (new)
- CLAUDE.md docs index — add a row for docs/cross-match.md in the next bead (stellata-dch.44)

# Commit

One commit. PR labelled skip-version-bump.

title: Phase 6 — SCIENCE.md catalogue-pipeline section rewrite with citations
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Rewrite the 'Multiple-star pipeline' and 'Stellar catalog ingestion' sections of SCIENCE.md to reflect the new source-ID-anchored architecture, with full citations for every cross-walk table and every science decision. Per the data-fidelity principle: 'an astronomer asking about Stellata's catalogue pipeline at any level should hear an answer we're proud of.' This is the public-facing science doc that delivers on that bar. Depends on all Phase 2-5 beads landed (the actual implementation; we document what was built).

# Per memory defer-doc-updates

Doc rewrite happens AFTER the code lands, not before. Reading the actual final diff for each phase, then writing the corresponding science prose, captures the as-built state and avoids the doc-drift trap.

# Deliverables

- SCIENCE.md — sections 'Multiple-star pipeline' and 'Stellar catalog ingestion' rewritten top-to-bottom. Content shape:

  ## Stellar catalog ingestion (rewrite)
  - AT-HYG v3.3 as base; what each *_src column means; per-row provenance.
  - Layer 1 reference data table (every committed external file, source, refresh cadence).
  - Layer 2 refresh scripts; manual-run rationale per frozen-external-data principle.
  - v6 binary format; gaia_source_id field; per-record provenance fields.
  - Cross-match passes: GCVS (gaia_source_id primary key), CCDM (HIP-anchored).
  - **Multi-layer distance-refinement pipeline** (NEW subsection, drives Beads Y + Z + AA):
    - The catastrophic-1/π-inversion problem (low-S/N parallaxes produce divergent posterior mean; biased high; ~20% of DR3 stars have negative parallaxes; Lutz-Kelker selection bias; Lindegren et al. (2021) zero-point offset).
    - **Bailer-Jones (2021) Bayesian posterior** (Bead Z / dch.47) — `r_med_photogeo` ‖ `r_med_geo` from VizieR I/352. Direction-dependent Galactic prior + CMD prior (BP/RP photometry). Handles negative parallaxes via prior dominance; self-targets noisy regime (near-no-op for high-S/N); zero-point correction applied upstream. Worked example: HIP 25733 (cat 14.3 kpc → B-J ~5-7 kpc).
    - **LMC kinematic + sky-cone override** (Bead AA / stellata-8l0) — Pietrzyński et al. (2019) eclipsing-binary distance 49.59 kpc anchored on sky-cone + proper-motion gate identifying LMC members. Why B-J alone doesn't fix this: smooth Galactic-density prior doesn't cover the LMC; posteriors land somewhere intermediate (5-20 kpc). The kinematic filter is the principled "solve the class" fix per stellata-data-fidelity-principle; extends to SMC/Sgr/M31 when those populations enter AT-HYG depth.
    - **`MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000` bounded-scope safety gate** — applied LAST, after refinement layers. Reframe SCIENCE.md:98-99 prose: cutoff is NOT a primary include/exclude filter; it's a modelled-scope statement tuned to the populations the catalog represents (Sol → LMC for v1). Future satellite refinements bump it.
    - **`dist_src` enum extension `{HIP, GJ, T, N, G_R3, BJ, LMC_KIN, ...}`** documented with layer-per-value semantics.
  - **Astrophysical parameters (Apsis) per star** (NEW subsection, drives Beads BB + CC — `dch.48` + `dch.49`):
    - What Apsis is: Gaia DR3's two astrophysical-parameter pipelines — `gspphot` (photometric fit to BP/RP + parallax) and `gspspec` (spectroscopic fit to RVS spectra). Each emits Teff / logg / [M/H], and gspphot additionally emits A0 (line-of-sight monochromatic extinction at 547.7 nm).
    - Coverage uplift: empirical probe in `stellata-zsr.1` (1000-star random sample of AT-HYG.gaia, seed 20260517) showed (Teff, logg) goes from 29.3% via spectral-class parsing to 84.8% via gspphot ∪ gspspec — a 2.87× improvement.
    - Routing in build-catalog.ts: prefer gspphot Teff (broader coverage; ~80%), fall back to gspspec where gspphot null (~5% incremental); fall back to spectral-class T_TABLE for the ~15% gap (faint stars without high-S/N BP/RP photometry).
    - Downstream consumers: Tier 1 colour LUT re-keys onto Apsis-direct Teff (`stellata-zsr.3`); Phase 5 mass-ratio gains direct logg for giants / subgiants (`stellata-dch.40` Bead T); Phase 5 eclipse photometry gains direct Teff for limb-darkening (`stellata-dch.41` Bead U); azero_gspphot cross-checks the Edenhofer dust map.
    - DR4 refresh: `refresh-gaia-apsis.py` re-keys onto DR4 source_ids when the next data release lands; cross-walk follows the same pattern as Bailer-Jones / astrometry.

  ## Multiple-star pipeline (rewrite)
  - Why this exists (current section, kept).
  - Three regimes (kept; updated to reference new astrometry sources).
  - Stage 1-7 catalogue builder architecture with citations.
  - Resolution chain: 5 tiers, each tier's data source + citation.
  - Astrometry routing: gaia_5p / gaia_nss_systemic / hip2_long_baseline; citation for each.
  - Orbital element selection: NSS > ORB6 with rationale + citation (Gaia DR3 NSS reference paper Pourbaix et al. 2022; ORB6 reference).
  - Optical-pair filter cascade: 4 tiers; asymmetric-Gaia-gate rationale + the Sirius A-C example as worked case.
  - Mass-ratio q tables: WD / giant / subgiant / main-sequence with Cox 2000 / Allen 2000 citations.

# Citations to include (with full bibliographic info)

- Gaia DR3 main reference: Gaia Collaboration et al. 2023, A&A 674, A1
- Gaia DR3 NSS reference: Pourbaix et al. 2022, A&A 674, A34
- Hipparcos van Leeuwen 2007 reduction: van Leeuwen 2007, A&A 474, 653
- Tycho-2: Hog et al. 2000, A&A 355, L27
- WDS: Mason et al. 2001, AJ 122, 3466
- ORB6: Hartkopf et al. (Sixth Catalog of Orbits of Visual Binary Stars; USNO)
- GCVS: Samus et al. 2017, ARep 61, 80
- SIMBAD: Wenger et al. 2000, A&AS 143, 9
- Allen 2000 / Cox 2000 (Allen's Astrophysical Quantities, 4th ed.)
- **Bailer-Jones et al. 2021, AJ 161, 147** (geometric + photogeometric distance posteriors)
- **Lindegren et al. 2021, A&A 649, A4** (Gaia DR3 parallax zero-point correction)
- **Andrae et al. 2023, A&A 674, A27** (Gaia DR3 GSP-Phot — photometric astrophysical parameters)
- **Recio-Blanco et al. 2023, A&A 674, A29** (Gaia DR3 GSP-Spec — spectroscopic astrophysical parameters)
- **Pietrzyński et al. 2019, Nature 567, 200** (LMC distance from eclipsing binaries — 49.594 ± 0.55 kpc)
- **van der Marel & Kallivayalil 2014, ApJ 781, 121** (LMC bulk proper motion)

# Acceptance

- Section flow: an external astronomer reading SCIENCE.md cold can follow the pipeline end-to-end and verify Stellata's choices against literature.
- Every science-driven decision has a citation.
- Worked examples (Sirius A-B, α Cen A-B+Proxima, Algol, HIP 25733 B-J refinement, an LMC supergiant) demonstrate the cascade behavior.
- The new "Multi-layer distance-refinement pipeline" subsection clearly explains why B-J alone doesn't fix LMC stars and why the cutoff is a bounded-scope statement rather than a filter.
- The new "Astrophysical parameters (Apsis) per star" subsection clearly explains gspphot vs gspspec, the 84.8% coverage number with its empirical-probe provenance, and the spectral-class fallback path for the ~15% gap.
- No v1.0 / v1-era prose (Phase 0 sweep already complete).
- grep 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX' SCIENCE.md returns no hits in the rewritten sections.

# Smoke

- Read SCIENCE.md cold (or have a reviewer read it).
- Verify two arbitrary stars from the catalogue (one nearby high-S/N, one distant low-S/N) can be traced through the pipeline by reading only this doc.
- Verify the cutoff reframing reads as a modelled-scope statement, not a hardcoded filter.

# Files to touch

- SCIENCE.md (rewrite of two named sections, keep others intact; update :98-99 cutoff prose)

# Commit

One commit. PR labelled skip-version-bump (docs-only).

title: Phase 4 — scripts/known-stars.test.ts: tier-A vitest assertions against built catalog
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Tier-A validation: vitest asserts every row in scripts/known-stars.tsv matches the built public/catalog.bin within documented tolerances. This is the regression-prevention surface for the entire pipeline at known-star granularity. Depends on stellata-dch.33 (v6 binary format), stellata-dch.37 (corpus), and benefits from stellata-dch.32 (multiples.tsv fully landed).

# Deliverables

- scripts/known-stars.test.ts (new) — vitest file. Loads scripts/known-stars.tsv via csv parser, loads public/catalog.bin via a reusable lookup library (see refactor below), for each corpus row asserts:
  - Primary lookup by HIP and by gaia_source_id both succeed
  - Primary distance within ±0.01 pc OR 1-sigma whichever is larger (toBeCloseTo with calibrated precision)
  - Primary absmag within ±0.05 (toBeCloseTo)
  - Primary spectral class index + subclass exact match (toBe)
  - For each companion: comp_letter present in multiples.tsv, comp's hip + gaia_source_id match, comp's absmag within ±0.05
  - For systems with orbital_period_days: orbit_via != 'none', period within ±5%
- scripts/verify-catalog.ts — REFACTOR (per memory stellata-named-constants-and-dry) to expose loadCatalog(path), lookupByHip(catalog, hip), lookupByGaiaSourceId(catalog, source_id) as library functions, so known-stars.test.ts can import them instead of duplicating parse logic.

# Test assertions per memory stellata-test-coverage-discipline

- Numeric headline claims pinned with toBe(N) where possible, NOT toBeLessThanOrEqual.
- Each test failure shows actionable diagnostic: 'Sirius A: expected distance 2.64 pc, got 2.71 pc (diff 0.07 pc > tolerance 0.01 pc)'.
- Per-tier breakdown: separate test describe blocks for single stars, visual binaries, NSS-orbited systems.

# Acceptance

- All 50+ corpus rows pass.
- Any failure shows a clear diagnostic naming the corpus row + the field + the discrepancy.
- npm test green.
- Test takes < 5 seconds (loads catalog once, iterates corpus).

# Smoke

- npm test (catalog.bin must exist)
- Deliberately mutate one corpus row's expected_distance and verify the test FAILS with a clear message; revert.

# Files to touch

- scripts/known-stars.test.ts (new)
- scripts/verify-catalog.ts (refactor for reusable lookup)

# Commit

One commit. PR labelled skip-version-bump (validation infra, not user-visible).

title: Phase 4 — scripts/known-stars.tsv: 50+ hand-verified system corpus (single stars + binaries)
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Curate the validation corpus: a TSV of 50-80 hand-verified stars/systems with expected values for use by Phase 4 Bead R (tier-A vitest assertions). MUST include single stars AND binaries — single-star coverage is part of the explicit requirement per parent epic 'Coverage across the full catalogue' section. No external dependencies; can run in parallel with all other phases. The LARGEST manual effort in the rewrite (~25-40 hours hand-verification work).

# Deliverables

- scripts/known-stars.tsv (new) — TSV with columns:
  - wds_id (nullable for single stars)
  - system_name (e.g. 'Sirius', 'α Cen', 'Vega', '61 Cygni')
  - primary_hip (nullable for HIP-less)
  - primary_gaia_source_id (int64, the canonical anchor)
  - primary_distance_pc (float)
  - primary_distance_pc_err (float, 1-sigma)
  - primary_absmag (float)
  - primary_spectral (string)
  - companions (semicolon-separated 'comp_letter:hip:gaia_id:absmag' tuples, EMPTY string for single stars)
  - orbital_period_days (nullable; only set for systems with well-determined orbits)
  - notes_source (string — citation: 'SIMBAD <permalink>' / 'ORB6 entry P=NN' / 'Gaia DR3 ADQL spot-check 2026-MM-DD' / 'van Leeuwen 2007 HIP2' / etc.)

# Coverage requirement (explicit)

Suggested mix of 50-80 entries:
- ~15 single stars: Sol (HIP 0 / synthetic), Vega (HIP 91262), Polaris (HIP 11767), Altair (HIP 97649), Procyon-as-single (HIP 37279 ignoring Procyon B), faint Tycho-population samples chosen for magnitude diversity (~5-10 stars at V > 11).
- ~25 famous visual binaries: Sirius AB (HIP 32349 + DA2 companion), α Centauri AB (HIP 71683 + HIP 71681), Castor system (HIP 36850 + HIP 36760, multi-component), 61 Cygni AB (HIP 104214 + HIP 104217), Procyon AB (HIP 37279 + WD), Algol (HIP 14576, eclipsing), β Cygni / Albireo (HIP 95947), γ Andromedae / Almach (HIP 9640), ζ Reticuli AB, etc.
- ~15 systems where current Stellata is known to mis-render (TBD via spot-checks during Phase 2 execution) OR where Gaia DR3 NSS provides orbital elements (so Phase 4 Bead R can validate NSS-vs-ORB6 selection).

# Distance-refinement regression cases (required)

Coupled to Phase 3 Beads Z (B-J distance override) and AA (LMC kinematic refinement). Each catches a different failure mode of naive 1/π distance:

- **B-J override regression cases** (currently rendered outside the galactic-disc wireframe by 1/π catastrophic inversion):
  - HIP 22365 (cat: ~9.96 kpc, gal R 17.9 kpc — outside the rim)
  - HIP 25733 (cat: ~14.33 kpc, gal R 22.4 kpc — outside the rim)
  - HIP 38430 (cat: ~12.66 kpc, gal R 17.9 kpc — outside the rim)
  - HIP 46144 (cat: ~9.19 kpc, gal z +6 kpc — above the ±400 pc thickness rings)
  
  Each row asserts post-B-J distance ≥ 25% smaller than current AT-HYG value (Tier-A vitest pins with `toBe(...)`).

- **B-J no-degradation regression guard** (well-measured nearby F-dwarf):
  - HIP 23785 (93 pc, normal high-S/N parallax)
  
  Row asserts post-B-J distance within ±5% of current AT-HYG value — B-J must not degrade good measurements.

- **LMC kinematic refinement regression cases** (currently mis-placed by 1/π inversion in the LMC direction):
  - 2-3 LMC B-supergiants from the HDE 268xxx range (e.g. HD 268749 "B 7 IAB", others TBD during corpus curation by grepping AT-HYG for HDE 268xxx-271xxx).
  
  Each row asserts post-LMC-kinematic distance = 49590 pc (Pietrzyński 2019), `dist_src = "LMC_KIN"`.

# Authoring discipline

- Every row has a notes_source citation (no row authored without traceability).
- Expected values verified against AT LEAST TWO independent sources where possible (SIMBAD + Gaia DR3 ADQL; ORB6 + Hipparcos van Leeuwen; etc.).
- Mass-ratio q expected values for binaries computed from spectral classes via current rules (so Bead T's mass-ratio refinement can produce expected updates).
- Tolerances documented in a header comment block of the TSV: distance ±0.01 pc OR 1-sigma whichever is larger; absmag ±0.05; spectral class index + subclass exact match; orbital period ±5%.

# Acceptance

- 50+ rows minimum, 80 maximum.
- Single-star coverage: at least 12 rows have empty companions field.
- Binary coverage: at least 20 rows have non-empty companions.
- Distance-refinement regression cases: 4 B-J override cases + 1 B-J no-degradation guard + 2-3 LMC kinematic cases (~7-8 rows total).
- Every row has a notes_source.
- Random 10-row spot-check via SIMBAD live (manual) reveals zero discrepancies.

# Smoke

- wc -l scripts/known-stars.tsv shows 50+ rows
- python3 -c 'import csv; rows = list(csv.DictReader(open("scripts/known-stars.tsv"), delimiter="\t")); print(sum(1 for r in rows if not r["companions"])); print(sum(1 for r in rows if r["companions"]))'
- Random sample 10 rows; cross-check against SIMBAD; expect zero discrepancies

# Files to touch

- scripts/known-stars.tsv (new)
- A small README block at top of the TSV documenting columns + tolerances + provenance discipline.

# Commit

One commit. PR labelled skip-version-bump (data-only, no user-visible behavior change). Standalone — independent of all other beads, can start immediately.

title: Phase 3 — build-catalog.ts GCVS cross-match: prefer gaia_source_id over hip/hd
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Re-anchor the GCVS variable-star cross-match to use Gaia DR3 source_id as the primary key, with HIP/HD as fallback. This benefits the ENTIRE catalog (every star, not just binaries) and is expected to increase the GCVS match rate for stars without HIP/HD identifiers. Depends on stellata-dch.33 (v6 binary surfaces gaia_source_id).

# Current state

build-catalog.ts:569-587 (applyVariability function) cross-matches GCVS rows to AT-HYG via byHip / byHd maps only. AT-HYG rows that have a gaia field but no hip/hd are NOT cross-matched even if GCVS has a Gaia source_id reference.

# Deliverables

- scripts/build-catalog.ts — extend applyVariability() to build a third index byGaia (gaia_source_id -> at_hyg_row) and check it first. Cross-match priority: byGaia (when both sides have gaia source_id) -> byHip -> byHd. Log match-source stats: 'GCVS matches: via gaia=X, via hip=Y, via hd=Z, unmatched=W'.
- GCVS cross-id parsing: scripts/catalog-pure.ts has GCVS parsers. Verify whether GCVS publishes Gaia DR3 source_ids in its cross-id catalog (crossid.txt). If yes, parse them. If no, fall back to existing logic.
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts — add tests for the new byGaia cross-match path.

# Acceptance

- Match-rate stats log shows non-zero via gaia counts.
- For a known variable star without HIP but with Gaia DR3 (TBD during execution by sampling), verify it's now matched (was missed in current pipeline).
- Overall GCVS match rate strictly higher than current baseline. Pin the new rate in scripts/build-counts-expected.json.

# Smoke

- npm run build:catalog
- Inspect GCVS match-stats log
- npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts: variable star count higher than before

# Files to touch

- scripts/build-catalog.ts (applyVariability + cross-id parsing)
- scripts/catalog-pure.ts (if GCVS cross-id parsing needs extending)
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts

# Commit

One commit. Likely minor version bump (more variable stars rendering = user-visible change).

title: Phase 3 — Binary format v6: surface gaia_source_id per record (8 bytes)
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Bump catalog binary format from v5 to v6, surfacing Gaia DR3 source_id (int64, 8 bytes) per record. This benefits the ENTIRE 313k-star catalog (not just binaries) — downstream code (search index, GCVS lookup, future POI features) can use the source_id for identifier-based operations. Depends on stellata-dch.27 (which extends AthygRow schema to carry the gaia column from AT-HYG).

# Format change

- Header magic: HYG5 -> HYG6
- Record size: 48 bytes -> 56 bytes (one 8-byte gaia_source_id field appended)
- Endianness, field offsets for existing fields: UNCHANGED so existing field reads still work after offset adjustment
- gaia_source_id stored as int64 little-endian. Value 0 = no Gaia source_id (sentinel). Sentinel only valid when AT-HYG row's gaia field is missing/null.

# Open design question (resolve here)

Whether to keep gaia_source_id inline in the record (8 bytes per row * 313k = ~2.5 MB increase) or use a side table indexed by record offset. Recommendation per parent epic: inline. AT-HYG carries gaia for ~98% of rows so side-table sparsity doesn't pay off. Inline is simpler + O(1) lookup. Confirm during implementation that the size budget is acceptable (current catalog.bin is ~13 MB; v6 will be ~16 MB).

# Deliverables

- scripts/catalog-pure.ts — update packers / unpackers / record-layout constants for v6 schema. New field gaia_source_id at offset 48 (after existing 48-byte v5 record), 8 bytes int64 LE.
- scripts/build-catalog.ts — extract gaia from AthygRow + emit it in the v6 record. Update HEADER_MAGIC constant.
- scripts/verify-catalog.ts — recognize v6 magic; dump gaia_source_id in record-inspection output.
- src/client/catalog-loader.ts — decode v6 records; expose gaia_source_id alongside other fields. Update Catalog struct.
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts — bump format-regression tests to assert v6 layout. Add field-offset test for gaia_source_id at byte 48; assert record stride = 56.
- Migration strategy: NONE (full rebuild). Old catalog.bin (v5) gets replaced when npm run build:catalog runs.

# Acceptance

- typecheck + vitest green
- npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts reports 'format v6, record size 56' and dumps sample records with gaia_source_id values
- Spot-check: Sirius (HIP 32349) in built catalog has gaia_source_id=2947050466531873024 surfaced
- npm run build:catalog completes without error; public/catalog.bin grows to ~16 MB

# Smoke

- npm run build:catalog
- npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts
- npm test green

# Files to touch

- scripts/catalog-pure.ts (record layout)
- scripts/build-catalog.ts (emit)
- scripts/verify-catalog.ts (decode)
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts (regression tests)
- src/client/catalog-loader.ts (client decode)

# Commit

One commit. Major version bump (v5 -> v6) is user-visible (no auto-upgrade) so this needs at least a minor package.json version bump per RELEASING.md.

title: Phase 2 — build-binaries.py Stages 5+6+7: optical-pair filter cascade, multiples.tsv schema, stats
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

The final Phase 2 bead. Three things land together:

(Stage 5) — rewrite the optical-pair filter cascade to be ID-anchored, with a new asymmetric-Gaia gate that catches Sirius-style spurious pairs directly. (Stage 6) — extend multiples.tsv schema with provenance columns. (Stage 7) — emit build-time statistics into scripts/build-counts-expected.json.

Depends on stellata-dch.28, .30, .31.

# Stage 5 — Optical-pair filter (4-tier cascade, ID-anchored)

For each WDS pair (component A, component B):

1. WDS Notes flag chars — S/U/X/Y reject (optical); T/V/Z keep (physical). Unchanged from current implementation.
2. Both-components-Gaia gate — when BOTH A and B have resolved Gaia astrometry (resolve_via in tiers 1-3, OR tier 4 successfully). Test: |plx_A - plx_B| < 3 * sqrt(e_plx_A^2 + e_plx_B^2) AND |pmra_A - pmra_B| < 5 mas/yr AND |pmdec_A - pmdec_B| < 5 mas/yr. Pass = physical; fail = optical. Expect this gate to engage for >95% of pairs vs ~73% in the dch.18 position-based implementation.
3. Asymmetric-Gaia gate (NEW) — when ONLY B has Gaia astrometry AND A has a known distance from AT-HYG/HIP2. Test: |plx_B - plx_A_known| < 3 * sqrt(e_plx_B^2 + e_plx_A_known^2). Pass = physical (companion at primary's distance); fail = optical (companion is a background star). CATCHES the Sirius A-C/D/E/F case directly: A's distance is 2.64 pc (known), C/D/E/F's Gaia parallax says 1+ kpc -> reject.
4. Magnitude-gap sanity heuristic — current tier-3 backstop unchanged. Used when both components miss Gaia (rare with source-ID anchoring, but possible for faint Tycho-only stars without HIP).

# Stage 6 — multiples.tsv schema

Add columns: resolve_via, astrometry_via, orbit_via, gaia_source_id. Preserve existing columns (system_id, comp, hip, x_pc, y_pc, z_pc, absmag, ci, spect, name, source, regime, orbital fields from dch.8).

# Stage 7 — Build-time statistics

Emit per-strategy + per-tier counts at end of build:
- Resolution: orb6_hip / wds_notes_hip / athyg_gaia_native / position_pm / position_nopm / unresolved
- Astrometry: gaia_5p / gaia_nss_systemic / hip2_long_baseline
- Orbits: gaia_nss / orb6 / orb6_spectroscopic / none
- Optical filter: wds_notes_kept / wds_notes_rejected / gaia_kept / gaia_rejected / asymm_kept / asymm_rejected / mag_heuristic_kept / mag_heuristic_rejected
- Position-fallback residual histogram (for position_pm matches)

Write to scripts/build-counts-expected.json (extend existing structure per memory stellata-test-coverage-discipline tier-2). Phase 4 Bead S consumes these for population statistical gates.

# Acceptance

- Zero SYN-NNN injections for Sirius A-C/D/E/F WDS entries (verify via grep '06451-1643' data/multiples.tsv).
- Sirius A-B retained with both resolve_via=orb6_hip (or athyg_gaia_native) and source_id surfaced.
- α Cen A-B retained with both source_ids.
- A random known optical pair (TBD during execution) correctly rejected via gate 2 or 3.
- Coverage: optical_rejected_rate > 5% of total pairs (matches WDS literature estimates that ~5-10% of WDS pairs are optical contamination).
- Stats written to build-counts-expected.json for tier-B consumption.

# Smoke

- npm run build:binaries --force
- Inspect data/multiples.tsv for new columns + Sirius/α Cen rows
- Inspect Stage 7 stats log

# Files to touch

- scripts/build-binaries.py (Stages 5, 6, 7 sections — replaces existing optical-pair filter at lines ~700-750)
- scripts/build-counts-expected.json (extend schema)
- scripts/build-counts.test.ts (add assertions for the new fields — Tier B in Phase 4 builds on this)

# Commit

One commit. This bead is the END of Phase 2. After it merges, data/multiples.tsv is ready for Phase 3 (binary format ingest).

title: Phase 2 — build-binaries.py Stage 4: NSS > ORB6 orbital-element selection per system
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

For each WDS pair, pick the BEST available orbital elements. Gaia NSS (when applicable) beats ORB6 because it's space-based and uses Gaia's astrometry. ORB6 (when no NSS) is the existing Regime 2/3 logic. Depends on stellata-dch.30.

# Selection priority

For each WDS system:

1. `gaia_nss` — Gaia NSS two-body solution is present for at least one component AND (period < 3 yr OR semimajor_axis < 1") — within Gaia's observability regime. Convert NSS Thiele-Innes constants to canonical Campbell elements (see below), then to the v5 orbital-elements section schema (already used by dch.8). Include formal Gaia uncertainties.
2. `orb6` — current Regime 2 logic (visual orbit) verbatim, with grade-tiebreak (lowest numeric grade wins; ties broken by most recent REF year).
3. `orb6_spectroscopic` — current Regime 3 logic verbatim (no inclination; conventional defaults).
4. `none` — Regime 1 (visual-only, no orbital elements).

# Open design question (resolve here)

The 3-yr period / 1" separation thresholds for NSS-vs-ORB6 are initial estimates. Some NSS solutions cover longer-period or wider systems (NSS max period observed in DR3 ≈ 9,936 d ≈ 27 yr; see dch.25 probe); we may want to broaden. Calibrate by counting NSS solutions per period bin and comparing against ORB6 coverage in the same bin. If NSS has reliable solutions out to e.g. P=5 yr, raise the threshold.

# Thiele-Innes → Campbell conversion (do it ourselves)

Decision (2026-05-18, Alex): implement the conversion in-repo rather than importing the ESA NSSTools package (https://gitlab.obspm.fr/gaia/nsstools — last updated 2+ years ago, no recent maintenance). Use NSSTools as a credit/sanity-check anchor: cite it in the helper's docstring, optionally pin a single-row fixture against its output for confidence.

Algebra (Heintz 1978 / Pourbaix; reproduced in Halbwachs+ 2023 Appendix B). Inputs A, B, F, G in mas; outputs a (mas), i (rad), Omega (rad), omega (rad).

    u = (A² + B² + F² + G²) / 2
    v = A·G − B·F
    a       = sqrt(u + sqrt(u² − v²))           # apparent angular semi-major axis (mas)
    tan²(i/2) = (u − sqrt(u² − v²)) / (u + sqrt(u² − v²))
    ω + Ω   = atan2(B − F, A + G)
    ω − Ω   = atan2(−B − F, A − G)

Vectorised numpy (~6 lines). Quadrant convention and Ω-by-π wrap match Halbwachs+ 2023.

Physical semimajor axis (AU) from photocentric a (mas): `a_AU = (a_mas / 1000) / plx_mas` for the photocentre orbit. Mass-ratio correction (NSS `mass_ratio` column where non-null) maps photocentre → primary semimajor axis if needed.

Solution types dch.25 surfaces in `nss_solution_type` route the conversion:
- `Orbital`, `OrbitalTargetedSearch*`, `OrbitalAlternative*` → astrometric Thiele-Innes → Campbell as above.
- `SB1`, `SB1C`, `SB2`, `SB2C` → spectroscopic, no inclination from astrometry alone; mark `i` as null/unknown and fill (e, ω, T) from spectroscopic fit.
- `AstroSpectroSB1` → combined; use Thiele-Innes + spectroscopic mass-ratio.
- `EclipsingBinary`, `EclipsingSpectro` → use orbital + eclipse-derived i.

# Deliverables

- `scripts/build-binaries.py` — Stage 4 section. New function `select_orbit(system, indices) -> (orbit_dict, orbit_via)` where `orbit_dict` is the canonical schema and `orbit_via in {gaia_nss, orb6, orb6_spectroscopic, none}`.
- `nss_to_canonical_elements(nss_row, plx_mas) -> orbit_dict` — Thiele-Innes → Campbell conversion routed by `nss_solution_type`. Pure helper, vitest-style coverage required.
- Optional: one-row fixture spot-check against NSSTools output (committed reference values) for confidence.

# Acceptance

- Stats log: 'orbits sourced: gaia_nss=X, orb6=Y, orb6_spectroscopic=Z, none=W'. Expect X > 0.
- Spot-check NSS system (TBD during execution, picked from dch.25 spot-check pool): orbit_via=gaia_nss; period matches NSS published.
- α Cen A+B: orbit_via=orb6 (P=79.9 yr, outside NSS regime).
- Regime-3 spectroscopic-only ORB6: orbit_via=orb6_spectroscopic.
- Visual-only pair (no ORB6): orbit_via=none.
- Conversion helper has full unit-test coverage for each routing branch.

# Smoke

- `npm run build:binaries --force`.

LABELS: phase-2, binaries, gaia, nss, orb6

title: Phase 2 — build-binaries.py Stage 3: astrometry attachment with NSS-systemic + HIP2-long-baseline fallback
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

For each WDS component resolved to a Gaia source_id (stellata-dch.28), attach the right astrometric measurement — defaulting to Gaia 5-parameter but falling back to NSS-systemic or HIP2-long-baseline when the 5p solution is unreliable. This is what makes Sirius A finally land at its true J2000 position. Depends on stellata-dch.28 + stellata-dch.29.

# Astrometry routing priority

For each resolved component, in order:

1. gaia_nss_systemic — when the source has a row in NSS two-body-orbit AND the 5-parameter solution is flagged unreliable (ruwe > 1.4 OR ipd_frac_multi_peak > 0.02). Use NSS's systemic position + PM (the orbit-removed motion of the center of mass) instead of the contaminated 5p solution.
2. hip2_long_baseline — when the system has a known companion within 5" AND |pmRA_gaia - pmRA_hip2| > 50 mas/yr OR |pmDE_gaia - pmDE_hip2| > 50 mas/yr. This is the classic bright-binary contamination case: Hipparcos's 1991.25 epoch + 4 yr baseline averages over a different window on the orbit than Gaia's 2014-2017 window, and the long-baseline PM is closer to the true single-star motion. Use HIP2's plx + PM + position (propagated to J2000 from HIP2's J1991.25 epoch).
3. gaia_5p — default. Use Gaia DR3's 5-parameter solution.

Each component records astrometry_via in multiples.tsv.

# Open design question (resolve here)

The 50 mas/yr threshold for Hipparcos PM fallback is an initial estimate. After this bead lands, validate against the known bright-close-binaries list (Sirius, α Cen A+B, Castor A+B, Algol, Procyon A) and tune if needed. The threshold should engage for these systems but NOT for stars with genuinely revised single-star PMs between HIP and Gaia (e.g. high-PM stars where DR3 is just more accurate).

# Deliverables

- scripts/build-binaries.py — new Stage 3 section calling out to a helper function attach_astrometry(component, indices). Helper documented inline + tested via Python unittest.

# Acceptance

- For Sirius A: astrometry_via=hip2_long_baseline. Verified: pmra=-546.05 (HIP2) not -462 (Gaia 5p contaminated).
- For α Cen A: astrometry_via=hip2_long_baseline.
- For α Cen B: astrometry_via=hip2_long_baseline.
- For Castor A primary: astrometry_via=hip2_long_baseline.
- For Algol B (eclipsing binary): astrometry_via depends — NSS coverage would route to gaia_nss_systemic; otherwise hip2_long_baseline.
- For Proxima Centauri (single star, isolated PM): astrometry_via=gaia_5p (no contamination).
- For a random Tycho-only star: astrometry_via=gaia_5p (no HIP, no NSS).
- Stats log: 'astrometry routing: gaia_5p=X, gaia_nss_systemic=Y, hip2_long_baseline=Z'.

# Smoke

- npm run build:binaries --force
- grep '06451-1643' data/multiples.tsv -- shows Sirius A with astrometry_via=hip2_long_baseline
- grep '14396-6050' data/multiples.tsv -- shows α Cen A+B with same

# Files to touch

- scripts/build-binaries.py (Stage 3 section)
- scripts/build-binaries.py.test.py (extend with attach_astrometry tests)

# Commit

One commit.

title: Phase 2 — scripts/refresh-gaia-astrometry.py + commit data/gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Gaia DR3 astrometry for the union of source_ids resolved by Phase 2 Stage 2 (stellata-dch.28). By querying by source_id rather than position, we bypass the orbital-contamination problem entirely for bright close binaries (Sirius, α Cen, etc.). Depends on stellata-dch.21 (refresh_lib) AND stellata-dch.28 (must know which source_ids to query).

# Deliverables

- scripts/refresh-gaia-astrometry.py (new) — reads data/gaia_astrometry_source_id_request.tsv (produced by Bead H), chunks the source_id list, queries gaiadr3.gaia_source by source_id IN (...), writes the result.
- data/gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv (new, LFS ~10-20 MB depending on resolution coverage) — columns: source_id, ra, ra_error, dec, dec_error, parallax, parallax_error, pmra, pmra_error, pmdec, pmdec_error, ref_epoch, ruwe, ipd_frac_multi_peak, phot_g_mean_mag, phot_bp_mean_mag, phot_rp_mean_mag. Note: ruwe + ipd_frac_multi_peak are used by Stage 3 (stellata-dch.30) to flag unreliable single-star solutions.
- package.json — "refresh:gaia-astrometry" alias.

# ADQL query template

SELECT source_id, ra, ra_error, dec, dec_error, parallax, parallax_error,
       pmra, pmra_error, pmdec, pmdec_error, ref_epoch, ruwe,
       ipd_frac_multi_peak, phot_g_mean_mag, phot_bp_mean_mag, phot_rp_mean_mag
FROM gaiadr3.gaia_source
WHERE source_id IN ({chunk_of_5000_ids})

(Chunked via refresh_lib batching helper; Gaia archive caps IN-list size ~5000.)

# Acceptance

- Output row count matches input source_id list size ±5% (some IDs may resolve to retracted sources or have no astrometry).
- Schema validated.
- Spot-check: Sirius A source_id 2947050466531873024 has parallax ~374 mas, pmra ~-462 mas/yr (verified via earlier 2026-05-15 X-Match diagnostic).

# Smoke

- After stellata-dch.28 has run once, npm run refresh:gaia-astrometry writes the TSV
- Re-run stellata-dch.28 (build-binaries.py); tier-4 (position_pm) now becomes active (uses this astrometry for HIP-less components)

# Files to touch

- scripts/refresh-gaia-astrometry.py (new)
- package.json (npm alias)

# Commit

One commit. After this lands, re-run build-binaries to engage tier 4.

title: Phase 2 — build-binaries.py Stage 2: resolve each WDS component to a Gaia source_id
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Replace the existing HIP-resolution chain (cone-match-first) in scripts/build-binaries.py with a source-ID-first five-tier resolution chain. The CORE of the rewrite. Depends on stellata-dch.27 (Stage 1 loader + indices). Note: stellata-dch.29 (refresh-gaia-astrometry.py) depends on THIS bead's output — write the resolved source_ids to a side file during this stage's run so Bead I can query Gaia for the union of needed source_ids.

# Resolution chain (strict priority)

For each WDS component:

1. orb6_hip — ORB6 entry's published HIP -> HIP cross-walk lookup -> gaia_source_id (existing ORB6 hip field path, now ID-anchored).
2. wds_notes_hip — WDS Notes prose 'HIP NNNNN' regex extract -> HIP cross-walk -> gaia_source_id (existing prose-regex path, now ID-anchored).
3. athyg_gaia_native — AT-HYG row's 'gaia' field (~98% of catalog rows have it natively) -> use directly. No cross-walk needed.
4. position_pm — PM-propagated position match against data/gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv at ~2" residual tolerance. ONLY runs when stellata-dch.29 has landed (astrometry.tsv exists). Until then, this tier is stubbed and always returns unresolved.
5. position_nopm — Position match without PM, for faint stars with no Gaia PM in the astrometry table.

Each component records its resolve_via strategy. Components that fail all five tiers are recorded as 'unresolved'.

# Deliverables

- scripts/build-binaries.py — replace resolve_primary_hip() (lines ~1323-1340) and the secondary cone-match block (lines ~1356-1365) with the new resolution chain. New function signature returns (gaia_source_id, resolve_via) per component.
- Side-file emission: data/gaia_astrometry_source_id_request.tsv — list of source_ids resolved in tiers 1-3 (whatever Gaia astrometry we DO need to query). Bead I consumes this.
- Resolution-strategy stats logged at end of Stage 2.

# Acceptance

- Build log line: "Resolution: orb6_hip=X, wds_notes_hip=Y, athyg_gaia_native=Z, position_pm=A, position_nopm=B, unresolved=C" with non-zero counts for each non-stub tier.
- For Sirius A (HIP 32349, WDS 06451-1643): resolve_via=orb6_hip (or wds_notes_hip), gaia_source_id=2947050466531873024.
- For Proxima Centauri (HIP 70890, WDS 14396-6050 C): resolve_via=athyg_gaia_native OR orb6_hip.
- For a HIP-less faint WDS component: resolve_via=position_pm (after Bead I) or unresolved.
- Coverage target: >90% of WDS components in tiers 1-3 (source-ID anchored, not position). Tier 4+5 < 10%.

# Smoke

- npm run build:binaries --force
- Inspect resolution stats log
- grep Sirius / α Cen / Proxima rows in data/multiples.tsv

# Files to touch

- scripts/build-binaries.py (Stage 2 section)
- scripts/build-binaries.py.test.py (NEW — Python unittest for the resolution chain, with fixture-based testing of each tier)

# Commit

One commit. Bead H lands BEFORE Bead I; the tier-4 path is stubbed until Bead I produces the astrometry file.

title: Phase 2 — build-binaries.py Stage 1: load all reference data + build identifier indices
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Create `scripts/build-binaries.py` from scratch with Stage 1: load all reference catalogs and build the identifier indices that Stages 2-7 (stellata-dch.28..32) consume. Per the epic NOTES § "Foundation commits preserved on archive/dch-pipeline-foundation", Phase 2 rewrites build-binaries.py from scratch rather than evolving the throwaway dch.5 version on the archive branch — that file is reference-only, not a starting point.

The Stage 2-7 resolution / matching / orbit-picking / optical-filter logic lands in subsequent beads. This bead only brings the data INTO the script and the indices needed for downstream lookup.

# Prerequisite — cherry-pick dch.4

WDS and ORB6 source files (`wds_summ.txt`, `wds_notes.txt`, `wds_refs.txt`, `orb6_orbits.txt`) are not yet on main; per the epic foundation table they are preserved on `archive/dch-pipeline-foundation`. Cherry-pick commit `b7eed29` as the first commit of this PR; the rest of the inputs (AT-HYG, GCVS, hip_ccdm, HIP2, Gaia HIP/Tyc xmatch, Gaia NSS) are already on main.

# Deliverables

- `scripts/build-binaries.py` — new file, Stage 1 only:
  - Load WDS pairs (~157k systems from `wds_summ.txt`).
  - Load WDS notes (prose HIP supplement and optical-flag indicators).
  - Load ORB6 orbits (~4k visual-binary orbital fits).
  - Load AT-HYG — Python `AthygRow` dataclass surfaces hip + tyc + gaia (Gaia DR3 source_id) alongside ra/dec/x0/y0/z0/dist/absmag/spect/etc.
  - Load GCVS (`gcvs5.txt` + `crossid.txt`).
  - Load CCDM TSV.
  - Load HIP2 van Leeuwen astrometry.
  - Load Gaia DR3 ↔ HIP cross-walk.
  - Load Gaia DR3 ↔ Tycho-2 cross-walk.
  - Load Gaia DR3 NSS two-body solutions.
  - (Gaia DR3 astrometry TSV — optional, present once stellata-dch.29 lands; skip with informational log line if missing.)
- Build identifier indices:
  - `hip → gaia_source_id` (from `gaia_dr3_hip_xmatch.tsv`)
  - `tyc → gaia_source_id` (from `gaia_dr3_tyc_xmatch.tsv`)
  - `gaia_source_id → nss_row` (from `gaia_dr3_nss_two_body.tsv`)
  - `hip → at_hyg_row`, `tyc → at_hyg_row`, `gaia_source_id → at_hyg_row`
  - `hip → hip2_row` (for the Stage 3 fallback)
  - `gaia_source_id → astrometry_row` (empty pre-Stage-2-I; the index is constructed so callers always see the same shape).
- Idempotency: skip when `data/multiples.tsv` exists AND is newer than `scripts/build-binaries.py` AND newer than every input file. `--force` bypasses.
- Build log: one line per loaded catalog with row count and (where built) index cardinality. Example: `loaded 99k Gaia HIP xmatch rows; built hip → gaia_source_id of cardinality 99k`. Same shape for every loaded catalog so a fresh reader can scan.
- Add `npm run build:binaries` script entry: `"build:binaries": "python3 scripts/build-binaries.py"`.

# Tests

- `scripts/build-binaries.test.py` (unittest module, same pattern as `refresh_lib.test.py`) — pin parser correctness on tiny fixture rows for each loader: AT-HYG row → AthygRow fields populated; WDS summary record → WdsPair fields; ORB6 line → Orb6Entry; HIP2 line; Gaia HIP / Tyc / NSS rows. Pin a known constant (e.g. AT-HYG Sol row has empty tyc + null gaia + hip 0) and at least one row that has all three classical IDs populated.

# Acceptance

- `npm run build:binaries -- --force` runs end-to-end and prints one load + index-cardinality log line per source.
- AT-HYG inspection: log line "X / Y AT-HYG rows carry gaia" — expected ~98% per AT-HYG documentation; flag if outside [0.90, 1.00].
- `python3 scripts/build-binaries.test.py` exits green.
- `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` stay green (no TypeScript changes in this bead).

# Smoke

- `npm run build:binaries -- --force`
- Inspect log for the per-catalog load + index-cardinality lines.
- `python3 scripts/build-binaries.test.py`.

# Files to touch

- `scripts/build-binaries.py` (new).
- `scripts/build-binaries.test.py` (new).
- `package.json` (`build:binaries` script entry).

# Not in scope (deferred to later Phase 2 beads)

- Resolution chain WDS-component → Gaia source_id (stellata-dch.28).
- Astrometry attachment / NSS-systemic fallback (dch.29-30).
- Orbit picking (dch.31).
- Optical-pair filter (dch.32).
- TypeScript-side `gaia_source_id` surfacing in `scripts/build-catalog.ts` / `scripts/catalog-pure.ts` / `public/catalog.bin` (Phase 3, stellata-dch.33).

# Commit

Two commits: (1) cherry-pick `b7eed29` (dch.4 data files); (2) new `scripts/build-binaries.py` + `scripts/build-binaries.test.py` + `package.json` script entry.
