title: Multiple-star matching: applyMultipleOverrides + MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC (first user-visible fix)
description:
Bead 3/7 of the stellata-dch decomposition (see parent for full spec).

# Goal

TS-side ingest of `data/multiples.tsv` into the AT-HYG build pipeline. **First user-visible payoff** — α Cen A and B render distinct; Sirius B appears in the catalog.

# Scope

- `scripts/catalog-pure.ts`:
  - `MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC = 5e-6` — new named const (~1 AU), exported, with comment documenting calibration intent per `stellata-named-constants-and-dry`
  - `applyMultipleOverridesPure(stars, overrides)` — pure helper:
    - HIP rows → locate existing BinaryStar by HIP, replace xyz/absmag/ci/spect/name from override values
    - SYN-NNN rows → construct + append new BinaryStar (HIP=0, no GCVS lookup, no constellation membership)
  - `inferBinaries` extension: if mutual pair below `MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC` AND neither component is in the overrides set, drop the fainter member with warn-log naming both HIPs. Above threshold: existing behaviour intact.
- `scripts/build-catalog.ts`:
  - `applyMultipleOverrides` wrapper — read `data/multiples.tsv`, call pure helper
  - Log overrides applied + synth injections + per-regime breakdown
  - Wire after `readStars`, before absmag sort
- `scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts` (new cases):
  - `applyMultipleOverridesPure` replaces position for existing HIP record
  - `applyMultipleOverridesPure` injects synthetic SYN-NNN row into output
  - `inferBinaries` drops fainter of below-threshold pair when neither overridden
  - `inferBinaries` keeps both components distinct when fixture has overrides applied first
  - Combined end-to-end fixture: α Cen-style co-located rows + override → two distinct records at 17 AU

# v4 binary format unchanged at this bead. Format bump lives in dch.7.

# Smoke

- `npm run build:catalog` log reports overrides applied + synth injections counts
- `npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts` dumps α Cen A, α Cen B, Sirius A, Sirius B at distinct xyz with correct absmag + spect class
- `npm test` green
- Manual (user-driven, no dev server by Claude): focus α Cen A in browser → A and B visible distinct at 17 AU; focus Sirius A → Sirius B visible at ~20 AU; search bar returns "Sirius B"

# Doc updates

- `docs/build-and-data.md` — applyMultipleOverrides flow + MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC rule + log line shape
close_reason: Landed in worktree-stellata-dch-binaries commit b23810a; tests + typecheck green; build-counts assertion passing; α Cen A/B distinct at 18.7 AU, Sirius A/B at 11.8 AU verified via verify-catalog.ts.

title: Multiple-star matching: build-binaries.py cross-match pipeline (Regimes 1+2+3 + overrides scaffold)
description:
Bead 2/7 of the stellata-dch decomposition (see parent for full spec).

# Goal

New Python preprocessor that cross-matches WDS + ORB6 + Gaia DR3 against AT-HYG and emits `data/multiples.tsv` (per-component rows). Mirrors the `build-clouds.py` / `build-dust.py` pattern: Python for science compute, output is a clean TSV that TS-side `build-catalog.ts` ingests.

# Scope

- `scripts/build-binaries.py` (NEW):
  - Read 4 source catalogs from dch.4
  - Cross-match WDS pair primary → AT-HYG via HIP (fallback HD); skip pairs whose primary isn't in AT-HYG
  - Optical-pair filter (priority order): WDS notes flag → Gaia parallax 3σ check → Gaia common PM (~5 mas/yr) → spectral/mag sanity fallback
  - Compute J2000 component positions:
    - Regime 1 (visually resolved): WDS ρ + θ at most recent epoch → tangent-plane projection
    - Regime 2 (ORB6 orbit): solve Kepler at J2000 with elements (P, T, e, a, i, ω, Ω) → project
    - Regime 3 (spectroscopic-only): a·sin i for separation magnitude + conventional PA=0
  - Apply hand-curated `data/multiples-overrides.tsv` last with precedence
  - Emit `data/multiples.tsv` with schema: `system_id, comp, hip, x_pc, y_pc, z_pc, absmag, ci, spect, name, source, regime`
  - Orbital elements columns also emitted for Regime 2+3 rows (consumed by dch.8) — but at this bead, downstream still ignores them
- `data/multiples-overrides.tsv` (NEW, schema header + comment block; initially empty)
- `.gitignore` — add `data/multiples.tsv` (generated output)
- `package.json` — `build:binaries` npm script; `build:catalog` invokes it first

# Smoke

- `python3 scripts/build-binaries.py` runs to completion, prints stats: N pairs kept, M optical rejected, K SYN-NNN injections, per-regime breakdown
- Generated `data/multiples.tsv` contains:
  - alf_cen A (HIP 71683) and B (HIP 71681) at distinct xyz
  - sirius A (HIP 32349) and B (SYN-001) at distinct xyz, B at ~20 AU offset from A
  - castor's 6 components
  - algol Aa1 / Aa2 at ~0.06 AU (Regime 3)
- Override scaffold (`data/multiples-overrides.tsv`) parses without error (header-only)
- `npm run build:catalog` still works — TS ingest still ignores multiples.tsv until dch.6

# Doc updates

- `SCIENCE.md` § "Multiple-star pipeline" — describe the 3-regime model + optical-pair filter
- `docs/build-and-data.md` § "Build-binaries pipeline" — schema of multiples.tsv, script invocation, scope
close_reason: build-binaries.py landed in worktree as commit 27636dc. Smoke targets verified: α Cen A/B with correct HIPs, Sirius A+B (Sirius B = SYN with name), Castor 8 rows incl. correctly-anchored Ba/Bb sub-pair, Algol Aa1/Aa2 via ORB6. Sub-pair anchoring via WDS components field implemented in same pass. SYN secondaries get search-friendly names from primary. Branch not yet pushed; PR opens at dch.10.

title: Multiple-star matching: pull and commit binary-system data sources (WDS, ORB6, Gaia DR3)
description:
Bead 1/7 of the stellata-dch decomposition (see parent for full spec). Pure data + docs commit; no code changes.

# Goal

Land the four frozen external catalogs that the cross-match pipeline (dch.5) will consume. Frozen-external-data convention applies: pulled manually, committed under data/, no live fetch at build.

# Scope

- `data/wds.tsv` — Washington Double Star Catalog, VizieR `B/wds/wds`. ~150,000 pairs with ρ, θ, magnitudes, spectral types, HD/HIP cross-IDs. ~10 MB, LFS.
- `data/wds-notes.tsv` — supplementary `B/wds/notes` + `B/wds/refs5` for HIP/HD cross-ref resolution. ~5 MB, LFS.
- `data/orb6.tsv` — Sixth Catalog of Orbits of Visual Binary Stars, VizieR `B/orb6`. ~3000 systems with full orbital element fits. ~1 MB.
- `data/gaia_dr3_binaries.tsv` — filtered Gaia DR3 subset: parallax + proper motion for both components of each WDS pair, retrieved by querying every Gaia source within 1 arcsec of every WDS A/B coordinate. ~20 MB, LFS.

# Doc updates

- `SCIENCE.md` — new section "Multiple-star data sources" citing each catalog with VizieR ID, paper reference, retrieval date.

# Smoke

- `git lfs ls-files | grep -E "(wds|orb6|gaia_dr3_binaries)"` shows all four files tracked under LFS
- `wc -l data/wds.tsv` returns roughly the expected ~150,000 line count
- `grep -i "alf cen\|HIP 71683" data/wds-notes.tsv` finds the α Cen cross-ref
- `grep "alpha cen\|HIP 71683" data/orb6.tsv` finds the α Cen orbital fit (P~80yr)
- `npm test` + `npm run build` unchanged (no code touched)

# Acceptance

- Files committed under LFS with valid TSV/text structure
- SCIENCE.md cites each source with VizieR ID + retrieval date
- No build pipeline or renderer changes — those land in dch.5+
notes:
Implementation deviations from original bead description (decided 2026-05-11
with Alex):

1. Filenames + format: raw fixed-width .txt from astro.gsu.edu (not VizieR
   TSV). Matches gcvs5.txt / crossid.txt convention; trivially re-fetchable
   via single URL per file. Final names: wds_summ.txt, wds_notes.txt,
   wds_refs.txt, orb6_orbits.txt. wds_refs.txt added (not in original) —
   it's the discoverer-code dictionary the parser needs to resolve
   designations.

2. Gaia DR3 deferred to dch.5. The Gaia ADQL query requires WDS
   coordinates as a user-table upload, but extracting those needs a WDS
   parser — which is dch.5's build-binaries.py. Cleaner partition: dch.4
   is pure upstream mirror; dch.5 emits the upload CSV as an intermediate,
   Alex runs the Gaia query manually, build-binaries.py consumes the
   result.

3. SCIENCE.md "Multiple-star data sources" entry covers WDS + ORB6 only.
   Gaia procedure documentation lands in dch.5 alongside the upload-CSV
   generator.

Updated smoke checks:
- git lfs ls-files | grep -E "(wds_|orb6_)" shows all 4 files
- wc -l data/wds_summ.txt ~ 100k+ entries
- grep "14396-6050\|RIG  6" data/wds_summ.txt finds α Cen
- grep "14396-6050" data/orb6_orbits.txt finds α Cen's 79.9-yr orbit
close_reason: Raw WDS + ORB6 catalogs committed as data/{wds_summ,wds_notes,wds_refs,orb6_orbits}.txt under LFS. Alex spot-checked α Cen AB row in orb6_orbits.txt (P=79.762 yr, Ake2021 grade 2). SCIENCE.md updated. Filenames diverged from original TSV plan — fixed-width raw, matches gcvs5.txt convention. Gaia DR3 deferred to dch.5 (needs WDS parser to extract upload coords).

title: Multiple-star matching: enforce threshold + manual override list in build pipeline
description:
**Goal:** apply the threshold rule from sibling **stellata-dch.1** and the data-source decision from sibling **stellata-dch.2** to the build pipeline. Famous close pairs render at distinct, physically-correct coordinates.

### Implementation
- **Threshold enforcement** in `scripts/catalog-pure.ts:inferBinaries`: reject (or merge per dch.1's chosen action) groupings whose components fall below the threshold.
- **Manual override list** under `data/` per the `KNOWN_MISSING_HIPS` pattern in `scripts/build-catalog.ts`. v1 list at minimum:
  - Proxima Centauri AB (component coordinates from chosen dch.2 source)
  - Sirius A + Sirius B
  - α Cen A + α Cen B
  - Any other famous close pairs surfaced during dch.2's audit.
- **Optional external dataset ingest** if dch.2's recommendation goes beyond a manual list (e.g. WDS subset).
- **Doc updates**: SCIENCE.md (data sources for multiple stars + threshold rule citation), `docs/build-and-data.md` and / or CLAUDE.md (pipeline behaviour) per `defer-doc-updates` convention. Write these as part of this commit, not earlier.

### Acceptance
- Build pipeline rejects (or merges) multiple-system groupings whose components fall below the chosen threshold from dch.1.
- Proxima AB, Sirius AB, α Cen AB render at distinct coordinates matching published component positions.
- Visual: discs/glows for each component are distinguishable at typical zoom; no full-overlap rendering.
- `npm test` covers the new override-list ingest + threshold rule.

### Depends on
**stellata-dch.1** (threshold rule), informed by **stellata-dch.2** (data source).
close_reason: Superseded by detailed decomposition into stellata-dch.4–.10. Decisions: MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC = 5e-6 pc + drop-fainter action; programmatic WDS + ORB6 + Gaia DR3 cross-match with thin hand-curated overrides; v5 binary format with per-system orbital elements + live per-frame orbital evolution via BinaryOrbitField runtime. See parent stellata-dch description for full spec.

title: Multiple-star matching: alt data-source investigation (WDS / CCDM / Gaia DR3)
description:
**Goal:** evaluate alternative data sources for resolved double/multiple stars with reliable component coordinates so famous close pairs can render at distinct positions. Spec context in parent **stellata-dch**.

### Sources to evaluate
- **WDS** (Washington Double Star catalog) — most comprehensive resolved-double-star catalog; per-pair separation + position-angle.
- **Hipparcos CCDM** — already partially used via `data/hip_ccdm.tsv` for the visual-doubles flag. Coverage limited to Hipparcos-era pairs.
- **Gaia DR3 binary catalog** — modern, but Gaia has its own bright-star saturation issues for famous close pairs.
- **Per-system scientific literature** — for the very small set of canonical pairs (Proxima AB, Sirius AB, α Cen AB) where catalog data is patchy.

### `frozen-external-data` rule applies
Any chosen source committed under `data/`, no live fetch at build. Document retrieval URL + date.

### Deliverable
Recommendation attached to this issue:
- Which source(s) to use, at what coverage cut.
- Per-source: schema, fields needed (component positions in ICRS or angular sep + position angle relative to primary), retrieval steps.
- Whether a hand-curated manual override list (à la `KNOWN_MISSING_HIPS`) is sufficient for the v1 famous-close-pair set, or a wholesale catalog ingest is justified.

### Independent of dch.1
Can run in parallel; the recommendation feeds **stellata-dch.3** (implementation).

### Pointers
- Existing CCDM ingest: `data/hip_ccdm.tsv`, `scripts/build-catalog.ts` `applyDoublesFlag`.
- Coordinate with **stellata-9mm.34** (in-flight `applyDoublesFlag` test coverage) so we don't duplicate work on the same flag bit.
- Catalog format / flag-bit registry: **stellata-v2j**.
close_reason: Superseded by detailed decomposition into stellata-dch.4–.10. Decisions: MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC = 5e-6 pc + drop-fainter action; programmatic WDS + ORB6 + Gaia DR3 cross-match with thin hand-curated overrides; v5 binary format with per-system orbital elements + live per-frame orbital evolution via BinaryOrbitField runtime. See parent stellata-dch description for full spec.

title: Multiple-star matching: separation-threshold + grouping rule (decision)
description:
**Goal:** decide a sensible minimum separation threshold below which a "multiple system" grouping should NOT be created during the cross-match in `scripts/catalog-pure.ts:inferBinaries`. Spec context in parent **stellata-dch**.

### Decisions to settle
- Threshold form: angular (arcsec at component distance), physical (pc / AU), or both?
- Action when below threshold: merge the rows into one catalog entry, suppress one component, or render at distinct positions if a true-geometry source is available?
- Whether the rule differs by system age / type (e.g. tight visual binaries with known interferometric components vs spectroscopic-only).

### Inputs to consider
- Current `BINARY_MAX_SEP_PC = 0.005 pc` ceiling for geometric-binary inference.
- Behaviour for famously-close systems: Proxima AB (~0.27 pc), Sirius AB (binary, 7.5 AU separation), α Cen AB (~17 AU).
- How chunked extinction + per-star PSF interact when two components are physically co-located.

### Deliverable
Written decision attached to this issue (rule + action + rationale). Drives sibling **stellata-dch.3** (implementation). Independent of sibling **stellata-dch.2** but can borrow from its data-source survey if the chosen action requires resolved component coordinates.

### Doc placement
Per parent: rule lands in SCIENCE.md (data-source-level decision) and `docs/build-and-data.md` (pipeline behaviour) per `defer-doc-updates` convention — write the doc updates as part of dch.3, not now.
close_reason: Superseded by detailed decomposition into stellata-dch.4–.10. Decisions: MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC = 5e-6 pc + drop-fainter action; programmatic WDS + ORB6 + Gaia DR3 cross-match with thin hand-curated overrides; v5 binary format with per-system orbital elements + live per-frame orbital evolution via BinaryOrbitField runtime. See parent stellata-dch description for full spec.

title: Orbit rings render over planet disc (depth/draw-order bug)
description:
Per-host orbit rings draw straight through planet body discs instead of being occluded by them. Reproduced viewing Mercury from the back (non-illuminated side) — the orbit ring line passes through the planet rather than disappearing behind it.

Likely a draw-order or depthTest/depthWrite mismatch between orbit-rings-layer.ts and planet-body-field.ts. The disc pass should occlude the ring where the body covers it.

Files: src/client/orbit-rings-layer.ts, src/client/planet-body-field.ts, src/client/shaders/planet.{vert,frag}.glsl.
notes: Fixed in PR #48 — added planet outer-disc occluder pass (uRenderMode == 3) at renderOrder 1.5. Awaiting manual smoke before close.

title: Alula Australis (ξ UMa) un-clickable — no visible neighbour, possible binary-stack occlusion
description:
# Bug

Alula Australis (ξ UMa, a famous visual binary) cannot be clicked. No
visible companion star is rendered nearby, even with the magnitude
slider pushed to 'all'.

# Reported by Alex 2026-05-08

> I can't click on Alula Australis, no visible stars nearby (even on
> 'all' stars magnitude), so I'm wondering if the bug here is some
> kind of stacking where because it's a double star, somehow the
> double is covering the clickable star, something like that?

# Repro

1. Search 'Alula Australis' (or ξ UMa) and warp / focus.
2. Push magnitude slider to its faintest setting ('all' stars).
3. Try to click on the star — the click does not resolve to it.
4. Observe: no second visible disc/glow nearby that would hint at
   what's intercepting the click.

# Hypotheses to investigate

This *might* be the same class as stellata-au3 (resolved-pair pick
occlusion at the Double-Double), but the no-visible-neighbour
detail makes it potentially distinct:

- **Stacking variant of au3**: a catalog companion exists at the
  same position, has a hitbox, but is too faint to render at
  on-screen pixel size — so it silently steals every click that
  should land on Alula Australis itself, and the user has no
  visual cue. If true, the fix may be the same hitbox cap
  proposed under au3, but the *symptom* (no visible neighbour)
  needs to be reproducible-by-design before that fix lands.

- **Catalog mismatch**: the AT-HYG entry pointed to by the search
  index might be a non-rendered companion, or the rendered entry
  might be at a slightly different position than the catalog
  entry the search/click resolves to.

- **Binary inference**: scripts/catalog-pure.ts inferBinaries logic
  may have collapsed/expanded the pair in a way that mismatches
  what gets rendered vs what gets indexed for picking.

# Diagnostics worth collecting

- `bd memories pickStar` — surface any prior notes on the pick path.
- Verify-catalog dump for ξ UMa (HIP 55203 + companion HIP) to
  confirm both components are present and at expected separations.
- Check whether one of the components has its disc-render disabled
  while still contributing a hitbox.

# Relation

Related to stellata-au3 — same overall theme (binary-star pick
hitbox occluding the intended target). Whether this is a duplicate
or a distinct bug depends on whether a faint companion is silently
stealing the click. To be confirmed during investigation.

title: PR #43 regression: focus typeahead dropdown re-opens in nav panel after X-clear inside Go modal
description:
Reported by Alex during smoke testing of PR #43.

Repro:
1. Focus a star (e.g. Sirius). Focus input shows 'Sirius'; X clear button visible.
2. Press G → Go modal opens with the focus row relocated into it.
3. Click the X (search-focus-clear) inside the modal.
4. Modal closes 'as expected'.
5. Bug: the focus typeahead's dropdown re-opens in the main navigate panel and stays open.
6. Workaround: click into the focus input then click out, OR press ESC.

Doesn't happen on main (pre-PR-#43). All other typeahead smoke tests pass.

Likely cause (read-only investigation; NOT empirically reproduced):

The new src/client/typeahead.ts (Typeahead<T> class) dropped two guards that the prior SearchBox class had:

a) render() no longer early-exits on empty/whitespace query:
   - Main's SearchBox.render: 'const q = query.trim(); if (!q) { resultsEl.hidden = true; ...; return; }'
   - PR #43's Typeahead.render: unconditionally calls runQuery(query) → renderResultsDom() → setHover(0) → hidden = (results.length === 0).

b) focus listener no longer guards on empty input.value:
   - Main: 'input.addEventListener("focus", () => { activeBox = this; if (input.value) this.render(input.value); });'
   - PR #43: 'input.addEventListener("focus", () => { this.group.active = this; this.render(input.value); });'

In the X-clear flow, setName('') is called, but because group.active === focusBox at that moment (the blur defer hasn't fired yet), 'input.value' is left at its prior value ('Sirius'). The clearBtn handler then calls 'input.focus()', which fires a focus event → render('Sirius') → results visible. The trick is that for unknown reasons on the user's setup, the modal does close (perhaps blur defer wins / pendingClose isn't cancelled), DOM relocates the widget back to the navigate panel, and the dropdown — visible from the focus-render — ends up visible in the navigate panel.

Tactical hypotheses for the fix (any one likely sufficient):
- In Typeahead.clearBtn handler, also clear input.value and displayName explicitly before refocusing, so the resulting focus → render path runs render('') which produces 0 results.
- In Typeahead.setName, when called with name='' and group.active === this, also set input.value = '' (overrides the 'preserve user typing' behaviour when the name is being cleared).
- Re-introduce the empty-query early-exit in render() and/or the empty-value guard in the focus listener, matching main's SearchBox behaviour.

Files to edit on PR #43's branch (worktree-stellata-xbj-typeahead-unify):
- src/client/typeahead.ts — Typeahead<T> class.

Verification: Alex's existing kb-modal smoke note (PR #43 body, 'X-clear inside the modal does not tear it down mid-edit') is the closest existing case. Add a follow-up smoke step: 'After X-clear inside the Go modal, the focus typeahead dropdown should NOT remain open in the navigate panel after the modal closes.'
notes: Reopened 2026-05-10. Initial fix (commit c70d735) was based on wrong root-cause assumption: it cleared input.value before refocus on the assumption that runQuery('') would produce 0 results. Verified empirically that Fuse v7's search('') returns the ENTIRE corpus, not [] — so render('') opens a dropdown with ~10 arbitrary stars. Combined with the modal-close path (G with focus visible focuses toInput, X-clear's input.focus targets focusInput, so onInputFocus on toInput never fires, 180ms close timer fires and tears the modal down) and modern Chrome's DOM-move-preserves-focus behaviour (so focusInput's deferred-blur-hide never fires after the move), the dropdown ends up persistently open in the main UI panel. Real fix: src/client/search.ts runQuery returns [] on empty trimmed query — focus/destination box should be silent until user types (constellation typeahead has its own runQuery already handling empty correctly).
close_reason: Real fix in commit 5a7e3d0 on worktree-stellata-xbj-typeahead-unify (PR #43). Root cause: Fuse v7's search('') returns the entire corpus, not [], so render('') was opening a dropdown with ~10 arbitrary stars on focus. Combined with the modal-close path (G-with-focus focuses toInput, X-clear's input.focus targets focusInput so onInputFocus on toInput never fires, 180ms close timer fires) and modern Chrome's DOM-move-preserves-focus, the dropdown stays open in the main panel until the user clicks elsewhere. Fix: src/client/search.ts runQuery returns [] on empty trimmed query — pushes empty-query semantics to the runQuery callback (constellation typeahead's filterConstellations already handles empty correctly with NONE+top9). Verified with quick node script that fuse.search('') returns the whole corpus, confirming the root cause. Initial commit c70d735's input.value='' still needed alongside (otherwise focus listener reads stale 'Sirius' and runQuery('Sirius') still returns matches).

title: Observe-mode warp arrival shows destination-star jitter
description:
When warping between two stars while in OBSERVE mode, the destination star has significant jitter as the camera rotates to point to the same direction as before starting the warp.

Same root cause shape as the earlier navigate-mode warp jitter that was fixed by engaging the focused-star pin (uPinFocusToCenter) on arrival. Suggestion from Alex: apply the same solution — pin the destination star on arrival — and consider DRY'ing it into a shared mechanism so navigate-mode and observe-mode warps share one pin-on-arrival path rather than two parallel ones.

Expected: destination star is rock-solid on arrival as the camera completes its post-warp rotation.

Likely files:
- src/client/stellata.ts — warp state machine, pin guard in animate(), setFocus's post-recenter snap-to-origin
- src/client/observe-controls.ts — look-around controller (rotation phase after warp completes)
- src/client/shaders/star.vert.glsl — uPinFocusToCenter use site
- docs/camera-modes.md — warp 3-phase state machine, OBSERVE camera mode
- pin-debug-hud (debug.pin() in dev console) — read latched extremes on observe-warp arrival to confirm whether target.lengthSq() crosses below the 1e-12 engage threshold or not

Historical: navigate-mode warp arrival snap was added as part of an earlier 'warp jitter' fix (see PR #35 v1.1.4 'warp + variable-pulse bug fixes ...'); observe-mode arrival likely missed that treatment, or the snap fires but the observe-mode rotation phase moves target away from origin before the per-frame pin guard re-engages.

Reported by Alex during smoke testing of unrelated work.
notes: Fix in PR #50. Recenter floating origin at phase-3 start (instead of finishWarp) so the parallax slerp runs in clean float32-local coords. Refactored swapObserveAnchor to be idempotent and share the recenter+focus half via new recenterFocusToStar helper.

title: Constellation lines render on top of unfocused star disc (correct when focused)
description:
When a star is focused, the renderer correctly draws constellation stick-figure lines behind the star's disc — the disc occludes line segments that pass behind it. When the same star is unfocused, the lines render on top of the disc instead.

Expected: lines occluded by disc regardless of focus state.

Repro:
1. Focus a constellation member star where a stick-figure line passes behind it (e.g. a star inside Orion).
2. Confirm: lines are correctly hidden behind the disc.
3. Unfocus the star (Esc, or focus elsewhere).
4. Bug: same line segments now render on top of the disc.

Likely files:
- src/client/constellation-overlay.ts (SVG asterism layer)
- src/client/disc-mask.ts (the SVG mask that tracks the focused star + companion discs)
- docs/overlays.md (read first — disc-mask.ts probably clips the SVG behind the focused disc; non-focused stars have no equivalent treatment)

Likely root cause: the disc-mask is keyed to the focused star only; without focus, every star's disc is below the SVG layer in z-order, so lines unconditionally cover any non-focused disc behind them. A general fix would be to mask SVG strokes against all visible star discs (expensive — many stars), or only against discs above some apparent-size threshold, or to render constellation lines on the canvas with depth-test instead of as SVG.

Reported by Alex during smoke testing of unrelated work.

title: Star pick hitbox occludes visually-distinct neighbours (Double-Double / ε¹ ε² Lyr)
description:
# Bug

Star-pick hit areas are too aggressive: when two stars are visually
resolved on screen as separate discs/glow blobs, the hitbox of one
can completely cover the other, making the occluded star
un-clickable.

# Repro (found during PR #42 smoke testing, 2026-05-08)

1. Search "ε¹ Lyr" and focus the pair (the Double Double in Lyra,
   ~3.5 arcmin separation).
2. Try to click ε² Lyr — the *brighter* of the wide pair — directly
   on its visible disc/glow.
3. The click resolves to ε¹ instead. ε¹'s hitbox extends past ε²'s
   on-screen position, so ε² cannot be picked.

# Why this matters

The Double Double is a famous test case but the same pattern hits
any tight resolved pair where two stars are unambiguously distinct
to the eye yet sit inside each other's hit radius. The contract
should be: if both stars are visually resolved on screen, both must
be independently clickable. Selection is currently asymmetric —
one star "wins" silently and the user has no recourse.

# Suggested fix direction

The pick path is in `pickStar` (the recent v1.1.4 PR #35 mentioned
"hot-path Vector3 perf" there). Options to explore:

- Cap hitbox radius below the on-screen distance to the nearest
  neighbour, so two visually-resolved stars never share interior
  hit area.
- When the cursor falls inside multiple stars' hitboxes, pick the
  one whose visible disc centre is closest in screen pixels (rather
  than e.g. brightest or first-tested).
- Shrink hitbox aggressively below a per-pixel threshold once
  neighbour discs are within N px of each other.

The first / second options compose; either alone fixes ε¹ vs ε²
Lyr but the combination is the more robust behaviour.

# Out of scope

This is about resolved pairs only. Unresolved pairs (single visible
disc representing two stars, e.g. a Bayer-tagged binary that
doesn't separate at typical FOV) should still pick the catalog
representative — that's the existing behaviour.
notes: Surfaced by Alex during PR #42 (stellata-v2j) manual smoke. Not a regression of #42 — pre-existing pick-path behaviour, exposed by the Double-Double smoke step. Filed at P2 since it's an edge-case hit-test bug; per stellata-prioritization-framework user-reported bugs default to P1, so this may warrant a bump if Alex hits more cases.

title: Catalogue pipeline — source-ID-anchored cross-match across all ingest stages
description:
# Why this epic exists

Position-based cross-matching has hit a structural limit: famous bright close binaries (Sirius, α Cen, Castor, Algol, Procyon) systematically miss any Gaia-based filter because Gaia's published 5-parameter PM for these stars is corrupted by orbital wobble, so backward-propagating it to J2000 lands them tens of arcsec off their WDS positions. The "tier just beyond the famous ones" fails the same way. The fix isn't a tighter heuristic — it's switching the **entire catalogue ingest** (AT-HYG / GCVS / CCDM / WDS+ORB6+Gaia) to **source-identifier-anchored** cross-matching, using canonical cross-walks (HIP↔Gaia DR3, Tycho-2↔Gaia DR3) that Gaia publishes for exactly this purpose. Position-match is retained only as a tertiary fallback for catalog rows with no classical identifier at all.

The bar: memory `stellata-data-fidelity-principle` — "best possible model based on current observational data and knowledge". Set by Alex 2026-05-15. An astronomer asking about Stellata's catalogue pipeline at any level should hear an answer we're proud of.

# Architecture — five layers

**Layer 1 — Committed external reference data** (frozen, under `data/`, LFS where >1 MB). Includes the existing WDS/ORB6/AT-HYG/GCVS/CCDM plus new committed files: `gaia_dr3_hip_xmatch.tsv` (HIP → Gaia source_id, ~118k rows), `gaia_dr3_tyc_xmatch.tsv` (Tycho-2 → Gaia, ~2.5M rows), `gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv` (per-source RA/Dec/plx/PM + errors + ref_epoch), `gaia_dr3_nss_two_body.tsv` (orbital binaries Gaia detected), `hip2_van_leeuwen.tsv` (improved Hipparcos astrometry — long-baseline PM fallback for bright-binary Gaia contamination), `simbad_sample.tsv` (validation snapshot), `bailer-jones-dr3.tsv` (Bailer-Jones 2021 Bayesian distance posteriors per Gaia DR3 source_id — VizieR I/352, ~300k rows for the AT-HYG.gaia subset, LFS), `gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv` (Gaia DR3 astrophysical_parameters / Apsis: gspphot ∪ gspspec — Teff / logg / [M/H] / A0 per source_id, ~266k matched rows, LFS).

**Layer 2 — Manual-run refresh scripts** (one per dataset, idempotent, NOT in `npm run build` per memory `frozen-external-data`). All share `scripts/refresh_lib.py` (TAP client + retry + batching + schema validation). Python deps pinned in `scripts/requirements-refresh.txt`. Each script supports both astroquery.gaia (ESA TAP) and CDS TAP with auto-fallback on archive 5xx.

**Layer 3 — Catalogue builder** (`scripts/build-binaries.py` rewrite): seven stages with explicit per-component provenance.

1. Load all reference data; build identifier-keyed indices.
2. Resolve each WDS component to a Gaia source_id, strict priority order: (1) ORB6 published HIP → HIP cross-walk; (2) WDS Notes prose `HIP NNNNN` → HIP cross-walk; (3) AT-HYG row's `gaia` field (native); (4) PM-propagated position match against gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv at ~2"; (5) position match without PM. Records `resolve_via`.
3. Attach astrometry: Gaia 5-parameter (default) → Gaia NSS-systemic (when 5p solution flagged unreliable: ruwe > 1.4 or ipd_frac_multi_peak > 2%) → HIP2 long-baseline PM (when known close binary AND |pmRA_gaia − pmRA_hip2| > 50 mas/yr OR |pmDE| > 50 mas/yr). Records `astrometry_via`.
4. Select orbital elements per system: Gaia NSS two-body > ORB6 (grade tiebreak) > ORB6 spectroscopic-only > none. NSS used when system in NSS catalog AND period < ~3 yr AND separation < ~1". Records `orbit_via`.
5. Optical-pair filter cascade (ID-anchored): (1) WDS Notes flag chars unchanged; (2) both-components-Gaia parallax-3σ + PM-5-mas/yr gate (engages for >95% of pairs vs ~73% in dch.18 position-based); (3) NEW asymmetric Gaia gate (when only secondary has Gaia, test plx vs primary's known distance from AT-HYG/HIP2 — catches Sirius A-C directly); (4) magnitude-gap sanity backstop.
6. Emit `multiples.tsv` with new columns: `resolve_via`, `astrometry_via`, `orbit_via`, `gaia_source_id`. Existing schema preserved otherwise.
7. Statistics output → `build-catalog-expected.json` per memory `stellata-test-coverage-discipline` tier-2.

**Layer 4 — Validation harness** — three tiers:
- Tier A: hand-curated `scripts/known-stars.tsv` (~50-80 systems incl. single stars + binaries) + vitest assertions per memory `stellata-test-coverage-discipline` (numeric headlines pinned with `toBe`).
- Tier B: extend `scripts/build-counts.test.ts` with population statistics (gaia_resolve_rate ≥ 0.95, optical_rejected_rate, NSS-vs-ORB6 fractions).
- Tier C: manual-run `scripts/validate-simbad-sample.py` random-10k cross-check.

**Layer 5 — Documentation** (at commit time per memory `defer-doc-updates`): SCIENCE.md pipeline sections rewritten with citations; new `docs/cross-match.md`; `docs/build-and-data.md` Layer 1+2 updates; CLAUDE.md module roster + Local Commands updates; v1.0 prose sweep across all docs (Phase 0 prerequisite).

# Phasing

- Phase 0 — v1.0 prose sweep (doc-only, skip-version-bump)
- Phase 1 — Layer 2 infrastructure + Layer 1 acquisition
- Phase 2 — build-binaries.py rewrite (stages 1-7 across multiple beads)
- Phase 3 — build-catalog.ts integration + v6 binary format (gaia_source_id surfaced per record)
- Phase 4 — validation harness (Tiers A/B/C)
- Phase 5 — spectral / mass refinement (folds existing dch.15-17 work)
- Phase 6 — docs sweep + landing
- Phase 7 — dch.10 (BinaryOrbitField runtime, existing bead) unblocked; executes against the new pipeline

# Coverage across the full catalogue

Multi-star ingest was the trigger but the rewrite uplifts single-star ingest as well. Single-star benefits concentrated in: v6 binary format (every star gets gaia_source_id surfaced), GCVS cross-match (every star benefits from gaia_source_id keying), **multi-layer distance refinement** (Bailer-Jones Bayesian posteriors for every star with a Gaia DR3 source_id + LMC kinematic override for Magellanic supergiants — replaces naive 1/π with a principled stack; fixes the "outside the disc" outlier class and the "stars in the void between MW and LMC" outlier class without per-star carve-outs), known-stars validation corpus (Sol/Vega/Polaris/Altair + faint Tycho-population samples + LMC B-supergiant samples + the named B-J regression cases), SIMBAD random-10k validator (all star types), v1.0 docs sweep (whole project), Hipparcos van Leeuwen reduction (every HIP star), **Gaia DR3 Apsis astrophysical parameters** (gspphot ∪ gspspec; ~85% (Teff, logg) coverage per zsr.1 empirical probe — 2.87× uplift over status-quo spectral-class parsing; surfaces via dch.49 for downstream consumption by Tier 1 colour LUT, Phase 5 mass / radius refinement, and dust-map cross-checks). Multi-star is where the architecture pays off most visibly because position-match fails worst there, but the architecture is uniform across the ingest.

# Open design questions to resolve at execution time

1. Binary format gaia_source_id surfacing: bump to v6 with 8-byte field per record (~16 MB total impact). Resolved at start of Phase 3.
2. NSS vs ORB6 tie-break: NSS for P < ~3 yr and separation < ~1"; ORB6 otherwise. Resolved at start of Phase 2 Stage 4.
3. Hipparcos PM fallback threshold: engage when system has companion within 5" AND |Δ pmRA OR Δ pmDE| > 50 mas/yr. Resolved at start of Phase 2 Stage 3.

# Bead structure

Phase 0–6 are decomposed into 30+ children (live count via `bd list --parent=stellata-dch`). Existing children dch.10, dch.11, dch.12, dch.13, dch.14 remain in-flight and independent. Existing dch.15, dch.16, dch.17 are superseded by Phase 3 / Phase 5 beads (closed with reasons). Existing dch.18, dch.19 are superseded by Phase 1 / Phase 2 beads (closed with reasons). The full execution plan + bead manifest + dependency graph lives in this epic's NOTES block (use `bd show stellata-dch` to view).

# Connects to memories

`stellata-data-fidelity-principle` (the bar), `stellata-prioritization-framework` (priority bands), `frozen-external-data` (committed inputs, no live fetch in build), `defer-doc-updates` (docs at commit time), `stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep` (sweep stale prose at commit time), `subagent-bd-prime-protocol` (subagents need `bd prime` instruction), `stellata-test-coverage-discipline`, `stellata-bd-operations`, `bead-authoring-scope`, `commit-granularity`, `stellata-pr-mechanics`, `stellata-stellata-smoke-tests`.
design:
# Foundation commits preserved on archive/dch-pipeline-foundation

Pushed to origin 2026-05-15 from the now-superseded worktree-stellata-dch-binaries branch. Seven commits, HEAD at 5bec24e:

| SHA       | Bead     | Status in new plan                                                  |
| --------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| b7eed29   | dch.4    | KEEP — data files (wds_summ.txt, wds_notes.txt, orb6_orbits.txt)    |
| ea8149b   | dch.5    | THROW (logically) — initial build-binaries.py; replaced by Phase 2  |
| 0c2e1de   | dch.6    | THROW (logically) — multiples-overrides + safety net; replaced      |
| 7542fd6   | dch.7    | KEEP — v5 binary format scaffolding (orbitIdx + orbital section)    |
| 4d36cc0   | dch.8    | KEEP — emits orbital elements into v5 binary                        |
| 296d10f   | dch.8.f  | KEEP — Regime 3 conventional-orientation docs follow-up             |
| 5bec24e   | dch.9    | KEEP — binary-orbit-pure.ts (Kepler + Thiele-Innes math, tested)    |

# How fresh sessions should consume this branch

The new plan's Phase 2 beads (dch.27, dch.28, etc.) rewrite build-binaries.py from scratch. They do NOT need to cherry-pick dch.5 or dch.6 — those commits are preserved as historical lineage only.

The new plan's Phase 3 bead dch.33 (v6 binary format) evolves the v5 format from dch.7+dch.8 into v6. Fresh-session workflow: branch from main, merge or cherry-pick the dch.7+dch.8+dch.8-followup commits from archive/dch-pipeline-foundation as the v5 foundation, then add the v6 gaia_source_id field on top.

The existing in-flight bead dch.10 (BinaryOrbitField runtime) and the new dch.41 (eclipse photometry) both build on dch.9's binary-orbit-pure.ts. Same pattern: cherry-pick or merge dch.9 (5bec24e) from archive/dch-pipeline-foundation when starting those beads.

# Why this branch exists rather than merging to main

The dch.5+dch.6 commits introduce build-binaries.py logic that the new plan's Phase 2 explicitly replaces. Merging them to main and then rewriting would create churn (commit then revert pattern). Keeping them on the archive branch means:
- The KEPT commits (dch.4, dch.7, dch.8, dch.8-followup, dch.9) are safely preserved on a durable, semantically-named origin branch.
- The THROWN commits (dch.5, dch.6) remain as historical context without polluting main.
- Per-bead workflows can cherry-pick only the keepers cleanly: `git cherry-pick b7eed29 7542fd6 4d36cc0 296d10f 5bec24e`.

# What to do with the old branch

The original worktree-stellata-dch-binaries branch on origin still points at the same SHA (5bec24e). It's now redundant with archive/dch-pipeline-foundation. Either branch can be deleted at any time; recommend keeping archive/dch-pipeline-foundation as the canonical one (durable name) and deleting worktree-stellata-dch-binaries after confirming no other references.
notes:
# Stellata catalogue pipeline rewrite — bead manifest ## Context Position-based cross-matching has hit a structural limit (dch.18: famous bright close binaries miss the Gaia gate because Gaia's published 5-parameter PM is corrupted by orbital wobble). The fix isn't a tighter heuristic — it's switching the entire catalogue ingest (AT-HYG / GCVS / CCDM / WDS+ORB6+Gaia) to **source-identifier-anchored** cross-matching per memory `stellata-data-fidelity-principle`. This plan's output is **bd operations only**. Each new bead carries enough scope to be executed in a fresh `/clear`ed session via `bd show `. The epic's description carries the architecture; each child's body carries focused per-task scope, files to touch, and acceptance criteria. On plan approval, the current session runs the listed `bd` commands and stops — no code work this session. Scope answers from plan-mode questions (Alex 2026-05-15): - **Full catalogue ingest** rewrite (not just multi-star matching). - **Keep dch.7–9** (v5 binary format, orbital-elements section, pure Kepler math); throw dch.5–6 and refresh-gaia-binaries.py. - **No version horizon** — quality over speed; ship at whatever version is current when done. Critical memories that govern this work: `stellata-data-fidelity-principle` (the bar), `stellata-prioritization-framework` (priority bands), `frozen-external-data` (committed inputs, no live fetch in build), `defer-doc-updates` (docs at commit time), `stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep` (sweep stale prose at commit time), `subagent-bd-prime-protocol` (subagents need `bd prime` instruction), `stellata-test-coverage-discipline`, `stellata-bd-operations`, `bead-authoring-scope`. --- ## Architecture (compressed — goes into epic description verbatim) Five layers. Layer 1 = committed reference data. Layer 2 = manual-run refresh scripts. Layer 3 = catalogue builder (Python). Layer 4 = validation harness. Layer 5 = docs. ### Layer 1 — Committed external reference data (`data/`, LFS where >1 MB) | File | Source | Purpose | | --- | --- | --- | | Already committed | WDS, ORB6, AT-HYG, GCVS, CCDM | core ingest | | `data/gaia_dr3_hip_xmatch.tsv` | `gaiadr3.hipparcos2_best_neighbour` | HIP → Gaia DR3 source_id | | `data/gaia_dr3_tyc_xmatch.tsv` | `gaiadr3.tyco2tdsc_merge_best_neighbour` | Tycho-2 → Gaia DR3 source_id (~2.5M rows, LFS) | | `data/gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv` | `gaiadr3.gaia_source` queried by source_id list | per-source RA/Dec/plx/PM + errors + ref_epoch | | `data/gaia_dr3_nss_two_body.tsv` | `gaiadr3.nss_two_body_orbit` | orbital elements for binaries Gaia detected | | `data/hip2_van_leeuwen.tsv` | VizieR I/311/hip2 | improved Hipparcos astrometry; long-baseline PM is the bright-binary fallback when Gaia is contaminated | | `data/simbad_sample.tsv` | SIMBAD random 10k snapshot | validation corpus | | `data/bailer-jones-dr3.tsv` | VizieR I/352 (`gedr3dis`) | Bailer-Jones 2021 Bayesian distance posteriors per Gaia DR3 source_id (~300k rows for AT-HYG.gaia subset, LFS) | | `data/gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv` | `gaiadr3.astrophysical_parameters` (Apsis: gspphot ∪ gspspec) | Teff / logg / [M/H] / A0 spectroscopic stellar parameters per source_id (~266k matched rows, LFS) | ### Layer 2 — Refresh scripts (manual, idempotent, NOT in `npm run build`) One per dataset. All share `scripts/refresh_lib.py` (TAP client + retry + batching + schema validation). Python deps pinned in `scripts/requirements-refresh.txt` (astropy, astroquery, numpy). Each script supports both astroquery.gaia (ESA TAP) and CDS TAP with auto-fallback on archive 5xx. ### Layer 3 — Catalogue builder (`scripts/build-binaries.py` rewrite) Seven stages with explicit per-component provenance: 1. **Load all reference data** into memory; build identifier-keyed indices (`hip → gaia_source_id`, `tyc → gaia_source_id`, `gaia_source_id → astrometry_row`, etc.). 2. **Resolve each WDS component to a Gaia source_id**, strict priority order: 1. ORB6 published HIP → HIP cross-walk 2. WDS Notes prose `HIP NNNNN` extraction → HIP cross-walk 3. AT-HYG row carries `gaia` field natively → use directly 4. PM-propagated position match against `data/gaia_dr3_astrometry.tsv` (narrow tolerance, ~2″) 5. Position match without PM (faint, no PM available) Each component records `resolve_via` strategy used. 3. **Attach astrometric data** with three sub-tiers per component: Gaia 5-parameter (default) → Gaia NSS-systemic (when single-star solution is flagged unreliable) → HIP2 long-baseline PM (when system is a known close binary AND |pmRA_gaia − pmRA_hip2| > 50 mas/yr OR |pmDE| > 50 mas/yr). Records `astrometry_via`. 4. **Select orbital elements per system**: Gaia NSS two-body > ORB6 (grade tiebreak) > ORB6 spectroscopic-only > none. Records `orbit_via`. 5. **Optical-pair filter cascade** (ID-anchored): 1. WDS Notes flag chars (unchanged) 2. Both-components-Gaia gate (parallax 3σ + PM 5 mas/yr) — engages for >95% of pairs vs ~73% in dch.18 position-based 3. **Asymmetric Gaia gate** (NEW) — when only secondary has Gaia, test plx vs primary's known distance. Catches Sirius A-C optical pairs directly. 4. Magnitude-gap sanity backstop 6. **Emit `data/multiples.tsv`** with provenance columns (`resolve_via`, `astrometry_via`, `orbit_via`) + `gaia_source_id` column. Existing schema preserved otherwise. 7. **Statistics output** — N resolved via each strategy, NSS-vs-ORB6 orbit counts, optical-rejected counts per tier, position-fallback residual histogram. Stats land in `build-catalog-expected.json` per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] tier-2. ### Layer 4 — Validation harness - **Tier A — Known-stars corpus** (`scripts/known-stars.tsv`): ~50–80 systems with hand-verified expected values (HIP, Gaia source_id, distance, absmag, spectral class, companions, periods). Test asserts catalog.bin matches within tolerances. Pinned with `toBe(N)` per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]]. - **Tier B — Population statistics** (extend `scripts/build-counts.test.ts`): gaia_resolve_rate ≥ 0.95, optical_rejected_rate, NSS-vs-ORB6 fractions. Snapshot-pinned, refreshed via `UPDATE_BUILD_COUNTS=1`. - **Tier C — SIMBAD sample cross-check** (`scripts/validate-simbad-sample.py`, manual-run): random 10k from snapshot, compare distance/PM/absmag against SIMBAD published. Residual histograms. ### Layer 5 — Documentation (at commit time per [[defer-doc-updates]]) - `SCIENCE.md` "Multiple-star pipeline" + "Stellar catalog ingestion" sections rewritten with citations for every cross-walk table. - `docs/cross-match.md` NEW developer-facing walk-through of stage-2 resolution. - `docs/build-and-data.md` updated for Layer 1 + Layer 2. - `CLAUDE.md` module roster + Local Commands sections updated. - v1.0 prose sweep (CLAUDE.md:252, :275; SCIENCE.md:77, :79, :187, :449, :636; docs/chart-mode.md:28, :30; docs/molecular-clouds.md:3, :13; docs/rendering.md:51; docs/solar-system.md:24, :61, :181) — Phase 0 prerequisite. ### Open design questions (resolve at start of relevant phase, no external research needed) 1. **Binary format for gaia_source_id surfacing** — bump to v6 with 8-byte field per record (~16 MB total impact). Resolved at start of Phase 3. 2. **NSS vs ORB6 tie-break** — NSS for P < ~3 yr and separation < ~1″; ORB6 otherwise. Resolved at start of Phase 2 Stage 4. 3. **Hipparcos PM fallback threshold** — engage when system has companion within 5″ AND PM discrepancy > 50 mas/yr (one-axis). Resolved at start of Phase 2 Stage 3. ### Phasing summary - **Phase 0** — v1.0 prose sweep (doc-only, skip-version-bump). - **Phase 1** — Layer 2 infrastructure + Layer 1 acquisition. - **Phase 2** — `build-binaries.py` rewrite (stages 1–7 across multiple beads). - **Phase 3** — `build-catalog.ts` integration + v6 binary format. - **Phase 4** — validation harness (Tiers A, B, C). - **Phase 5** — spectral / mass refinement (folds existing dch.15, .16, .17). - **Phase 6** — docs sweep + landing. - **Phase 7** — dch.10 (BinaryOrbitField runtime, existing bead) executes against the new pipeline. Each phase's beads can largely parallelise within phase; phases land sequentially. ### Coverage across the full catalogue (not just multi-star systems) The rewrite was triggered by multi-star matching failures (dch.18), but the architecture uplifts single-star ingest as well. Single-star benefits are concentrated in: - **Bead M (v6 binary format with `gaia_source_id`)** — every star in the 313k-star catalog, not just binaries, surfaces a Gaia source_id through to `catalog.bin`. Single-star downstream code (search index, GCVS lookup, future POI / observation features) can use it. - **Bead N (GCVS cross-match prefers `gaia_source_id`)** — variability matching for every star benefits; the GCVS match rate is expected to rise for stars without HIP/HD identifiers because AT-HYG carries Gaia source_id for Gaia-DR3-sourced rows (the ~98% of the catalog with Gaia distances). - **Distance refinement (Beads Y + AA — Bailer-Jones posteriors + LMC kinematic override)** — three-layer principled distance stack replaces naive 1/π for every Gaia-source-id-bearing star: - **Bead Y (B-J Bayesian)**: `r_med_photogeo` with `r_med_geo` fallback from VizieR I/352. Direction-dependent Galactic prior + CMD prior. Self-targets the noisy-parallax regime. Applies Lindegren et al. (2021) zero-point correction. - **Bead AA (LMC kinematic)**: sky-cone + PM gate identifies ~60 LMC supergiants in AT-HYG; distance overridden to Pietrzyński 2019's 49.59 kpc. Fills the gap B-J's Galactic prior can't (LMC isn't in the prior). - Layers compose: B-J runs first across the catalog, LMC kinematic runs second on B-J's output, the `MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000` safety gate runs last as a bounded-scope filter. - `dist_src` enum extends to `{HIP, GJ, T, N, G_R3, BJ, LMC_KIN, ...}` — future satellite refinements (SMC, Sgr, M31) follow the same pattern. - Fixes two distinct outlier classes: "stars outside the disc wireframe" (B-J catastrophic-inversion fix) AND "stars in the void between MW and LMC" (LMC kinematic fix). - **Bead Q (known-stars validation corpus)** — required to include single non-binary canonical stars (Sol, Vega, Polaris, Procyon-as-single-not-double-test, sample faint Tycho-population stars) alongside the binaries. Tier-A vitest (Bead R) asserts on both. - **Beads F + S (SIMBAD random 10k + Tier-C validator)** — random sample stratified by magnitude covers all star types, validating the full catalogue not only the binary subset. - **Phase 0 (v1.0 prose sweep)** — touches the entire docs surface, not multi-star-scoped. - **Phase 1 Bead D (Hipparcos van Leeuwen)** — improved astrometry for every HIP star, not just close binaries. - **Phase 1 Bead BB + Phase 3 Bead CC (Gaia DR3 Apsis astrophysical parameters — dch.48 + dch.49)** — Apsis (gspphot ∪ gspspec) brings (Teff, logg) coverage from 29.3% (status-quo spectral-class parsing) to 84.8% across the catalog. Per stellata-zsr.1 empirical probe (1000-star sample, seed 20260517) — 2.87× uplift. Tier 1 colour LUT (zsr.3), Phase 5 mass / radius refinement, and azero_gspphot dust-map cross-check all benefit from Apsis-direct parameters where available; spectral-class fallback retained for the ~15% gap. The multi-star pipeline is where the source-ID-anchored architecture pays off most visibly (because position-match fails worst there), but the architecture itself is uniform across the ingest. --- ## Bead operations All operations target the existing `stellata-dch` epic. Rename epic title, rewrite description with the architecture above, close superseded beads, reparent existing in-flight refinement beads, create new children. Throughout: priorities use the project framework (`stellata-prioritization-framework`). The catalogue rewrite is **physical-reality** work → P1 for in-flight stages, P2 for refinements that aren't blocking the spine. The rewrite as a whole is a single unbroken sequence, but Phase 0 + Phase 1 can parallelise. ### 1. Update epic ``` bd update stellata-dch \ --title="Catalogue pipeline — source-ID-anchored cross-match across all ingest stages" \ --description="" ``` The new description starts with a "Why" section anchored on memory `stellata-data-fidelity-principle`, then includes the architecture as written. References to dch.10 (the headline-feature child that runs against the new pipeline) preserved. ### 2. Position-anchored Gaia upload approach replaced with HIP/Tycho/source_id-anchored ADQL queries. The 43k-row TSV produced today is discarded; refresh-gaia-binaries.py likewise." bd close stellata-dch.19 --reason="folded into Phase 2 Stage 2 source-ID resolution chain (stellata-dch.27). AT-HYG↔Gaia dedupe now an output of the resolution chain rather than a separate pass." ``` ### 3. --type= --labels= --description=""`. Use HEREDOC pattern per memory `stellata-bd-operations`. After creation, add inter-bead dependencies via `bd dep add`.
