- `npm test` + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build:catalog`. No user-visible scene change.

# Connects

- [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]] — rule 2 (parameterise differences between near-identical implementations).
- [[stellata-pattern-coverage-across-peers]] — applies sister-layer extension: dch.55/56/58 are the queued peers; ensure each lands using the shared helper.
- `stellata-dch.55`, `stellata-dch.56`, `stellata-dch.58` — the imminent peers. This bead should land before any of them merge.

title: build-binaries: post-parse sanity nets for WDS_SUMM / ORB6 fixed-offset parsers
description:
`parse_wds_summ` (`build-binaries.py:207-218`) and `parse_orb6` (`:299-328`) are fully positional fixed-width parsers. If WDS_SUMM or ORB6 shifts a column by even one character (which has historically happened on these catalogues), every numeric field becomes garbage and `safe_float` returns None. The script reports "loaded N rows" with no flag that 100% of `P_val` is None or every `theta_last` is garbage.

## Acceptance

For each parser, after parsing assert that the non-null rate of at least one representative field is within a known band. Example shapes:

- `parse_orb6`: at least 90% of rows must have non-None `P_val` (period is the headline column; nearly every ORB6 entry carries one). If <90%, raise `SystemExit` with a column-offset-drift diagnostic.
- `parse_wds_summ`: at least 95% of rows must have non-None `precise_ra_deg` / `precise_dec_deg` (the precise-coord block has been mandatory since 2008-era WDS releases). If <95%, raise `SystemExit`.

Calibrate the thresholds against current committed data — empirically these should pass at ~100%.

## Smoke

Re-run `build-binaries` on committed inputs; both sanity nets pass with current data. Then test the failure mode: deliberately shift the offsets in a local copy of one parser, confirm the sanity net raises with the diagnostic message.

title: Refresh scripts: pin spot-check rows for hip-xmatch, tyc-xmatch, bailer-jones
description:
Three refresh scripts have only structural guards (schema + row-count bounds). A future column rename, unit shift, or value drift that preserves row count would pass silently:

- `refresh-gaia-hip-xmatch.py` — only row count [99,400, 99,600]
- `refresh-gaia-tyc-xmatch.py` — only row count [2,510,000, 2,530,000]
- `refresh-bailer-jones.py` — row count + 90% coverage floor, no per-row pin

`refresh-hipparcos2` / `refresh-gaia-nss` / `refresh-gaia-apsis` all pin specific rows. The three uncovered scripts are precisely the foundational ones: Bailer-Jones is the distance override for ~300k AT-HYG stars; HIP/Tyc xmatches are how every classical identifier reaches a Gaia source.

## Acceptance

For each of the 3 scripts:

- Pin 2-3 well-known rows (e.g. a couple of HIP→Gaia pairs documented externally, a Bailer-Jones distance for a famous Gaia source).
- Spot-check format follows `refresh-gaia-nss`'s existing `check_spot_row` pattern (delta-list, all-mismatches-in-one-failure). This pattern should be lifted to `refresh_lib` per the refactor bead (sibling under 9mm); land that helper first OR file as a follow-up to keep this bead scoped.
- For Bailer-Jones, at minimum cross-check one source's `r_med_geo` / `r_med_photogeo` against the values in the published paper (Bailer-Jones et al. 2021) so the script asserts agreement with the upstream of record, not just internal self-consistency.

## DR4 forward-compatibility (H8 from the retrospective)

Pinned Gaia DR3 source_ids could be retired in a maintenance re-release. Two options:

- Pin 5-6 IDs per script and tolerate 1-2 retirements as soft failures.
- Resolve pinned IDs through an external anchor (HIP X → look up its Gaia source_id at runtime, then assert pinned values).

Recommended approach: external-anchor for the xmatch scripts; pin-with-tolerance for Bailer-Jones. Document the choice in each script's spot-check comment block.

## Smoke

Re-run each script with `--force`. All three should pass with current pinned values.

title: AT-HYG ingest: stop swallowing KeyError, unify '0 = missing' convention, lift read_athyg_source_ids
description:
The AT-HYG CSV is consumed by three scripts (`refresh-bailer-jones`, `refresh-gaia-apsis`, `build-binaries.py:parse_athyg`). Four coupled defects surfaced in the retrospective on PRs 90-97.

**H4 — `parse_athyg` swallows KeyError.** `build-binaries.py:115-123` catches `KeyError` alongside `ValueError`, so an AT-HYG column rename (e.g. `absmag` → `absMag`) silently drops every row. The driver then logs `"loaded 0 AT-HYG rows"` with no error. The except clause should not swallow `KeyError`; a missing header column is a fatal misconfiguration, not per-row dirty data.

**H5 — `"0"` sentinel handled inconsistently.** AT-HYG uses `""` / `"0"` as the missing-sentinel for `hip` / `tyc` / `gaia`. `refresh-bailer-jones` (line 133) and `refresh-gaia-apsis` (line 187) filter on `cell != "0"`. `parse_athyg` in `build-binaries` does NOT — `safe_int("0") → 0` and the row gets installed under `hip=0` / `gaia=0` keys. Any downstream `src_to_athyg.get(0)` returns a sentinel-0 star instead of None. Convention must live in ONE place and be applied everywhere.

**D1 — `read_athyg_source_ids` is duplicated.** Verbatim copy between `refresh-bailer-jones` (lines 120-135) and `refresh-gaia-apsis` (lines 172-189). The apsis docstring even says "Same contract as refresh-bailer-jones.read_athyg_source_ids; identical selection." Canonical "extract at second usage" case from CLAUDE.md DRY rules.

**D7 — Asymmetric `safe_int` / empty-string handling.** `parse_athyg`'s `tyc` handling uses `(r.get("tyc") or "").strip() or None` (correctly converts empty to None) but the int fields don't — `safe_int("0")` returns `0` silently. Symmetric handling required across the row.

## Acceptance

1. Lift `read_athyg_source_ids(path) -> list[int]` to `refresh_lib` with the "skip empty AND skip '0'" convention documented in the docstring. Both refresh scripts call it.
2. `parse_athyg`'s `try/except` no longer catches `KeyError` — a missing required column raises and surfaces immediately.
3. `parse_athyg` installs hip/gaia indices keyed only by truthy non-zero ids: `if row.hip not in (None, 0)`, same for `gaia`. Document the "0 = missing" convention in one CLAUDE.md / docstring location.
4. Tests: (a) fixture with a renamed AT-HYG column → `parse_athyg` raises (not silently empty); (b) fixture with `hip="0"` and `gaia="0"` → both excluded from indices; (c) `refresh_lib.read_athyg_source_ids` round-trip on a 3-row fixture (one valid, one empty, one `"0"`).

## Smoke

Re-run `build-binaries.py` on committed AT-HYG; cardinality of `hip_to_athyg` / `src_to_athyg` unchanged or off by the count of any pre-existing `hip=0` / `gaia=0` sentinel rows (which would be a finding, not a regression).

title: Phase 3: OGLE Cepheid cross-match for AT-HYG LMC members
description:
Phase 3 refinement on top of stellata-dch.8l0. Replace the flat 49.594 kpc LMC anchor with per-star distances from OGLE-IV's LMC Cepheid catalog where the AT-HYG row cross-matches a Cepheid. Layered AFTER dch.8l0 (which catches the population) and BEFORE dch.56 (structural-disc fallback for non-Cepheid LMC members).

# Goal

For each AT-HYG LMC supergiant flagged by dch.8l0 (~57 stars post-PM-widen), check if it's an LMC Cepheid in OGLE-IV. If yes, override its distance with OGLE's PL-derived per-star distance modulus (precision ±1-2%, i.e. ±0.5-1 kpc at 50 kpc — far better than dch.56's structural disc ±3 kpc or dch.8l0's flat anchor).

# Coverage estimate

Of the ~57 AT-HYG LMC supergiants:
- Most are B-supergiants (HDE 268xxx range, non-pulsating) — no OGLE entry.
- ~5-15 are likely classical Cepheids (long-period, V ~11-12 — brightest end of the LMC Cepheid PL relation overlaps AT-HYG's bright cut). These get OGLE individual distances.
- Remainder fall back to dch.56's structural disc, or to dch.8l0's flat anchor if neither lands.

# Data ingest

- **Source**: OGLE-IV Magellanic System Variables — LMC Cepheids (Soszyński et al. 2015, AcA 65, 297).
- **Public download**: http://ogledb.astrouw.edu.pl/~ogle/OCVS/data/lmc/cep/ident.dat (or via VizieR mirror).
- **Refresh script**: `scripts/refresh-ogle-lmc-cepheids.py` — fetches the catalog, parses to TSV, writes to `data/ogle-lmc-cepheids.tsv` (LFS-tracked since it's ~1-5 MB).
- **Columns needed**: source ID (OGLE-LMC-CEP-NNNN), RA, Dec, period, mode (FU/FO/multi), V_max, I_max, distance modulus, Gaia DR3 source_id (if available — the OGLE team has cross-matched).
- **Cross-match key**: Gaia DR3 source_id when present; fall back to (RA, Dec) within 1″ for any rows without Gaia ID.

# Build-catalog.ts integration

Order in `readStars()`:
1. B-J override (dist_src gated, dch.47 + dch.47-fix).
2. dch.8l0 LMC kinematic cone+PM filter (sets a star's distance to a temporary flat anchor + flags it as LMC).
3. **NEW: OGLE Cepheid cross-match** — if the LMC-flagged star has a matching OGLE-LMC-CEP entry, override flat anchor with OGLE PL distance. Tag dist_src = `OGLE_CEP` or similar.
4. (Future dch.56) structural-disc fallback for LMC-flagged stars not matched by OGLE.
5. MAX_DIST_PC cutoff.

Per-OGLE-row distance: `d = 10^((m - M - μ_0)/5 + 1) pc` where μ_0 is the OGLE-published distance modulus, m the observed mag, M the absolute mag. OGLE publishes μ_0 directly per Cepheid — use it; don't re-derive from PL relation.

# DRY note

dch.55 (SMC kinematic) will want the same OGLE cross-match for SMC Cepheids. File a sibling SMC-OGLE bead later; the cross-match helper should be generic (population-parameterised) from this PR. Per CLAUDE.md "Extract at second usage" — extract the helper now, parameterise on `OGLE_LMC_CEPHEIDS` vs future `OGLE_SMC_CEPHEIDS`.

# Acceptance

- `scripts/refresh-ogle-lmc-cepheids.py` exists and produces `data/ogle-lmc-cepheids.tsv` deterministically.
- `scripts/catalog-pure.ts` exports `parseOgleCepheidTsv` and `applyOgleCepheidDistance` (or similar names matching existing B-J pattern).
- New tests in `catalog-pure.test.ts`:
  - Cross-match round-trip: a fixture Cepheid with known μ_0 → distance recomputed within 1%.
  - Non-Cepheid LMC star (no OGLE entry) → null override (falls through to dch.56 / flat anchor).
  - Cepheid outside LMC (foreground Galactic Cepheid) → null override (gated by LMC flag from dch.8l0, not by sky position alone).
  - Cross-match priority test: OGLE wins over dch.8l0's flat anchor on the same star.
- `npm run build:catalog` logs new line: `OGLE Cepheid override: N / M LMC-flagged stars (X%) → dist_src='OGLE_CEP'`.
- BuildCounts schema gains `ogleCepheidMatched` and `ogleLmcEligible` fields.
- Manual smoke: a known LMC long-period Cepheid (e.g. HV 12198 / OGLE-LMC-CEP-0451 if it's in AT-HYG) ends up at its OGLE distance, not the flat 49.594 kpc.

# Out of scope

- Non-Cepheid distance refinement (dch.56 structural disc).
- SMC Cepheid OGLE cross-match (file separately once dch.55 lands).
- RR Lyrae cross-match — they're ~3 mag fainter than Cepheids, very few would overlap AT-HYG's bright cut.
- Full LMC stellar-population overlay (filed as separate epic).

# References

- Soszyński et al. 2015, AcA 65, 297 — OGLE-IV LMC Classical Cepheids
- Pilecki et al. 2018, ApJ 862, 43 — OGLE Cepheid PL relation calibration
- OGLE Collection of Variable Stars: http://ogle.astrouw.edu.pl/

title: Distance-regression QA: SIMBAD + self-consistency build gate
description:
Post-mortem from the dch.47 dist_src bug (fixed in PR #99 alongside dch.8l0): the bug rendered 11 stars at 16-40 kpc instead of their correct 0.2-1 kpc Hipparcos distances. It went undetected through dch.47's merge because nothing in the pipeline cross-checks our final pipeline distance against an authoritative external source.

Alex flagged this as a high-priority QA gap: "I'm constantly worried that we are letting bugs like this in".

# Two regression-detection approaches, file as one bead with two acceptance criteria

# (1) SIMBAD distance-consistency check

We already ingest `data/simbad_sample.tsv` (committed via dch.26 / commit 7f2c150). For each star in the sample:

- Pipeline final distance (from catalog.bin) vs SIMBAD's recommended distance (from parallax / spectral classification / standard candle).
- Flag any star where the ratio is > 5× off in either direction.
- Flag if SIMBAD's object type is incompatible with our absmag (e.g. SIMBAD calls it a star but our absmag implies it's a galaxy, or vice versa).

For the four spotcheck HIPs that exposed the dch.47 bug:
- HIP 25097: SIMBAD ~300 pc, pipeline 18.5 kpc → 62× — TRIPS THE CHECK.
- HIP 23527: SIMBAD ~250 pc, pipeline 36.4 kpc → 146× — TRIPS THE CHECK.
- HIP 7912:  SIMBAD ~800 pc, pipeline 16.2 kpc → 20× — TRIPS THE CHECK.
- HIP 3934:  SIMBAD ~700 pc, pipeline 34.2 kpc → 47× — TRIPS THE CHECK.

Even with the small sample (`simbad_sample.tsv` is a sample, not the full SIMBAD), all four would have been flagged.

# (2) Self-consistency check (no external dependency)

For each star: compare AT-HYG `dist` (the input value, with its `dist_src` tag) against the pipeline final distance:

- Compute `|log10(final / athyg_dist)|`.
- If the star's dist_src is HIP or GJ AND |ratio| > 0.5 (factor of 3+), flag — Hipparcos / Gliese-Jahreiß distances are reliable for nearby stars; a 3× shift from those sources indicates an override misfire.
- If the star's dist_src is G_R3 or G_R2 AND |ratio| > 1.5 (factor of 30+), flag — even catastrophic-inversion outliers shouldn't shift by more than 30×.

Print the top 20 most-extreme distance shifts at the end of the build (sorted by ratio descending), grouped by dist_src.

This catches the dch.47 bug from first principles, without needing SIMBAD at all.

# Implementation

Both checks live alongside `assertOrUpdateBuildCounts` in `build-catalog.ts`. They run after the binary is written; produce a structured report; fail the build only on the most extreme outliers (configurable threshold). Outliers below the threshold are logged but don't fail — a star intentionally moved 20% by B-J shouldn't fail the gate.

Same pattern as the existing build-counts assertion: committed `build-distance-outliers-expected.json` (or `.json.gz` for size) tracks the known-acceptable outliers; the build fails if the set changes. Refresh deliberately with `UPDATE_DISTANCE_OUTLIERS=1`.

For SIMBAD: enable as opt-in (`SIMBAD_CHECK=1 npm run build:catalog`) since `simbad_sample.tsv` is a sample, not exhaustive — but bind to CI so a regression on the sample fails the workflow.

# Acceptance

- New build-time module `scripts/distance-regression-check.ts` exports `runDistanceRegressionCheck(catalog: Star[], simbadSample?: Map<...>): RegressionReport`.
- Report has structured fields: top-N outliers per dist_src category, SIMBAD mismatches (when enabled), threshold-passing failures.
- `build-catalog.ts` calls the check at the end of main() and exits non-zero on threshold-passing failures.
- Tests in `scripts/distance-regression-check.test.ts`: 
  - Synthetic catalog where a HIP-sourced star is shifted 20× → check trips.
  - Synthetic catalog where a G_R3 star is shifted 5× → check passes (within G_R3 tolerance).
  - Synthetic catalog with no outliers → empty report.
  - SIMBAD-disabled run produces only the self-consistency portion.
- CI workflow (`.github/workflows/test.yml` or equivalent) runs `SIMBAD_CHECK=1 npm run build:catalog` as a separate gate.
- The committed `build-distance-outliers-expected.json` snapshot ships with PR; bead description includes a one-paragraph explanation of how to refresh.

# Out of scope

- A full SIMBAD batch-query of every AT-HYG star — would require a large data download and live querying. We use the existing committed sample.
- Comparison against Gaia DR3 separately (would duplicate B-J's role).
- Spectral-class consistency: AT-HYG vs SIMBAD spectral classes. Useful but a separate check, file separately if useful later.

# References

- `data/simbad_sample.tsv` — committed sample (added in dch.26).
- `scripts/refresh-simbad-sample.py` — refresh script.
- The four HIPs flagged in this bead's preamble — use as the canonical regression fixtures.

title: Phase 3: SMC kinematic distance refinement
description:
Phase 3 sibling of stellata-dch.8l0. After the Bailer-Jones DR3 override (dch.47), AT-HYG carries a small number of SMC supergiants that get pulled to ~28 kpc by B-J's Galactic-density prior tail instead of their true ~62 kpc.

# Exemplar found by dch.8l0 spot-check

| Star     | dist_src | AT-HYG dist | B-J photogeo | sep° from SMC | pm_ra | pm_dec | Δpm vs SMC bulk |
|----------|----------|-------------|--------------|---------------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| HIP 5267 | G_R3     | 82 084 pc   | 28 353 pc    | 1.14          | 0.87  | -1.26  | (0.18, 0.03)    |

Sky position 1.1° from SMC centre, PM matches SMC bulk to (0.18, 0.03) mas/yr — well inside any reasonable tolerance. After B-J fix it renders at 28 kpc; should be at ~62 kpc.

# Why a separate bead from dch.8l0

dch.8l0 was deliberately scoped to LMC because SMC supergiants are at the bright edge of AT-HYG's brightness cut and the population is much smaller (single-digit candidates vs. ~60 for LMC). Mirrors the dch.8l0 pattern with different parameters.

# SMC parameters (literature)

- **Distance**: SMC mean distance 62.1 ± 1.9 kpc (Graczyk et al. 2014, ApJ 780, 59 — eclipsing binaries; mirrors Pietrzyński's LMC method).
- **Photometric centre**: RA ≈ 13.16° (0.877 h), Dec ≈ -72.80°.
- **Bulk PM**: μ_α* cos δ = +0.69, μ_δ = -1.23 mas/yr (Kallivayalil et al. 2013, ApJ 764, 161; van der Marel & Sahlmann 2016 / 2018 give consistent values).
- **Cone half-angle**: SMC's optical extent is smaller than LMC's (~3-4° vs ~5-7°). A 10° half-angle should be more than enough while keeping confusion with the SGP / Magellanic Bridge low.

# Approach (mirrors dch.8l0)

In `scripts/build-catalog.ts` `readStars()`, after the LMC kinematic override and before the `MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000` cutoff:

1. Test sky position: inside SMC cone (centre RA 13.16°, Dec -72.80°, half-angle 10°)?
2. Test proper motion: `(pm_ra, pm_dec)` within ±0.5 mas/yr of `(+0.69, -1.23)`?
3. If both pass, override the distance to 62 100 pc. Recompute x0/y0/z0 from (ra, dec, distance_override). Recompute absmag.
4. Tag the row's dist_src as `SMC_KIN`, joining the enum `{HIP, GJ, T, N, G_R3, BJ, LMC_KIN, SMC_KIN}`.

Parameters live as named exports in `catalog-pure.ts` alongside LMC_*:
- `SMC_DISTANCE_PC = 62_100`
- `SMC_CENTRE_RA_HOURS = 0.877`
- `SMC_CENTRE_DEC_DEG = -72.80`
- `SMC_CONE_HALF_ANGLE_DEG = 10`
- `SMC_PM_RA_CENTRE = 0.69`, `SMC_PM_DEC_CENTRE = -1.23`
- `SMC_PM_TOLERANCE = 0.5`

# DRY consideration

LMC and SMC overrides share the same shape — same checks, different parameters. Per CLAUDE.md "Extract at second usage" rule: at the second call site (this bead), factor out `applyKinematicOverride(params, ra, dec, mag, pmRa, pmDec)` taking a `KinematicPopulation` struct (centre RA/Dec/PM + cone half-angle + PM tolerance + target distance + dist_src tag), with LMC and SMC as two `KinematicPopulation` constants. The bead's PR includes this refactor as a topical commit.

# MAX_DIST_PC = 50_000 cutoff

The cutoff was set at 50 kpc for LMC scope. SMC at 62 kpc would be dropped by the current cutoff. Bump to `60_000` would still drop SMC. Bump to `70_000` to admit SMC plus a safety margin for Magellanic Stream stars (if any later land in scope).

Document the new scope: "bounded-scope final gate after refinement layers; admits LMC + SMC modelled populations." Update lives in dch.42 (SCIENCE.md rewrite) per the parent epic.

# Acceptance

- `npm run build:catalog` runs cleanly.
- New tests in `catalog-pure.test.ts` exercising the SMC override:
  - SMC-direction + SMC-PM star → distance overridden to 62 100 pc.
  - SMC-direction + NON-SMC-PM star → unchanged.
  - NON-SMC-direction + SMC-like PM star → unchanged.
- HIP 5267 ends up at 62 100 pc post-build.
- LMC override remains unchanged (no regression — 54 LMC stars still caught).
- Ordering: SMC override runs after LMC override; if a star somehow passes both (impossible given disjoint cones), document the precedence.
- `DIST_SRC_SMC_KIN = 'SMC_KIN'` exported and distinct from BJ / LMC_KIN / AT-HYG namespace.
- Refactor: `applyKinematicOverride(params, ...)` accepts a `KinematicPopulation` struct; LMC + SMC reduce to constants in the same module.

# Visual smoke

In the SMC sky direction (RA 0-2 h, Dec -70 to -76°), confirm any "smear" of stars stretched between 5-100 kpc converges to a concentrated 62 kpc shell.

# References

- Graczyk et al. 2014, ApJ 780, 59 — SMC eclipsing-binary distance
- Kallivayalil et al. 2013, ApJ 764, 161 — SMC bulk PM
- van der Marel & Sahlmann 2016 — SMC PM internal kinematics

# Out of scope

- LMC Dec PM widening (filed as a separate bead).
- LMC structural-disc per-star distance (filed as a separate bead).
- Magellanic Bridge / SMC Stream stars — most are below AT-HYG's brightness cut.

title: dch.8l0 follow-up: widen LMC Dec PM tolerance
description:
Phase 3 follow-up to stellata-dch.8l0 (PR #99). Spot-check on the LMC kinematic override surfaced three real LMC supergiants whose pm_dec values sit just over the +0.70 cutoff and are therefore not caught by the override:

| Star      | dist_src | AT-HYG dist | B-J photogeo | sep° from LMC | pm_ra | pm_dec | Δpm_dec |
|-----------|----------|-------------|--------------|---------------|-------|--------|---------|
| HIP 27655 | G_R3     | 85 470 pc   | 15 065 pc    | 3.33          | 2.09  | 0.78   | 0.57    |
| HD 269953 | G_R3     | 24 893 pc   | 16 974 pc    | 2.26          | 1.89  | 0.72   | 0.52    |
| HD 270100 | G_R3     | 62 206 pc   | 22 040 pc    | 3.23          | 1.57  | 0.74   | 0.54    |

All three have tight pm_ra (Δ ≤ 0.28), distances consistent with LMC supergiants whose B-J posterior pulled them in to ~15-22 kpc (B-J's prior has no LMC), and HDE/HD numbers in the LMC catalog range. They are almost certainly LMC members rendered as a residual smear at 15-22 kpc.

# Root cause

The dch.8l0 PM gate is symmetric ±0.5 mas/yr around (+1.85, +0.20). The actual LMC Dec PM distribution per van der Marel & Kallivayalil 2014 + per-source DR3 scatter clusters higher than +0.20 — empirically a chunk of the population sits at pm_dec ∈ [+0.6, +0.8]. The +0.20 centre is dominated by the literature centre-of-mass value; the spread is genuine LMC internal kinematics + disc rotation + per-source noise.

# Approach (options)

1. **Widen Dec tolerance to ±0.6 mas/yr** (asymmetric on the LMC params not yet supported). Catches the three outliers. Risk of admitting halo runaways scaling with the tolerance.
2. **Shift Dec centre to +0.25 mas/yr with ±0.5 tolerance**. Same window width, recentred. Worth checking the actual empirical centre on the 54 currently-flagged LMC stars before committing.
3. **Make PM gate asymmetric / per-component** (allow `pm_dec ∈ [-0.30, +0.85]` directly). Most flexible, ugliest API.

Lean: option 2 (shift centre) — empirically validate the centre against the 54 stars already caught, then expand tolerance only if needed.

Empirical check (recommended before committing): query the catalog for the 54 LMC_KIN stars after rebuild, compute mean pm_dec across them, compare against the literature +0.20. If empirical mean is ~+0.4, shift centre and keep ±0.5.

# Acceptance

- All three outlier stars (HIP 27655, HD 269953, HD 270100) end up at 49 594 pc post-build.
- The 54 currently-caught LMC stars remain caught (no regression).
- The halo controls in catalog-pure.test.ts (in-cone but not LMC, out-of-cone with LMC-like PM) remain rejected.
- Empirical mean pm_dec across the post-fix LMC_KIN population is documented in the commit message or bead notes.

# Out of scope

- HIP 28618 (in cone, dist 20 kpc, pm_dec +4.7): inconclusive, possibly Magellanic Cloud hypervelocity escapee or DR2 mismeasurement. Not addressable by widening tolerance — would need fresh Gaia DR3 / DR4 astrometry. Leave alone.
- SMC kinematic layer (filed separately).
- LMC structural disc per-star distance refinement (filed separately).

# References

- van der Marel & Kallivayalil 2014, ApJ 781, 121 — LMC bulk PM and dispersion
- Pietrzyński et al. 2019, Nature 567, 200 — distance anchor

title: Bayestar19 3D voxel supplement to Edenhofer in per-star dust raymarch
description:
**Goal:** add Bayestar19 (Green / Schlafly et al. 2019, ApJ 887:93) as a 3D dust-density supplement to the Edenhofer voxel cube in the per-star raymarch. Fills the high-fidelity-3D shell between the 1.25 kpc Edenhofer cube and the analytical slab fallback (stellata-74e). Spec context in parent **stellata-ty4**.

# Why

Edenhofer's 1.25 kpc cube is the current high-fidelity 3D source. Bayestar19 reaches out to ~4-5 kpc with useful precision (HEALPix nside=1024 ≈ 3.4 arcmin pixels, 120 log-spaced distance bins, ~3/4 sky north of Dec ≈ -30°). Inside that shell, Bayestar gives measured 3D extinction that captures large-scale structure (including arm overdensities as a side effect — see the Open question in stellata-ty4) instead of the smooth analytical slab.

# Data + pipeline

- **Source:** `dustmaps` Python package (Green 2018, JOSS). Same access shape as the Edenhofer pipeline already in `scripts/build-dust.py`.
- **Frozen-data convention:** download once, commit Bayestar19 chunks under `data/dust/bayestar19/`. The `dustmaps` package supports caching a local copy; bake from cache at build time.
- **Build script:** extend `scripts/build-dust.py` to emit a second chunked 3D texture for the Bayestar volume, with its own manifest entry alongside Edenhofer. Reuse the chunking machinery (LFS-friendly 2 MiB chunks already supports a larger AABB).
- **Shader:** `star.vert.glsl` raymarches camera→star. Two texture samples per step (Edenhofer if inside its AABB else Bayestar if inside its AABB else zero), accumulate. Per-vertex AABB tests gate which texture is read.

# Voxelisation choice

Bayestar's native projection is angular (HEALPix) × log-distance, not Cartesian. Resampling onto a regular Cartesian grid for shader sampling requires a resolution choice. Probably 10-25 pc voxels so the 8 kpc cube fits in a GPU-budget-sane texture (~512³ × 1B = ~128 MiB at 16 pc, comparable to the existing Edenhofer texture). Document the choice in `data/dust/manifest.json`.

# Coverage gap — southern sky

Bayestar has no coverage below Dec ≈ -30°. Options:
- Leave the southern shell as analytical-slab-only (74e fallback). Simplest; acceptable for v1.
- Use a different southern-sky 3D map (Lallement+2022 explores both hemispheres at ~3 kpc) as a follow-up bead if/when the gap matters visually.

# Seam handling at the Edenhofer cube boundary

Inside Edenhofer cube → Edenhofer voxels. Outside Edenhofer but inside Bayestar AABB → Bayestar voxels. The two maps should agree on absolute A_V at their shared boundary — needs calibration:
- Compute mean A_V across each face of the Edenhofer cube in both Edenhofer and Bayestar.
- Shift Bayestar by the offset so boundary values match (analogous to mosaic photometric zero-point pinning).
- Sanity: a sightline that slowly crosses the boundary should show no extinction discontinuity.

# Acceptance

- A northern-sky star at 2 kpc shows reddened/dimmed colour consistent with its Bayestar A_V (verified against a published Bayestar sample, e.g. `dustmaps.bayestar.BayestarQuery.query()` against the same RA/Dec/distance).
- Slow sightline traverse across the Edenhofer cube boundary has no visible extinction discontinuity in renderer.
- No regression in near-Sol fidelity (Edenhofer values inside the cube unchanged).
- Vertex-shader texture-read count documented in PR; if it doubles in the worst case, profile against the 313k-star draw and decide if chunk-skip optimisation is needed.

# Files

- `scripts/build-dust.py` — extend with Bayestar chunk pipeline.
- `data/dust/bayestar19/*` — committed via Git LFS.
- `data/dust/manifest.json` — Bayestar manifest entry alongside Edenhofer.
- `src/client/shaders/star.vert.glsl` — second texture sample in `dustExtinctionAV`.
- `src/client/loaders/dust-loader.ts` — load Bayestar chunks alongside Edenhofer.
- `SCIENCE.md` — add Bayestar19 to the data-source citations.

# Related

- **stellata-74e** — analytical slab fallback for sightlines outside both 3D maps.
- **stellata-ju3** — arm-overdensity contributions; overlap discussed in parent ty4 (Bayestar measures arm structure within its volume as a side effect).
- **stellata-ju3.1** — already evaluates Bayestar as a candidate for arm dust; that evaluation should settle the overlap question for both beads.

# Out of scope

- Southern-sky 3D coverage (Lallement+2022) — separate follow-up bead.
- Arm-overdensity modelling outside Bayestar's volume (kept in ju3).

title: Dust modelling — multi-source extinction stack (Edenhofer / Bayestar / slab / arms)
description:
**Goal:** treat dust extinction as a multi-source layered stack rather than the current single-source implementation. `star.vert.glsl` raymarches one texture (Edenhofer); anything beyond the 1.25 kpc cube is unattenuated by default. As stellata expands toward extragalactic scales (stellata-0hx, stellata-36y), this gap becomes visible.

# The stack — lowest fidelity → highest

Applied along the camera→star sightline; later layers override earlier ones inside their AABB.

1. **Smooth analytical disc slab** (everywhere). ρ(R, z) = exp(-(R-R₀)/L_R) × exp(-|z|/h_z), Galactic frame, h_z ≈ 125 pc. The `analyticalDustDensity` chunk already in `milkyway.frag.glsl` is the canonical source — lift into a shared dust include. Tracked by **stellata-74e**.
2. **Bayestar19 3D voxel shell** (≤ ~4-5 kpc, northern sky only). Pan-STARRS + 2MASS + Gaia DR2 stellar photometry → 3D dust (Green+2019, ApJ 887:93). Distributed via the `dustmaps` Python package. Fills the shell between the Edenhofer cube and the analytical slab. **NEW bead.**
3. **Edenhofer voxel cube** (≤ 1.25 kpc, all-sky around Sol). Current star.vert.glsl source. Unchanged.
4. **Arm-overdensity contributions** modulating the above. Tracked by **stellata-ju3** + children.

# Why now

Triggered 2026-05-18 by an exploratory Q: "would the apparent magnitude of a MW star be correct viewed from M31?" The distance modulus is correct (camera-to-star, star.vert.glsl line 217) but the dust raymarch zeros out beyond the 1.25 kpc cube — so an intergalactic-vantage sightline through the MW disc is physically under-attenuated by orders of magnitude. The multi-layer stack closes the gap.

# Seam handling (TBD per child)

The cube boundary needs Edenhofer and Bayestar (and the slab) to read continuously — no jump in extinction as a sightline crosses an AABB. Options to evaluate per-bead:
- Hard switch at AABB boundary; calibrate normalisations so boundary values agree.
- Smooth crossfade across a thin shell at the AABB.
- Higher-fidelity source per sample with normalisation pinned at boundary voxels.

# Open question — Bayestar shell vs arm overdensity baking

`stellata-ju3.1` evaluates Bayestar19 as a candidate data source for *arm overdensities*, alongside Drimmel & Spergel 2001, Marshall 2006, Lallement 2022. The new Bayestar near-shell bead uses Bayestar as a *general* 3D extinction supplement to Edenhofer — which already captures arm overdensities within its volume as a side effect of being a measured (not modelled) dust map. ju3's scoping should settle whether:
- Bayestar near-shell subsumes the near-Sol portion of ju3's arm work, leaving ju3 to model arms outside Bayestar's volume; or
- The two are independent and stack (Bayestar supplies 3D fidelity, ju3 supplies analytic arm-overdensity correction beyond data coverage).

# Children

- **stellata-74e** — analytical slab fallback (already filed; now positioned as one layer of the stack)
- **stellata-ju3** — galactic arm dust (sub-epic; ju3.1 + ju3.2 unchanged)
- **NEW** — Bayestar19 3D voxel supplement to Edenhofer
- **NEW** — Extragalactic-vantage extinction for non-stellar layers (P3)

# Out of scope

- Replacing Edenhofer near-Sol. The high-fidelity local data stays the source of truth inside its cube.
- Cosmological-distance reddening (z-dependent dust attenuation across the observable universe). Negligible compared to MW slab even at Mpc scales.
- Per-object visualisation overlays showing dust-layer boundaries — file under stellata-io3 if wanted.
- Wavelength-selective extinction physics refinements per molecular cloud — tracked by stellata-c7u.3 under the MC epic, not here.

title: Local Group luminosity — design gate + LVDB completeness survey
description:
# Scope

Design gate for the [[stellata-gxx]] epic. Outputs a written spec
(new section in docs/local-group.md) and a per-object data plan.
No code lands in this bead other than the docs.

# Deliverables

1. **LVDB completeness survey.** For every object in `local-group.json`
   today (LMC, SMC, Sagittarius, M32, NGC 205, M31, M33 standalones +
   all LVDB dwarfs), tabulate which of these fields are populated and
   to what precision:
   - `apparent_magnitude_v`, `M_V`
   - `rad_sersic`, `n_sersic`
   - `surface_brightness_rhalf`
   - `rhalf_physical` (parsec half-light radius)
   - `ellipticity`, `position_angle`
   Identify rows with missing photometry or missing Sérsic params and
   pick a fallback (King profile, half-light-radius spheroid with
   default n=1, or hand-curated override row).

2. **Profile-family assignment per object class.**
   - Sérsic spheroid for dSphs, M32, NGC 205, Sagittarius dSph.
   - Exponential thin disc + Sérsic bulge for M31, M33.
   - LMC: decide whether disc or spheroid is more honest at LG-render
     scale (current 4500/4500/1000 pc inclined-disc shell suggests disc;
     interior bar+arms not modelled — see epic out-of-scope note).
   - SMC: spheroid with extreme line-of-sight elongation (current
     3730/4960/6000 pc shell — keep matched profile).

3. **Calibration scheme spec.** Derive the per-object DENSITY0 solver:
   given catalog `apparent_magnitude_v`, distance, and profile geometry
   (R_eff, n, axis ratios), what offline integral solves for the
   density normalisation so that a fragment raymarch through the proxy
   mesh integrates to the right total flux? Mirror milkyway's approach
   (docs/milky-way.md § Magnitude consistency with stars) — the
   conversion path is the same; only the integration over the profile
   shape differs per family.

4. **Camera-inside-vs-outside decision.** milkyway's shader handles
   "camera inside the proxy mesh" (we live in the disc) — LG objects
   are normally viewed from outside, but the user CAN warp into LMC /
   SMC / M31. Decide the split: ray entry = camera position when
   inside, front-face intersection when outside. Same shape as
   milkyway's logic; document the parallel.

5. **Dust handling.** None — LG objects are well outside the MW dust
   slab, and their internal dust is below the photometric tolerance.
   Confirm and write this into the spec so the shader doesn't carry
   needless dust uniforms.

6. **Per-object data plan.** Concrete list of what fields need to be
   plumbed through the build pipeline for each object. Drives the
   pipeline bead (next child).

# Acceptance

docs/local-group.md gains a "Physical luminosity model" section with
all six items above resolved. PR review-ready (no TODOs, no
"decide later" gaps).

# Smoke

Docs read review by Alex.

# Connects

Parent: [[stellata-gxx]]. Blocks the pipeline + shader beads.

title: Local Group physical luminosity — integrated apparent V-mag from any camera position
description:
# Problem

The Local Group layer (`src/client/local-group/local-group.ts`) renders
structural wireframes (LMC inclined disc, SMC line-of-sight elongation,
dSph projected ellipses, M31/M33 tilted discs, etc.) but emits NO light.
Each object's apparent V-magnitude is implicitly +inf from every camera
position. From any viewpoint near the Local Group this is unphysical:
M31 (m_V ≈ 3.4) should read as a bright extended glow from inside the
MW, the LMC (m_V ≈ 0.9) should dominate parts of the southern sky, and
the dSph host's brightness should rise as the camera approaches.

This is a tier-2 "physical reality fix" per [[stellata-prioritization-framework]].

# Scope

Per LG object, render an analytically-modelled luminous volume such
that the integrated apparent V-magnitude observed from the camera
matches the catalog measurement for that object, from ANY camera
position inside or outside the Local Group.

Parallel to the existing volumetric Milky Way (`milkyway.ts` +
shaders/milkyway.{vert,frag}.glsl, see docs/milky-way.md):
raymarched proxy mesh + analytical density profile per fragment +
emission converted to apparent V-mag via the same magnitude-
consistency conversion the star renderer + MW already use. The LG
variant extends this to N per-object instances with two profile
families:

- **Sérsic spheroid** — for dSphs, compact ellipticals (M32, NGC 205),
  and bulges. LVDB rows already carry `rad_sersic` (arcmin),
  `n_sersic`, `surface_brightness_rhalf`. Where Sérsic params are
  missing, fall back to a King / half-light-radius spheroid using the
  fields already present.
- **Exponential thin disc + Sérsic bulge** — for M31, M33, and
  potentially LMC. Disc scale length sourced from papers (DOIs in
  overrides.tsv per existing override schema). M31 R_d ≈ 5.3 kpc,
  M33 R_d ≈ 1.8 kpc.

The structural wireframe layer continues to render as a low-opacity
overlay defining each object's silhouette. The volumetric pass adds
emission inside that silhouette. Both share the per-object
`LocalGroupObject` record so structural edits propagate to both.

# Calibration target

Each fragment converts analytical emission → effective apparent V-mag
using the same path as `milkyway.frag.glsl`'s magnitude-consistency
block (see docs/milky-way.md § Magnitude consistency with stars).
Per-object DENSITY0 is solved offline (in the build pipeline) so that
the analytical surface-brightness integral over the proxy mesh equals
the catalog `apparent_magnitude_v` at the object's known distance.
Tolerance: ±0.1 mag from any camera viewpoint.

# Children (sequential — each is one commit/PR + smoke step)

1. **Design gate (P2)** — LVDB completeness survey per object,
   profile-family assignment, calibration scheme spec, dust handling
   (none — LG objects are well outside the MW dust slab), camera-
   inside-vs-outside split decision. Output: docs/local-group.md
   physical-luminosity section + per-object data plan.
2. **Data pipeline extension** — `scripts/build-local-group.ts` +
   `overrides.tsv` surface V-mag + profile-type + profile-params into
   `local-group.json`. Add M31/M33 photometry + structural details
   (Sérsic bulge, exponential disc scale length, paper DOIs) to
   overrides. Offline DENSITY0 solver runs at build time.
3. **Sérsic-spheroid shader** — `local-group-emission.{vert,frag}.glsl`
   raymarched per-instance Sérsic profile (per-instance density0,
   n_sersic, R_eff, axis ratios). LMC/SMC/M32/NGC 205/Sgr + all dSphs.
4. **Exponential-disc shader (M31/M33)** — disc + Sérsic-bulge
   composite per-instance. Likely shares the spheroid pass with the
   bulge component layered on the disc proxy mesh.
5. **Calibration test** — automated vitest places synthetic camera at
   N viewpoints (deep MW interior, at Sun, near LMC, between MW and
   M31, beyond M33), samples rendered flux per object silhouette,
   converts back to apparent V-mag, asserts ≤ 0.1 mag deviation.
   Pinned numbers per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] rule (b).
6. **Debug panel + URL flag + chart-mode** — `local-group-tuning.ts`
   section, `lge=0` to disable, chart-mode handling (likely hide the
   volumetric layer in chart mode, mirroring milkyway's chart-mode
   isobar swap).

Children 3 and 4 may execute in parallel after the design gate +
pipeline land. Per [[bead-authoring-scope]] they stay split because
the shaders are distinct sub-systems even when they share a parent.

# Out of scope

- Per-star resolution of LG objects (statistical/aggregate regime per
  [[stellata-cds-data-sources-and-detail-gradient]]).
- Internal structure (spiral arms in M31, bar+arms in LMC). Smooth
  axisymmetric profile is the right detail tier — same call milkyway
  made when shelving spiral-arm overdensities.
- Variable / time-dependent emission.
- Colour decomposition by stellar population (single per-object
  population colour, configurable via overrides if needed).
- Dust extinction internal to LG objects. MW dust slab does not
  reach them; their own dust is below the photometric tolerance.

# Connects

- [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] — solve the class of problem;
  no hand-tuned per-object calibration outside the LVDB-derived path.
- [[stellata-cds-data-sources-and-detail-gradient]] — integrated
  brightness is the right detail tier for far-field objects.
- [[stellata-defer-detail-until-zoom-affordance]] — integrated profile,
  not per-star.
- [[frozen-external-data]] — all photometry lives in committed
  data/local-group/ files.
- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule 1 — new files
  land in src/client/local-group/.

title: Phase 3 — Surface Gaia DR3 Apsis (Teff/logg/[M/H]/A0) per record in catalog binary
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture). Phase 3 sibling to `stellata-dch.48` (Phase 1 Apsis ingest). Parallels the `dch.46` (B-J ingest) + `dch.47` (B-J distance override) split.

Brings Gaia DR3 Apsis astrophysical parameters (Teff / logg / [M/H] / A0 from gspphot ∪ gspspec) into the catalog binary so the runtime shader path can re-key onto Apsis-direct values. Coverage uplift validated empirically by `stellata-zsr.1`: (Teff, logg) goes from 29.3% (status-quo spectral-class parsing) to 84.8% with Apsis — a 2.87× gain.

# Scope

Surface Apsis fields per record in the catalog binary, keyed by the v6 `gaia_source_id` field that lands in `stellata-dch.33` (Bead M). Apsis coverage is ~85%, so the layout decision is non-trivial.

# Open design question (resolve at start of execution)

Inline vs sparse side-table.

- **Inline**: 7 floats (teff_gspphot, logg_gspphot, mh_gspphot, azero_gspphot, teff_gspspec, logg_gspspec, mh_gspspec) × 4 bytes × 313k = ~10 MB delta. Simpler; O(1) per-record access; null sentinels for the 15% gap.
- **Side-table**: keyed on gaia_source_id; ~85% × ~10 MB = ~8.5 MB delta (saves ~1.5 MB). Costs a second binary section + a hashmap lookup at decode.

Likely choice: **inline** — 1.5 MB savings doesn't pay for the complexity given catalog.bin is already ~13 MB. May trigger a v6→v7 bump or fold into a single v6 bump if dch.33 hasn't shipped yet (decide based on whether dch.33 is in flight or already merged when this bead starts).

# Deliverables

- **`scripts/build-catalog.ts`** — load `data/gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv`, index by `source_id`, attach Apsis fields to each v6/v7 record keyed off `gaia_source_id`. Null-row handling for the 15% gap.
- **`scripts/catalog-pure.ts`** — packer / unpacker updates for the new fields. Field offsets + record stride constants bumped. Pure helpers (TSV parser, source_id → ApsisRow indexer) lift to `*-pure.ts` siblings per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]].
- **`scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts`** — field-offset + null-sentinel + round-trip regression tests. Byte-signature pin on a fixture record.
- **`scripts/verify-catalog.ts`** — recognise the new format version; dump Apsis fields in record-inspection output.
- **`src/client/catalog-loader.ts`** — decode the new fields; expose `teff_apsis` / `logg_apsis` / `mh_apsis` / `azero_apsis` / `teff_gspspec` / `logg_gspspec` / `mh_gspspec` on the Catalog struct.

# Downstream re-routings (NOT in this bead's scope — each filed separately)

- **Tier 2 colour-LUT re-routing (`stellata-zsr.4`)** re-keys Teff source for the blackbody → sRGB LUT (shipped via `stellata-zsr.3` / PR #89) from Ballesteros(B-V) → Apsis-direct where available (Apsis preferred; Ballesteros fallback; spectral-class fallback; WD-subclass fallback; solar fallback). Folds in the per-WD Teff routing that PR #89 explicitly deferred (Sirius B / Procyon B / 40 Eri B) because Apsis carries Teff for WDs natively.
- **Phase 5 Bead T (mass-ratio, `stellata-dch.40`)** gains Apsis-direct logg for giants / subgiants — improves the mass estimate for the giant/subgiant tier of the mass-ratio fallback table.
- **Phase 5 Bead U (eclipse photometry, `stellata-dch.41`)** gains direct Teff for limb-darkening coefficient selection.
- **Stefan-Boltzmann radius refinement** using direct logg — filed separately when desired.
- **azero_gspphot cross-check against Edenhofer dust map** — filed separately when desired.

# Acceptance

- ≥80% of records have a non-null `teff_apsis` OR `teff_gspspec` (matches the 84.8% empirical-probe figure from `stellata-zsr.1` within sampling tolerance).
- Spot-check Sirius / α Cen A/B / Vega Apsis values match `data/gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv` byte-for-byte after round-trip through pack → unpack.
- `npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts` reports the new format version and dumps Apsis fields per sample record.
- typecheck + vitest green.

# Smoke

- `npm run build:catalog` regenerates `public/catalog.bin` (~16 + 10 = ~26 MB).
- `npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts` shows the new Apsis fields.
- Manual: load the app and verify the colour LUT path doesn't regress at the hot / mid / cool extremes (the actual user-visible chromaticity shift lands when `stellata-zsr.4` consumes these fields; this bead alone only surfaces the data — no rendered change yet).

# Confidence (per `alex-large-pr-honesty` at PR-open time)

- **High**: deterministic build script + field-offset regression tests + byte-signature fixture pin.
- **Medium**: client decoder integration (uniform feeds unchanged; routing logic adjacent to today's code).
- **Low (needs eyeballs)**: actual catalog.bin size delta + null-sentinel behaviour for the 15% gap. Manual smoke step — Alex inspects the size + spot-checks a few faint stars without Apsis to confirm graceful fallback.

# Origin

Filed alongside `stellata-dch.48` (Phase 1 Apsis ingest) when the epic was locked. Mirrors the `dch.46` + `dch.47` split for Bailer-Jones: Phase 1 produces the TSV, Phase 3 surfaces it into the binary build.

title: Phase 1 — scripts/refresh-gaia-apsis.py + commit data/gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture). Phase 1 sibling to dch Beads B/C/D/E (HIP xmatch, Tycho xmatch, HIP2 reduction, NSS orbits).

Brings Gaia DR3 spectroscopic stellar parameters (Apsis) into Stellata's per-star data. Empirical coverage probe in `stellata-zsr.1` showed (Teff, logg) goes from 29.3% (status quo via spectral-class parsing) to 84.8% with gspphot ∪ gspspec — a 2.87× improvement.

Full research and rationale: `research/star-spectral-rendition/RECOMMENDATION.md` § Tier 2 + § Coordination with stellata-dch. **Read it before starting.**

# Scope

Refresh script + committed TSV for Gaia DR3 Apsis astrophysical parameters per source_id. Depends on `scripts/refresh_lib.py` (stellata-dch.21).

Surfacing into the binary catalog is a SEPARATE follow-on — owned by `stellata-dch.49` (Bead CC), filed alongside this bead as the Phase 3 surfacing sibling (parallels the `dch.46` ingest + `dch.47` surface pattern for Bailer-Jones). This bead is TSV-only first per the lighter-integration path in RECOMMENDATION.md.

# Deliverables

- **`scripts/refresh-gaia-apsis.py`** (new) — ADQL pull from `gaiadr3.astrophysical_parameters`. Idempotent (skip if output newer than script). Documents the full ADQL query in module docstring.
- **`data/gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv`** (new, LFS-tracked — ~15-25 MB for ~266k matched rows) — columns: `source_id` (int64), `teff_gspphot` (float), `logg_gspphot` (float), `mh_gspphot` (float), `azero_gspphot` (float), `teff_gspspec` (float), `logg_gspspec` (float), `mh_gspspec` (float). Null-valued cells emitted as empty fields.
- **`package.json`** — new `refresh:gaia-apsis` npm alias. Manual run, NOT part of `npm run build` (per `frozen-external-data`).
- **`.gitattributes`** — verify `data/*.tsv` LFS pattern covers it.

# ADQL query

```sql
SELECT source_id,
       teff_gspphot, logg_gspphot, mh_gspphot, azero_gspphot,
       teff_gspspec, logg_gspspec, mh_gspspec
FROM gaiadr3.astrophysical_parameters
WHERE source_id IN (<source_id batch list from AT-HYG.gaia>)
ORDER BY source_id
```

Batched to fit Gaia TAP query limits (refresh_lib handles batching + retry per dch.21).

# Acceptance

- Row count ~266,000 ± 5% (projected from the 1000-source-id empirical probe, seed 20260517). Pinned in script as `EXPECTED_ROW_COUNT_MIN / MAX`; out-of-range fails the build.
- Coverage stats logged: % of input source_ids returned by Apsis, % with gspphot (Teff AND logg), % with gspspec, % union. Expected ~85% union from probe.
- TSV columns exactly: source_id, teff_gspphot, logg_gspphot, mh_gspphot, azero_gspphot, teff_gspspec, logg_gspspec, mh_gspspec. Schema validated by refresh_lib helper.
- Deterministic regen: re-running with source unchanged produces a byte-identical TSV.

# Smoke

- `npm run refresh:gaia-apsis` writes the TSV deterministically.
- `wc -l data/gaia_dr3_apsis.tsv` shows expected row count (~266k + header).
- Coverage log line shows union ≥80%.
- Spot-check: Sol's nearest neighbours (Proxima, α Cen A/B) appear with Apsis parameters that round-trip through `research/star-spectral-rendition/apsis_sample.py`.

# Commit

One commit. Single bead. Standard PR.

# Downstream uses (NOT in this bead's scope)

- v6 binary surfacing of `teff_apsis` etc. per record — owned by `stellata-dch.49` (Bead CC). Inline-vs-side-table layout decision deferred to dch.49 execution time.
- Tier 1 LUT re-keyed from Ballesteros(B-V)-derived Teff to Apsis-direct Teff where available (re-uses Tier 1's shader path — only the upstream Teff source changes).
- `azero_gspphot` cross-check against Edenhofer dust map (filed separately when desired).
- Stefan-Boltzmann radius refinement using direct logg (filed separately).
- Phase 5 (spectral / mass refinement) gains Apsis-grade Teff + logg for stars with spectra.

# Confidence (per `alex-large-pr-honesty` at PR-open time)

- **High**: deterministic refresh script + row-count assertions + LFS-tracked output.
- **Medium**: schema validation against refresh_lib helper.
- **Low (needs eyeballs)**: manual smoke step — Alex runs `npm run refresh:gaia-apsis` and inspects the log line + spot-checks Sol's neighbours.

# Origin

Filed from `stellata-zsr.1` research (RECOMMENDATION.md § Tier 2 + § Coordination with stellata-dch). Apsis ingest is the natural Phase 1 sibling: TAP query, source_id-anchored, idempotent TSV output — matches the existing dch pattern exactly.

title: Tier 1 — replace ciToColor with Ballesteros + blackbody → sRGB LUT
description:
Implement the Tier 1 recommendation from `stellata-zsr.1` research: swap the 3-stop piecewise B-V gradient in `src/client/shaders/star.vert.glsl` for a build-time-precomputed blackbody → sRGB LUT, keyed on B-V-derived Teff (Ballesteros 2012). Fixes the visible cool/red oversaturation without waiting on any external data ingest.

Full research and acceptance rationale: `research/star-spectral-rendition/RECOMMENDATION.md` § Tier 1. **Read it before starting.** The recommendation file also carries the Mu Cephei case study, FAQ / Terminology, and the empirical evidence behind the Tier 1 case (ΔE > 50 on Antares / Betelgeuse / Aldebaran).

# Scope

Single PR. No catalog schema bump. One shader change + one LUT helper + tests + SCIENCE.md citation.

# Deliverables

- **`scripts/blackbody-lut.ts`** (new) — TypeScript port of `research/star-spectral-rendition/blackbody_color.py`'s `blackbody_to_srgb`. Same Planck + CIE 1931 (Wyman 2013) + sRGB D65 transform. Emits a 256-entry RGB LUT spanning B-V ∈ [-0.4, 2.0] via Ballesteros 2012 (B-V → Teff) + blackbody → sRGB. Output embedded as a static `Uint8Array` constant in `src/client/shaders/blackbody-lut.ts` (build-time precomputation, no runtime cost).
- **`src/client/shaders/blackbody-lut.ts`** (new, generated) — the LUT data + index helpers. Generated by `blackbody-lut.ts` at build time (mirror the existing build-time codegen pattern in `scripts/`).
- **`src/client/shaders/star.vert.glsl`** (modified) — replace the `ciToColor` function with a LUT sample. Lines 137-143 today.
- **Routing logic** in `star.vert.glsl`:
  - if `ci` present: Teff = Ballesteros(B-V), sample LUT.
  - elif `spect` parseable: Teff = T_TABLE(class, subclass), sample LUT.
  - if white-dwarf: Teff = 50400 / wd_subclass (existing `tempKelvin` in `catalog-pure.ts`), sample LUT.
  - else: solar fallback (B-V = 0.65 → existing path, no behaviour change).
- **Dust reddening**: untouched — existing `effectiveCi = iCi + absorbAV / R_V` path remains; reddened B-V feeds Ballesteros and the LUT. Interface shape identical; observer-position-dependent rendering preserved (per the case study in RECOMMENDATION.md § Mu Cephei).
- **SCIENCE.md** — new citation for Ballesteros 2012 + Wyman 2013 CIE fits + blackbody → sRGB derivation. Add at commit time per `defer-doc-updates`.

# Acceptance

- HR diagram in-scene matches `research/star-spectral-rendition/hr_panels.png` panel C (continuous, physical chroma).
- Per-star RGB values for Antares / Betelgeuse / Aldebaran / Sirius B / Mintaka / Mu Cephei match the expected column from `research/star-spectral-rendition/sample_comparison.txt` (with Mu Cep validated against the case study, ~(255, 178, 96) Earth-perspective).
- No regression for warm A/F/G main sequence stars (smallest ΔE bucket — current shader is already close).
- LUT byte signature pinned by vitest — future changes go through a conscious update.

# Tests (per `stellata-test-coverage-discipline`)

- **Unit test for `scripts/blackbody-lut.ts`**: verify Ballesteros formula matches the Python reference within float epsilon at 5 sample B-V values (-0.4, 0.0, 0.65, 1.5, 2.0).
- **Unit test for the LUT generator**: byte-signature pin (`expect(lutBytes).toEqual(...)`). Anchored so any chromaticity or transform change goes through a conscious bump.
- **Shader output test** (if feasible without a GPU smoke): nominally pin Antares / Betelgeuse / Aldebaran / Sirius B / Mintaka / Sol expected RGBs via the same LUT-sample path the shader uses. Pure helpers — extract LUT sampling logic so it's testable from a `*-pure.ts` sibling.

# Smoke (per `alex-stellata-smoke-tests`)

Manual only — Alex tests in own browser, no dev server, no Playwright. After typecheck + vitest pass, Alex pulls the branch and inspects:

- **Cool / red end (the main fix):** Antares, Betelgeuse, Aldebaran, Mu Cephei. Expected: noticeably less aggressively red than today, warm peach / pumpkin / amber rather than near-pure red.
- **Mu Cephei specifically**: confirm observer-position-dependent dust reddening still works — colour drifts toward intrinsic peach as you warp closer. The case study in RECOMMENDATION.md walks through expected behaviour.
- **Hot end (no regression):** Mintaka, Sirius A, Vega. Should be blue-white, similar to today (current shader is already close at the hot end).
- **Mid range (no regression):** Sol-anchor, Procyon, Capella. Solar-ish whites unchanged.
- **HR diagram qualitative shape:** density distribution along B-V should match panel C continuity.

# Confidence (per `alex-large-pr-honesty` at PR-open time)

- **High**: build-time LUT generator (deterministic, byte-signature pinned in unit tests).
- **Medium**: shader integration (uniform-feed unchanged; routing logic adjacent to today's code).
- **Low (needs eyeballs)**: actual rendered colour vs expectation — only manual smoke catches a per-star visual regression. Hence the explicit named-star smoke list above.

# Out of scope (filed elsewhere)

- Gaia DR3 Apsis ingest — Tier 2 lives under `stellata-dch` as a separate bead.
- Display P3 wide-gamut — filed `stellata-zsr.2`, deferred.
- Pecaut & Mamajek 2013 extended T_TABLE — Tier 3, deferred.
- Metallicity-aware colour offsets — Tier 3, deferred.

# Origin

Filed from `stellata-zsr.1` research. Single-commit single-PR. Ships standalone — not a `stellata-dch` dependency.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #89 (merged 2026-05-18). Bead status drift only — code is on main.

title: Volume-complete Gaia inner sphere: catalog ingest expansion + 'you can wander here' representational overlay
description:
Gaia DR3 has made the inner ~50–100 pc around Sol effectively **volume-complete** for stellar surveys — every star down to G ~17 (catches all M dwarfs, most brown dwarfs, many faint white dwarfs) is detected by Gaia. This is unprecedented in stellar astronomy: a sphere of space where Stellata could honestly claim *every single star you can see in this volume is in the model, no selection bias.*

# The exciting framing

The inner sphere becomes a **scientifically honest navigation zone** — the user can fly to any point within it and trust that what they see is what really exists. Powerful framing for Stellata's data-fidelity principle ([[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]]). The boundary is the edge of "what we currently know about every star."

# Why the current catalog doesn't have this

`scripts/build-catalog.ts` consumes the AT-HYG **classic-IDs** subset — stars with HD/HR/HIP/Bayer/Flamsteed/Gl/TYC identifiers. This filter discards the bulk of faint M dwarfs and white dwarfs that exist in Gaia DR3 but lack classical names. So even within the inner volume-complete sphere, Stellata today represents only the brighter named subset, not the full Gaia census.

# Two coupled deliverables

## Part 1 — Catalog ingest expansion

- Supplementary pull from Gaia DR3 of all `gaia_source` rows within R pc of Sol that aren't already in AT-HYG classic-IDs.
- Required columns: source_id, ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, phot_g_mean_mag, bp_rp colour, plus Apsis Teff where available.
- New `data/gaia_dr3_inner_sphere.tsv` (LFS-tracked, idempotent refresh script — per [[frozen-external-data]]).
- Binary-catalog integration: concatenate with AT-HYG records. Likely needs a per-star "in-volume-complete-region" flag bit so the renderer can distinguish supplement stars from the bright AT-HYG core.

## Part 2 — Representational boundary overlay

The user *sees* where the survey-complete region is. Options to evaluate:

- Translucent sphere wireframe at the completeness radius (like Local Group wireframes).
- Density-based gradient: opacity falls off as a function of survey-completeness fraction at distance.
- Chart-mode-specific: different isobar / colour treatment inside vs outside the sphere.
- Navigation hint: brief HUD readout on entry / exit ("entering volume-complete zone").

The overlay slots into the artificial-visualisation framework (parent stellata-io3). Pattern parallel to stellata-io3.2 (dataset boundary overlay for Edenhofer dust volume) — same idea, different dataset.

# Research questions to settle before implementation

1. **Right R?** Gaia DR3 completeness ladder:
   - **50 pc** — gold-standard complete including faint WDs (GCNS — Gaia Catalogue of Nearby Stars).
   - **100 pc** — complete for all M dwarfs and most WDs (the standard "100-pc sample" used in galactic archaeology).
   - **200+ pc** — substantially incomplete at the faint end; would need careful selection-function modelling.
2. **Ingest strategy?** Push upstream to AT-HYG project (David Nash) vs maintain our own supplementary Gaia volume-complete TSV.
3. **Boundary representation?** Crisp sphere vs. fading gradient vs. chart-mode-only.
4. **Beyond-R interaction?** Stars outside R are still magnitude-limited; the user needs visual cue distinguishing the two regimes.
5. **Chart-mode handling?** Chart treats all stars uniformly today — should it highlight the volume-complete region?
6. **Scale of representation?** Just a radius (one number) vs a survey-completeness-fraction-per-shell layer (more sophisticated).

# Why deferred

- **stellata-dch** (catalog-architecture rewrite) in flight changes ingest mechanics significantly; doing this before dch lands means redoing it.
- **stellata-3f8** (DR4 refresh) extends volume-completeness depth — DR4 reaches further with more confidence. Doing it now means re-deciding R when DR4 lands.
- The **stellata-io3** artificial-visualisation framework is still scoping; boundary-overlay pattern needs to settle there first.

When un-deferred: **P2** (high user excitement, squarely on the data-fidelity-principle critical path). Currently P2 to mark high interest, but deferred status keeps it out of `bd ready`.

# Origin

Filed during the `stellata-zsr.1` wrap-up conversation on 2026-05-18. Surfaced while discussing why field-star HR diagrams can't give individual ages without volume-complete sampling — the Gaia 100-pc volume-complete sample is the gold standard, and Stellata could *physically* represent that as a navigable region inside the model. Conversational origin preserved in `research/star-spectral-rendition/RECOMMENDATION.md` § FAQ.

# Related

- [[stellata-dch]] — catalog rewrite; ingest mechanics overlap.
- [[stellata-3f8]] — DR4 refresh; extends volume-completeness depth.
- [[stellata-3f8.1]] — FLAME ages; same Gaia data pipeline.
- [[stellata-io3]] — artificial-viz layer; parent for the overlay.
- [[stellata-io3.2]] — dataset boundary overlay for dust; parallel pattern.
- [[stellata-zsr.1]] — research conversation that surfaced this.

title: Focus engagement gives no visible feedback when camera is already at park pose
description:
# Problem

When the user clicks to focus a star (search-select, in-3D click-to-focus, URL restore) and the camera is already at or very close to the focus-park pose for that star, the focus-park lerp runs through its motion but the visual delta is below user-perceivable thresholds. The user receives no signal that their action registered — search box updates synchronously and the focus ring engages, but those signals are quiet enough that on a busy screen the user can think the app didn't respond.

Extreme case: clicking to focus a star while exactly at its `parkDistForStar` pose (e.g. re-clicking the same focused star, or focusing from a URL restore that landed on the same pose). The lerp runs ~2 s of near-identity motion and the camera doesn't move a perceivable pixel.

UX principle (Alex): affordances need a visible effect, otherwise the app reads as broken.

This is a pre-existing issue, not a regression from any recent PR.

# Proposed solution — two coupled changes, ship together

1. **Skip near-zero focus-park lerps.** When the camera→park-pose delta (eye distance error + angular error) is below a small visual threshold, snap to the parked pose without animating. Honest camera — no fake motion on a physics-accurate viz.

2. **Add a focus-ring "engage pulse" to `focus-ring-overlay.ts`.** On the `'focus'` event, the ring fires a one-shot animation (radius bump-and-settle, or a colour flash) over ~200 ms regardless of whether the camera moved. Gives the user a "I did a thing" signal that's independent of camera state.

Critical: the pulse should ALSO fire when the camera DOES glide (the non-degenerate cases). Single consistent affordance — focus engaged = ring pulses — instead of branching the affordance on "did the camera move."

# Acceptance

- Clicking to focus a star at its current parked pose: snaps (no lerp), focus ring pulses.
- Clicking to focus a star far from its parked pose: glides as today (focus-park lerp animates over `FOCUS_LERP_MS`), focus ring pulses on engagement.
- Clicking to focus a star at intermediate distance: animates if the visual delta exceeds the threshold, snaps if not; pulse fires either way.
- Pulse plays across navigate-mode focus, search-select focus, URL-restore focus. SHOULD NOT play during observe-mode anchor swaps (`swapObserveAnchor` in WarpController) where the user perception is different (camera was already pinned at local origin).
- Tests:
  - `focus-transition.test.ts` (or a paired pure helper) covers the early-exit threshold — pin the threshold value as a constant, test the "snap when below" / "lerp when above" branches.
  - `focus-ring-overlay.test.ts` covers the pulse animation lifecycle (engage on `'focus'`, decay to baseline after ~200 ms, no pulse on null→null).

# Smoke

- Sirius is the canonical test — focus from Sol park pose (close), then from Vega park pose (far).
- Focus Sirius via search while already parked at Sirius: ring should pulse, camera should snap silently.
- Focus Sirius from across the sky: ring pulses, camera glides.
- URL-restore landing on a focus that matches the current pose: pulse fires, no console warning.

# Files likely touched

- `src/client/camera/focus-transition.ts` — early-exit threshold in `newFocusLerpFrom` or at the call site in `Stellata.focusStar`.
- `src/client/overlays/focus-ring-overlay.ts` — pulse animation state + tick.

# Connects

- [[alex-stellata-smoke-tests]] — manual smoke after.

title: Hybrid linear-d + angular-size arrival curve
description:
## Why

Smoke testing of stellata-2br.8's trapezoidal curve revealed that no
single-regime profile on log-d space can produce the perceptual rhythm
Alex wants. Camera linear velocity is `d · f' · |log_ratio| / T_warp`;
because `d` shrinks geometrically, linear velocity peaks early (~1.3 s
into an 8 s Fly) and decreases monotonically regardless of curve
shape. Background-star parallax flow tracks linear velocity, so the
camera always reads as decelerating after the early peak. No knob in
arrival-curves.ts can move that peak past the first quarter of the
warp.

## Goal

Two-regime hybrid:
- **Far approach** (parallax-driven): linear-d piecewise-quadratic
  (`f = 2τ² | 1 − 2(1−τ)²` on `d_target = d_0 − f·(d_0 − d_seam)`).
  Reproduces pre-2br.3 main-branch rocket-impulse feel exactly, on
  the truncated `[d_0, d_seam]` range.
- **Close approach** (angular-driven): quintic smootherstep on
  angular size `θ(σ) = θ_seam + S(σ)·(θ_end − θ_seam)` with
  `S(σ) = 10σ³ − 15σ⁴ + 6σ⁵`. Zero velocity AND zero acceleration
  at u = 1 → clean perceptual landing.

Seam at `d_seam = seam_k · parkDist`, default `seam_k = 500`.
Time split is auto-computed:
`u_seam = clamp(log(d_0/d_seam) / log(d_0/d_end), 0.3, 0.85)`.

## Encoding trick

Hybrid is exposed as a fifth arm of `resolveArrivalCurve` that
returns a closure `(u) → number`. The closure encodes the hybrid
trajectory in real distance space but returns the log-d-equivalent
`f(u) = log(d_target(u)/d_0) / log(d_end/d_0)`. Consumer
(`camera-motion.ts:tickArrival`) is unchanged — it still applies
`d(u) = d_0 · (d_end/d_0)^f(u)`.

## Files

- `src/client/arrival-curves.ts` — add `easeHybrid` + `'hybrid'`
  arm of `resolveArrivalCurve`; refactor signature to take a params
  object + optional `ArrivalCurveContext`.
- `src/client/focus-target.ts` — new `physicalRadius(): number | null`
  method on the interface; star impl reads
  `catalog.physicalRadius[idx] * R_SUN_PC`, cloud impl returns null.
- `src/client/warp-tuning.ts` — `'hybrid'` in dropdown; `seam k`
  slider (default 500, range 100–2000); thread context into
  `warpArrivalEaseFn(ctx?)`.
- `src/client/stellata.ts` (4 call sites) and
  `src/client/focus-transition.ts` — compute and pass `ctx` at
  resolution time.
- `src/client/arrival-curves.test.ts` — endpoints, seam value, seam
  velocity, regime parity, quintic landing, degenerate-d_0, null-R
  fallback.
- `docs/camera-arrival.md` — § "Hybrid linear-d + angular-size
  profile" addendum.
- `docs/camera-warp.md` § Fly — extend cross-ref.
- `package.json` — 1.11.0 → 1.12.0.

## Decisions made (record so future-Alex isn't confused by missing knobs)

1. **Default `seam_k = 500`** (geometric midpoint of the empirical
   100–1000 × parkDist range cited in docs/camera-arrival.md).
   Rejected: per-star angular threshold (R varies 4+ orders of
   magnitude → fixed θ_seam is non-uniform across stars).
2. **`u_seam` is auto-computed, NOT a slider.** Log-proportional with
   `[0.3, 0.85]` clamp. Rejected: slider override (would let u_seam
   drift from seam_k, confusing the model) and fixed 0.5 (doesn't
   adapt to warp distance).
3. **Inner uses quintic smootherstep** (`10σ³ − 15σ⁴ + 6σ⁵`), not
   cubic. Quintic has `S''(1) = 0` — matches Alex's "derivative of
   velocity = 0 at the end" intent. Cubic has nonzero acceleration
   at landing → faint "snap to stop."
4. **Hybrid is opt-in in 1.12.0, not the default.** Trapezoidal
   (1.11.0) stays in the panel as a backup. Cubic-Hermite remains
   default until manual smoke confirms hybrid feels right end-to-end.
   Promotion is a one-line change later.
5. **Cloud / null-R fallback is silent, to cubic-Hermite.** Avoids
   force-switching per call site; one console.debug per warp so
   panel users see why hybrid isn't active.
6. **Outer→inner handoff at v=0**, not velocity-continuous at v>0.
   Both regimes naturally arrive at the seam with zero velocity
   (piecewise-quad ends at v=0; quintic starts with `S'(0)=0`).
   Sidesteps the velocity-continuity constraint that killed the
   previously-rejected dWindow split.
7. **LINEAR smoothstep on θ, not on log θ.** Smoothstep on log θ is
   identical to smoothstep on log d (the current cubic-Hermite
   default) which is exactly what Alex's complaint is about. Linear
   in θ means the on-screen angular growth follows S(σ) directly.

## Smoke (manual, Alex's browser, no dev server)

- `/?debug=1`, expand warp section. Curve → `hybrid`. Verify
  `seam k` slider appears.
- Scenarios 5 (Betelgeuse → Sol) and 9 (Xi1 Sgr → Sol) from PR #66:
  outer phase should feel like pre-2br.3 main-branch (parallax
  sweep); inner phase: Sol's disc grows smoothly to fill screen
  with no perceptual standstill.
- Seam handoff should be imperceptible — momentary v=0 coast
  perceptually masked by parallax-collapse / angular-onset overlap.
- `seam_k = 100` vs `1000` slider sweep to find the right
  transition point.
- Short-warp edge: Alpha Centauri → Sol (~1.3 pc) — u_seam clamp
  should give inner ≥ 30 % of time.
- Cloud destination: silent fallback to cubic-Hermite.

## PR

Stack on PR #66 (still open) or open new PR if merged.

title: Extract star-physics helper from stellata.ts (parkDistForStar, renderedSizePx, fovMinorRad, etc.)
description:
Lift the cluster of stellata.ts methods that compute per-star camera/screen geometry into a new pure module `camera/star-physics.ts` (post-194.1 folder reorg). Consumers: Picker, FocusController, chart-mode pipeline, overlays.

# Code to move

- renderedSizePx (stellata.ts:3066)
- renderedDiscPxAtPeak (stellata.ts:3259)
- fovMinorRad (stellata.ts:3182)
- peakAmplitudeFactor (stellata.ts:3196)
- getChartDiscParams (stellata.ts:2229)
- getFocusedStarPeakDiscRadiusPx (stellata.ts:3175)
- parkDistForStar (stellata.ts:3241)
- minOrbitDistForStar (stellata.ts:3213)
- binaryCompanionFloorPc (stellata.ts:3223)

These currently read from `this.material.uniforms`, `this.camera`, `this.catalog`, `this._localPositions`, `this.filter`. After extraction they take those as plain arguments — pure functions parameterised on `(catalog, idx, camPos, uniforms, filter)`.

# Public surface

```ts
// Per-star pixel/distance geometry. All inputs explicit; no `this` capture.
renderedSizePx(args: { catalog, idx, camPos, localPositions, uniforms, filter, now }): number
renderedDiscPxAtPeak(args: { catalog, idx, camPos, localPositions, uniforms }): number
fovMinorRad(camera: PerspectiveCamera): number
peakAmplitudeFactor(catalog, idx): number
parkDistForStar(args: { catalog, idx, fovMinorRad }): number
minOrbitDistForStar(args: { catalog, idx, fovMinorRad }): number
binaryCompanionFloorPc(catalog, idx): number
getChartDiscParams(uniforms): { maxPx, minPx, magBright }
```

# Existing extracts to compose

- `star-geometry.ts` already exports `physSizePx`, `varEffectiveAmplitude`, `distAtFillFraction`, `peakAmplitudeFactor` (pure). star-physics.ts is the thin per-frame wrapper around those + shader uniforms + the per-star catalog read.

# Independence

- Blocked by 194.1 (folder reorg lands first so `camera/star-physics.ts` lives in its final home).
- Blocks 194.8 (FocusController consumes parkDistForStar / minOrbitDistForStar / peakAmplitudeFactor; cleaner if it imports from star-physics.ts rather than carrying them inline).
- Parallel-trackable with 194.5, .6, .7 — touches helper code, not animation state.

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts no longer holds renderedSizePx / parkDistForStar / etc. as methods.
- camera/star-physics.test.ts pins numeric outputs:
  - `expect(parkDistForStar({catalog, idx: SIRIUS_IDX, fovMinorRad: ...})).toBe(N)`
  - `expect(renderedSizePx({...})).toBe(M)` for a representative star + camera configuration
  - peakAmplitudeFactor edge cases (period=0, period>0+amplitude=0, large amplitude)
- All callers (Picker, FocusController, chart-mode, overlays) import from camera/star-physics.ts.
- typecheck + vitest pass.

# Smoke

- Visual: focus a few stars (Sirius, Betelgeuse, Algol) and confirm orbit floor + park distance look identical to pre-extract.
- Focus a binary (alpha Cen) and confirm BINARY_VIEWPORT_HALF_ANGLE_RAD floor still applies.
- Variable star (Algol) at peak + trough — disc envelope matches.

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule (4) — pure helpers extract at second use, never duplicated.
- [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]] — DCAM_LOG_FLOOR_PC + R_SUN_PC must come from canonical modules, not be redefined here.
- [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] — numeric outputs pinned with toBe(N), never toBeLessThanOrEqual.

title: Extract FocusController from stellata.ts (focus FSM + pin engage + lerp)
description:
Move focus FSM ownership + pin-engage state + lerp orchestration from stellata.ts into `camera/focus-controller.ts`. Absorbs the FocusOps shim that WarpController has been carrying since .5; WarpController's + ObserveTransition's import seams update in one line each.

# Code to move

- `focusedStar`, `focusedCloud`, `focusedPlanetSystem`, `planetSystemToken`, `focusLerpState` state fields.
- `setFocus`, `setFocusedCloud`, `focusStar`, `flyToCloud`, `setOrbitTarget`, `setOrbitTargetCloud`, `unfocus`.
- `startFocusLerp` / `endFocusLerp` / `cancelFocusLerp` / `cancelUnfocusLerp`.
- `updateFocusLerp` → `tick`.
- `refreshPlanetSystem`.
- `recenterFocusToStar` (internal seam used by WarpController).
- `isPinEngaged`, `getPinEngageThresholdSq`.
- `makeStarFocusTarget`, `makeCloudFocusTarget`, `currentFocusTarget` (from #66 — these ARE the FocusOps surface).
- `parkDistForStar` / `minOrbitDistForStar` / `binaryCompanionFloorPc` — IF 194.9 hasn't landed yet. If 194.9 has landed (preferred), import from `camera/star-physics.ts`.
- Constants: `GLOBAL_MIN_DIST_PC`, `ZOOM_FLOOR_FRACTION`, `VAR_TROUGH_FLOOR_FRACTION`, `PIN_ENGAGE_THRESHOLD_SQ_PC`, `BINARY_VIEWPORT_HALF_ANGLE_RAD`, `BINARY_MIN_DIST_FACTOR`. (`FOCUS_LERP_MS` and `DCAM_LOG_FLOOR_PC` are in `camera/timing.ts` per 194.1.)

# FocusOps interface (the cross-controller seam)

FocusController's public surface IS the `FocusOps` interface other controllers (WarpController, ObserveTransition) consume. Export the interface from `camera/focus-controller.ts`. The runtime swap in `Stellata` from `focus: this` to `focus: this.focus` is the integration-shell change in this PR.

# Public surface (finalised in 194.2; this IS the FocusOps interface)

```ts
import type { FocusTarget } from './focus-target';

interface FocusOps {
  // queries
  getFocusedStar(): number | null
  getFocusedCloud(): number | null
  getFocusedPlanetSystem(): PlanetSystem | null
  isPinEngaged(): boolean
  isFocusLerpActive(): boolean
  currentFocusTarget(): FocusTarget | null
  makeStarFocusTarget(idx: number): FocusTarget
  makeCloudFocusTarget(idx: number): FocusTarget | null
  parkDistForStar(idx: number): number
  minOrbitDistForStar(idx: number): number

  // commands
  focusStar(idx: number, opts?: { animate?: boolean }): void
  setFocus(idx: number | null): void
  setFocusedCloud(idx: number | null): void
  setOrbitTarget(idx: number): void
  setOrbitTargetCloud(idx: number): void
  flyToCloud(idx: number, opts?: { animate?: boolean }): void
  unfocus(opts?: { animate?: boolean }): void
  cancelFocusLerp(): void
  cancelUnfocusLerp(): void

  // internal seams (called by warp / observe — exported via @internal)
  recenterFocusToStar(idx: number): Vector3 | null
  startFocusLerp(state: FocusLerpState): void
}

class FocusController implements FocusOps {
  constructor(deps: {
    camera: PerspectiveCamera
    controls: TrackballControls
    catalog: Catalog
    bus: EventBus
    frameAnchor: FrameAnchor               // recenterOrigin + getWorldOffset
    observe: ObserveTransition             // for startUnfocusLerp dispatch
    uHideFocusIdxRef: { value: number }
    getCameraMode: () => CameraMode
    getClouds: () => MolecularClouds | null
  })

  // ...all FocusOps members + tick(nowMs), dispose()
}
```

# Bus events

`'focus': number | null`, `'cloudFocus': number | null`, `'planetSystem': PlanetSystem | null`, `'focusLerp': boolean`.

# FrameAnchor interface (stays on Stellata)

```ts
interface FrameAnchor {
  recenterOrigin(newOrigin: Vector3): Vector3 | null
  getWorldOffset(): Readonly<Vector3>
}
```

`Stellata implements FrameAnchor` — `worldOffset`, `_localPositions`, the `iPositionAttr.needsUpdate` write, and the per-frame pin uniform write all stay on the composition shell. Cleaner extraction is coupled to the StarPipeline extract 9mm.43 and deferred until then.

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts under 1500 lines — primary epic-level acceptance gate. (Bigger lift now after #66 + #72; .9 helps reduce by lifting star-physics.)
- `camera/focus-controller.test.ts` covers near-focus (no warp) + far-focus (triggers warp) + cancel mid-lerp + pin engage threshold + observe-cleanup branch + unfocus close-zoom + worldOffset preservation on unfocus + FocusTarget round-trip (makeStarFocusTarget → applyFocus → emitFocusEvents).
- WarpController's `focus: FocusOps` swap (from `stellata` to FocusController) is in this PR.
- ObserveTransition's `focus: FocusOps` swap is the same.
- typecheck + vitest pass.

# Smoke

- Focus a near star (Sirius from a wide view): short lerp, no warp.
- Focus a distant star (Deneb): triggers warp.
- Focus a cloud (Taurus complex).
- Focus a Local Group object (LMC) — distance + lerp interplay.
- Unfocus by close-zoom past minDistance.
- Unfocus by clicking blank sky.
- Unfocus from observe via search X (clearFocusOnExit path).
- Pin engage threshold: zoom in until pin engages, confirm transition is clean.
- Sol-as-default-focus (catalog.solIndex) auto-engage on first paint.

# Dependencies

Blocked by 194.6 (ObserveTransition extracted first — FocusController.unfocus() dispatches into observe.startUnfocusLerp).
Blocked by 194.9 (star-physics helper) — FocusController imports parkDistForStar / minOrbitDistForStar / peakAmplitudeFactor from `camera/star-physics.ts`.

# Doc updates landing in this PR (per defer-doc-updates)

- `docs/architecture.md` § "FocusTarget contract" — point at FocusController as the canonical producer (makeStarFocusTarget / makeCloudFocusTarget / currentFocusTarget).
- `docs/architecture.md` § "Floating origin" — name the FrameAnchor interface seam.
- `docs/architecture.md` § "Pin-to-center" — point at FocusController.isPinEngaged.

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule (3).
- [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] single-source-of-truth — recenterOrigin caller still uses Stellata's FrameAnchor; no duplicate worldOffset state.
- docs/architecture.md — pin-engage invariant, floating-origin contract, FocusTarget contract. The contract FocusController inherits.

title: Chart-mode tooltip styling
description:
