Restyle `#tooltip` for the paper aesthetic when chart mode is engaged.

# Scope

- Verify whether chart-mode already toggles a `body.chart-mode` class. If yes, reuse; if no, add it in `chart-mode.ts` engage/disengage paths.
- `src/client/styles.css` — add `body.chart-mode #tooltip { … }` selector chain mirroring the chart-mode paper aesthetic (font, border, background — match the chart-labels.ts glyph styling already in place).
- No engine change; payload identical.

# Acceptance

- Toggle chart mode; hover each class (star / planet / LG / heliopause apex) → tooltip restyles to paper aesthetic; payload unchanged from non-chart hover.

# Smoke

- Toggle chart mode (C key), hover representative object from each class.

Ships as part of lo5 single-PR rollup; close on PR merge. Depends on lo5.2.
close_reason: merged via PR #72 (commit 13: chart-mode tooltip satisfied by existing body.monochrome .tooltip rule; convention captured as Rule 4 in stellata-lo5-hover-conventions)

title: Extract WarpController from stellata.ts (3-phase warp FSM, consolidate warp-pure)
description:
Move WarpState + the 3-phase warp state machine into `camera/warp-controller.ts` (folder home from 194.1). `camera/warp-pure.ts` stays as the sibling pure module. WarpController also imports `camera/arrival-curves.ts` + `camera/camera-motion.ts` for the hybrid arrival profile that landed in PR #66.

# Code to move

- `WarpState` — refactored on main to carry `dest: FocusTarget` instead of `{destKind, destIdx}`, plus the new `recenteredToDest: boolean` field.
- `startWarp`, `finishWarp`, `updateWarp`, `tryMidFlyRecentre` (new from #66 — mid-Fly origin recentre)
- `warpTo`, `warpToCloud`, `skipWarp`, `getWarpPhase` (new from #66), `getWarpInfo`
- `swapObserveAnchor` — only consumer is `finishWarp`.
- `warpQ0`, `warpQ1`, `warpTmp`, `_tmpAnimateLocal` scratch state.

(`destLocalPositionInto` collapses — WarpState.dest is a FocusTarget so `dest.localPositionInto(out)` replaces it.)

# Constants

All from `camera/timing.ts` (renamed from warp-constants.ts in 194.1): `WARP_T_MIN_MS`, `WARP_T_MAX_MS`, `WARP_T_K_MS`, `WARP_REORIENT_MS`, `OBSERVE_TRANSITION_MS`, `WARP_BASE_DIR`.

# FocusOps shim (the cross-controller coupling)

WarpController's `startWarp` and `finishWarp` call into focus state that hasn't been extracted yet (FocusController is 194.8). For this PR, WarpController takes a `stellata: Stellata` reference (typed as `FocusOps` so the seam is interface-shaped from day 1) and calls:

- `stellata.currentFocusTarget()` (returns FocusTarget | null; from #66's refactor)
- `stellata.makeStarFocusTarget(idx)`, `stellata.makeCloudFocusTarget(idx)` (also from #66)
- `stellata.recenterFocusToStar(idx)` for the mid-Fly + phase-3 recentre
- `stellata.setVectorTo(null)` / `stellata.setVectorToCloud(null)`
- `stellata.parkDistForStar(idx)` (until 194.9 lifts to camera/star-physics.ts; then imported directly)

In 194.8, `FocusOps` becomes FocusController's interface; WarpController's import seam updates in one line.

# Public surface (finalised in 194.2)

```ts
class WarpController {
  constructor(deps: {
    camera: PerspectiveCamera
    controls: TrackballControls
    observeControls: ObserveControls
    uHideFocusIdxRef: { value: number }    // material.uniforms.uHideFocusIdx
    bus: EventBus
    getCameraMode: () => CameraMode
    getClouds: () => MolecularClouds | null
    focus: FocusOps                         // = stellata (shim) in .5, = FocusController in .8
  })

  warpTo(destIdx: number): void
  warpToCloud(destIdx: number): void
  skip(): void
  tick(nowMs: number): void
  isActive(): boolean
  getWarpInfo(): WarpInfo | null
  getWarpPhase(): WarpPhase                  // (new public surface from #66)
  dispose(): void
}
```

`WarpInfo` + `WarpPhase` types stay as they exist on main; exported alongside the controller for `docs/camera-warp.md` references.

# Bus events

`'warp': boolean` — emitted at startWarp + finishWarp.

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts no longer holds warp state fields, the tick methods, swapObserveAnchor, or tryMidFlyRecentre.
- `camera/warp-controller.test.ts` covers:
  - 3-phase FSM transitions (reorient → Fly → phase 3 / post-arrival).
  - Hybrid arrival profile in Fly phase (cubic-Hermite log-d default + the 'hybrid' linear-seam-then-angular curve when FocusTarget.physicalRadius() returns a value).
  - Source/dest variants: star→star, star→cloud, cloud→star, observe→observe.
  - skipWarp during each phase.
  - Mid-Fly recentre — tryMidFlyRecentre is exercised when the camera crosses into the destination's local frame.
  - Coincident-source bail (distPc < 1e-6).
  - getWarpPhase outputs across phases.
- WARP_T_* constants imported from camera/timing.ts; never redefined.
- typecheck + vitest pass.

# Smoke

- Warp star→star: Sirius → Deneb (long; tests log-linear duration cap + hybrid profile if applicable).
- Warp star→cloud: Sirius → LMC cloud (cloud arrival distance applies).
- Warp cloud→star: any cloud → Sol.
- Warp observe→observe: focused-and-observing → another star.
- Skip during each phase → arrival behaviour matches non-skipped warp.
- Confirm Sol / GC arrow fade behaviour during warp unchanged (per stellata-ml8 retrospective).
- Focus engage after warp arrival; observe→observe stays in observe mode throughout.

# Dependencies

Blocked by 194.4 (AimController extracted first — startWarp cancels any in-flight aim).

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule (3).
- [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] sentinel-init — WarpState's optional fields (flyEndQuaternion, reorientEndQuaternion, recenteredToDest) are sentinel-init poison-checked.
- docs/camera-warp.md, docs/camera-arrival.md — the contracts WarpController inherits.

title: Extract ObserveTransition from stellata.ts (navigate↔observe orchestrator)
description:
Move ObserveTransitionState + the navigate↔observe mode-switch orchestrator from stellata.ts into `camera/observe-transition.ts` (folder home from 194.1). The state slot is OWNED here even though `Stellata.unfocus()` writes into it — ObserveTransition exposes `startUnfocusLerp(...)` from day 1, and the focus path calls in.

# Code to move

- `ObserveTransitionState` (stellata.ts:298)
- `setCameraMode` (stellata.ts:1335) → `setMode` on the controller
- `startObserveExit` (stellata.ts:1383) → `startExit` on the controller
- `updateObserveTransition` (stellata.ts:3817) → `tick` on the controller
- `finishObserveTransition` (stellata.ts:3826)
- `getObserveTransitionProgress` (stellata.ts:1239) → `getProgress`
- `isObserveTransitionActive` (stellata.ts:1147) → `isActive` (excludes 'unfocus' kind, matches existing contract)
- New: `isAnyActive()` includes 'unfocus' kind — used by Stellata.isCameraBusy().
- `alignCameraUpToQuaternion` (stellata.ts:3889) lifts to `camera/up-align-pure.ts` so the controller imports it without inheriting from Stellata.
- Constant `OBSERVE_TRANSITION_MS` lives in `camera/constants.ts` per 194.1.

# Code that STAYS in stellata.ts

- `unfocus()` (stellata.ts:1889) — orchestration spanning vector clearing + observe-exit + navigate-close-zoom + focus clear. Calls `observe.startExit(...)` or `observe.startUnfocusLerp(...)` depending on path.
- `observeUpdateTarget` (stellata.ts:3894) — animate-loop per-frame target re-pin for observe mode; lives with the animate dispatcher.

# Public surface (finalised in 194.2)

```ts
class ObserveTransition {
  constructor(deps: {
    camera: PerspectiveCamera
    controls: TrackballControls
    observeControls: ObserveControls
    uHideFocusIdxRef: { value: number }
    bus: EventBus
    focus: FocusOps                        // for startExit's clearFocusOnExit + getFocusedStar
  })

  setMode(mode: CameraMode, opts?: { animate?: boolean }): void
  startExit(opts: { animate: boolean; clearFocusOnExit: boolean }): void
  startUnfocusLerp(fromPos: Vector3, toPos: Vector3, finalMinDist: number): void
  tick(nowMs: number): void
  isActive(): boolean                     // excludes 'unfocus' per current contract
  isAnyActive(): boolean                  // includes 'unfocus'; drives isCameraBusy
  getProgress(): { f: number; kind: 'enter' | 'exit' } | null
  dispose(): void
}
```

# Bus events

`'cameraMode': CameraMode` — emitted at setMode + startExit (every exit path goes through startExit, including the navigate-mode toggle and search-X clear). `'state'` is emitted in lockstep with cameraMode.

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts no longer holds ObserveTransitionState or the setCameraMode / startObserveExit / updateObserveTransition / finishObserveTransition methods.
- `camera/observe-transition.test.ts` covers all 4 mode-switch entry points + the swap-anchor edge case + the unfocus-lerp variant with its `finalMinDistance` write-back + dispose clears state without flicker.
- `camera/up-align-pure.test.ts` exists for the lifted helper (paired with the existing camera-up-align.test.ts fixture).
- 9mm.159 (swapObserveAnchor naming) — close as superseded by 194.5's swapObserveAnchor → WarpController-private move, OR fold the rename into .5. Decide before opening .5 PR.
- typecheck + vitest pass.

# Smoke

Mode-switch entry points to verify:
- Mode pill click (top-right card).
- Keyboard O.
- Click while focused in NAVIGATE → enters OBSERVE.
- Double-click while focused in OBSERVE → exits to NAVIGATE.
- Chart mode engage / disengage in OBSERVE.
- Search-row X button (clearFocusOnExit=true path).
- Navigate-mode close-zoom unfocus (the 'unfocus' lerp kind).

# Dependencies

Blocked by 194.5 (WarpController extracted first — observe transitions cancel any in-flight warp's observeControls state).

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule (3).
- docs/camera-observe.md — the contract this controller inherits.
- docs/integration-shell-decomposition.md § "ObserveTransition".

title: Extract AimController from stellata.ts (aim slerps + observe-aim)
description:
Move aim state machines from stellata.ts into `camera/aim-controller.ts` (folder home from 194.1). Stellata composes AimController.

# Code to move

- `AimState` (stellata.ts:327)
- `ObserveAimState` (stellata.ts:339)
- `aimAt` (stellata.ts:2760) — the mode-aware entry point; both navigate (orbit-pivot slerp) and observe (in-place quaternion slerp) branches move with it.
- `updateAim` (stellata.ts:2846)
- `updateObserveAim` (stellata.ts:2883)
- `aimQ`, `aimTmpDir`, `observeAimQ` scratch state.
- `AIM_T_MIN_MS` constant. `AIM_T_MAX_MS` lifts to `camera/constants.ts` in 194.1; AimController imports it.
- `WARP_BASE_DIR` is shared with warp; it lives in `camera/constants.ts` too.

# Code that STAYS in stellata.ts

- `aimAtConstellation` — the constellation-centroid computation. Stays as a thin dispatcher into `this.aim.aimAt(centroid)`.

# Public surface (finalised in 194.2)

```ts
class AimController {
  constructor(deps: {
    camera: PerspectiveCamera
    controls: TrackballControls
    observeControls: ObserveControls
    bus: EventBus
    getCameraMode: () => CameraMode
  })

  aimAt(pointLocal: Vector3): void
  cancel(): void                         // called by warp.start, focus changes
  isActive(): boolean                    // navigate-mode aim
  isObserveAimActive(): boolean
  tick(nowMs: number): void              // navigate slot
  tickObserve(nowMs: number): void       // observe slot
  dispose(): void
}
```

# Bus events

No new bus event in this bead. An `aim: boolean` event is a follow-up bead, filed only if/when an overlay or HUD needs it. Keeping .4 mechanically pure (move-only) reduces review risk.

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts no longer holds AimState / ObserveAimState fields or the aim tick methods.
- `camera/aim-controller.test.ts` covers slerp lifecycle, cancellation mid-slerp, navigate vs observe branches, dispose tear-down.
- The `AIM_T_MIN_MS` (250 ms) floor case + the `2·acos(|q0·q1|)` formula are pinned with toBe(N) per stellata-test-coverage-discipline.
- typecheck + vitest pass.

# Smoke

- Keyboard R / G / C (aim slerps to a target in navigate).
- HUD Sol/GC arrow labels (observe + navigate).
- aimAtConstellation in navigate (constellation picker change handler).
- observe-mode double-click → aimAt(unprojected ray).
- Camera busy mid-aim: try to launch another aim → suppressed.

# Dependencies

Blocked by 194.3 (Picker extracted first — bottom-up rule).

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule (3) — controllers extracted at write time.
- [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] lifecycle pairing — every `bus.on(...)` and `observeControls.disable()` pair with their teardown in the same diff.

title: Heliopause apex hover provider + pickbox
description:
Add hover pick + label for the heliopause apex marker.

# Scope

title: Local Group hover provider + pickbox
description:
Add hover pick + label for Local Group wireframes.

# Scope

- `src/client/local-group.ts` — new `pick(clientX, clientY, pxThreshold) → HoverHit | null`:
  - Project each object's `centerAbs - worldOffset` to screen space.
  - Estimate screen-extent from semi-axes + orientation quat (max projected radius).
  - Two-tier: prime if cursor inside the projected envelope; fallback if within `pxThreshold` of projected centroid.
- `src/client/hover/formatters/local-group-hover-format.ts` — fields: name, kind (dSph / dE / dIrr / disc — `kind` field already in JSON, plus override-display-name rules from local-group-loader), distance from Sol via `fmtDist` (auto Mpc/kpc/pc), size summary (semi-axes major × minor in pc / kpc / Mpc).
- `src/client/hover/formatters/local-group-hover-format.test.ts` — golden output for M31 (disc kind), LMC (override), Sgr dSph (ellipsoid kind), a faint LVDB dwarf.

# Acceptance

- Hovering each LG object surfaces correct name/kind/distance/size.
- LMC display name resolves via override (not LVDB's raw entry).

# Out of scope

- Apparent V mag — blocked by stellata-7de (LG JSON schema has no `M_V` plumbed yet). Add the line in a follow-up bead once 7de lands.

# Smoke

- Camera at Local Group scale; hover M31, LMC, Sgr dSph, a faint dwarf.

Ships as part of lo5 single-PR rollup; close on PR merge. Depends on lo5.2.
close_reason: merged via PR #72 (commit 9: Local Group provider + pickbox + formatter)

title: Sol planet hover provider + per-instance pickbox
description:
Add per-instance hover pick + label for Sol's planets.

# Scope

- `src/client/planet-body-field.ts` — `pick(camera, rect, x, y, t)` walking every attached host's planets (no focus gate per Rule 2 of [[stellata-lo5-hover-conventions]]):
  - Project each per-instance `iLocalRel + iHostLocalPos` to screen space.
  - Compute screen-pixel distance to centroid and the rendered disc radius from `iRadiusPc` (mirrors star approach).
  - Two-tier: prime if inside disc (cursor < rendered radius); fallback if within `pxThreshold`.
  - Skip planets whose appMag exceeds the slider cutoff + 0.5 mag (same kill condition the planet vertex shader uses to drop the quad).
- Expose a per-instance apparent V mag getter that mirrors the shader-side calc (already mirrored by `planetApparentMagnitude` in `perceptual-magnitude.ts`; reuse rather than duplicating).
- `src/client/hover/formatters/planet-hover-format.ts` — `formatPlanetHover(planetIdx, ctx)`. Final layout per [[stellata-lo5-hover-conventions]] Rule 1a:

      <Planet name>
      <host distance> · Vmag <m>
      Period <years> yr
      Radius <km> km

- `src/client/hover/formatters/planet-hover-format.test.ts` — golden output for Mercury / Earth / Jupiter / Pluto across `fmtDistAuto` tier transitions + +sign positive-mag case + null-input degenerate guard.

# Visibility gate (Rule 2 of [[stellata-lo5-hover-conventions]])

No focus-state gate. The provider walks every attached host; visibility is the only predicate (matches the renderer's draw decision). v1 attaches Sol once at startup and Sol stays attached for the session, so the picker effectively walks Sol's planets — regardless of which star (if any) is focused.

# Acceptance

- Hovering each visible Sol planet surfaces correct name/distance/Vmag/period/radius.
- Vmag matches the shader-side disc calculation exactly.
- Provider does NOT gate on Sol being the focused host. Hovering a Sol planet while focused on (say) Vega still surfaces the planet card if the planet is visible.

# Smoke

- Default first-load view (Sol focus); hover each of the 9 planets through Pluto.
- Focus a distant star; verify Sol's planets still hover-pickable when they're on-screen and visible.

Ships as part of lo5 single-PR rollup; close on PR merge. Depends on lo5.2.
close_reason: merged via PR #72 (commits 4-5: hoist MIN_DISC_HIT_RADIUS_PX + planet provider/pickbox)

title: Star hover provider (extract existing path)

Extract the existing star hover affordance into a `StarHoverProvider` implementing `HoverProvider`.

title: Hover engine + disambiguator hoist
description:
Implement the shared hover-label engine and hoist the picker disambiguation logic.

# Scope

- New directory `src/client/hover/`:
  - `hover-types.ts` — HoverProvider, HoverHit, HoverPayload types.
  - `hover-engine.ts` — pointermove listener + 280 ms timer + provider registry + dispatch + #tooltip render. Replaces bindHoverTooltip body in main.ts.
  - `hover-pick-disambiguator.ts` — cross-provider rule: prime always beats fallback; within tier, smallest cameraDistancePc wins. Sits on top of the generalised pickFromCandidates in star-geometry.ts.
  - `hover-pick-disambiguator.test.ts` — prime > fallback; camera-distance tie-break; empty/single/multi-hit.
- Generalise `pickFromCandidates` in `star-geometry.ts:127–150` with a `scoreFn` parameter so non-star providers can pass their own scorer. Existing star caller passes `pickScore`. `pickScore` stays in star-geometry.ts (star-specific sub-pixel mag bias).
- Replace the hover-side inline pick at `main.ts:285-286` with engine dispatch through the disambiguator. Only the star provider is registered at this bead's landing (others arrive in lo5.3–lo5.6).
- Click-side inline pickStar→pickCloud at `stellata.ts:3309–3310` stays as-is — it has a richer downstream click-state machine that doesn't share scope with hover. `observeSingleClick` (`stellata.ts:3404`) is already star-only; nothing to change there.
- Preserve `HOVER_DELAY_MS = 280` and 14 px threshold verbatim.

# Acceptance

- All existing star hover behaviour identical (visually + manual smoke).
- `hover-pick-disambiguator` unit-tested.
- `pickFromCandidates` now reusable by future providers via scoreFn parameter.

# Smoke

- Hover Vega + Mira + an unnamed catalog star: tooltip shows same fields as before.

Ships as part of the lo5 single-PR rollup; close on PR merge.
close_reason: merged via PR #72 (commits 1-2: star-geometry refactor + hover engine/disambiguator)

title: Folder reorganisation — flat src/client/* → thematic subfolders
description:
Mechanical move of src/client/*.ts files into thematic subfolders per the 194.2 design-gate taxonomy. SINGLE PR — partial folder moves create transitional confusion. Also renames `warp-constants.ts` → `camera/timing.ts` (the file already holds camera-wide lerp constants beyond just warp) and lifts `WARP_BASE_DIR` + `DCAM_LOG_FLOOR_PC` from stellata.ts into it.

# Taxonomy (finalised in 194.2; reconciled with origin/main as of PR #72)

Two folder categories. Per-subsystem folders own the renderer + loader + tuning + pure helpers + tests for that subsystem. Cross-cutting type folders own plumbing that isn't owned by any single subsystem. hover/ already exists from #72 and stays as-is (validates rule 1; its formatters/ sub-subfolder is a precedent).

- **Per-subsystem folders:**
  - `solar-system/` — planet-system, orbit-rings-layer, planet-body-field, perceptual-magnitude, perceptual-magnitude.test, planet-labels, time, time.test, time-readout, time-readout.test, ephemeris, ephemeris.test, astronomy-constants, astronomy-constants.test, heliopause, heliopause.test, first-load, first-load.test, phase-function, phase-function.test
  - `local-group/` — local-group, local-group.test, local-group-loader, local-group-loader.test, local-group-tuning
  - `milkyway/` — milkyway, milkyway-tuning
  - `galactic/` — galactic-disc, galactic-fade, galactic-fade.test, galactic-grid, galactic-coords
  - `molecular-clouds/` — molecular-clouds, molecular-clouds.test, cloud-loader (moved from loaders/)
  - `chart-mode/` — chart-mode, chart-labels, chart-labels.test, **chart-disc-pure, chart-disc-pure.test** (new from #66)
  - `hover/` — UNCHANGED (already in place from #72): hover-engine, hover-types, hover-pick-disambiguator + test, star/cloud/planet/local-group/heliopause-hover-provider, formatters/* (5 files + 5 tests)

- **Cross-cutting type folders:**
  - `overlays/` — constellation-overlay, disc-mask, disc-mask-pure, disc-mask-pure.test, distance-vector-overlay, distance-vector-overlay.test, focus-ring-overlay, hud-overlay, hud-overlay.test, poi-overlay, dirty-attr, dirty-attr.test, overlay-project, arrow-fade, arrow-fade.test, arrow-path, arrow-path.test
  - `camera/` — controls, controls.test, observe-controls, observe-controls.test, focus-transition, focus-transition.test, **focus-target** (new from #66), **arrival-curves, arrival-curves.test** (new from #66), **camera-motion, camera-motion.test** (new from #66), warp-pure, warp-pure.test, warp-button, mode-toggle, camera-up-align.test, star-geometry, star-geometry.test, **warp-tuning** (new from #66), **NEW: camera/timing.ts (renamed from warp-constants.ts; adds WARP_BASE_DIR + DCAM_LOG_FLOOR_PC lifted from stellata.ts)**
  - `loaders/` — catalog-loader, catalog-loader.test, dust-loader (cloud-loader moved to molecular-clouds/, local-group-loader stays in local-group/)
  - `ui/` — panel-layout, scale-bar, theme-toggle, unit-toggle, distance-util, distance-util.test, distance-gated-label, distance-gated-label.test, keyboard-shortcuts, keyboard-shortcuts-pure, keyboard-shortcuts-pure.test, dom-util, dom-util.test
  - `util/` — event-bus, event-bus.test, url-state, url-state.test
  - `typeahead/` — typeahead, typeahead-util, typeahead-util.test, constellation-typeahead, constellation-typeahead.test, search, search.test
  - `modals/` — info-modal, brand-modal, help-modal, modal-dismiss
  - `debug/` — debug, debug-panel, perf-hud, perf-hud.test, pin-debug-hud, arrow-fade-debug-hud, star-tuning

- **Root kept flat:** main.ts, stellata.ts, index.html, styles.css, globals.d.ts, shaders/, stellata-events.test.ts, disc-blend.test.ts (last moves with 9mm.43).

# camera/timing.ts (renamed from warp-constants.ts in this PR)

The on-main `warp-constants.ts` already covers camera-wide timing — its first comment says "Warp + camera-lerp timing constants." Rename clarifies. Module owns:
- CAMERA_LERP_MS (canonical 2 s; FOCUS_LERP_MS aliases it)
- WARP_T_MIN_MS, WARP_T_MAX_MS, WARP_T_K_MS, WARP_REORIENT_MS
- AIM_T_MIN_MS, AIM_T_MAX_MS
- OBSERVE_TRANSITION_MS
- **NEW (lifted from stellata.ts in this PR):** WARP_BASE_DIR (Vector3), DCAM_LOG_FLOOR_PC

Tests import from here; never redefine. warp-tuning.ts already imports from warp-constants today — its import seam updates.

# Co-location

Tests + *-pure.ts siblings move alongside their parent file. Shaders stay in src/client/shaders/.

# Acceptance

- src/client/ root contains only main.ts, stellata.ts, index.html, styles.css, globals.d.ts, shaders/, stellata-events.test.ts, disc-blend.test.ts.
- All imports updated to new paths.
- camera/timing.ts exports the constants listed above (including the two new lifts); stellata.ts imports from it.
- typecheck passes.
- vitest passes.
- CLAUDE.md "Repo layout" block updated to reflect new layout (per defer-doc-updates rule, this is the right PR for that doc edit).

# Smoke

Full manual UX pass per stellata-smoke-tests:
- Focus a star (Sirius), warp to it, unfocus.
- Toggle navigate ↔ observe.
- Open debug panel + a couple of tuning sections (including warp-tuning).
- Toggle chart mode in observe.
- Constellation overlay on, then off.
- Hover over stars / Local Group dwarfs / heliopause apex → tooltip works (no regression from the import-path churn).

# Dependencies

Blocked by 194.2 (taxonomy + camera/timing.ts naming finalised in design gate).

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] — rules (1) and (2); CLAUDE.md update reinforces.
- [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]] — camera/timing.ts is the canonical source application.
notes: Implemented in PR #74 (bundled with 194.2 per Alex's directive to use the existing design-gate worktree). 117 git mvs into 14 thematic folders + warp-constants → camera/timing rename + WARP_BASE_DIR/DCAM_LOG_FLOOR_PC lift + CLAUDE.md repo-layout refresh. typecheck + 870 tests + production build all clean.

title: Design gate — controller boundaries + folder taxonomy + extraction sequence
description:
Design gate for stellata-9mm.194 (layout & integration-shell decomposition). Produces concrete bd updates that replace the "TBD in 194.2" sections of children .1/.3/.4/.5/.6/.8 with finalised public surfaces. No code change in this bead; no standalone design doc either — the controller architecture lives in code, the conventions in CLAUDE.md + bd memory, and each extraction PR updates its corresponding docs/*.md per [[defer-doc-updates]] as the boundary it documents stabilises.

# Deliverables

- bd updates to 194.1 / .3 / .4 / .5 / .6 / .8 with finalised public surfaces
- New bd `stellata-9mm.194.9` filed for `camera/star-physics.ts` extraction (parallel-trackable post-.1, blocks .8)
- New bd memory `stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions` (5 rules, loads every session via bd prime)
- CLAUDE.md gets "Folder & module conventions" section (peer to "Code conventions — DRY overrides the system prompt"); section is self-contained and references the bd memory
- Plan file in ~/.claude/plans/ holds the in-flight execution detail (which child PR consumes which dep, the FocusOps shim strategy in .5, etc.)

# Scope of the design (in the plan file + child bead descriptions, not duplicated here)

1. Folder taxonomy — per-subsystem folders + cross-cutting type folders + minimal root. Hovers/ already exists from PR #72; validates rule (1) of the conventions.
2. Controller boundaries — Picker / Aim / Warp / ObserveTransition / Focus. Public surfaces, owned state, owned bus events, dispose contracts.
3. Cross-cutting decisions — FrameAnchor stays on Stellata; click FSM stays on Stellata; camera/timing.ts (renamed from warp-constants.ts) is the shared-constants single source; star-physics extracts as own bead 194.9.
4. Bottom-up sequence — .1 (folder reorg + camera/timing.ts rename) → .3 (Picker) → .4 (Aim) → .5 (Warp; takes stellata via FocusOps shim) → .6 (Observe) → .8 (Focus; absorbs the shim).
5. Test strategy — per-controller state-machine + dispose tests; stellata-events.test.ts extended per step; star-physics numeric pinning in .9.

# Reconciliation with origin/main

Two PRs merged during this session — #66 (stellata-2br, camera-arrival profile) and #72 (stellata-lo5, hover engine). Impact captured in the plan file + the refreshed child bead descriptions:
- FocusTarget abstraction landed (focus-target.ts). FocusOps's surface uses currentFocusTarget() not currentFocusLocalPos().
- WarpState refactored to use dest: FocusTarget; tryMidFlyRecentre + getWarpPhase + recenteredToDest field added.
- warp-constants.ts grew to cover camera-wide timing constants — renames to camera/timing.ts in 194.1.
- hover/ folder + 5 providers + 5 formatters + disambiguator already in place. The pickXxxHit methods on stellata.ts absorb into Picker (194.3).
- New top-level files (arrival-curves, camera-motion, focus-target, warp-constants, warp-tuning, chart-disc-pure) added to 194.1's move list.

# Acceptance

- Children .1 / .3 / .4 / .5 / .6 / .8 carry concrete public surfaces in their descriptions (no "TBD in 194.2" remaining)
- bd memory stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions is filed and readable via bd prime
- CLAUDE.md "Folder & module conventions" section is in place and concise (rules 1-3 short form)
- 194.9 (star-physics) filed with deps: blocked by .1, blocks .8

# Smoke

Design review with Alex — no code changes in this bead. Doc-only PR with skip-version-bump label.

# Connects

- [[stellata-code-quality-epic]] — 9mm.194 sub-epic context.
- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] — the new memory this bead files.
- [[defer-doc-updates]] — controller architecture goes in docs/*.md per child PR, not in a one-shot doc here.

title: Extract Picker from stellata.ts (target resolution; click FSM stays in stellata.ts)
description:
Move all per-kind pick paths from stellata.ts into `camera/picker.ts` (folder home finalised in 194.1). Picker is a **pure resolver**: it answers "what's under (x, y)?" and nothing more. The click-state machine + observe single/double-click handlers STAY in stellata.ts as composition-layer orchestration. The hover engine (`src/client/hover/hover-engine.ts`, already in place from PR #72) wraps Picker's hover-pick methods via its provider classes.

# Code to move

Click picks:
- `pickStar`
- `pickCloud`

Hover picks (from PR #72; consumed by hover/*-hover-provider.ts):
- `pickStarHit`
- `pickCloudHit`
- `pickPlanetHit`
- `pickLocalGroupHit`
- `pickHeliopauseHit`

Plus:
- `sortedDistRange` helper — used by pickStar AND by `shouldEnableCoreMask`. Stays exported from picker for the core-mask gate's use OR lifts to camera/star-geometry.ts as a windowed-scan primitive. Pick one in implementation; either is fine as long as there's no duplication.
- `MIN_DISC_HIT_RADIUS_PX` constant.
- `cloudRaycaster` + `tmpNdc` scratch state.
- Heliopause sample-points scratch.

# Code that STAYS in stellata.ts (intentionally)

- `Target` type union + `sameTarget` (composition-shell state — used by onPointerUp).
- `onPointerUp` click-state machine.
- `handleObserveClick` + `observeSingleClick` + `observeDoubleClick`.
- `dblClickRay` / `dblClickAimPoint` scratch state.

# Existing extracts to compose

- `star-geometry.ts` exports `pickFromCandidates`, `angularToPx`, `physSizePx`, type `PickCandidate`. Picker imports these.
- `hover-pick-disambiguator.ts` consumes the HoverHits Picker returns (no Picker-side dependency on the disambiguator — Picker just produces hits).

# Public surface (finalised in 194.2)

```ts
import type { HoverHit } from '../hover/hover-types';

class Picker {
  constructor(deps: {
    domElement: HTMLElement
    camera: PerspectiveCamera
    catalog: Catalog
    sortedByDistFromSol: Uint32Array
    sortedDistFromSol: Float32Array
    getLocalPositions: () => Float32Array
    getFilter: () => Readonly<FilterState>
    getClouds: () => MolecularClouds | null
    getLocalGroupLayer: () => LocalGroupLayer | null
    getHeliopause: () => Heliopause
    getPlanetBodyField: () => PlanetBodyField
    getWarpActive: () => boolean
    renderedSizePxFn: (idx: number) => number   // until 194.9 lifts to camera/star-physics.ts
    fovYRadRef: { value: number }
    viewportRef: { value: Vector2 }
  })

  // Click picks
  pickStar(clientX: number, clientY: number, pixelThreshold?: number): number  // -1 = miss
  pickCloud(clientX: number, clientY: number): number | null

  // Hover picks (HoverHit carries tier + cameraDistancePc for cross-provider disambiguation)
  pickStarHit(clientX: number, clientY: number, pxThreshold?: number): HoverHit | null
  pickCloudHit(clientX: number, clientY: number, pxThreshold?: number): HoverHit | null
  pickPlanetHit(clientX: number, clientY: number, pxThreshold?: number): HoverHit | null
  pickLocalGroupHit(clientX: number, clientY: number, pxThreshold?: number): HoverHit | null
  pickHeliopauseHit(clientX: number, clientY: number, pxThreshold?: number): HoverHit | null
}
```

No state, no bus events, no dispose.

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts no longer holds pickStar / pickCloud / pickStarHit / pickCloudHit / pickPlanetHit / pickLocalGroupHit / pickHeliopauseHit as methods.
- The click FSM in onPointerUp now calls `this.picker.pickStar(...)`.
- hover/*-hover-provider.ts files update their pick() implementations to call `picker.pickStarHit(...)` etc. instead of `stellata.pickStarHit(...)`.
- New `camera/picker.test.ts`: click picks (prime + fallback, cluster + Double-Double, spectral-mask, distance window); hover picks (basic round-trip + cameraDistancePc ordering correctness — the disambiguator depends on it).
- typecheck + vitest pass.

# Smoke

- Click stars + clouds in NAVIGATE mode → focus + vector-to behaviour unchanged.
- Click stars + clouds in OBSERVE mode → POI + aim behaviour unchanged.
- Double-Double (ε¹/ε² Lyrae) — both members independently clickable when discs overlap.
- Hover stars / planets / clouds / Local Group dwarfs / heliopause apex → tooltips render with correct content (no regression from the import-path churn).
- 9mm.115 cloud-picker asymmetry is OUT of scope here — flag it in the PR description if the extraction touches that code path.

# Dependencies

Blocked by 194.1 (folder reorg + camera/timing.ts rename lands first).

# Connects

- [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule (3).
- [[stellata-pattern-coverage-across-peers]] — every pick path landed; no per-kind hover/click pick remains on Stellata.

title: Layout & integration-shell decomposition
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm focused on the two biggest LLM-context-cost reductions identified 2026-05-16: (a) flat src/client/ → thematic subfolders mirroring the docs/*.md taxonomy; (b) splitting the 3947-line stellata.ts integration shell into a thin composition layer + focused controller classes; (c) trimming multi-paragraph in-code prose by relocating physics/derivations to SCIENCE.md + docs/*.md.

# Scope

- Folder reorganisation: src/client/* → overlays/, solar-system/, camera/, local-group/, milkyway/, galactic/, chart-mode/, debug/, modals/, typeahead/, loaders/, ui/, util/. Single mechanical PR; no logic changes.
- Controller extraction from stellata.ts: Picker, AimController, WarpController, ObserveTransition, FocusController. Bottom-up order — leaves first, integration shell last.
- Render-pipeline extracts (StarPipeline = 9mm.43, DustParticleLayer = 9mm.70) reparented here as siblings; they can land in parallel with the controller chain because they touch resource ownership rather than animation state.
- Comment density audit: multi-paragraph in-code derivations relocate to SCIENCE.md / docs/*.md with code-side pointers.

# Why a dedicated epic

Theme is 'reduce the cost of working in the integration shell' for BOTH LLM context (smaller files per task) AND human review (each controller in isolation, not one 4kloc class). Distinct from 9mm.192 which is heterogeneous 'core internals' with catalog + picker tests mixed in.

# Extraction sequence (bottom-up)

The bd IDs assigned out of intent order — execution order is by dep graph (use `bd ready`), not by .N suffix:

1. **194.2** — Design gate (folder taxonomy + controller boundaries + sequence)
2. **194.1** — Folder reorganisation (mechanical, single PR)
3. **194.3** — Extract Picker (leaf, no animation-state deps)
4. **194.4** — Extract AimController
5. **194.5** — Extract WarpController (consolidates warp-pure.ts)
6. **194.6** — Extract ObserveTransition
7. **194.8** — Extract FocusController (most integrated — last)

Parallel tracks:
- **194.7** — Comment density audit (independent file edits; runs any time after 194.1 lands)
- **9mm.43** (StarPipeline) + **9mm.70** (DustParticleLayer) — render-pipeline extracts; reparented here as siblings, can run in parallel with the state-controller chain (touch resource ownership, not animation state).

# Acceptance

- stellata.ts under 1500 lines (from 3947).
- src/client/ root contains only main.ts, stellata.ts, index.html, styles.css, globals.d.ts, shaders/.
- CLAUDE.md module roster reflects new layout.

# Priority

P2 default — context-cost reductions feed directly back into Alex's iteration speed. Individual children can sit at P3 if not blocking in-flight work.

# Connects

- [[stellata-code-quality-epic]] — meta umbrella; this memory needs the 194 reference added.
- [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] — lifecycle pairing in every extracted controller.
- [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]] — extracted constants stay at canonical source modules.
- [[stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep]] — 194.7 directly targets the same drift class.
- [[defer-doc-updates]] — CLAUDE.md module roster updates land in 194.1's PR.
close_reason: All 11 children complete (9mm.194.1-9 + 9mm.43 StarPipeline + 9mm.70 DustParticleLayer). Integration-shell decomposed: stellata.ts dropped from ~3947 to ~2262 lines, six controller classes extracted to camera/, render-pipeline extracts to src/client/, folder taxonomy + comment-density audit landed.

title: Trapezoidal arrival curve (log-d cruise zone)
description:
## Why

Smoke testing of PR #66's `cubic-hermite` log-d arrival profile shows the
camera appears to come to a standstill in the last ~10–20 % of warp time
even though log-d is still being covered. With `f(u) = u²(3 − 2u)` slope
falls from 1.5 at u=0.5 to 0 at u=1, while the disc renderer kicks in
mid-warp (Sol from 10 pc: u ≈ 0.46; Betelgeuse from 200 pc: u ≈ 0.59).
The back half of every warp is spent inside "disc visible" territory
with progressively slower angular-size growth — that collapse-to-zero in
log-d velocity reads as "we stopped."

## Goal

Add a `'trapezoid'` arrival curve to `arrival-curves.ts`: quadratic accel
ramp → linear cruise → quadratic decel ramp, all in log-d. Two tunable
widths (t_accel, t_decel) parameterise the spectrum. Defaults
`t_accel = 0.15`, `t_decel = 0.10` give a sustained cruise plus the
~10 % decel window Alex asked for, with a smooth gentle launch and a
zero-velocity arrival preserved.

## Math

    v = 1 / (1 − t_accel/2 − t_decel/2)

    u ∈ [0, t_accel]:        f(u) = (v/(2·t_accel)) · u²
    u ∈ [t_accel, 1-t_decel]: f(u) = v·u − v·t_accel/2
    u ∈ [1-t_decel, 1]:       s = (u − (1−t_decel))/t_decel
                              f(u) = 1 − (v·t_decel/2)·(1 − s)²

C¹-continuous at both joins; f(0)=f'(0)=0, f(1)=1, f'(1)=0. The
degenerate case `t_accel=t_decel=0.5` recovers the legacy main-branch
piecewise-quadratic feel (constant accel/decel, no cruise).

## Plan reference

Full design in
`/Users/alexm/.claude/plans/ok-the-curve-definitely-indexed-ladybug.md`.

## Files

- `src/client/arrival-curves.ts` — add trapezoid curve + extend
  `resolveArrivalCurve` signature for the two widths.
- `src/client/arrival-curves.test.ts` — endpoints, slope continuity at
  both joins, degenerate-case match against piecewise-quad, monotonicity.
- `src/client/warp-tuning.ts` — add `'trapezoid'` to dropdown, two
  sliders (gated like the `p` slider), summary line.
- `docs/camera-arrival.md` § new "Trapezoidal alternative" subsection.
- `docs/camera-warp.md` § Fly — curve description cross-ref.
- `package.json` — 1.10.2 → 1.11.0.

## Smoke

Manual on Alex's browser (no dev server):
- `/?debug=1`, expand warp section. Switch curve to `trapezoid`.
  Verify two new sliders appear, `p` slider hides.
- Warp Sol from ~10 pc with trapezoid default — close-approach should
  feel like sustained zoom-in until the last ~10 %.
- `t_accel=t_decel=0.5` reproduces legacy main-branch warp feel.
- `t_decel=0.05` — landing should still feel smooth, not slam.
- Chart-mode warp (Scenario 10 from PR #66) — plateau trigger fires
  against camera position, not u, so behaviour unchanged.

## PR

Stack on PR #66 (umbrella).

title: Camera envelope + distance formatting + fade-band refactor for Mpc/Gpc scales
description:
**Goal:** lift stellata's hard-coded camera envelope + distance formatter + fade bands so subsequent tier impls just pass a regime parameter rather than touch core code. Architectural enabler for stellata-0hx.

# Why

Today `PerspectiveCamera.far = 500000` and `TrackballControls.maxDistance = 250000` are magic literals in `src/client/stellata.ts:670-689`. `fmtDistAuto` in `src/client/distance-util.ts:40-50` switches at "k pc" past 10 kpc but has no Mpc/Gpc/Gly branch. `galactic-fade.ts` exports a single FADE_INNER_PC/FADE_OUTER_PC pair that works for MW + LG only.

Each subsequent tier (.3 LV, .4 LSC, .5 Lan, .6 PCs, .7 BOSS, .8 CMB) would otherwise need a code edit to `stellata.ts`. Lift once, parameterise per regime.

# Scope

1. **Camera envelope: regime enum** — introduce `CameraRegime` = STELLAR / GALACTIC / SUPERCLUSTER / COSMIC with per-regime `{far, maxDistance, near}` tuples. The regime is chosen at runtime based on camera distance from Sol (with hysteresis bands to avoid flicker on the boundary). Initial set:
   - STELLAR: near=1e-10, far=500_000 (today's values)
   - GALACTIC: near=1e-6, far=3e6 (3 Mpc — stellata-1ui's reach)
   - SUPERCLUSTER: near=1, far=3e8 (300 Mpc — Tier 3)
   - COSMIC: near=1e3, far=2e10 (20 Gpc — Tiers 5–6)
2. **fmtDistAuto extension** — add Mpc/Gpc + Mly/Gly branches to `src/client/distance-util.ts`. Today's pc/ly threshold logic stays; new format kicks in past 1 Mpc.
3. **Fade-band builder** — refactor `src/client/galactic-fade.ts` to export `FadeBand(inner, outer, sample)` rather than free constants. Existing `FADE_INNER_PC` / `FADE_OUTER_PC` consumers (`galactic-disc.ts`, `local-group.ts`) migrate. New constants `LG_FADE_BAND`, `LV_FADE_BAND`, `LSC_FADE_BAND` defined for subsequent tiers. Per [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]] — extract at second usage, parameterise the differences.
4. **Log-depth precision smoke** — verify `near=1e-10` doesn't break log-depth at `far=2e10`. May need to raise near per regime (already in the regime tuple above).

# Acceptance

- `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` green.
- New unit tests for `CameraRegime` resolution + `fmtDistAuto` Mpc/Gpc branches.
- No visual regression: at default zoom (Sol focal), behavior identical to today.

# Smoke (per [[alex-stellata-smoke-tests]])

- Default first-load: identical to today.
- Pull camera back past 250 kpc: regime auto-transitions to SUPERCLUSTER, no jitter.
- Distance readout switches to Mpc past 1 Mpc, Mly past 3 Mly.
- Manual zoom to 100 Mpc out: no log-depth precision failure (foreground stars still render correctly).

# Out of scope

- Actual far-field rendering — that's .3+. This bead is plumbing only.
- Data pipeline — .3.

# Blocks

stellata-0hx's tier-impl children (.3 through .8).

# Blocked by

stellata-0hx.<design-gate-child> (the .1 child) — confirm regime cutoffs.

title: Research + design-gate: extragalactic deep-field roadmap doc
description:
**Goal:** write `docs/extragalactic-roadmap.md` capturing data inventory, tier breakdown, naming policy, cosmology choice, manifest schema, and per-tier data-source URLs. The design-gate for the rest of stellata-0hx's children.

Per [[stellata-prioritization-framework]] — design-gate of a layer epic = parent's priority (P2). All implementation children sit at P3 behind this.

# Why

The parent epic (stellata-0hx) describes WHAT to build at each tier. This bead writes the durable in-repo design document that captures HOW: cosmology bake, naming-vs-PGC policy, manifest schema, per-tier data fetch instructions, selection-bias documentation for BOSS-tier. Future sessions execute against the doc instead of re-deriving from the epic body.

# Scope

`docs/extragalactic-roadmap.md` covering:

1. **Per-tier data inventory** — UNGC, Pace LVDB, EVCC, 2MRS, Cosmicflows-4, Dupuy 2023 watersheds, NED-LVS, SDSS Main, BOSS/eBOSS, Planck SMICA. URL, retrieval steps, expected file size, licence, citation. Per [[frozen-external-data]], each must commit-once via a one-off `scripts/refresh-<tier>.{ts,py}` excluded from `npm run build`.

2. **Manifest schema** for committed extragalactic catalogues under `data/extragalactic/`:
   - Per-galaxy record: position (ICRS xyz pc), distance, M_K (or apparent K), morph_type (Hubble T), name (or null), PGC ID, source tier.
   - Tier 3+ uses binary format (parallel to `public/catalog.bin`); Tier 1–2 uses JSON (parallel to `public/local-group.json`).
   - Tier 4+ chunks: HEALPix Nside=4 sky cells + distance bands.

3. **Naming policy** — labels only on named objects. Define which catalogues to harvest names from (NGC, IC, UGC, Messier; PGC is NOT a "name"). Label-rank function spec: spatial octree of named galaxies; per-frame ranking on the bucket containing camera + neighbours; 1-pixel hysteresis; ~30 visible cap across all tiers combined.

4. **Cosmology choice for Tier 5+**: Planck 2018 (H0=67.4 km/s/Mpc, Ω_m=0.315, Ω_Λ=0.685) — decide and document the z→comoving-distance conversion (numerical integration baked into the build pipeline).

5. **Selection-bias documentation** for Tier 5 (BOSS/eBOSS LRG/ELG/QSO sample is non-uniform). Document the selection function; consider a debug-panel toggle to visualise it.

6. **Morphological-type → color palette** decision for the billboard renderer. Hubble T-type → RGB. Choose Buta/de Vaucouleurs convention (Sa = blue-ish, E = red-ish) or simple type-code bucket.

7. **Architectural sketches** for the three cross-cutting changes (camera regime enum, billboard pipeline, label-rank with spatial bucketing) — point at the .2 and .3 beads for impl.

# Acceptance

- `docs/extragalactic-roadmap.md` committed.
- Index entry added to `CLAUDE.md` § Documentation index.
- All per-tier data-source URLs verified live at write time (capture retrieval dates inline).

# Smoke

This is a docs bead — no runtime smoke. PR review by Alex IS the gate.

# Out of scope

- Any code changes. Pure docs.
- Actual data fetches — that's the per-tier `refresh-*` script, filed under each impl bead.

# Blocks

stellata-0hx's other children (.2 through .8) — they reference decisions made here.

title: Hover labels — design gate (decide field shapes, trigger semantics, layout)
description:
**Status:** design / decision. No code lands until this clears.

# Goal

Resolve the open design questions captured in the parent epic stellata-lo5 so the per-class implementation children have a settled payload shape, trigger model, and layout to build against.

# Questions to answer (verbatim from the epic body)

1. **Hover trigger semantics.** Always-on near-pointer? Modifier-key gated? Click-to-pin? Touch behaviour?
2. **Layout — fixed corner vs near-cursor floating tooltip.** Interaction with the existing focus-info card?
3. **Schema consistency vs class-specific richness.** Single field template or per-class?
4. **Always-shown vs expanded-on-click split.** Hover ≤ 5 lines vs full modal?
5. **Chart-mode interplay.** Same payload, styled subset, or hidden?
6. **Picker priority on overlapping layers.** Star-in-front-of-cloud-edge — which wins?
7. **Touch / mobile parity.** Long-press? Or defer to mobile pass entirely?

# Per-class suggested fields (from epic — confirm or refine each)

The epic enumerates suggested fields for each class. The design gate either:
- Confirms each class's list as-is and tags it as the implementation contract; OR
- Trims / extends it, recording the final shape here so each implementation child has one source-of-truth field list.

# Deliverable

A short design doc (in this bead's description or a `docs/hover-labels.md` if it earns a doc), specifying:
- Trigger + layout model.
- Per-class field lists (final).
- Picker-priority ordering.
- Chart-mode behaviour.
- Mobile / touch decision (implement vs defer).

Once shipped, implementation children spin up as separate beads, each parented to stellata-lo5.

# Smoke / acceptance

Doc reviewed by Alex; per-class lists frozen; implementation beads filed with explicit dependency on this gate's closure.
close_reason: Design gate resolved by approved plan: existing star hover trigger reused verbatim (280 ms delay, 14 px pickbox, near-cursor #tooltip, hide on leave); no click-to-pin; single #tooltip element (no separate focus card — focus info lives in scale-bar widget); shared engine with per-class HoverProvider contract (pick + format); 5-line cap, no expand-on-hover; chart-mode = CSS swap only; picker disambiguation hoisted from star-geometry.ts + stellata.ts to a shared module, prime-beats-fallback + closest-camera tie-break extended across all layers; mobile deferred to a follow-up bead. Per-class schemas frozen: stars unchanged; Sol planets = name/distance/V mag/radius/period; LG = name/kind/distance/size; heliopause apex = name/distance/description; clouds = formatter only, not registered at v1.0. Implementation beads filed as lo5.2-lo5.9.

title: Hover labels — generalised across stars / planets / LG / clouds / heliopause
description:
**Status:** design epic. Captures every renderable that is hover-targetable but has no hover info today, and proposes a generalised hover-label mechanism that covers stars, Sol's planets, future exoplanets, Local Group wireframes, molecular clouds, and the heliopause apex. **No decisions made — every shape below is a suggestion that needs Alex's call before implementation children land.**

# Why a generalised mechanism

Today the only hover affordance is the star pickbox (focused-star path). Adding hover labels per object class one at a time would split into N parallel implementations — same shape every time, each likely to drift. The goal of this epic is a single hover-label engine that:

1. Owns the SVG / DOM surface for the floating tooltip.
2. Routes pickbox hits from each layer's picker to a typed label payload.
3. Has a per-class formatter so each kind of object surfaces the right summary.

The picker side already exists per-layer (stars + clouds + planets all have raycast / pickbox paths). The label side is the missing piece.

# Candidate object classes + suggested fields per class

For each class below, the listed fields are **suggestions to evaluate, not decisions**. Star hover today shows roughly name / distance / app-mag / abs-mag / spectral / constellation; suggestions for other classes try to mirror that shape while swapping spectral for whatever's diagnostic for the class.

## Stars (existing — re-audit at design time)

- Name (preferred designation)
- Distance from Sol
- Apparent V mag
- Absolute V mag
- Spectral class
- Constellation
- (Suggestion to consider): variability type if GCVS-matched

## Sol's planets (currently no hover)

- Name (Mercury / Venus / …)
- Distance from Sol (live, since it's animated by ephemeris)
- Distance from camera (separate, useful when host-focused)
- Apparent V mag at camera vantage
- Physical radius
- Orbital period (or current orbital phase)
- (Suggestion to consider): JPL Standish ephemeris epoch + accuracy band

## Exoplanets (future — parametric-on-focal-host)

- Name (system + planet letter)
- Apparent V mag (likely "below threshold" for most)
- Physical radius / mass
- Orbital period + semi-major axis
- Host star context (distance from Sol via host)
- Discovery method + reference

## Local Group wireframes (stellata-1ui shipped, no hover yet)

- Display name (M31, NGC 205, Sextans dSph, …)
- Type (dSph / dE / dIrr / spiral disc) — derived from `kind` + the override / display-name rules
- Distance from Sol (always pc / ly / Mpc via fmtDist)
- Apparent V mag — depends on stellata-7de unblocking
- Absolute V mag — from LVDB M_V (M31 / M33 hand-curated)
- Half-light radius — for ellipsoid kind; disc radius for disc kind
- (Suggestion to consider): heliocentric radial velocity from LVDB `vlos_systemic`; proper motion from `pmra` / `pmdec`. These feel "deep dive" rather than hover summary — consider a follow-up modal instead.

## Molecular clouds (stellata-c7u — exists, hover currently absent)

- Cloud name (Taurus, Orion A, …)
- Distance from Sol
- Physical extent (semi-axes from the ellipsoid model)
- Mass (Zucker 2021 Table 3 — currently unused but committed)
- (Suggestion): cloud type — diffuse vs molecular, when a type label is meaningful

## Heliopause apex (Sol-focused only, currently has anchor label)

- Anchor label is the existing apex marker — hover suggestion is to surface AU distance + estimated boundary type (termination shock vs heliopause vs bow shock).

# Open design questions (the design-gate child below holds these — Alex decides)

1. **Hover trigger semantics.** Always-on hover when pointer is near a pickable? Modifier-key gated (alt-hover)? Click-to-pin? Single-tap on mobile?
2. **Layout — fixed corner vs near-cursor floating tooltip.** The existing focus-info card is fixed; hover preview probably wants near-cursor. How does it interact with the focus card?
3. **Schema consistency vs class-specific richness.** Force every object class to fit the same field template, or per-class layouts?
4. **What fields are "always shown" vs "expanded on click"?** Hover summary likely wants ≤ 5 lines; the rest of the data lives in a click-to-pin / focus modal.
5. **Chart-mode interplay.** Chart mode has its own minimal aesthetic — does hover show the same payload, a chart-styled subset, or nothing?
6. **Picker priorities.** When two layers overlap at the cursor (star in front of a cloud edge), which wins?
7. **Touch / mobile parity.** Hover doesn't exist on touch — long-press? Card always visible after pick?

# Children to file once the design gate clears

- Hover-engine module (DOM / SVG plumbing + typed payload routing) — single PR.
- Per-class hover formatter + picker wire-up: one bead each for stars, Sol planets, LG objects, molecular clouds, heliopause apex. Filed when the design gate clears so the expected payload shape is settled.
- Mobile / touch parity bead (lower priority — desktop first per stellata-prioritization-framework).
- Click-to-pin / focus-modal expansion bead (separate from hover summary).

# Related work

- stellata-7de — LG apparent magnitude depends on the magnitude column being plumbed; hover labels for LG depend on stellata-7de if "show apparent V mag" makes the suggested list.
- stellata-c7u — molecular-cloud rendering epic.
- stellata-3re — solar-system planets layer.
- stellata-bk5 — future exoplanet layer.

title: Cloud-warp arrivals: lookAt jitter (no recentre infrastructure)
description:
Same Float32-ULP lookAt jitter as stellata-2br.5 (navigate-mode star warp), but for cloud destinations. setFocusedCloud doesn't call recenterOrigin — the floating origin stays on the previous focus across a cloud-warp arrival. So lookAt(cloudCenter) in the source-local frame suffers the same precision degradation as star arrivals did pre-2br.5, but there's no existing infrastructure to fix it.

The cubic-Hermite log-d Fly profile exposes this for cloud destinations the same way it does for star destinations.

# Possible approaches

1. Extend setFocusedCloud to recentre origin onto the cloud's centroid at focus time. Mirror setFocus's geometric half.
2. Add a recenterFocusToCloud sibling to recenterFocusToStar, called mid-Fly when destKind === 'cloud'. Needs WarpState.recenteredToDest and shiftWarpWaypoints / shiftArrivalWaypoints (both extant after 2br.5).
3. Live with it — cloud warps are typically shorter (Zucker cloud distances are 100s of pc, similar regime to stellar warps).

Probably (2) — extends 2br.5's pattern cleanly. But the floating-origin-tracks-cloud-centroid invariant needs cross-checking against the cloud overlay's render path (clouds are 3D ellipsoids in cloud-loader / molecular-clouds; the centroid may not be the centre of mass for picking).

# Out of scope of PR #66

Discovered during 2br.5 design but not fixed there.
close_reason: Folded into stellata-2br.5 — cloud-warp arrivals now recentre origin via FocusTarget contract

title: Modal & welcome UX polish
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm covering small user-visible modal/welcome defects.

Scope: welcome-modal copy is a sentence fragment after #info-star-count substitution, welcome vs about modals use different headline framings ('physics-based' vs 'observational'), modal-dismiss querySelectorAll runs once at bind time so late-added dismiss buttons are unwired, modal-dismiss per-modal keydown listener means multiple open modals all close on a single ESC, plus the deferred 'show visible star count under total' HUD task.

Why a dedicated epic: only 5 beads but every one of them shows up in the user's first 30 seconds with the app. Pulling them out of 9mm makes them visible as a 'first-impression polish' bucket rather than buried in code-quality.

Priority: P2 — small but user-facing.

title: Planet pipeline DRY, tests, halo correctness
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm collecting follow-ups from the solar-system pipeline (stellata-3re).

Scope: PlanetMaterialUniforms duplicates 17 star-pipeline uniforms by hand (DRY), PlanetBodyField.update() pipeline zero test coverage past the cull gate, flushAttributeRange marks all 7 buffers dirty when only iLocalRel changes, getFocusedPlanetLocalPositions returns a subarray that's stale after growCapacity (lifecycle pairing gap), peakPhaseFactor name overpromises (only φ(0)), outer-disc near-z write defeats the disc-pass gl_FragDepth=1.0 halo trick, evaluate whether stars should adopt the planet-style outer-disc occluder.

Why a dedicated epic: the planet pipeline diverged from the star pipeline in PR #48 (3 passes vs 4) and the parallel implementations need decisions before more sister-host work (clouds, exoplanets) lands. Grouping makes those decisions one design pass instead of seven scattered judgement calls.

Priority: P2 on the user-visible halo defect (123) and the test/cull regressions; rest default P3.

title: SVG overlay dirty-tracking & sentinel-init hygiene
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm covering the rest of the SVG overlay dirty-tracking rollout (stellata-akk) plus the broader sentinel-init / cache-key hygiene findings.

Scope: focus-ring-overlay (the sixth peer missed in PR #55), scale-bar.ts whole-frame vs per-attribute strategy divergence, ATTR_DIRTY_PX hoist into a shared util, sentinel-init poison audit across overlay visibility tracking, hud-overlay/poi-overlay numeric-sentinel reset on hide, applyFade pointerEvents sentinel first-frame skip, disc-mask inverted threshold guard, getComputedTextLength cache-key missing font-load + CSS dimensions, projectWithNearClip single-caller-per-frame contract.

Why a dedicated epic: ~10 findings all in the same overlay layer that should converge on one canonical strategy. Memory rules at play: stellata-consistency-at-the-seam §3 (sentinel-init), stellata-pattern-coverage-across-peers (all SVG overlays must match), stellata-named-constants-and-dry rule 1 (hoist ATTR_DIRTY_PX at second usage).

Priority: P2 — visible mis-paints + perf cliffs hit the user.

title: Event-bus & lifecycle hygiene
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm carving out follow-ups from the event-bus consolidation (stellata-77m) and adjacent lifecycle/dispose hygiene work.

Scope: EventBus API hardening (clear(), EmitArgs typing, varargs allocations, mid-emit ordering), unsubscribe leaks at component teardown (createTimeReadout, perf-hud, debug HUDs), the eleven-event surface integration test, stale on*Change comment sweep, perf marker rename to match the new 'frame' event, and the dispose smoke test.

Why a dedicated epic: ~13 follow-ups all gated on the same mental model — the typed EventBus from stellata-77m is the central seam, and the lifecycle-pairing rule (see memory stellata-consistency-at-the-seam §1) wants every bus.on() to ship its unsub in the same diff. Grouping these makes it tractable to land them in 2-3 PRs rather than scattered across the meta umbrella.

Priority: P2 because lifecycle leaks are real bugs (dispose() in test setups grows memory between runs); test/doc sub-items default to P3.

title: Phase 5 — Eclipse photometry for EA/EB/EW from orbital elements
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Supersedes stellata-dch.17. Implement physically-grounded eclipse photometry for EA/EB/EW (GCVS eclipsing-binary classes) by combining: stellata-dch.14 (suppress intrinsic pulsation for eclipsing classes — currently in-flight) + the orbital elements emitted by Phase 2 (stellata-dch.32) + the corrected mass ratios from Phase 5 stellata-dch.40. Depends on stellata-dch.14 + stellata-dch.32 + stellata-dch.40.

# Approach

For each EA/EB/EW system with orbital elements:

1. Render the two stars as their actual physical-radius discs (already done in current shader).
2. From the orbital elements (P, T, e, a, i, omega, Omega — now Gaia NSS or ORB6-sourced via Phase 2 Stage 4), compute the per-component sky-plane position at runtime t (the existing dch.9 / dch.10 binary-orbit machinery does this).
3. Compute the per-frame fraction of the brighter component's disc occulted by the dimmer component's disc (circle-circle occlusion math: closed-form when both radii + center distance are known).
4. Subtract the occulted luminosity from the apparent magnitude of the rendered primary. Real eclipse-shaped photometry: deep primary minimum when the cooler / dimmer star transits the hotter / brighter one; shallower secondary minimum on the other side of the orbit.

# Deliverables

- src/client/eclipse-photometry.ts (new) — closed-form circle-circle occlusion math; per-component luminosity subtraction; integration with the existing apparent-magnitude computation in the star vertex shader (uniform / per-instance attribute path TBD at impl).
- src/client/shaders/star.vert.glsl — accept an eclipse_dim per-instance attribute (or uniform) that the eclipse-photometry layer writes per-frame. Wired into the existing magnitude path.
- Integration with the binary-orbit runtime (the existing stellata-dch.10 in-flight bead): the orbital evaluator already computes per-frame component positions. Eclipse-photometry reads those and writes the dim factor.
- src/client/eclipse-photometry.test.ts — unit tests for circle-circle occlusion math (degenerate cases: no occlusion, partial overlap, full coverage, equal radii).

# Acceptance

- Algol (β Persei, HIP 14576, EA, P=2.87 days): visible eclipses under warp animation; primary minimum drop ≈ 1.3 mag depth; secondary minimum drop ≈ 0.1 mag (matches published lightcurve).
- β Lyrae (HIP 92420, EB, P=12.94 days): continuous brightness variation with two unequal-depth minima.
- W UMa (HIP 56619, EW, P=0.33 days): near-continuous variation, near-equal-depth minima.
- Non-eclipsing binaries (α Cen, Sirius, Castor) show no eclipse-photometry dip when orbiting under warp.
- Variable types EA/EB/EW with no orbital elements (no Gaia NSS, no ORB6) gracefully fall back to GCVS amplitude-driven pulsation (current behavior).

# Smoke (manual per memory stellata-stellata-smoke-tests)

- Focus Algol -> advance t under warp -> primary minimum visible at t ≈ T + n*P.
- Focus β Lyr -> see continuous variation.
- Compare against published lightcurves (AAVSO).
- Verify no eclipses on non-EA/EB/EW systems.

# Files to touch

- src/client/eclipse-photometry.ts (new)
- src/client/shaders/star.vert.glsl + star.frag.glsl (eclipse_dim attribute integration)
- src/client/eclipse-photometry.test.ts (new)
- src/client/stellata.ts (wire eclipse-photometry into the per-frame loop alongside the binary-orbit runtime)

# After this lands

Close stellata-dch.17 with reason="superseded by stellata-dch.41".

# Commit

One commit (or split if shaders + runtime are big enough to warrant separation per memory commit-granularity). Minor version bump (user-visible feature: real eclipse photometry).

title: Phase 5 — Spectral-class-aware mass-ratio q (WD/giant/subgiant fallbacks)
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Supersedes stellata-dch.15. Replace the current L^(1/3.5) main-sequence mass-luminosity mass-ratio q estimate with a spectral-class-aware lookup that handles WD / giant / subgiant fallbacks correctly. Depends on stellata-dch.32 (Stage 5+6+7 lands the mass-ratio emission path) + stellata-dch.36 (composite-spectral-types-split per component).

# Why

Current implementation: q = M_B / (M_A + M_B), with M = L^(1/3.5) from absmag. This fails badly for evolved companions. Example: Sirius B (V=11.36 absmag) yields q ≈ 0.07; true value is q ≈ 0.33 (M_A=2.06 M_sun, M_B=1.02 M_sun). The orbit shape is unaffected but the split-of-motion between components is wrong by ~5×.

# Approach

Per-component mass estimation table-lookup by parsed spectral class:

- WD (luminosity class VII / spect prefix 'D'): mass ≈ 0.6 M_sun ± 0.1 (a typical WD mass; main-sequence-progenitor info unavailable in WDS)
- Giants (class III): use mass-from-MK-class table (e.g. K giants ≈ 1.2 M_sun, G giants ≈ 1.5 M_sun, etc.) per Cox 2000 §15.2 / Allen 2000 tables
- Subgiants (class IV): interpolate between main-sequence and giant tables
- Main-sequence (class V): current L^(1/3.5) relation (correct for these)
- Supergiants (class I): higher-mass table

q = M_B / (M_A + M_B) computed per-pair using these tables.

# Deliverables

- scripts/catalog-pure.ts — new function massFromSpectralClass(spect_str, absmag) returning estimated M / M_sun. Tables for WD / giant / subgiant / supergiant. Main-sequence remains L^(1/3.5).
- scripts/build-binaries.py — Stage 4 / 6 — when computing q for a system, call the new helper instead of the inline L^(1/3.5). Per-component spectral classes (from stellata-dch.36) now feed in correctly.
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts — pinned-value tests: Sirius B q ≈ 0.33, Procyon B q ≈ 0.4 (rough estimates; calibrate at impl from published masses).
- scripts/known-stars.tsv (stellata-dch.37 corpus) — verify the corpus's expected q values for binaries with WD / giant companions match the new tables, refresh corpus where needed.

# Acceptance

- Sirius B q within ±0.05 of 0.33 (currently 0.07 = off by 4×).
- Procyon B q within ±0.05 of expected from known-mass table lookup.
- α Cen B q ≈ 0.45 (both main-sequence, current logic correct).
- known-stars.test.ts (Phase 4 Bead R, stellata-dch.38) passes mass-ratio assertions for all corpus binaries with WD/giant companions.

# Smoke

- npm run build:binaries --force
- grep '06451-1643' data/multiples.tsv shows Sirius A q + B q with the new values
- npm test green

# Files to touch

- scripts/catalog-pure.ts (mass lookup helper)
- scripts/build-binaries.py (Stage 4 / 6 q computation)
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts (per-class assertions)

# After this lands

Close stellata-dch.15 with reason="superseded by stellata-dch.40".

# Commit

One commit. Minor version bump (user-visible: Sirius B's mass-ratio rendering will change).

title: Phase 4 — scripts/validate-simbad-sample.py + extend build-counts.test.ts with statistical bounds
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Two validation surfaces land in this bead: Tier-C SIMBAD random-sample cross-check (manual-run, against the snapshot from stellata-dch.26) and Tier-B population statistical bounds (snapshot-pinned in build-counts.test.ts). Depends on stellata-dch.26 (SIMBAD sample) + stellata-dch.32 (Phase 2 stats output).

# Tier-C — scripts/validate-simbad-sample.py

Manual-run Python script (npm alias validate:simbad). Reads data/simbad_sample.tsv, for each row looks up the entry in public/catalog.bin by HIP / gaia_source_id, compares Stellata's distance / PM / absmag against SIMBAD published values. Outputs:
- Residual histograms per axis (absmag, distance, pmra, pmdec) written to docs/validation-residuals-<date>.md (or plain JSON; one or the other, decide at impl)
- Top-50 outliers flagged: stars with absmag residual > 0.5 OR plx residual > 3-sigma
- Summary stats: median + percentile residuals; pass/fail per axis against thresholds defined in script
- Exits non-zero if median absmag residual > 0.1 OR median plx residual > 3-sigma (these are smoke thresholds for the script itself, not for the build)

# Tier-B — extend scripts/build-counts.test.ts

Add pinned statistical bounds that the build must produce, snapshot-style per memory stellata-test-coverage-discipline:

- gaia_resolve_rate (fraction of WDS components resolved via tiers 1-3, i.e. source-ID anchored): pin >= 0.90 with current snapshot value
- optical_rejected_rate (fraction of WDS pairs rejected by the cascade): pin actual value with ±20% tolerance
- nss_orbit_rate (fraction of orbits sourced from NSS vs ORB6): pin actual ratio
- hip2_fallback_rate (fraction of components routed via HIP2 fallback): pin actual rate
- The existing tier-2 counts (recordCount, binaryPairs, gcvsMatched, ccdmFlagged, etc.) stay; this just adds the new metrics

Snapshots refreshed via UPDATE_BUILD_COUNTS=1 (existing mechanism). Failing snapshots halt the build.

# Acceptance

- npm run validate:simbad runs against current catalog.bin, produces residual report. Median absmag residual < 0.05; median plx residual < 1-sigma; outlier count < 1% of sample.
- npm test green with new Tier-B assertions in build-counts.test.ts.
- scripts/build-counts-expected.json updated with new fields.

# Smoke

- npm run build:binaries && npm run build:catalog (full pipeline)
- npm run validate:simbad
- Inspect residual histograms

# Files to touch

- scripts/validate-simbad-sample.py (new)
- scripts/build-counts.test.ts (extend)
- scripts/build-counts-expected.json (extend schema)
- package.json (npm alias)

# Commit

One commit. PR labelled skip-version-bump (validation, not user-visible).

title: Phase 3 — parseSpectral splits composite WDS types (A1Vm+DA2 → per-component)
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Supersedes stellata-dch.16. Extends parseSpectral to handle composite WDS spectral types (e.g. 'G2V+DA2' for Sirius, 'A1V+M4' for 40 Eridani), splitting on '+' and parsing each component separately. The current implementation parses only the first half, which misassigns the spectral class of secondary components in close binaries. Depends on stellata-dch.33 (v6 binary format provides per-component spectral storage via multiples.tsv rows).

# Deliverables

- scripts/catalog-pure.ts — extend parseSpectral function. New signature: parseSpectral(input, componentLetter?='A') returns a per-component spectral classification. When the input contains '+', split it; the A component gets the first half, B gets the second half, etc. Default to first half if no component letter provided (backward-compatible).
- scripts/build-binaries.py — when emitting multiples.tsv rows, pass each component's letter to parseSpectral so each row's spect column reflects the actual component's spectral class.
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts — add tests covering: 'G2V+DA2' splits to G2V (A) and DA2 (B); 'A1V+M4' splits to A1V (A) and M4 (B); 'F5V' (no +) returns F5V for any component letter; 'G2V+DA2+M5' (triple) handles correctly.

# Acceptance

- Sirius A row: spect=G2V (existing behavior).
- Sirius B row: spect=DA2 (NEW — currently inherits 'G2V' or composite).
- 40 Eridani B (white dwarf): spect=DA (NEW).
- Tests pin these specific values via toBe.

# Smoke

- npm test green with new assertions
- npm run build:binaries --force; grep '06451-1643' data/multiples.tsv shows Sirius A=G2V, B=DA2 in spect column

# Files to touch

- scripts/catalog-pure.ts (parseSpectral)
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts (per-component tests)
- scripts/build-binaries.py (pass component letter through)

# Commit

One commit. Close stellata-dch.16 with reason="superseded by stellata-dch.36" after this bead lands.

title: Phase 3 — build-catalog.ts CCDM cross-match uses HIP via new cross-walks
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Align the CCDM (Catalogue of Components of Double and Multiple Stars) cross-match pass in scripts/build-catalog.ts with the new HIP-anchored infrastructure. CCDM is referenced via the existing data/hip_ccdm.tsv file; this bead ensures the cross-match path is consistent with Phase 2's HIP cross-walk usage. Depends on stellata-dch.33.

# Deliverables

- scripts/build-catalog.ts — verify the CCDM cross-match pass uses HIP cross-walk infrastructure consistently. If currently using ad-hoc HIP lookups, refactor to share the loaded HIP indices. Document any divergence (CCDM might use its own HIP keying scheme).
- scripts/catalog-pure.test.ts — extend CCDM tests with assertions tied to the new indices.

# Acceptance

- CCDM match-rate stats logged at end of build (counts of CCDM rows successfully mapped to AT-HYG rows via HIP).
- A known CCDM-flagged star (e.g. a famous visual double) renders with the ccdmFlag bit set in the v6 binary.
- No regression in existing ccdmFlagged count in build-counts.test.ts (current snapshot pinned).

# Smoke

- npm run build:catalog
- npx tsx scripts/verify-catalog.ts: ccdmFlagged count matches expected
- Spot-check a CCDM-flagged star

# Files to touch

- scripts/build-catalog.ts (CCDM cross-match section)
- scripts/catalog-pure.ts (if cross-id parsing needs adjustment)

# Commit

One commit. Mostly internal alignment; likely no user-visible behavior change → skip-version-bump label OK if no functional difference.

title: Phase 1 — scripts/refresh-simbad-sample.py + commit data/simbad_sample.tsv
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Random 10k SIMBAD sample with stable seed for reproducible validation. Used by Phase 4 Tier-C validator (stellata-dch.39) to cross-check Stellata's catalog against an independent external authority. Depends on stellata-dch.21 (refresh_lib). NOT blocking Phase 2 (P2 priority).

# Deliverables

- scripts/refresh-simbad-sample.py (new) — SIMBAD TAP query with seeded random sample, stratified by V magnitude for diversity.
- scripts/refresh_lib.py — add simbad_backend() factory + SIMBAD_TAP_URL constant (first non-Gaia/non-VizieR backend; refresh-bailer-jones.py's cds-only override is the precedent). Unit test added in refresh_lib.test.py.
- data/simbad_sample.tsv (new, LFS ~1-2 MB).
- package.json — "refresh:simbad" alias.

# TSV columns (15)

Schema fixes vs the original draft (live SIMBAD TAP probe 2026-05-18 — basic columns are not what the original spec assumed):

- simbad_oid          int — SIMBAD basic.oid (stable primary key; useful for re-queries).
- simbad_main_id      str — SIMBAD basic.main_id (e.g. "* alf Car"). Was "simbad_main_id"; preserved.
- hip                 int|null — extracted from ident.id LIKE 'HIP %' (HIP integer; null if not present in Hipparcos).
- gaia_source_id      int|null — extracted from ident.id LIKE 'Gaia DR3 %' (Gaia DR3 source_id; null if not in DR3).
- ra                  float — basic.ra (deg, ICRS J2000). Was "ra_icrs"; basic.ra IS ICRS already by SIMBAD design, no _icrs suffix on the TAP column.
- dec                 float — basic.dec (deg, ICRS J2000). Was "dec_icrs"; same reasoning.
- plx_value           float|null — basic.plx_value (mas). Was "plx"; actual column is "plx_value".
- plx_err             float|null — basic.plx_err (mas).
- pmra                float|null — basic.pmra (mas/yr). Was "pm_ra"; actual column is "pmra".
- pmdec               float|null — basic.pmdec (mas/yr). Was "pm_de"; actual column is "pmdec".
- v_mag               float — allfluxes.V (mag). Was implicit; needed in the TSV so the validator can verify the stratification it received.
- distance_pc         float|null — derived: 1000/plx_value when plx_value>0; else null. (Original bead promised this as if a basic column — it isn't; the script computes it. mesDistance carries posterior distances but is too sparse and inconsistent for a sample TSV.)
- absmag              float|null — derived: v_mag − 5*log10(distance_pc/10) when both exist; else null. (Original bead promised this as if a basic column — it isn't; the script computes it.)
- sp_type             str|null — basic.sp_type. Was "spectral_type"; actual column is "sp_type".
- otype               str — basic.otype (e.g. '*', 'PM*', 'EB*'; SIMBAD's hierarchical short code).

# Sample stratification

10k rows split as: 1000 with V < 6 (naked-eye bright), 3000 with 6 <= V < 9 (HIP completeness regime), 4000 with 9 <= V < 11.5 (Tycho-2 completeness), 2000 with 11.5 <= V < 15 (faint Tycho-Gaia merge). Stable seed = 20260515 so the sample is reproducible.

Live bin populations (filter: basic.otype IN (otypedef WHERE path LIKE '*%'); allfluxes.V NOT NULL):
  V in [-,    6.0): 5,056 stars  → target 1000  → fetch all (no server-side subsample needed)
  V in [6.0,  9.0): 125,047 stars → target 3000 → MOD(oid, K) subsample to ~9k candidates
  V in [9.0, 11.5): 1,414,045    → target 4000 → MOD(oid, K) subsample to ~12k candidates
  V in [11.5, 15.0): 2,321,952   → target 2000 → MOD(oid, K) subsample to ~6k candidates

Stars are identified via SIMBAD's otype hierarchy: query otypedef once (path LIKE '*%') to enumerate all star otype codes (~122 codes, ~720-char IN-clause). Note: SIMBAD's TAP does NOT support LIKE on basic.otype directly (live probe error: "LIKE not supported on otype !"), so the otypedef-precompute step is mandatory.

# Backend — first non-ESA, non-VizieR refresh script

SIMBAD TAP at https://simbad.cds.unistra.fr/simbad/sim-tap is its own service (not VizieR; not ESA). Add simbad_backend() to refresh_lib.py alongside esa_backend()/cds_backend() and pass backends=[rl.simbad_backend()] to TapClient — same single-backend-override pattern refresh-bailer-jones.py uses for cds_backend().

# Acceptance

- 10_000 rows exactly.
- gaia_source_id non-null for >=95% of rows overall (live probe: V<6 bin runs ~94%, fainter bins run ~96-97%; weighted total ~96%). NOT per-bin — V<6 stars hit Gaia's bright-end saturation cliff (e.g. Canopus is missing).
- Deterministic regen with seed=20260515 — re-running with the same seed produces a byte-identical TSV.

# Smoke

- npm run refresh:simbad writes the TSV
- Re-running with the same seed produces a byte-identical TSV
- Spot-check 5 random rows against SIMBAD's web UI

# Commit

One commit. Standard PR.

title: Phase 0 — Sweep v1.0 prose from CLAUDE.md, SCIENCE.md, docs/*.md
description:
Part of the source-ID-anchored catalogue-pipeline rewrite (see parent epic for architecture).

# Scope

Stellata is past v1.0. Remove all v1.0 / v1-era prose from project docs so future doc updates don't have to navigate around stale framing. Set by Alex 2026-05-15.

# Files + lines to update

Confirmed via grep on 2026-05-15:

- CLAUDE.md:252 — "Temporarily shelved (v1.0)" section heading
- CLAUDE.md:275 — "WASD / flight controls (removed after v1 review)"
- SCIENCE.md:77 — "Molecular cloud sources shelved for v1.0"
- SCIENCE.md:79 — "ellipsoid layer, which is committed but not rendered in v1.0"
- SCIENCE.md:187 — "for v1 this is documented and accepted"
- SCIENCE.md:449 — "In v1 t is pinned to now with no scrubber"
- SCIENCE.md:636 — "framings v1 affords"
- docs/chart-mode.md:28 — "v1.0 caveat"
- docs/chart-mode.md:30 — "Milky Way isobar is also disabled for v1.0"
- docs/molecular-clouds.md:3 — "Status (v1.0): Shelved"
- docs/molecular-clouds.md:13 — "in v1.0 the toggle is gone"
- docs/rendering.md:51 — "Molecular clouds (shelved v1.0)"
- docs/solar-system.md:24 — "v1 hardwires planets ⇔ Sol"
- docs/solar-system.md:61 — "v1 pins it to now"
- docs/solar-system.md:181 — "v1 places the"

# Replacement guidance

Where the prose describes a current limitation, rephrase to a tense-agnostic "currently" or "in the present implementation" form. Where it implies "will lift the constraint in v1.1+", rewrite to describe the constraint without the version anchor (state the limit, not the timeline). For "shelved" status (molecular clouds, MW isobar), keep the "shelved" status as a current fact.

# Acceptance

grep -rn 'v1.0\|until v1\|(v1)\|v1 review\|v1 affords\|v1 hardwires\|v1 pins\|v1 places' CLAUDE.md SCIENCE.md docs/ returns no hits.

# Smoke
