Sibling research bead to stellata-cns.1 (m11 vs m12 perf + first-load eval). cns.1 covers WHAT depth + render-budget for the deep-tier expansion; this bead covers WHICH ingest pipeline drives the rows at that depth — AT-HYG reduced_m11/m12 monolithic vs Gaia-native query vs hybrid. Both feed into the same parent (stellata-cns) and inform its implementation design.

# The choice

After stellata-dch lands, AT-HYG's value-add narrows by depth tier:

- **At classic_ids (~313k bright stars)** — AT-HYG genuinely earns its keep. It carries the curated brightness cut + HIP/HD/Bayer/Flamsteed/proper-name attribution + V-band Johnson photometry per row. None of those are trivially reproducible: HD/Bayer/Flamsteed/proper-name attribution requires a SIMBAD or Stellarium-style join.

- **At reduced_m11 (~800k) and reduced_m12 (~2M)** — most rows are Tycho-only or Gaia-only. Classical-ID attribution thins out (very few HD/Bayer/Flamsteed entries at apparent mag > 6.5). AT-HYG at that depth is effectively a pre-computed Gaia magnitude cut with the BP/RP→V transformation baked in. Both pieces are reproducible: one Gaia TAP query + the published Riello+ 2021 photometric transformation.

Critically, the stellata-dch architecture is source-ID-keyed end-to-end. Bailer-Jones distances, Apsis Teff/logg/A0, Gaia 5-parameter astrometry (with NSS / HIP2 fallbacks), and the LMC kinematic override all attach via gaia_source_id — irrespective of whether the row originated from AT-HYG or from a direct Gaia query. That makes a HYBRID pipeline architecturally cheap: AT-HYG classic_ids as the "labeled bright tier" + Gaia-native for the deep fill, both feeding the same source-ID-keyed refinement stack and the same v6/v7 binary output.

# Options to evaluate

1. **AT-HYG monolithic** — swap classic_ids → reduced_m11 or reduced_m12 (per cns.1's outcome). One ingest path. Loses the classical-ID attribution density advantage at the deep tier (because it's mostly absent there anyway) but keeps a single schema and a single refresh cadence.

2. **Hybrid** — keep AT-HYG classic_ids as the labeled bright tier (313k); add a Gaia-native row driver for stars beyond classic_ids' coverage up to the chosen magnitude floor. Two row drivers feeding one source-ID-keyed refinement stack.

3. **Gaia-native monolithic** — replace AT-HYG entirely with a Gaia query, reconstructing classical IDs from the committed cross-walks (gaia_dr3_hip_xmatch.tsv, gaia_dr3_tyc_xmatch.tsv) + SIMBAD or Stellarium for HD/Bayer/Flamsteed/proper-name. Largest engineering scope; lowest upstream-dependency surface.

# Tradeoffs to weigh

- **Identifier preservation**: HIP/HD/Bayer/Flamsteed/proper-name drives every label, hover, search, and constellation legend. Options 1 and 2 preserve trivially. Option 3 needs reconstruction via SIMBAD/Stellarium join — non-trivial.
- **V-band photometry**: AT-HYG carries Johnson V. Gaia gives BP/RP/G. Riello+ 2021 publishes the BP/RP→V transformation; star-physics.ts + the colour LUT (zsr) both consume V, so any Gaia-native row driver needs the transformation as a build step.
- **Storage / commit-data**: reduced_m12 CSV is ~400 MB (LFS), output binary ~80 MB. B-J subset for 2M rows is ~100-160 MB (LFS); Apsis similar. All tractable. Storage starts to bind closer to 30M+ rows (raw Gaia G<12 across the whole sky) — not at the cns scale.
- **Render budget**: 800k vs 2M instances is addressed in cns.1, independent of row-driver choice.
- **Refresh cadence**: AT-HYG releases bundle the BP/RP→V transformation + ID attribution. Gaia-native means refreshing those pieces independently — but the dch Phase 1 refresh-script pattern (scripts/refresh_lib.py + per-source refresh scripts) already handles this shape.
- **dch architectural fit**: all three options fit cleanly because dch's refinement stack is source-ID-keyed. Hybrid and Gaia-native are slightly "more dch-native"; AT-HYG-monolithic just uses AT-HYG's gaia field as the source_id link.

# Acceptance

Written recommendation in this bead's notes for the row-driver strategy stellata-cns implementation should follow. Should pick one of the three options (or a variant) with explicit reasoning. Verify the recommendation against [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] (the bar for catalogue choices) and [[stellata-prioritization-framework]] (deep-tier sits at queue position 7).

# Smoke / followup

Decision-bead — no implementation. The recommendation feeds back into stellata-cns's description; the implementation that follows is what's blocked on stellata-dch shipping (the source-ID-keyed refinement stack is the prerequisite that makes hybrid / Gaia-native cheap).

# Connects

- Sibling: stellata-cns.1 — m11 vs m12 perf + first-load eval. Orthogonal axis: cns.1 picks the depth, this bead picks the row driver.
- Architectural prerequisite: stellata-dch — source-ID-keyed refinement stack (B-J, Apsis, 5p astrometry, NSS, LMC kinematic) is what makes the hybrid option cheap to implement.
- Memory: [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]], [[stellata-prioritization-framework]].

title: LG luminosity — debug-panel tuning, URL flag, chart-mode handling
description:
# Scope

Wire user/dev surfaces around the LG luminosity layer:
debug-panel knobs, URL flag, chart-mode treatment. Blocked by both
shader beads landing.

# Deliverables

1. **`local-group-tuning.ts` section** in the debug panel — per-
   object emission-strength multiplier (for visual calibration in
   the field), global on/off, profile-family selector for spot-
   testing alternatives. Mirrors milkyway-tuning.ts structure.

2. **URL flag** — `lge=0` (Local Group emission) disables the
   layer. Add to url-state.ts envelope per docs/url-state.md
   process. Default-on; serialised only when non-default per the
   v3 envelope's presence-mask rule.

3. **Chart-mode handling** — decide between:
   (a) Hide the volumetric layer entirely (milkyway's pre-isobar
       choice), letting the existing wireframes carry the LG
       chart-mode aesthetic.
   (b) Emit an isobar contour pass (milkyway's current choice),
       drawing surface-brightness contours per object.
   Default to (a) for v1; the wireframes already read well in chart
   mode. Re-evaluate after first smoke.

4. **Picker / hover integration.** Confirm the existing
   `local-group` hover provider keeps working unchanged (it gates on
   the wireframe's visibility, which is unchanged). Add no new pick
   logic — the volumetric pass is decorative-emission on top of the
   wireframe silhouette which already drives picks.

# Smoke

Open debug panel → toggle LG emission off/on. Try `?lge=0` in URL,
confirm layer hides + URL round-trips. Switch to chart mode, confirm
volumetric layer hides per choice (a). Confirm hover cards still
appear on LG objects.

# Connects

Parent: [[stellata-gxx]]. Per [[stellata-lo5-hover-conventions]]
rule 2 — hover gating stays on visibility only, no additional
focus-gating introduced by this bead.

title: LG luminosity — apparent-magnitude calibration test across N viewpoints
description:
# Scope

Automated vitest verification that the rendered Local Group luminous
volumes integrate to the correct apparent V-magnitude from any
camera position, within ±0.1 mag tolerance. Blocked by both shader
beads landing.

# Deliverables

1. **`src/client/local-group/local-group-emission-calibration.test.ts`**
   — places a synthetic camera at N viewpoints:
   - Deep inside MW (e.g. galactic centre)
   - At the Sun
   - 30 kpc out of the disc (top of the galaxy)
   - Near LMC (50 kpc)
   - Between MW and M31 (400 kpc)
   - Beyond M33 (1 Mpc)

   For each viewpoint, for each of {LMC, SMC, M31, M33, one bright
   dSph}, sample the rendered offscreen framebuffer over the object's
   projected silhouette, integrate flux, convert back to apparent
   V-mag via the inverse of the shader's m_V conversion, and assert
   ≤ 0.1 mag deviation from the analytical prediction.

2. **Pinned numbers.** Per [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]]
   rule (b) — every catalog m_V value cited in the test asserts with
   `toBe(...)` against a fixed expected value, not `toBeCloseTo`
   with a wide tolerance. The ±0.1 mag tolerance applies to the
   RENDERED-vs-CATALOG comparison, not to the catalog values
   themselves.

3. **Pure helper.** The flux → m_V inverse lives in
   `local-group-emission-pure.ts` (lifted from any inline math in
   the shader-pair beads, per [[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]]
   rule 4 second-use trigger). Test-imported, never redefined.

# Smoke

`npm test` passes. CI catches regressions automatically.

# Connects

Parent: [[stellata-gxx]]. This is the GOAL of the epic — without
this test, the calibration claim is unverified.

title: LG luminosity — exponential-disc + Sérsic-bulge shader for M31/M33
description:
# Scope

Implement the volumetric exponential-disc + Sérsic-bulge composite
emission pass for M31 + M33. Blocked by the data-pipeline bead.
Can land in parallel with the spheroid-shader bead OR share that
shader's spheroid pass and add the disc-component on top.

# Deliverables

1. **Exponential-disc fragment integration** in
   `shaders/local-group-emission.frag.glsl` (or sibling
   `local-group-disc-emission.frag.glsl` if the two profiles diverge
   enough that one shader is the wrong abstraction). Profile:
   `density(R,z) = density0 · exp(-R/R_d) · exp(-|z|/h_z)` per
   milkyway's disc form. Per-instance uniforms: density0_disc, R_d,
   h_z, plus the spheroid params for the bulge component overlayed
   on the same mesh.

2. **Per-instance disc proxy mesh.** Flattened ellipsoid sized to
   ~10 R_d in plane, ~5 h_z in z, oriented per `local-group.json`'s
   disc orientation (i, PA) for M31 and M33.

3. **Composite emission.** Disc component + bulge Sérsic component
   add in fragment space. Total per-fragment emission converts to
   m_V via the shared magnitude-consistency path.

4. **Tests.** Per-instance read-back of disc params + bulge params.
   Profile integral check: closed-form exponential-disc total flux
   = 2π · density0 · R_d² for a face-on infinite disc; mesh-truncated
   integral should match to within the truncation residual.

# Smoke

Render M31 + M33 from:
- The Sun → confirm M31 reads at ~m_V 3.4 (visible bright extended
  glow ~3° across, matching naked-eye observation), M33 at ~5.7
  (perceptible but faint).
- A camera position halfway between MW and M31 → M31 should fill
  much more of the field.
- A camera inside M31's disc → brightness should rise to luminous-
  interior levels.

Defers tolerance verification to the calibration-test bead.

# Blocks

The calibration-test bead.

# Connects

Parent: [[stellata-gxx]]. If this and the spheroid-shader bead end up
sharing >50% of their bodies, fold per [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]]
rule 2 — the disc-component becomes a parameterised add-on to the
shared spheroid raymarch.

title: LG luminosity — Sérsic-spheroid raymarched shader + per-instance mesh
description:
# Scope

Implement the volumetric Sérsic-spheroid emission pass for LG objects
of the spheroid profile family: dSphs, M32, NGC 205, Sagittarius dSph,
LMC, SMC. Blocked by the data-pipeline bead.

# Deliverables

1. **`shaders/local-group-emission.{vert,frag}.glsl`** raymarched
   Sérsic-spheroid profile in mesh-local frame. Per-instance uniforms:
   density0, n_sersic, R_eff (parsec), axis ratios (a/b/c), population
   colour. Camera-inside-vs-outside ray split mirrors milkyway.
   Magnitude-consistency conversion at the fragment level is shared
   with milkyway (extract to a `magnitude-consistency.glsl` chunk if
   needed, otherwise duplicate per [[stellata-named-constants-and-dry]]
   rule 2's "extract a builder" trigger).

2. **`src/client/local-group/local-group-emission.ts`** owns the
   InstancedBufferGeometry, per-instance uniform block, mesh
   construction (proxy ellipsoid sized to enclose the profile out to
   ~10 R_eff). One mesh per object via instancing.

3. **Integration with `local-group.ts`** — the emission layer is a
   sibling renderer to the wireframe layer (not a replacement). Both
   read from the same loaded `LocalGroupObject` array. The volumetric
   layer adds at additive blend; the wireframe sits at low opacity on
   top.

4. **Tests.** `local-group-emission.test.ts` exercises:
   - Instance buffer population (read-back of per-instance density0,
     n, R_eff from a known set of objects).
   - Dispose path clears all instanced geometry + uniform buffers
     ([[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] rule 1).
   - Profile integral check via a pure helper that mirrors the GLSL
     raymarch on CPU for one canonical sample.

# Smoke

Render LMC + SMC + M32 + Sagittarius + 1-2 dSphs from a few viewpoints.
Visually confirm:
- Each object has brightness perceptible at its catalog m_V at the
  Sun.
- Brightness rises as the camera approaches.
- Profile shape (centrally peaked for high n; flat-core for low n)
  reads in the silhouette.

Defers full apparent-magnitude correctness verification to the
calibration-test bead.

# Blocks

The calibration-test bead (must run after BOTH shader beads land).

# Connects

Parent: [[stellata-gxx]]. Per
[[stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions]] rule 1 — files live
in src/client/local-group/. Per rule 3 — the emission layer is its
own controller (state + tick + dispose), not bolted onto
local-group.ts.

title: LG luminosity — extend build pipeline + overrides for photometry & profile params
description:
# Scope

Plumb per-object photometric + profile data through the LG build
pipeline so the renderer has what it needs. Blocked by the design-gate
bead's per-object data plan.

# Deliverables

1. **Extend `scripts/build-local-group.ts`** to surface from LVDB:
   - `apparent_magnitude_v` (and `_em`, `_ep`)
   - `rad_sersic`, `n_sersic` (with units → parsec at object distance)
   - `surface_brightness_rhalf` (mag/arcsec²)
   - `rhalf_physical` (parsec) as the fallback half-light radius when
     Sérsic params are absent
   - `ellipticity`, `position_angle` (already used by wireframe, may
     need surfacing if not already in the loader output)

2. **Extend `data/local-group/overrides.tsv`** schema with optional
   columns for hand-curated photometry on standalone rows (M31/M33)
   and override columns for LVDB rows where the dwarf_all summary is
   too coarse:
   - `apparent_magnitude_v_override`
   - `profile_family` (`sersic` | `exp_disc_sersic_bulge`)
   - `disc_scale_length_pc` (exp-disc family only)
   - `bulge_re_pc`, `bulge_n` (exp-disc family only)
   - `population_color_rgb` (optional, per-object population tint —
     defaults to a neutral if absent)
   Populate M31 + M33 photometry: M31 m_V ≈ 3.4, M33 m_V ≈ 5.7,
   M31 disc R_d ≈ 5.3 kpc, M33 R_d ≈ 1.8 kpc. Cite paper DOIs in
   `ref_doi` column.

3. **Offline DENSITY0 solver** runs as a build step (in
   `scripts/build-local-group.ts` or a sibling `solve-densities.ts`):
   given per-object catalog m_V + distance + profile geometry, solve
   the per-object density normalisation that makes the analytical
   profile integral equal the catalog flux. Implementation mirrors
   the closed-form result in docs/milky-way.md § Magnitude
   consistency. Result is written into `local-group.json` as a per-
   object `density0` field consumed by the shader.

4. **Schema update + tests.** `scripts/build-local-group-pure.ts`
   gains test coverage for the photometry → density0 solver
   (closed-form check on a known Sérsic profile, vs an exponential
   disc, vs M31's documented parameters). Per
   [[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] rule (b), pin numeric
   solver outputs with `toBe(N)`.

# Smoke

Regen local-group.json + spot-check 5 entries (LMC, SMC, M31, M33,
one ultra-faint dwarf) — confirm density0 + profile_family + V-mag
fields are present and within expected ranges.

# Blocks

The Sérsic-spheroid shader + exponential-disc shader beads.

# Connects

Parent: [[stellata-gxx]]. Per [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]]
the solver must work for ANY LVDB row, not a famous-star carve-out.
Per [[frozen-external-data]] no live fetches.

title: Ingest Gaia FLAME stellar ages + masses when DR4 releases
description:
Ingest Gaia FLAME (Final Luminosity Age Mass Estimator) per-star age + mass estimates from `gaiadrN.astrophysical_parameters` for the AT-HYG.gaia source-id subset, so future Stellata work has age as a per-star attribute alongside Teff / logg / [M/H].

# Why deferred to DR4 (not DR3)

FLAME ages in DR3 are produced by isochrone-fitting on Apsis-derived (Teff, L, [M/H]). For MS field stars (the bulk of Stellata's catalogue) the uncertainty is ±2-4 Gyr typical — too noisy to drive any honest user-visible visualisation. Turnoff stars and giants are tighter, but they're a minority of what we render.

DR4 substantially improves FLAME because:
- Longer astrometric baseline → tighter parallaxes → tighter luminosity.
- Apsis pipeline upgrades feed cleaner (Teff, logg, [M/H]) inputs to FLAME.
- Expanded epoch photometry → some asteroseismic enhancement for evolved stars.

Per ESA's published roadmap, DR4 FLAME posteriors are expected to narrow significantly relative to DR3. Not worth a separate one-shot DR3 ingest only to redo it on DR4 release.

# Origin

Filed out of the `stellata-zsr.1` star-colour-calibration research (`research/star-spectral-rendition/RECOMMENDATION.md` + apsis_coverage probe). The Apsis empirical coverage work made it concrete that FLAME columns (age_flame, mass_flame, plus uncertainties) sit in the same `astrophysical_parameters` table as Apsis Teff/logg, so the ingest mechanics are largely shared with the Apsis Teff ingest filed separately under stellata-zsr.

# Scope (revisit at un-defer time)

- New `scripts/refresh-gaia-flame.py` querying `gaiadrN.astrophysical_parameters` for FLAME columns by source_id list. Reuse the shared TAP client (will exist by then — currently in flight as stellata-dch.21).
- Output: `data/gaia_drN_flame.tsv` (LFS, ~10-15 MB for ~300k rows × ~6 columns).
- Optional v6+ binary surfacing: add `age` (float16, ±1 Gyr precision) and `mass` (float16) per record. Whether to bake into the binary or load as a side table keyed by source_id is a runtime-vs-bundle-size call at un-defer time.
- Acceptance: per-star age + uncertainty pinned for AT-HYG.gaia rows where FLAME returns. Coverage stats logged. Smoke on a known-age population (Pleiades members ~110 Myr; Sol ~4.6 Gyr) confirms returned ages are within expected bounds.

# Downstream uses (unlocked by, not required by, this ingest)

- Age-based representational layer (artificial-viz filter under stellata-io3).
- Mass refinement for the Stefan-Boltzmann physical-radius chain (currently uses spectral-class-only Teff for non-WD stars).
- Stellar population age maps for Local Group / future galactic-arm visualisation.

# Why NOT under stellata-dch

User-stated decision (Alex, 2026-05-18): keep this separate from the dch source-ID-anchored pipeline rewrite. dch's scope is astrometry + cross-walks + NSS + Bailer-Jones; FLAME ages are a downstream science-product ingest that rides naturally on AT-HYG refresh timing rather than on the catalogue-architecture rewrite.

# Why parent stellata-3f8

stellata-3f8 ("Refresh AT-HYG (or replace) when Gaia DR4 releases") is the umbrella for DR4-gated catalog work. FLAME ingest is naturally a child of that umbrella — when DR4 lands, refresh AT-HYG AND pull FLAME products in the same release cycle.

title: Display P3 wide-gamut output for star colour (zsr.1 Tier 3)
description:
Part of `stellata-zsr.1` research outcome — Tier 3 follow-on.

# Why

`stellata-zsr.1` shipped a Planck + CIE 1931 → sRGB blackbody pipeline
for star colours (Tier 1, separate bead). Alex asked whether Display P3
output on supporting devices would buy additional fidelity. Empirical
finding in the research probe:

- Display P3 and sRGB share the **same blue primary** (DCI-P3 blue is
  (0.15, 0.06), identical to sRGB blue) — there is NO gamut win at
  the hot blue end (O/B stars) where I initially expected one.
- The Planckian locus stays inside both gamuts for all T > ~1700 K.
  Stellata's catalog floor is ~3000 K (Proxima at 3170 K is the
  coolest commonly-displayed star). So the per-star LUT sits
  comfortably inside sRGB.
- See `research/star-spectral-rendition/p3_swatches.png` (P3-ICC-tagged):
  only the synthetic 1500 K row shows a perceptible delta. Every
  actual reference star in our catalog renders identically on P3
  vs sRGB monitors.

So Tier 3 is **explicitly NOT a colour-fidelity win for blackbody stars.**
File it anyway because:

1. The infrastructure (canvas colour-space negotiation, dual-LUT
   pattern, gamut-aware shader audit) is a generic prerequisite for
   any future wide-gamut work in Stellata (nebula emission lines —
   strong Hα ~ 0.708xy, well outside sRGB; planet auroral colours;
   chart-mode legend chips with saturated UI palettes).
2. Documenting "P3 was investigated and found to be a no-op for the
   current renderer" prevents future sessions from re-investigating.

# What to do

Build the wide-gamut output path so that it's available the day
non-Planckian saturated colours appear in the renderer. The work itself:

1. **Device detection** — `window.matchMedia('(color-gamut: p3)').matches`
   + optional override via `?gamut=p3` URL param for QA.
2. **Canvas colour-space switch** — request P3 framebuffer:
     `renderer.getContext().getContextAttributes()` to confirm + set
     `renderer.outputColorSpace = THREE.DisplayP3ColorSpace` on detect.
   Fallback to `SRGBColorSpace` otherwise. Requires Three.js r152+
   (currently shipped in this project per package.json).
3. **Dual LUT emit** — extend the Tier 1 LUT generator
   (`scripts/blackbody-lut.ts`) to emit both `blackbody-lut-srgb.bin`
   and `blackbody-lut-p3.bin`. Single Python helper, two output paths
   (mirrors `research/star-spectral-rendition/blackbody_color.py`'s
   `XYZ_TO_LIN_SRGB` + `XYZ_TO_LIN_P3` factoring).
4. **Texture colour-space audit** — every texture sampled in the
   star/planet/cloud/dust shaders must carry the correct
   `texture.colorSpace`. Currently most are sRGB-encoded — moving to
   a P3 canvas without retagging inputs causes subtle hue shifts.
   Scope: enumerate every `new THREE.*Texture()` callsite.
5. **Additive-blend audit** — additive blending must happen in LINEAR
   space, not gamma-encoded. Current shaders do some math in
   gamma-encoded space (super-Gaussian intensity is sRGB-encoded then
   summed). Going wide-gamut without auditing causes hue drift where
   multiple star glows overlap. Audit `star.frag.glsl`,
   `planet.frag.glsl`, `cloud.frag.glsl`, `dust-particle.frag.glsl`,
   `perceptual-disc.glsl`.
6. **Bloom / post-process** — if any post-processing passes exist,
   confirm they operate in linear-light and round-trip through the
   canvas colour space correctly. No bloom shipped today; flag for
   future.
7. **Test** — visual smoke on a P3 monitor (Alex has one):
   `npm run dev`, focus a saturated chart-mode glyph or a future
   nebula layer, verify the gamut expansion is visible. Compare
   against sRGB-only viewing (Chrome Incognito on a non-P3 display,
   or a forced-sRGB DevTools toggle).

# Files likely touched

- `scripts/blackbody-lut.ts` (new in Tier 1) — emit two LUTs.
- `src/client/star-pipeline.ts` (or wherever Tier 1 lands the LUT
  load) — runtime branch on detected gamut.
- `src/client/stellata.ts` — `renderer.outputColorSpace` set on init.
- `src/client/shaders/*.glsl` — audit additive-blend math + texture
  decode operations.
- `SCIENCE.md` — document the P3 path in the colour-rendering section.
- `docs/rendering.md` — render-stack section gets a "colour-space"
  paragraph naming the choice (P3 on detect, sRGB otherwise).

# Acceptance

- LUT generator emits both `*-srgb.bin` and `*-p3.bin` artefacts;
  the P3 variant is byte-validated against the
  `research/star-spectral-rendition/blackbody_color.py`
  `blackbody_to_displayp3` reference (vitest assertion).
- Canvas colour space is observed via `renderer.getContext()` on a
  P3 monitor; observed value is `'display-p3'`.
- Every texture in the scene has an explicit `colorSpace` field set;
  no defaults.
- Manual smoke (Alex): the chart-mode legend chips and (if shipped)
  any nebula emission renders show visibly more saturated chroma on
  a P3 monitor vs forced-sRGB. Per-star blackbodies render IDENTICALLY
  in both — that's the expected no-op outcome.

# Out of scope

- HDR (Display P3 is wide gamut but standard dynamic range; HDR
  output would be a separate epic).
- BT.2020 / Rec2100 — wider than P3, no broad device support yet.

# Connects

- `stellata-zsr.1` — research bead that surfaced this work
- The Tier 1 blackbody LUT bead (filed separately under `stellata-zsr`) — must land first; this
  bead extends its LUT generator + load path.
- `stellata-data-fidelity-principle` — "best possible model based on
  current observational data" implies using the best display path
  available too, not just the best data source.

title: Gaia-DR3-aware filter for CCDM-flagged optical doubles (Albireo, etc.)
description:
# Problem

`applyDoublesFlag` in `scripts/build-catalog.ts` sets the chart-mode binary wings glyph on AT-HYG rows where the Hipparcos CCDM cross-match (`data/hip_ccdm.tsv`) lists a CCDM ID **and** Hipparcos's `MultFlag ∈ {C, G, O}`. This rule is pre-Gaia: it trusts Hipparcos's own multiplicity confidence call, which was made on positional / photometric evidence in the 1990s without parallax precision below ~mas.

Gaia DR2 (2018) and DR3 have since revealed that some CCDM/MultFlag-flagged pairs are **optical doubles**, not gravitationally bound systems. The components sit along nearly the same line of sight from Sol but are at very different distances. The discovered case is Albireo (β¹ + β² Cyg), where both rows have Gaia DR3 distances:

- β¹ Cyg (HIP 95947, AT-HYG `proper="Albireo"`): 111.34 pc
- β² Cyg (HIP 95951, AT-HYG `proper="Albireo B"`): 122.11 pc
- 3D separation: **10.77 pc / 35.1 ly** — too distant to be gravitationally bound by orders of magnitude.

Current behaviour: chart mode draws the binary wings glyph on Albireo, asserting binarity that current astronomy retracted around 2018. This contradicts the project's data-fidelity principle. Similar misflags likely exist on a small handful of other culturally significant CCDM/MultFlag-confirmed pairs where Gaia later split the distances.

# Proposed fix

Add a Gaia-aware suppression step to `applyDoublesFlag`:

1. When BOTH components of a CCDM-flagged system have **Gaia-derived distances** available in AT-HYG (`dist_src ∈ {G_R3, BJ, LMC_KIN}` — the `BJ` value is introduced by Bead Z / dch.47 (Bailer-Jones distance override); the `LMC_KIN` value is introduced by Bead AA / stellata-8l0 (LMC kinematic refinement); both extend the dist_src enum). Compute the 3D separation in pc.
2. If the 3D separation exceeds a "definitely-not-bound" threshold (suggested: **1.0 pc**, well above the widest known bound systems at ~1 pc with multi-million-year orbits), suppress the wings glyph for that system — do not OR `0x10` onto the primary's flags.
3. Preserve current behaviour when either component lacks a Gaia-derived distance (fall back to trusting Hipparcos's MultFlag, as today).

This is the natural place to fit it into the source-ID-anchored rewrite — Gaia-derived distances are authoritative for the new Layer-1 data set, so the 3D-separation check is a one-line addition once the cross-walk is in place. The predicate accepts ALL Gaia-derived distance values (raw `G_R3`, Bayesian-refined `BJ`, kinematic-refined `LMC_KIN`) so the filter stays correct as new refinement layers are added.

# Acceptance

- Albireo's chart-mode binary wings glyph no longer fires (β¹/β² 3D separation of 10.77 pc exceeds the 1.0 pc cut).
- True wide binaries within the cut survive — Mizar+Alcor (0.21 pc / 0.68 ly), ε¹+ε² Lyr (0.29 pc / 0.94 ly), α Cen A+B (~0 pc within catalogue precision) all keep their glyphs.
- Predicate uses `dist_src ∈ {G_R3, BJ, LMC_KIN}` so it stays correct after Beads Z + AA land.
- The suppressed-glyph set should be auditable: surface in a build-log line or a small artefact so each removal is reviewable against the literature, rather than silent.

# Open question

Whether to *also* introduce a "this looks like an optical double, not a binary" UI affordance for the rare suppressed cases. Probably out of scope for this issue — discuss in design before adding.

# Discovered from

PR #78 (README rewrite, "Things to try" section). When verifying that Albireo's two AT-HYG rows render as separate stars, noticed they're at very different distances; the modern post-Gaia consensus is that Albireo is an optical pair, not a binary.

# Out of scope

- Generalising to non-CCDM-anchored binary inference (geometric pass, etc.) — separate concern.
- Building a full "optical double catalogue" with affordances — see open question above.
- Refreshing CCDM itself or replacing MultFlag with Gaia NSS — the broader source-ID rework that parent epic already covers.

title: Manual-override path for AT-HYG-dropped culturally-important stars (Mu Cep / Garnet Star, etc.)
description:
# Problem

Some culturally-important stars are entirely absent from the model because AT-HYG's upstream row for them lacks the fields the preprocessor requires (`x0`, `y0`, `z0`, `absmag`). The preprocessor drops these rows in `scripts/build-catalog.ts`, so they never appear in `catalog.bin` or `search-index.json`. Searching by HIP, HD, Bayer, or proper name all return nothing — the star isn't there.

# Known case

**μ Cephei (Herschel's Garnet Star).** HIP 107259 / HD 206936. AT-HYG's row carries `bayer=Mu`, `con=Cep`, `proper=Garnet Star`, but every distance / position / magnitude field is empty (`dist_src=N`, indicating "no distance available"). Distance to μ Cep is genuinely hard — historically uncertain Hipparcos parallax, and Gaia DR3 saturates on bright cool supergiants — but ground-truth values from the literature exist (~870 pc; absmag around -7).

# Likely other cases

Other bright, well-known cool/red supergiants are exposed to the same Gaia-DR3-saturation failure mode and may be similarly dropped. Worth a one-off audit: count AT-HYG rows that have a `proper` or `bayer` field but no `dist`. A handful of those are likely culturally important and recoverable.

# Proposed fix

Mirror the existing `data/local-group/overrides.tsv` pattern: a small whitelist of stars where the maintainer supplies distance + absmag + (optionally) spectral class from primary literature, merged in at preprocessing time after the AT-HYG read but before the drop filter. Each override row cites its source paper / DOI to preserve the data-fidelity principle (SCIENCE.md).

Suggested shape:

- `data/star-overrides.tsv` — columns: `hip`, `dist_pc`, `absmag`, `spect` (optional), `source_doi`, `notes`.
- `scripts/build-catalog.ts`: load the overrides table after the AT-HYG read; for any HIP in both the AT-HYG and the overrides tables, fill in missing `dist`/`x0`/`y0`/`z0`/`absmag`/`spect` from the override (recomputing Cartesians from the AT-HYG ra/dec + override distance). Rows present in overrides but not in AT-HYG are skipped (not in our scope).
- SCIENCE.md: new subsection under "Stellar catalog ingestion" or "Modelling decisions deliberately not made" documenting the override path and listing the entries.

# Acceptance

- μ Cep renders in the model, findable by "Mu Cep", "Garnet Star", HIP 107259, and HD 206936.
- Override entries are cited with primary literature DOIs in SCIENCE.md.
- Override table is small (single-digit entries to start) and grows by explicit maintainer decision, not by sweep.

# Out of scope

- Refreshing AT-HYG when Gaia DR4 lands (separate concern; DR4 may resolve some of these natively).
- Adding stars that have no AT-HYG row at all.
- Catalogue-wide reprocessing or filling in absmag for all distance-missing rows (over-aggressive; would pollute the model with bad data).

# Discovered from

Curating the "Things to try" section in PR #78 (README rewrite); paired with stellata-tqh (GCVS-search-index gap).

title: Add GCVS variable-star designations to search-index.json
description:
# Problem

Variable stars whose primary cultural name is their GCVS designation (R CrB, T Tau, VY CMa, χ Cyg, RR Lyr, SS Cyg, RS Oph, ZZ Cet, etc. — anything from the GCVS letter sequence R, S, T … RR, RS … through ZZ then AA … QZ) are findable in the app only via HIP / HD numeric lookup. Searching by the familiar GCVS name returns nothing.

Discovered while curating a "Things to try" list for the README: tried to point users at VY Canis Majoris by name, found that "VY CMa" / "VY Canis Majoris" / "VY" all return no result because AT-HYG carries no `proper`, `bayer`, or `flam` for the row — only `hip=35793` / `hd=58061`.

# Why this happens

The GCVS cross-match in `scripts/build-catalog.ts` already attaches `period` and `mag_amplitude` to each AT-HYG row by looking up the row's HIP (then HD) in `crossid.txt` and pulling the matching `gcvs5.txt` row. The GCVS name is the lookup key for that join, but it is currently consumed and discarded — it never gets emitted into `search-index.json`.

# Proposed fix

Carry the GCVS designation through the cross-match into the search index as an additional indexed alias for the AT-HYG row. Likely smallest change:

- `scripts/build-catalog.ts`: when the GCVS cross-match succeeds, attach the GCVS designation (e.g. `"VY CMa"`, `"R CrB"`) onto the in-memory star record alongside the existing period / amplitude.
- `scripts/build-catalog.ts` (search-index emit path): include the GCVS name as an additional searchable string for that star, alongside `proper`, `bayer`, `flam`, `hip`, `hd`, `hr`, `gl`.
- Typeahead: should match the new field automatically if the search layer iterates over all string fields of each row — verify, no shader / renderer changes needed.

# Acceptance

- Searching `VY CMa`, `R CrB`, `T Tau`, `χ Cyg`, `RR Lyr` in the app resolves to the corresponding underlying AT-HYG row.
- Mixed case / spacing tolerated as elsewhere (`vy cma`, `R Coronae Borealis` if the full GCVS form is stored — TBD whether to expand or keep abbreviated).
- ~3,700 GCVS-cross-matched stars become findable by their GCVS name.

# Out of scope

- Adding ALL GCVS catalogue entries (only the ones successfully cross-matched into AT-HYG; non-cross-matched variables stay invisible).
- Resolving GCVS-named stars that don't match any AT-HYG row (those are still unaddressable).

# Discovered from

Curating the "Things to try" section in PR #78 (README rewrite).

title: Doc migration from memory grooming (blocked on 9mm.194)
description:
Migrate canonical content from 6 bd memories to docs / SCIENCE.md per the durable-principles-in-codebase-not-memory rule established with Alex 2026-05-16. Blocked until 9mm.194 closes — Alex 2026-05-16: "all PRs are blocked until that epic is done." Memory grooming session 2026-05-16 drafted the migrations in worktree `memory-grooming-doc-migration`; worktree exited without committing per [[alex-never-push-to-main]] (bd-only sessions don't open PRs).

# Why deferred

The grooming-pass session started doc edits in CLAUDE.md (new "Folder & module conventions" section) and in SCIENCE.md (new "Scope principles" section). The CLAUDE.md edits collide directly with 9mm.194.2's stated deliverable ("CLAUDE.md gets 'Folder & module conventions' section"). The folder-roster updates that 9mm.194.1 ships also touch CLAUDE.md's repo-layout block. Even non-conflicting doc edits (SCIENCE.md, docs/build-and-data.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/hover.md) are gated by the all-PRs-blocked rule, so the whole set defers.

# The migration plan — mechanical after 194 closes

Each step lifts content from a memory into a permanent home, then bd-forgets the memory. Each is ~one topical commit per [[commit-granularity]]. Do all five in one PR with skip-version-bump label (pure docs).

## 1. docs/hover.md (NEW FILE)

Source: `bd memories stellata-lo5-hover-conventions`.

Content shape:
- Brief architecture overview: hover-engine.ts (owns pointer listener, 280ms dwell, 14px pick threshold, disambiguator); hover-types.ts (HoverProvider contract: pick + format); hover-pick-disambiguator.ts (camera-distance + prime/fallback tiering); *-hover-provider.ts (one per layer); formatters/*-hover-format.ts (one per layer, pure with vitest coverage).
- Rule 1 — Spell out unit/quantity terms (Radius, Period, Vmag verbatim).
- Rule 1a — Line ordering: name → distance+Vmag → Period → Radius (observer-relative first, intrinsic below).
- Rule 2 — Visibility ⇒ hoverable; no focus-gate on hover. Provider gate mirrors renderer draw predicate. No focus/selection/mode/route state in the gating.
- Rule 3 — Whole-object hit surface for extended visible objects. Three reusable pick mechanisms: Three.js raycast against mesh (clouds), projected sample-point AABB (heliopause via shared HELIOPAUSE_SAMPLE_POINTS_LOCAL), per-object angular-size disc (LG). Hit-test SVG label bbox when present, pulling element id from shared exported constant.
- Rule 4 — HTML hover-card typography stays var(--font-mono) even in chart mode. Chart-mode body.monochrome .tooltip CSS swaps bg/color only, not font-family. The boundary is HTML-vs-SVG, not chart-vs-not-chart.

Then add doc-index entry in CLAUDE.md (under docs/overlays.md):
- "**docs/hover.md** — hover-label engine, per-layer providers and formatters, and the four UX conventions (spell out units, no focus-gate on hover, whole-object hit surface for extended objects, HTML monospace typography in chart mode). Read when touching anything in src/client/hover/ or adding a hover surface to a new layer."

Then: `bd forget stellata-lo5-hover-conventions`.

## 2. SCIENCE.md § Scope principles (NEW section, right after intro before "## Data sources")

Sources: `bd memories stellata-data-fidelity-principle`, `bd memories stellata-cds-data-sources-and-detail-gradient`, `bd memories stellata-defer-detail-until-zoom-affordance`.

Three subsections under one umbrella "Scope principles" heading:

a. **Data fidelity — "best possible model based on current observational data and knowledge"** — six rules: default to upfront generalised solution not famous-star carve-out + heuristic fallback; prefer official source-ID cross-walks (HIP, Gaia, NSS, SIMBAD) over position-based matching; hand-curated overrides only for singular edge cases or catalog-construction omissions (M31/M33 in LVDB dwarf_all); prefer ship-more-accurate-later for catalog work; all matching must survive catalog upgrades; validation at scale (parallel automated check, not 5-star spot-check).

b. **Detail gradient — highest-density measurable info near Earth, simpler model further out** — per-object near, statistical far. When scoping a layer beyond AT-HYG reach, prefer statistical / aggregate sources (HiPS-derived counts, binned populations) over hand-extending per-object data. Cross-references: bd memory stellata-prioritization-framework (closer-to-Sol layer queue); bd issue stellata-36y (CDS/VizieR ecosystem orientation).

c. **Defer detail until zoom affordance** — billboarded discs sized θ = 2·atan(R/d) floor at pixel-size minimum at any host-relative camera distance more than a few thousand body-radii. Coding detail before the user can perceive it is wasted effort. File per-object detail (textures, banding, haloes, ring systems, exoplanet bodies) under the relevant zoom-in epic (stellata-2f6 for planets, stellata-bk5.3 for exoplanet bodies); ship the layer without it.

Then: `bd forget stellata-data-fidelity-principle stellata-cds-data-sources-and-detail-gradient stellata-defer-detail-until-zoom-affordance`.

(NOTE: SCIENCE.md already references all three by name at lines ~414, 419, 598. After the migration, those references resolve to in-doc anchors instead of memory keys — update them in the same PR.)

## 3. docs/build-and-data.md § Frozen external data (NEW section)

Source: `bd memories frozen-external-data`.

Content: external scientific catalogs in the pipeline are committed under data/ and read from disk at build time. No requests.get / urllib / astroquery / fetch in npm run build or the Python preprocessor. Why: build keeps working when sources go offline / change schemas / move URLs. Existing pattern (AT-HYG, GCVS, Stellarium, Edenhofer, LVDB, Hipparcos CCDM) reflects this. Adding new external data: fetch once, commit raw file under data/ (LFS if >1 MB), document source URL + retrieval date in SCIENCE.md, build scripts read from disk only, fetch helpers named explicitly (scripts/refresh-*.py) and gated from npm run build.

Then: `bd forget frozen-external-data`.

## 4. docs/architecture.md § Authoring patterns — consistency at the seam (NEW section, append at end)

Source: `bd memories stellata-consistency-at-the-seam`.

Four shape subsections:

a. **Lifecycle pairing** — every long-lived resource has its teardown wired in the SAME diff that introduces it. bus.on() returns unsub stored on dispose; pool/buffer growth has hard cap or shrinkIfIdle; subarray views across method boundary document lifetime. Example: EventBus had no clear(), cross-session subscriptions leaked.

b. **Sibling symmetry** — sibling functions/helpers/branches (lambertian vs mallama, encode vs decode, v2 vs v3 schema, prime vs fallback picker) must clamp/assert/log identically or document the asymmetry. Example: mallamaPhaseFactor didn't clamp α while lambertianPhaseFactor did.

c. **Sentinel-init for dirty-track** — sentinel initial value MUST fail comparison on first write (NaN, -Infinity, poison-string). Hide/dispose/reset paths reset every numeric sentinel and cached input. Cache keys include every input dimension. Example: pointerEvents = "" sentinel matched steady-state, first-frame write was skipped.

d. **Single source of truth for time / camera state / world offset** — code needing wall-clock t reads via Stellata.getT(), never Date.now(). Mid-animation state mutations either make the struct frame-coherent or document an explicit phase invariant. Example: PlanetBodyField.attachHost called Date.now()/1000 instead of getT().

Closing: when to apply. Write-time rules, not review-time. When adding bus.on, find dispose path of same file in same diff. When implementing one of a sibling pair, copy-skim the sibling. When adding a sentinel, write the first-write assertion explicitly.

Then: `bd forget stellata-consistency-at-the-seam`.

## 5. stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions (handled by 194.2)

Already 9mm.194.2's stated deliverable. After 194.2 ships AND CLAUDE.md "Folder & module conventions" section contains all 5 rules (not just 1-3), `bd forget stellata-folder-and-controller-conventions`. Audit before forgetting: confirm rules 4 (pure helpers extract at second use) and 5 (no multi-paragraph in-code prose) are in the CLAUDE.md section — those are the rules the memory says it carries in full vs CLAUDE.md's short form.

# Acceptance

- Five doc edits land (one PR, five topical commits per [[commit-granularity]]).
- skip-version-bump label set; release-notes-guard CI passes.
- Six memories forgotten (1 → 1; 2 → 3; 3 → 1; 4 → 1; 5 → 1 by 194.2; total 7 forgotten across this bead + 194.2).
- bd memories count drops from 29 → 22 (verify via `bd memories | head -5` or memory count in bd prime header).
- SCIENCE.md cross-references at lines ~414, 419, 598 updated from memory keys to in-doc anchors.

# Smoke

After each `bd forget`, search the target doc for the key phrase from the dropped memory to confirm the concept is reachable. E.g. after forgetting stellata-lo5-hover-conventions, `grep -i 'vmag\|visibility ⇒ hoverable' docs/hover.md` should hit.

# Connects

- [[stellata-9mm.194]] — hard blocker; 194's CLAUDE.md edits must land first.
- [[stellata-9mm.194.2]] — handles rule 5 above (folder/controller migration).
- [[bd-data-grooming-protocol]] — this bead is the carry-over from a memory-grooming pass.
- [[defer-doc-updates]] — five doc additions ride one coherent PR, not piecemeal.
- [[alex-never-push-to-main]] — the bd-only-session exit rule that aborted the grooming-session worktree.

title: Hover-engine misc hygiene: unused params + CSS-tied magic numbers
description:
# Findings from PR #72 (stellata-lo5) review — bundle of two nits

## (1) Unused \`_pixelThreshold\` in heliopause + cloud Stellata pickers

\`Stellata.pickHeliopauseHit(clientX, clientY, _pixelThreshold = 14)\`
and \`Stellata.pickCloudHit(clientX, clientY, _pixelThreshold = 14)\`
accept a pxThreshold the engine threads through, but neither uses it
(heliopause runs a silhouette-bbox + label-rect test; cloud
delegates to raycast). Underscore prefix marks intentional but
shadows the engine-contract signature.

Either:
- Drop the param from the heliopause / cloud public signatures (and
  matching providers stop forwarding it), or
- Document why the param is ignored (one-line comment in each method).

## (2) \`TOOLTIP_MAX_HEIGHT_PX = 96\` not tied to CSS

\`hover-engine.ts:29\` clamps the tooltip's bottom edge to
\`window.innerHeight - 96\`, where 96 is a hand-calibrated worst-case
height for "5 lines + name". The constant has no programmatic tie to
\`styles.css\` — tweaks to tooltip line-height / padding silently drift
the clamp.

Fix: derive at runtime via \`getBoundingClientRect()\` on the rendered
tooltip (one frame's worth of measure-then-position) so the clamp
follows whatever the CSS produces.

# Why

Both are minor — neither user-visible at v1.0. Bundled because both
sit in the same file and the same code-quality theme. P3 unless
either becomes blocking.

# Acceptance

- One commit per sub-task, or a single combined commit if both stay
  small.

title: Tighten HoverPayload contract — providers return null on degenerate
description:
# Finding from PR #72 (stellata-lo5) review `hover-engine.ts` calls `winner.provider.format(winner.hit)` and renders the returned `{ name, lines }` directly. The disambiguator guards `winner === null` (no provider hit) but NOT `winner.provider.format(...)` returning a degenerate payload. Planet, Local Group, cloud, and star formatters currently all emit `{ name: '', lines: [] }` on degenerate inputs (\`!planet\`, \`!obj\`, \`!cloud\`, etc.). If that path were ever reached, the engine would render an empty card (\`
\`) at the cursor. # Scope Two clean options, pick one: (a) Tighten the contract: `HoverProvider.format` returns \`HoverPayload | null\`. Engine guards \`null\` payload alongside the existing null-winner check. All four formatters change \`return { name: '', lines: [] }\` to \`return null\`. (b) Have the engine guard \`!payload.name && payload.lines.length === 0\` explicitly. Formatters keep their current signature. (a) is the principled fix — null is the right shape for "no card warranted". (b) is one line in the engine. # Why [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] Rule 1 (lifecycle pairing): the provider's "valid hit" contract should match the engine's render gate. Today the contract is silently looser on the format side. # Acceptance - One of (a) or (b) lands; the empty-card path is provably unreachable. - The disambiguator test covers a "format returns null" case (or whichever shape the chosen option uses).

title: Export + test phaseFactorFor in planet-body-field
description:
# Finding from PR #72 (stellata-lo5) review

`phaseFactorFor` is a pure helper at file-scope in
`src/client/planet-body-field.ts:192` but isn't exported, so it can't
be unit-tested. It dispatches between Mallama (when a polynomial
exists) and Lambertian (the default fallback for Pluto + exoplanets)
mirroring the planet vertex shader's branch.

# Scope

1. Export `phaseFactorFor` (or extract to a `*-pure.ts` sibling).
2. Add vitest coverage:
   - Pluto (no Mallama coefs, falls through to Lambertian) — verify
     the branch by passing `coefs: undefined` and checking against
     a `lambertianPhaseFactor` direct call.
   - One body with Mallama coefs (e.g. Venus / Mars) at known α — pin
     against the direct `mallamaPhaseFactor(coefs, alpha)` value.
   - Degenerate viewer / host coincidence (lenV or lenHp ≤ 0) returns 1.
   - α = 0 (viewer aligned with host) returns the same as `peakPhaseFactor`.

# Why

[[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] (a): pure helpers need to be
testable. Theme matches parent 189 (planet pipeline DRY, tests).

# Acceptance

- `phaseFactorFor` is exported (or hoisted to a `phase-function.ts` /
  `planet-body-field-pure.ts` sibling).
- A vitest covers at minimum the four cases above.

title: Unit tests for LocalGroupLayer.pick + Stellata.pickHeliopauseHit + pickCloudHit
description:
# Finding from PR #72 (stellata-lo5) review

Three hover-picker integration paths land in PR #72 with no unit
coverage:

- `LocalGroupLayer.pick` — projects each LG object centroid, builds
  candidates, reduces via `pickFromCandidates`. PR body Medium.
- `Stellata.pickHeliopauseHit` — silhouette bbox + label rect; two
  regimes (outside-shell vs inside-shell, near-plane guard).
- `Stellata.pickCloudHit` — Three.js raycast delegation + visibility
  gate.

# Scope

Fakes a camera, world offset, DOMRect, and (for heliopause) the SVG
label element. Cases (representative):

LG (per the post-review reducer refactor — winner carries
cameraDistancePc + tier on the candidate):
- Centroid inside hitRadius → prime hit.
- Centroid within pxThreshold but outside hitRadius → fallback.
- `group.visible = false` short-circuits to null.
- Two overlapping objects: closest cameraDistancePc wins within tier.

Heliopause:
- Outside-shell: cursor inside silhouette bbox → fallback hit.
- Inside-shell: at least one sample point behind near plane → bails.
- Label rect: getBoundingClientRect overlap → fallback hit (even
  when silhouette test bails on near plane).

Cloud:
- Raycast returns idx → fallback hit with the right cameraDistancePc.
- `cloudLayer === null` or `group.visible = false` → null.

# Why

[[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] (c): integration paths.
Theme matches parent 192 (catalog binary + picker tests; P3 default
for non-user-visible refactors).

# Acceptance

- Three vitest files (or one shared) under
  `src/client/hover/` covering the cases above.
- Each uses synthetic fakes — no real renderer, no real catalog data.

title: Unit tests for PlanetBodyField.pick + appMagFor + evalPlanetView
description:
# Finding from PR #72 (stellata-lo5) review

`PlanetBodyField.pick`, `PlanetBodyField.appMagFor`, and the private
`evalPlanetView` helper (the shared geometry+photometry math, after
the post-review DRY pass) all lack unit tests. PR body flagged the
pick path as Medium confidence.

# Scope

- Cover `evalPlanetView(host, planetIdx, camPos)` directly — pin the
  `(appMag, planetX, planetY, planetZ, dVp)` outputs against
  known-good inputs (e.g. Mercury, Saturn, Pluto) and validate that
  appMag matches the `planetApparentMagnitude` ground-truth helper.
  This is the pure-math shared path; covering it is the highest-value
  test of the chain.
- Cover `appMagFor` null branches (host not attached, planetIdx out
  of range) and one happy path.
- For `pick`, an integration test with a fake camera + fake bufLocalRel
  setup that exercises:
  - prime-tier hit on a planet whose disc contains the cursor
  - fallback-tier hit on a planet centroid near the cursor but no
    disc hit
  - kill condition: appMag > maxAppMag + 0.5 drops candidates
  - cross-host walk: planets from two attached hosts both eligible

# Why

[[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] (c): integration paths through
new state machinery need read-back assertions. Theme matches parent
189 (planet pipeline DRY, tests, halo correctness).

# Acceptance

- vitest tests landing for `evalPlanetView` + `appMagFor` + `pick`.
- `evalPlanetView` is the new shared helper from the lo5 review-fallout
  commit (\`hover: share appMag math between planet pick + appMagFor\`)
  and is the priority test target — covering it pins the whole math
  chain that both consumers ride on.

title: Unit tests for hover-engine FSM (lifecycle, registry, clamps)
description:
# Finding from PR #72 (stellata-lo5) review

`src/client/hover/hover-engine.ts` is integration-only — no unit
tests. Covers the engine's state machine (idle / pending-timer / shown
/ dragging), register/unregister deduplication, dispose path
teardown, and the tooltip placement clamps.

# Scope

- Faking the canvas via `EventTarget` + `vi.useFakeTimers()` is the
  natural test pattern (vitest's jsdom env provides both).
- Cases to pin (representative, not exhaustive):
  1. pointermove → 280 ms timer → providers walked → tooltip shown.
  2. Drag (pointerdown before timer fires) suppresses the hover.
  3. pointerleave hides the tooltip and clears the timer.
  4. register / unregister: same provider not double-added; remove
     drops it from the walk.
  5. dispose: clears the timer, removes the four canvas listeners,
     empties the provider list, hides the tooltip.
  6. Tooltip placement clamps when cursor approaches right / bottom
     edge (`Math.min(x + offset, maxLeft)`).

# Why

[[stellata-test-coverage-discipline]] (c): lifecycle FSMs need a
read-back assertion, not just "does not throw". Theme matches the
parent epic (187 = event-bus & lifecycle hygiene).

# Acceptance

- vitest file `src/client/hover/hover-engine.test.ts` covering the
  cases above.
- Each timer-driven assertion uses fake timers + advanceTimersByTime
  rather than real waits.

title: Consolidate physicalRadius floor: Math.max(..., 1e-6|1e-9) magic numbers across 6 sites
description:
Six call sites in `src/client/stellata.ts` floor catalog `physicalRadius[idx]` before multiplying by `R_SUN_PC`, with two different floor values and no shared constant:

- `1e-6` floor: lines 820, 3477, 3665
- `1e-9` floor: lines 3611, 3640 (pre-existing) plus 2495, 2592 (new in PR #66)

PR #66 lands `MIN_PHYSICAL_RADIUS_R_SUN = 1e-9` for the two NEW sites; this bead is the cleanup of the pre-existing six. Decisions:

1. Settle on one floor value (1e-9 or 1e-6) — needs a check on which sites use the result for "is this star a valid renderable" gating (1e-6 ≈ Jupiter radius, 1e-9 ≈ ~0.7 km — different physical floors).
2. Migrate all sites to the named constant.
3. Likely consolidate with similar floors in star-geometry / pickers if they exist (look for the pattern around line 820).

Why P3: cosmetic — no current bug, just inconsistency and a magic-number violation of stellata-named-constants-and-dry rule 1.

Smoke: typecheck + vitest after migration. No user-visible change.

Source: surfaced during PR #66 review (camera-arrival hybrid landing).

title: Hand-curated famous-SNR overrides (SN 1987A, Cas A)
description:
Sibling to the unified nebulae ingest pipeline (9hp.1). Carries hand-
curated overrides for individually-famous SNRs that the MW-centric
pipeline either misses (extragalactic) or would surface with thin /
mismatched data (very young + well-resolved MW SNRs that warrant
richer per-object detail than the SIMBAD-TAP filter returns).

# Scope

Two named seeds, expandable later:

- **SN 1987A** — LMC (~50 kpc). Famously the only naked-eye SN in
  modern times. **Will NOT be caught by 9hp.1's pipeline** as currently
  scoped — the epic's data sources filter to MW (~3 kpc cut) and most
  SNR catalogs (Green's, etc.) are MW-centric. Needs a hand-curated
  entry with the LMC-anchored position.
- **Cassiopeia A** — MW (~3.4 kpc, right at the pipeline cut). The
  youngest known MW SNR (~340 yr), filamentary, well-imaged by Chandra,
  HST, JWST. May be caught by the unified pipeline but the canonical
  reference data (Reed 1995 distance, Hwang 2004 morphology) probably
  isn't what the pipeline ingests. Override to upgrade detail.

Future additions of similar shape: **Crab Nebula** (M1, also MW, but
already a strong-PN-like emitter so already covered by other catalogs),
**SN 1006**, **Tycho's SNR**, **Kepler's SNR**, **SN 1604**, **N49** (LMC),
**N132D** (LMC). File as follow-on overrides as needed; this bead is the
infrastructure plus the first two seeds.

# Why a separate bead rather than fold into 9hp.1

Two reasons matching the local-group/overrides.tsv precedent:

1. **Pipeline-data mismatch.** Like the M31/M33 standalones in
   local-group/overrides.tsv (major spirals excluded from LVDB's
   dwarf_all table), these famous SNRs sit outside what the unified
   pipeline catches. Hand-curation is the only path.

2. **Per-object detail.** Famous SNRs warrant well-sourced morphology
   parameters (shell radius / thickness, asymmetry axes for SN 1987A's
   triple-ring system) — closer in shape to the local-group/overrides.tsv
   axes+orient schema than to the auto-pipeline's flat "blob with
   distance + size" record.

# Schema (proposed, draft)

`data/nebulae/snr-overrides.tsv` or similar — one row per famous SNR:

  name | ra_deg | dec_deg | distance_pc | shell_radius_pc | age_yr |
  shape (sphere|filamentary|three-ring|...) | ref_doi

# Out of scope

- The render pipeline for SNRs in general — that's 9hp.2 (raymarching
  shader) + 9hp.3 (type-specific visuals). This bead provides the
  per-object data; 9hp.2/3 consume it alongside the auto-ingested rows.

# Acceptance

- Build artifact contains SN 1987A and Cas A entries with verified
  position + distance + size.
- Stellata renders both at the expected sky positions when 9hp's
  rendering lands.
- Pattern is documented so future-famous-SNRs can be added without
  re-thinking the schema.

# Relates to

- 9hp.1 (unified ingest) — produces this bead's "everything else" peer.
  Verify 9hp.1's filter to confirm Cas A is or isn't naturally caught
  before the override decision is final.
- local-group/overrides.tsv (the precedent for hand-curated overrides
  to a catalog-driven layer).

# Status

P3, no urgency. Filed during stellata-lo5.5 conversation when
SN 1987A surfaced as a notable absence; hand-curation pattern noted.

title: Hover labels — mobile/touch parity (long-press)
description:
Deferred follow-up: mobile / touch parity for hover labels.

# Why deferred

Per stellata-prioritization-framework, mobile work sits below desktop. v1.0 ships desktop-only hover labels.

# Scope (when picked up)

- Long-press on a pickable surface → show hover card in fixed position above the press location.
- Tap elsewhere → dismiss.
- Reuse the same HoverProvider registry + disambiguateHits from the desktop engine.
- Investigate whether the existing touch-disambiguation in main.ts/stellata.ts needs extending or replacing.

# Acceptance

- iOS Safari + Chrome Android: long-press each class → tooltip appears; tap elsewhere dismisses.

Filed when the design gate lo5.1 closed; sits at P3 until desktop hover work merges.

title: Comment density audit — relocate multi-paragraph in-code prose to SCIENCE.md / docs/*.md
description:
Sweep multi-paragraph in-code prose (3+ lines of running prose, not list-of-fields docblocks) in src/client/*.ts. For each block:

- **Invariant or non-obvious constraint** → keep, trim to ≤1 paragraph.
- **Physics derivation, calibration rationale, tuning history** → relocate to SCIENCE.md or relevant docs/*.md; leave a one-line code-side pointer.
- **Redundant** (paraphrasing the named identifier or describing 'what' rather than 'why') → delete.

# Why

Long in-code prose costs LLM context tokens on every Read AND rots faster than docs (no tests, no review catches stale comments — see stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep memory). Centralising in SCIENCE.md / docs/*.md makes the prose maintainable (defer-doc-updates rule applies) AND lets future Claude sessions load it on-demand via the CLAUDE.md doc index rather than every time a code file is Read.

# Examples of in-code prose to relocate

- stellata.ts § 'Star size physics' (~13-line docblock with the σ·√(STAR_PHYSICS_FACTOR·Δm) derivation) → SCIENCE.md (likely already there); code-side pointer: '// see SCIENCE.md § Star size physics'.
- FilterState field docblocks (currently inline per-field 'why' prose) — keep one-line invariants, move history notes ('showMolecularClouds removed — see CLAUDE.md') out.
- Anywhere stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep flagged stale prose in 9mm retrospective beads — apply the trim.

# Pointer comment shape

Consistent so they are greppable from CLAUDE.md doc references:
```
// see SCIENCE.md § Star size physics
// see docs/galactic-overlay.md § Shaft-start lerp
```

# Soft ceiling

No comment block over 12 lines in src/client/*.ts unless documenting a hard invariant a reader MUST grok to understand the next line (e.g. event-bus emit-order ordering, sentinel-init poison values).

# Acceptance

- Grep for multi-paragraph comments → zero unjustified blocks > 12 lines.
- SCIENCE.md / docs/*.md gain the relocated content under appropriate sections (per defer-doc-updates: doc edits live in this PR, not earlier).
- Code-side pointers use the consistent shape above.

# Smoke

typecheck passes (move is pure-prose; no behavioural change).

# Independence

Parallelisable with the controller-extraction chain (194.3-.7) — independent file edits, no shared code paths. Can land any time after 194.1 (folder reorg) lands so file paths are stable.

# Connects

- [[alex-pr-review-style]] — DRY of explanatory prose between code + docs.
- [[stellata-rename-and-stale-prose-sweep]] — same drift class.
- [[proactive-drift-correction]] — preventative version of the same rule.
- [[defer-doc-updates]] — doc edits land in this PR.

title: Cloud hover provider (written, not registered)
description:
Write the cloud hover provider/formatter; do NOT register at v1.0 (clouds are shelved per CLAUDE.md).

# Scope

- `src/client/hover/formatters/cloud-hover-format.ts` — fields: name, distance from Sol via `fmtDist`, size summary (semi-axes).
- `src/client/hover/formatters/cloud-hover-format.test.ts` — golden output for Taurus + Orion A.
- A `CloudHoverProvider` class exists but is gated on `FilterState.showMolecularClouds === true`. Since that flag is permanently false at v1.0, the provider does not appear in the engine's registry.
- Pick path reuses existing `MolecularClouds.raycast` (`molecular-clouds.ts:197–203`) — no change to that file.

# Acceptance

- Formatter unit-tested.
- Registration toggle works (verify by temporarily flipping the flag in dev; verified behaviour expected: hovering a cloud surfaces the card; flag back to false → no card).

# Why P3

The cloud layer itself is shelved; this is "engine-ready" work, not user-visible at v1.0. Lifts the provider out of "needs eyeballs" when clouds un-shelve.

Ships as part of lo5 single-PR rollup; close on PR merge. Depends on lo5.2.
close_reason: merged via PR #72 (commit 12: cloud provider written, auto-registers when clouds un-shelved)

title: HudOverlay two-pass alpha sequencing has no integration regression test (ml8 follow-up)
description:
# The gap

PR #70 (stellata-ml8) restructured `HudOverlay.update()` into two passes — commit Sol/GC arrow geometry first, then compute the shared fade alpha from THIS frame's `max(solDrawnLen, gcDrawnLen)`. Removed: the `Stellata._navArrowFadeAlpha` pre-pass alpha that read last-frame's `getDrawnLengths()` (the cause of the toggle-on flash, ml8 symptom 1).

`src/client/arrow-fade.test.ts:91-104` is labelled "ml8 symptom 1 regression" but it tests the pure-helper guard:

```ts
expect(focusedArrowFadeAlpha('navigate', null, 200, 0, 28)).toBe(1);
```

That asserts the `shaftLength=0 → α=1` contract. It does NOT exercise the actual integration bug — `HudOverlay.update()` computing alpha against stale last-frame `getDrawnLengths()`. A future regression that re-introduces a pre-pass alpha computation (or moves the alpha calc back ahead of `updateOne`) would slip past every vitest.

Per `stellata-test-coverage-discipline` rule 5: "PR test plan flags any path as 'manual smoke'? Promote the cheapest of those (5–10 line vitest) to automated." PR #70's confidence section graded the HUD refactor as Medium and explicitly defers to manual smoke.

# What needs to land

An integration test that exercises the two-pass sequence end-to-end:

1. Mock the SVG element shims `HudOverlay`'s constructor accepts (`SVGCircleElement`, `SVGPathElement` × 4, `SVGTextElement` × 2). The applyFade-side mock infra in `hud-overlay.test.ts:5-12` (`makeFadeEls`) is a starting point but needs extending — `update()` also calls `setAttribute('d', …)`, `setAttribute('x'/'y', …)`, `textContent`, and `style.display`.
2. Construct a `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` aimed so Sol projects to a known on-screen position. Likely needs `camera.updateMatrixWorld()` + `camera.updateProjectionMatrix()` after positioning.
3. Call `hideAll()` (simulate toggle-off — sets `solDrawnLen = 0`, `gcDrawnLen = 0`).
4. Call `update()` with `focusedDiscRadiusPx` large enough to engage the disc-coverage fade (coverage > 0.5 against the expected this-frame shaft length).
5. Assert `getCurrentFadeAlpha()` reflects the fade computed from this-frame's geometry — NOT 1 (which is what the pre-fix code would return when it read the stale-0 `refLen`).

# Acceptance

- `hud-overlay.test.ts` gains a new `describe` block (suggested name: `HudOverlay two-pass alpha sequencing (ml8)`) covering the integration above.
- Test fails if `getCurrentFadeAlpha()` is computed against stale `getDrawnLengths()` (i.e., would fail against the pre-#70 code).
- Test passes against current `main`.

# The wrong claim to replace

The PR #70 description ([https://github.com/alexmensch/stellata/pull/70](https://github.com/alexmensch/stellata/pull/70)) labels the `shaftLength=0 → α=1` test as the "regression for symptom 1". **Wrong** — that's the pure-helper guard, not the integration regression. The integration regression has no automated coverage. Acceptance of this bead corrects that.

# Smoke

`npm test` should grow by ~1 describe block. Test should fail if anyone reverts `HudOverlay.update` to a pre-pass alpha computation (e.g. moves the `focusedArrowFadeAlpha` call ahead of the `updateOne` calls).

# Cost / why P3

Integration test requires non-trivial mock infrastructure (SVG + camera), ~30-50 lines. Beyond the 5-10 line threshold `stellata-test-coverage-discipline` would normally promote. P3 because (a) the prior pre-pass design has been removed, so the regression vector has no live precursor in the codebase, (b) manual smoke catches it in practice (the symptom is a visible one-frame flash). But the test is the proper regression guard per the discipline rule.

# Related

- `stellata-9mm.131` — broader test-coverage gap across overlays. This bead is the specific HUD-two-pass instance; .131 covers dirty-track sentinels and per-overlay smoke. Linked rather than merged because .131 was already filed for PR #55 fallout.

title: CMB last-scattering backdrop — Planck 2018 SMICA skybox (~14 Gpc horizon)
description:
**Goal:** render the Planck 2018 SMICA CMB temperature map as a textured skybox at the observable horizon (comoving distance ~14 Gpc). The natural "back wall" of stellata's universe model.

**Tier 6** of stellata-0hx. Optional capstone.

# Why

The CMB last-scattering surface IS the boundary of the visualisable universe — at z ≈ 1100, no objects exist past it (by definition). It completes the model: everything stellata renders (stars, planets, MW, LG, LV, LSC, Laniakea, cosmic web, BOSS-tier galaxies) floats inside this sphere.

# Scope

## Data

- **Planck 2018 SMICA component-separated CMB map** (HEALPix Nside=512, ~50 MB compressed) from the Planck Legacy Archive. Picked Nside=512 over Nside=2048 (200 MB) — 50 MB fits in initial-load + the screen-pixel resolution doesn't justify finer.
- Alternative: Commander or NILC maps. SMICA is the most commonly visualised; defer to .1's design-gate.
- Commit under `data/extragalactic/cmb-planck-smica-nside512.fits` (LFS-tracked).
- One-off `scripts/refresh-cmb.py` excluded from build per [[frozen-external-data]].

## Pipeline (`scripts/build-cmb.ts`)

- Parse HEALPix FITS → cubemap or equirectangular projection.
- Output: `public/cmb-skybox.{ktx2,png}` for GPU upload.
- Apply colour LUT: temperature → colour (Planck's blue-red anomaly scale, or grayscale with overlay).

## Renderer

- New module `src/client/cmb-backdrop.ts`.
- Rendered as a textured sphere with inward-facing normals, **outside the floating-origin rebase entirely** — drawn last, behind everything, at "infinity" (large fixed radius, modulating with the camera-COSMIC regime's far plane).
- Skybox-style: no depth writes, no depth tests against scene geometry. Always at the back.
- Toggle in the panel: ON/OFF. Default ON when camera regime is COSMIC.

# Smoke

- Pull camera back to maximum (~10+ Gpc out). CMB temperature pattern visible as a textured sphere around the entire model.
- Galaxies from .6 + .7 visible inside the CMB sphere.
- Toggle off → CMB disappears; toggle on → restores.
- No depth-fighting between CMB sphere and Tier 5/6 galaxies — sphere consistently behind.

# Out of scope

- CMB polarisation overlay (separate fancy viz).
- Foreground subtraction comparison (SMICA already foreground-cleaned).
- Cosmic-variance / fluctuation analysis — pure aesthetic backdrop, not an analytical tool.

# Blocks

Nothing — this is the capstone.

# Blocked by

.7. Once BOSS-tier objects are rendering past 1 Gpc, the CMB sphere at 14 Gpc is the natural completion.

title: BOSS / eBOSS reach + redshift colouring (z ≲ 0.7, ~3 Gpc / ~8 Gly comoving)
description:
**Goal:** extend reach to z ≲ 0.7 using SDSS BOSS / eBOSS LSS galaxy + quasar catalogues. ~2M point-glow primitives, colour-coded by redshift. Cosmology bake-in for z → comoving distance.

**Tier 5** of stellata-0hx. The cosmological-survey regime.

# Why

Past NED-LVS / SDSS Main, the only catalogues with spectroscopic redshifts are the BAO surveys (BOSS, eBOSS). They sample biased sub-populations (LRGs, ELGs, QSOs) but the redshift is essentially the distance — and the BAO peak at ~150 Mpc emerges as a natural feature of the rendered distribution.

# Scope

## Data

- **SDSS BOSS LSS galaxy catalogue (DR12 + final SDSS-IV DR17)** — 1.5M LRGs + ELGs.
- **eBOSS quasars** — ~500k quasars to z ~3.5.
- All commit-once via `scripts/refresh-boss.py` per [[frozen-external-data]]. Output binary parallel to `public/cosmic-web/`.

## Pipeline (`scripts/build-boss.ts`)

- Cosmology bake: Planck 2018 (H0=67.4 km/s/Mpc, Ω_m=0.315, Ω_Λ=0.685). z → comoving distance via numerical integration of `c · ∫dz/H(z)`. Precompute as a lookup table or polynomial fit; bake into binary record.
- HEALPix Nside=4 chunking continues from .6.
- De-dup against `public/cosmic-web/`.

## Renderer

- Re-uses `extragalactic-billboards.ts` from .3, fed by chunked binary loader from .6.
- **Redshift colouring** as primary visual: hue → redshift (e.g. blue→z=0, red→z=0.7). Literalises the z signal.
- No per-object labels.
- Camera envelope regime COSMIC (far=8 Gpc, maxDistance=4 Gpc per .2).

## Float32 floor — emulated double-precision

At ~3 Gpc = 3×10^9 pc, float32 precision is ~300 pc. The far-pass split from .6 isn't sufficient — need emulated double-precision (DSFUN90 / floatfloat) for camera-relative position math.

- CPU-side: compute `(cam.pos - obj.pos)` in float64.
- Split into hi + lo floats; hand both to GPU.
- GPU shader: reconstruct via Kahan-like summation in vertex shader.
- The existing star pipeline already does this trick (floating-origin rebase). Lift the pattern to galaxies.

## Selection-bias documentation

BOSS/eBOSS is heavily biased (LRG = luminous red galaxies; ELG = emission-line galaxies; QSO = active galactic nuclei). The visualisation will look "spikier" than reality. Per [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]], present honestly. Document selection function in `docs/extragalactic-roadmap.md`. Add a debug-panel toggle to colour by population (LRG/ELG/QSO) so the user can SEE the selection bias.

# Smoke

- Pull camera to ~5 Gpc. The redshift-coloured filaments visible; BAO ~150 Mpc feature (if visible at this resolution) as a faint shell-like overdensity around overdense regions.
- Quasars visible as the highest-z points, blueshift cluster at low z.
- Distance readout in Gpc (verifies .2 plumbing at extreme scale).
- Emulated-double smoke: no jitter on per-galaxy positions at 5 Gpc out.

# Out of scope

- DESI DR1+ — eBOSS is the sample at this tier; DESI is much larger and would be a separate refresh bead.
- Galaxy cluster catalogues (redMaPPer, AMICO) — separate viz.
- CMB lensing overlay — separate viz.

# Blocks

.8 (CMB backdrop).

# Blocked by

.6 (re-uses chunked loader + hybrid LOD; emulated-double extends the .6 float32 mitigation).

title: Pisces-Cetus + Sloan Great Wall + cosmic web — NED-LVS / SDSS Main (~1 Gpc reach)
description:
**Goal:** extend reach to ~1 Gpc / ~3 Gly. ~1.9M galaxies from NED-LVS or ~700k from SDSS Main, rendered as point-glow billboards. The Pisces-Cetus Supercluster Complex (~380 Mpc, core at ~200 Mpc) and Sloan Great Wall (~420 Mpc long at ~300 Mpc distance) are the named structures.

**Tier 4** of stellata-0hx. First tier where the cosmic web (filaments + voids + walls) is the dominant visual.

# Why

The natural endpoint for "per-object spectroscopic redshift" coverage. SDSS Main reaches z=0.25; NED-LVS reaches z=0.2 (1000 Mpc). Past Laniakea, the supercluster watershed picture fragments into the cosmic web.

# Scope

## Data

- **NED-LVS** (Cook et al. 2023, arXiv:2306.06271) — 1.9M objects with distances to 1000 Mpc. 100% complete bright to ~400 Mpc, 70% complete fainter to ~300 Mpc. Includes redshift-independent distances for closest; cz/H0 past ~40 Mpc.
  - Pro: includes structural data for the bright tail. Inhomogeneous quality but documented per source.
  - Con: file size ~1+ GB raw FITS.
- **SDSS Main spectroscopic** — ~700k galaxies to z ≈ 0.25, Northern footprint only (cuts off ~50% of the sky).
  - Pro: clean uniform selection function.
  - Con: not all-sky.
- **Decide at impl time** (gate at design-gate .1 if not already decided): NED-LVS for all-sky + variable quality, OR SDSS Main for clean-but-Northern. Probably NED-LVS.

## Pipeline (`scripts/build-cosmic-web.ts`)

- Parse FITS or CSV → binary format with HEALPix Nside=4 sky-cell chunking (192 cells, ~10k galaxies per cell). Pattern: dust chunk format (`data/dust/chunk_X_Y_Z.bin` + `manifest.json`).
- Output: `public/cosmic-web/chunk_<healpix>_<distance-band>.bin` + manifest.
- De-dup against `public/laniakea.bin`.

## Renderer

- Camera envelope: regime COSMIC kicks in (far=2 Gpc, maxDistance=1 Gpc per .2).
- **Hybrid LOD**:
  - Per-galaxy billboards for the nearest ~50k galaxies (within ~300 Mpc of camera). Existing pipeline.
  - Volumetric density-field representation for everything past that — model the cosmic web as a smooth density volume (analogous to MW disc/bulge volumetric layer). Per-galaxy smoothstep handover avoids LOD pop.
  - Tradeoff: two render paths to maintain. Pays off in perf + visual quality.
- **Spatial chunking**: per-frame, only chunks in or near camera frustum upload to GPU. ~20-chunk pinned cache. The dust-loader is the precedent.
- Labels on ~10 named cosmic-web structures: Pisces-Cetus, Sloan Great Wall, Perseus-Pegasus chain, Boötes Void, CfA Great Wall (Coma Wall), Pavo-Indus, Ophiuchus Supercluster. No per-galaxy labels at this tier.

## Float32 floor mitigation

At ~1 Gpc = 10^9 pc, float32 precision is ~100 pc. Most galaxies are far enough that camera-relative coords stay within float-safe range, but the closest-to-camera galaxies in a far-zoom view need care. Split rendering into near (parsec-scale, current pipeline) + far (Mpc-scale, this tier) passes — Cesium / Outerra pattern.

# Smoke

- Pull camera back to ~1 Gpc out. Pisces-Cetus filament visible (RA 00h33m, Dec −21°, distance ~200 Mpc).
- Sloan Great Wall (RA ~12h, Dec −10°, distance ~300 Mpc) visible as a coherent ribbon.
- Hybrid LOD handover: at very far zoom, the cosmic web is volumetric; at moderate zoom, individual galaxies emerge.
- Distance readout in Gpc/Gly (verifies .2 plumbing).
- Floating-origin stable at 1 Gpc out (no jitter on near-side galaxies).
- Chunk loader fires on camera motion; "loading deep field…" indicator on uncached regions.

# Out of scope

- Survey-completeness corrections (we render as-observed, per [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]] — honest about selection).
- Cosmological-simulation overlays (Millennium, IllustrisTNG) — separate epic if ever.

# Blocks

.7 (BOSS/eBOSS). Re-uses the chunked binary format + hybrid LOD.

# Blocked by

.5. Re-uses progressive loading from there.

title: Laniakea + sibling supercluster watersheds — Cosmicflows-4 ingest (~150 Mpc reach)
description:
**Goal:** add the Laniakea supercluster + 5 sibling watersheds (Apus, Hercules, Lepus, Perseus-Pisces, Shapley) as wireframe shells, plus ~56k Cosmicflows-4 galaxies as billboards. ~150 Mpc reach.

**Tier 3** of stellata-0hx. The "supercluster as gravitational basin" headline visual.

# Why

The MW's true cosmographic home is Laniakea (Tully et al. 2014, Dupuy & Courtois 2023). Sol's position relative to the Laniakea basin attractor and the Great Attractor is the answer to "where in the universe are we, really" — far more revealing than the Local Group context alone.

# Scope

## Data

- **Cosmicflows-4** (Tully et al. 2023) — 55,877 galaxy distances + peculiar velocities z ≤ 0.1. EDD (edd.ifa.hawaii.edu). PGC-keyed. Distance is the redshift-independent measurement, not cz/H0. Commit `data/extragalactic/cosmicflows4.csv` (~20 MB).
- **Dupuy & Courtois 2023 watersheds** (A&A 678, A176, arXiv:2305.02339) — 6 watershed surfaces as 3D meshes. Fetch from the paper's data repository (or contact authors); commit `data/extragalactic/watersheds/{laniakea,apus,hercules,lepus,perseus-pisces,shapley}.{obj,json}`.

## Pipeline

- `scripts/build-laniakea.ts` — Cosmicflows-4 → binary format (first tier to ditch JSON; mirror `public/catalog.bin` pattern from the star pipeline). De-dup against `public/lsc.json`.
- Output: `public/laniakea.bin` (~5 MB) + manifest. Watershed meshes pass through as JSON in `public/laniakea-watersheds.json` (small data).

## Renderer

- Camera envelope: regime SUPERCLUSTER (far=300 Mpc, maxDistance=150 Mpc).
- **6 watershed shells** as `LineSegments` — cheap, ~10k segments per shell × 6 = 60k segments total. Picked LineSegments over translucent surfaces (fill-rate heavy). Reconsider later if wireframe-only reads poorly.
- ~56k galaxies via `extragalactic-billboards.ts` (from .3). Sub-pixel-floor culling kicks in heavily at this distance.
- Labels on the 6 watershed basins (Laniakea, Apus, Hercules, Lepus, Perseus-Pisces, Shapley) — anchored to each basin's attractor centroid. Per [[stellata-prioritization-framework]], cosmographic structure labels are the high-value adds here, not per-galaxy.
- **Progressive loading**: this tier's catalog binary fetches on camera-cross past 30 Mpc (dust-loader precedent). User sees a "loading deep field…" indicator on first cross; cached after.

# Smoke

- Pull camera back to ~150 Mpc out from Sol. The 6 watershed shells visible as low-opacity LineSegments; Sol sits inside the Laniakea shell.
- Galaxies inside Laniakea visibly clump into filaments converging on the Great Attractor.
- Watershed labels visible at appropriate distance.
- Progressive-load indicator fires on first zoom past 30 Mpc.
- Distance readout in Mpc/Mly (verifies .2 plumbing at this scale).

# Out of scope

- Filament-skeleton overlays (DisPerSE-style) — fancier viz, separate bead if ever.
- BAO feature visualisation — Tier 5.
- Voids — Tier 4 (Boötes Void at ~250 Mpc is just past this tier).

# Blocks

.6 (Pisces-Cetus). Re-uses progressive loading + chunking pattern (extended to HEALPix Nside=4 in .6).

# Blocked by

.4. The binary format introduced here is the template for .6 / .7.
