title: LSC + Virgo Cluster tier — EVCC + 2MRS ingest (~30 Mpc reach)
description:
**Goal:** extend the model to the Local Supercluster including the Virgo Cluster (~30 Mpc, ~100 Mly), ~5–10k galaxies, billboards-primary, with wireframe shells for Virgo + Fornax cluster boundaries.

**Tier 2** of stellata-0hx.

# Why

Galaxy density jumps an order of magnitude past Local Volume. Virgo cluster substructure (M87 + ellipticals) is one of the cosmological landmarks the user should see — anchors the MW's location in the Local Supercluster.

# Scope

## Data

- **EVCC (Kim et al. 2014)** — 1589 spectroscopic galaxies in 725 deg² around Virgo, SDSS DR7 footprint. Commit `data/extragalactic/evcc.csv`.
- **2MRS bright-end** (Huchra et al. 2012, arXiv:1108.0669) — 44,599 all-sky galaxies to Ks ≤ 11.75. Filter to ≤30 Mpc for this tier. Commit `data/extragalactic/2mrs.tsv`.
- **HyperLEDA** for morphological-type cross-walk where 2MRS doesn't carry it.
- Distance: cz/H0 with peculiar-velocity correction via Cosmicflows-4 model (loaded from `.5`'s data file, or hand a coarse PV map at this tier).

## Pipeline (`scripts/build-lsc.ts`)

- Merge EVCC + 2MRS + HyperLEDA crosswalk on PGC ID.
- De-dup against `public/local-volume.json` (within ~12 Mpc).
- Output: `public/lsc.json` (~5–10k galaxies).

## Renderer

- All-billboard via `src/client/extragalactic-billboards.ts` from .3.
- **Wireframe overrides** for ~20 famous Virgo + Fornax galaxies (M87, M86, M84, M49, M104 Sombrero, M60, M59, M64).
- **Cluster boundary shells** for Virgo (centred on M87, ~2 Mpc radius) and Fornax. Coarse wireframe sphere or ellipsoid; one entry per cluster in `lv-overrides.tsv` (re-use the schema).
- Camera envelope: regime SUPERCLUSTER kicks in at this scale (.2's regime enum).
- Fade band: `LSC_FADE_BAND` (e.g. 5 Mpc – 50 Mpc).

# Smoke

- Pull camera back to ~30 Mpc, point at Virgo (RA 12h30m, Dec +12°). The Virgo cluster shell appears with M87 anchor visible as a wireframe inside, surrounded by ~thousand billboard galaxies.
- Distance readout in Mpc (verifies .2 + .3 plumbing).
- Floating-origin stable; no jitter on stars or LG.

# Out of scope

- Pure ICM / X-ray cluster visualisation (would need ROSAT or Chandra data; separate bead if ever).
- Galaxy cluster mass / dynamics — same.
- Coma cluster (~100 Mpc) — Tier 3 (.5).

# Blocks

.5 (Laniakea).

# Blocked by

.3 (re-uses billboard renderer + naming infrastructure).

title: Local Volume tier — UNGC ingest + billboard galaxy pipeline (~12 Mpc reach)
description:
**Goal:** extend the model envelope from stellata-1ui's 1.5–2 Mpc out to the Local Volume (~12 Mpc), with per-galaxy wireframes for ~50 best-resolved galaxies and billboarded discs for the long tail (~1000 total).

This is **Tier 1** in the parent epic (stellata-0hx). First tier where billboards become the primary rendering primitive.

# Why

Per parent epic: Local Volume has the best per-galaxy distance + structural data in the universe (TRGB, Cepheids, SBF; ~5% distance errors). ~50 of the brightest have measured inclination/PA/axial ratio suitable for wireframe primitives (M81, M82, M101, NGC 4258, NGC 5128 / Cen A, Sculptor Group anchors, etc.). The long tail (~900 dwarfs) is per-object measured but only by position + magnitude — billboards.

# Scope

## Data pipeline (`scripts/build-local-volume.ts` — new)

- Source: Updated Nearby Galaxy Catalog (UNGC, Karachentsev et al. 2013, arXiv:1303.5328) text table from sao.ru/lv/lvgdb, OR uncap the existing Pace 2024 LVDB ingest (its `dwarf_all` extends well past 250 kpc). Decide which at impl time based on what UNGC gives that LVDB doesn't.
- Commit raw under `data/extragalactic/ungc.csv` per [[frozen-external-data]]. LFS if >1 MB.
- Filter: distance ≤ 12 Mpc.
- De-dup: drop objects already in `public/local-group.json` (within 2 Mpc) to avoid double-render.
- Schema parallels `public/local-group.json` (`name, id, center, kind, axes, quat, source, distance`) with two new fields per object: `morph_type` (Hubble T-type), `m_k` (apparent K-band magnitude).
- Output: `public/local-volume.json` (Tier 1–2 stays JSON; binary format introduced at Tier 3).
- Pure helpers split into `build-local-volume-pure.ts` with vitest coverage (parallel to `build-local-group-pure.ts`).

## Overrides (`data/extragalactic/lv-overrides.tsv`)

Same schema as `data/local-group/overrides.tsv`. Hand-curated structural rows for the ~50 wireframe-tier galaxies. Cite Cepheid/SBF distance refs + structural-paper refs per object.

## Client runtime

- `src/client/local-volume-loader.ts` — fetch + parse `public/local-volume.json`. Parallel to `local-group-loader.ts`.
- `src/client/local-volume.ts` — wireframe layer for the ~50 with override rows. Mirrors `local-group.ts` pattern. Re-uses `galactic-fade.ts`'s `LV_FADE_BAND` (from .2).
- **New module `src/client/extragalactic-billboards.ts`** — instanced quad pipeline modelled on `src/client/shaders/star.{vert,frag}.glsl`. Per-billboard attributes: position (ICRS pc), m_k (apparent K), morph_type (uint8 → colour LUT), distance (for size scaling). Re-uses `perceptual-disc.glsl` for the shading profile. This module is the engine; subsequent tiers (.4–.7) re-feed it.
- Label gating: re-use `src/client/distance-gated-label.ts` for the ~50 wireframe galaxies. For named billboards (NGC/IC/Messier), feed the label-rank function from .1's spec — limited to ~30 visible at once.

## Smoke (per [[alex-stellata-smoke-tests]])

1. `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` green.
2. Build script idempotent: re-running `build:catalog` doesn't rewrite `public/local-volume.json` if inputs unchanged (mtime check, mirror `build-local-group.ts`).
3. Catalogue sanity:
   - M81 at (RA 09h55m, Dec +69°, 3.63 Mpc)
   - M101 at (RA 14h03m, Dec +54°, 6.86 Mpc)
   - Cen A at (RA 13h25m, Dec −43°, 3.8 Mpc)
   - Sculptor group anchors at expected positions.
4. Manual browser smoke:
   - From first-load (Sol focal), pull camera back. At ~5 kpc, MW + LG fade in (existing). At ~500 kpc, LG canonicals (stellata-1ui). At ~3 Mpc, M81 group + Cen A group + Sculptor group appear.
   - Wireframe vs billboard split: ~50 famous have visible wireframe primitives; long tail is just disc/glow billboards.
   - Distance readout switches to Mpc past 1 Mpc, Mly past 3 Mly (verifies .2 plumbing).
   - Labels on M81, M101, Cen A, Sombrero (if in scope), Sculptor + M81 group anchors. No labels on dwarf billboards.
   - Floating-origin stable at 12 Mpc out — no jitter on the AT-HYG starfield.

# Out of scope

- LSC / Virgo (Tier 2) — separate bead (.4).
- Naming search-typeahead extension for new galaxies — file as related-link to stellata-l2x.
- Mobile — Tier 1 is fine on desktop only for now.

# Blocks

.4 (LSC + Virgo). Re-uses this bead's `extragalactic-billboards.ts` renderer.

# Blocked by

.2 (camera envelope + distance formatting).

title: Extragalactic deep-field — far-field galaxy + cosmic-web model out to the observable horizon
description:
**Goal:** complete the spatial scale of stellata's model from the Local Group (stellata-1ui's ~2 Mpc cap) out to the limits of current observational data — Local Volume → Virgo Supercluster → Laniakea → Pisces-Cetus / Sloan Great Wall → SDSS spectroscopic horizon → CMB last-scattering backdrop.

# Why

Stellata's detail-gradient mission: highest-density measurable info near Earth, simpler model further out, per [[stellata-cds-data-sources-and-detail-gradient]]. The far-field beyond LG is currently absent. Real observational catalogues exist with per-galaxy positions + structural data out to ~150 Mpc (Cosmicflows-4), and per-galaxy positions only (redshift-derived) out to ~1 Gpc (NED-LVS, SDSS Main). Past that, individual luminous probes (LRGs, quasars) reach ~3 Gpc. The CMB is the back wall.

# Tier breakdown — what catalogues exist where, and where per-object detail dies

## Tier 1 — Local Volume (≤ ~12 Mpc / ~40 Mly)

Per-galaxy distances + structural data best-in-class.

- **Updated Nearby Galaxy Catalog (UNGC), Karachentsev et al. 2013** (arXiv:1303.5328) — 869 galaxies, CC0-equivalent, sao.ru/lv/lvgdb.
- **Pace 2024 LVDB** — already ingested under data/local-group/, currently capped at 250 kpc.
- **Structural data**: ~100–200 of brightest have measured inclination, PA, ellipticity, half-light radius.
- **SGA-2020** (arXiv:2307.04888) — Sérsic decomposition for ~383k galaxies, DESI footprint only.
- **Distance accuracy**: ~5% (TRGB, Cepheids, SBF).
- **Names**: ~100% NGC / IC / UGC / Messier.
- **Render**: wireframes for ~50 best-resolved (M81, M82, M101, NGC 4258, NGC 5128 / Cen A, Sculptor anchors). Billboarded discs for the long tail.

## Tier 2 — Local Supercluster + Virgo (~30 Mpc / ~100 Mly)

Density jumps an order. Cluster substructure (Virgo, Fornax, Eridanus) dominates.

- **EVCC** (Kim et al. 2014) — 1589 galaxies in 725 deg², SDSS DR7.
- **2MRS** (Huchra et al. 2012, arXiv:1108.0669) — 44,599 galaxies all-sky to Ks ≤ 11.75, 97.6% complete.
- **HyperLEDA** — ~1M galaxies with PA + axial ratio + morphological type.
- **Distance accuracy**: ±5–10% from cz/H0 after PV correction.
- **Render**: billboards primary. Wireframes for ~20 famous (M87, M86, M84, M49, M104). Virgo + Fornax cluster boundary shells.

## Tier 3 — Laniakea + sibling superclusters (~150 Mpc / ~500 Mly)

The supercluster-as-watershed picture.

- **Cosmicflows-4** (Tully et al. 2023) — 55,877 galaxy distances z ≤ 0.1. EDD (edd.ifa.hawaii.edu). PGC-keyed.
- **Dupuy & Courtois 2023** (A&A 678, A176, arXiv:2305.02339) — Laniakea + Apus, Hercules, Lepus, Perseus-Pisces, Shapley watersheds as 3D meshes.
- **2MRS** continues to cover this tier.
- **Distance accuracy**: ±10–20% PV band, ±5% past it.
- **Render**: pure billboards for galaxies. ~6 watershed wireframe shells. MW position relative to Laniakea basin centre is the headline.

## Tier 4 — Pisces-Cetus Supercluster Complex + Sloan Great Wall (~200 Mpc – 1 Gpc)

Cosmic web becomes dominant visual.

- **NED-LVS** (Cook et al. 2023, arXiv:2306.06271) — 1.9M objects to 1000 Mpc. 100% complete bright to ~400 Mpc; 70% complete fainter to ~300 Mpc.
- **SDSS Main spectroscopic** — ~700k galaxies to z ≈ 0.25, Northern footprint.
- **Distance accuracy**: PV-dominated below 100 Mpc, Hubble-dominated above. Past 200 Mpc, z·c/H0 IS the distance to ~1%.
- **Pisces-Cetus**: ~380 Mpc long, ~160 Mpc wide, core at ~200 Mpc.
- **Sloan Great Wall**: ~420 Mpc long at ~300 Mpc distance.
- **Render**: galaxies as point-glow primitives. Labels only on ~10–20 named cosmic-web structures.

## Tier 5 — BOSS / eBOSS reach (z ≲ 0.7, ~2.5 Gpc / ~8 Gly comoving)

The cosmological-survey regime.

- **SDSS BOSS/eBOSS DR16/DR17** — 1.5M LRGs + ELGs + ~500k quasars.
- **Distance**: spectroscopic z + ΛCDM → comoving. ~1% accuracy.
- **Cosmology**: Planck 2018 (H0=67.4, Ω_m=0.315) baked into build pipeline.
- **Structural data**: essentially none — galaxies are point sources.
- **Render**: redshift-coloured point glows. Selection bias (LRG/ELG/QSO) documented and accepted per [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]].
- **Beautiful**: BAO ~150 Mpc feature emerges from the distribution if power-spectrum-weighted.

## Tier 6 — Beyond z~1 (out to the horizon at z~1100)

- **Quasar samples** — DESI / eBOSS to z~3.5. Sparse + biased.
- **JWST high-z** — pencil beams only.
- **CMB last-scattering sphere** — Planck 2018 SMICA HEALPix temperature map, comoving radius ~14 Gpc. The natural back-wall of the visualisable universe.

# Where data quality dies (three different walls)

| Aspect | Where it dies |
|---|---|
| Per-galaxy structure (PA, inclination, axial ratio) | ~100 Mpc median, ~500 Mpc for most luminous |
| Redshift-independent distances | ~150 Mpc (Cosmicflows-4 z<0.1) |
| Per-galaxy spectroscopic z | ~1 Gpc (SDSS Main), ~3 Gpc (BOSS LRG/ELG) |
| Sky completeness | 2MRS all-sky to ~150 Mpc; SDSS North-only past that |
| Individually-named galaxies | ~10k named, mostly < 30 Mpc; PGC numbers past |

Natural endpoint: SDSS-reach universe + CMB backdrop. Past z ≈ 0.5 the picture is fundamentally statistical.

# Architectural changes the proposal asks for

1. **Camera envelope as configurable tier** (not magic literal in stellata.ts). Regime enum: STELLAR / GALACTIC / SUPERCLUSTER / COSMIC. Each sets far/maxDistance/near-plane appropriately. The .2 bead.
2. **Galaxy billboard pipeline as parallel to star pipeline**. Instanced quads sized by M_K + distance, coloured by morphological type. Shaders extend `perceptual-disc.glsl`. The .3 bead.
3. **Naming policy**: labels only on named objects. Label-rank function with spatial bucketing, hysteresis, hard cap (~30 visible at once across all tiers combined). Extends `local-group.ts:338–383`.

# Performance considerations

Stellata baseline: ~313k instanced-quad stars at 60 fps. Challenge isn't "can we render" — it's how float32 precision, label-engine cost, and bundle size scale.

## Cross-cutting

1. **Floating-origin float32 budget** (~7 sig figs):
   - Tier 1–3 (≤ 200 Mpc): existing rebase sufficient. Precision ~15 pc near 150 Mpc, well below galaxy-position uncertainty (~Mpc).
   - Tier 4+ (≥ 1 Gpc): split rendering into near (parsec-scale) + far (Mpc-scale) passes. Cesium / Outerra-style.
   - Tier 5–6: emulated-double-precision on CPU; hand GPU only camera-relative float32. The star pipeline already does this trick — lift it to galaxies.

2. **Camera envelope + log-depth precision**. Far plane growth ~3×10^4×. Log-depth handles it (~1 bit per doubling). Mitigate depth-fighting via reverse-z + dynamic near-plane growth per regime.

3. **Label engine cost**. Today 884 ops/frame (52 LG objects × ~17 ops). Naive Tier 4 → 32M ops/frame. Mitigations: named-only label set (~10k), spatial octree bucketing (~100–500 per-frame candidates), 1-pixel hysteresis, ~30 visible cap.

4. **Initial-load bundle size**. Today ~13 MB catalog + 2 MB search + dust. Tier cumulative ~150–200 MB naive — not viable. **Progressive loading per regime** (dust-loader pattern): Tier 1+2 cold, Tier 3+ fetched on zoom-cross. **Region chunking** Tier 4+ (HEALPix Nside=4 = 192 cells, ~10k galaxies/cell). Frustum-culled GPU upload + small chunk cache.

5. **Build pipeline**. Each tier's `build-*.ts` is one-shot. Per [[frozen-external-data]]: raw fetch is one-off `refresh-*.py` excluded from `npm run build`; commit processed binary. CI build under a minute.

## Per-tier perf table

| Tier | Objects | Wireframes | Billboards | New perf concerns | Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 LV | ~1000 | ~50 | ~950 | None | None |
| 2 LSC | ~10k | ~20 + cluster shells | ~10k | Label set growth | Top-N rank extension |
| 3 Lan | ~56k | 6 watershed shells | ~56k | Binary format; lazy-load | LineSegments shells |
| 4 PCs | ~1.9M | 10 labels | ~1.9M | Float32 floor; chunking; LOD | Hybrid: billboards near + volumetric far |
| 5 BOSS | ~2M | None | ~2M | Emulated-double; cosmology bake | LRG/ELG/QSO selection bias |
| 6 CMB | 1 sphere | None | 1 texture | Skybox outside floating-origin | Nside=512 (50MB) vs Nside=2048 (200MB) — picks 512 |

## Tier-specific tradeoffs called out

- **Tier 3 watersheds**: LineSegments (cheap, ~60k segments total) over translucent surfaces (pretty but fill-rate-heavy).
- **Tier 4 hybrid LOD**: per-galaxy billboards within ~300 Mpc of camera; volumetric density-field for past that. Per-galaxy smoothstep handover avoids LOD pop. Cost: two render paths to maintain.
- **Tier 4 chunking**: HEALPix Nside=4 sky cells, dust-loader precedent. Cost: GPU upload churn on fast motion; mitigate with ~20-chunk pinned cache.
- **Tier 5 selection bias**: per [[stellata-data-fidelity-principle]], present data honestly. Document selection function; debug-panel toggle.
- **Tier 6 CMB sphere**: textured skybox outside floating-origin. Necessarily occludes anything past it (by definition).

## Deferred from perf scope

- Log-depth additional precision tier past 1 Gpc — wait for actual symptoms.
- GPU memory ceiling at 1.9M billboards — hardware-dependent; add perf-hud readout in .3.
- Mobile: likely Tier 1–2 only (per [[stellata-prioritization-framework]] mobile is tier 6).

# Out of scope (deliberate)

- Time-evolution of cosmic web (snapshot only, like MW + LG today).
- Dark matter halo overlays — separate epic if ever.
- Per-galaxy stellar population / SED detail (billboards take morphology-bucketed colour).
- AGN / quasar host distinction (single magnitude-coloured point).

# Children + sequencing

- **.1 Research / design-gate (P2)** — `docs/extragalactic-roadmap.md` with data inventory, cosmology choice, manifest schema. Blocks .2–.8.
- **.2 Camera envelope + distance formatting refactor (P2)** — regime enum, fmtDistAuto Mpc/Gpc/Gly branches, FadeBand builder. Blocks .3–.8.
- **.3 Local Volume tier (P3)** — UNGC/LVDB ingest to 12 Mpc, billboard pipeline. Blocks .4.
- **.4 LSC + Virgo (P3)** — EVCC + 2MRS bright-end, cluster shells. Blocks .5.
- **.5 Laniakea + watersheds (P3)** — Cosmicflows-4 + Dupuy 2023. Blocks .6.
- **.6 Pisces-Cetus + Sloan Great Wall (P3)** — NED-LVS / SDSS Main, chunking + LOD. Blocks .7.
- **.7 BOSS / eBOSS + redshift colouring (P3)** — emulated-double, cosmology bake. Blocks .8.
- **.8 CMB last-scattering backdrop (P3)** — Planck Nside=512 SMICA skybox.

# References to consult at impl time

- Planning session that filed this epic: /Users/alexm/.claude/plans/i-m-about-to-implement-harmonic-pizza.md
- Predecessor: stellata-1ui (M31/M33 + 1.5–2 Mpc LG expansion).
- Sibling far-field epic: stellata-36y (HiPS-derived cumulative glow below AT-HYG floor).

title: Warp / navigate to extended deep-field objects (Local Group + future deep field)
description:
**Status:** decision-pending. Sibling to stellata-l2x (search-catalogue ingest); the two ship the end-to-end "type M31, press enter, arrive" UX but the design questions are independent. Filed separately per the navigate-from/to vs search-ingest seam.

# Goal

A working warp-target model for extended Mpc-scale objects. Today's warp lands at a point (a star, a focused planet, a cloud centroid) at a calibrated minDistance. Galaxies aren't points — landing at the centroid of M31 plants the camera *inside* the disc, where the wireframe is invisible by definition (the same MW-disc-inside guard applies). The user needs to park *outside* the disc at a distance where the silhouette reads.

# Open design questions

1. **Park-distance heuristic.** Suggestions:
   - `3-5 × maxAxis` of the target's local-frame axes.
   - Solve for a target apparent angular size (e.g. the galaxy fills ~30% of the screen at the user's FOV).
   - Per-object override in the TSV.
   - The molecular-cloud warp already solves a near-identical problem — Alex confirmed the reuse direction is the right one in chat; this bead carries the actual application.

2. **Park direction.** Where in the sky does the camera arrive? Suggestions:
   - On the line of sight from Sol — keeps the "sky direction matters" intuition.
   - Perpendicular to the disc normal (for disc kinds) — gives the canonical "face-on" silhouette.
   - At the same galactic-latitude direction the user was at — preserve user orientation.
   - User toggle.

3. **MW-disc passage during warp.** A warp from inside the MW disc to M31 crosses the disc surface (and visually the disc fade band) en route. Decisions:
   - Animate the transit so the user sees the MW disc receding behind them, then M31 approaching?
   - Cut to the arrival vantage with a fade?
   - Reuse stellata-2br's log-distance arrival profile across Mpc scales (currently calibrated to ≤ 250 kpc).

4. **Focus model after arrival.** Galaxies aren't stars — there's no orbit ring, no heliopause, no per-star physical body. Does focusing a galaxy:
   - Surface a custom info panel (the hover/click-pin from stellata-lo5)?
   - Re-show the wireframe in a chart-style halo?
   - Just park the camera and let the user inspect?

5. **Departure (warp from a galaxy to somewhere else).** Today warp departure is anchored to the camera's current focus. Galaxy-focus exit semantics:
   - Click outside in OBSERVE mode unfocuses (same as star) — but star-unfocus closes to the focused star, and "close to" a galaxy is ambiguous.
   - Drop focus on warp-out, same as the cloud-to-star transition handles it.

6. **Outside Local Group (future).** This bead's design needs to forward-compat to deep-field DSOs (`stellata-m40.4`), galaxy groups past 2 Mpc, eventually clusters. The park-distance heuristic shouldn't be hard-coded to 2 Mpc.

# Related work

- stellata-l2x — Search-catalogue ingest for LG. Companion bead; together they ship the end-to-end UX.
- stellata-2br — Camera-arrival profile (log-distance deceleration). The arrival profile probably needs Mpc-scale validation; file a follow-up bead under 2br if anything breaks at 2 Mpc.
- stellata-c7u molecular-cloud warp — the existing pattern for "warp to extended object". Look here first.
- stellata-1ui — The 2 Mpc envelope.
- stellata-m40.4 — Chart-mode glyphs for distant DSOs (Andromeda / Messier on celestial sphere). Different rendering, same navigate-to question.
- stellata-lo5 — Hover labels' design gate informs the post-arrival focus panel.

# Smoke / acceptance (post-decision)

- Warp from Sol to M31: camera lands outside the M31 disc at a vantage where the inclined disc plate reads as a coherent shape, not edge-on at the centroid.
- Warp from M31 to M33: arrival vantage shows the M33 disc clearly; the M31 silhouette recedes behind / off-screen.
- Warp from M31 back to a focused star (e.g. Sirius): transition closes back into the MW with no clipping artifacts at the disc fade band.
- Chart-mode arrival reads cleanly with the paper aesthetic.

# Out of scope

- Search-catalogue ingest (sibling bead stellata-l2x).
- Stellar-population resolved rendering inside LG members (out of scope per the original LG epic).

title: Add Local Group objects to the search catalogue / typeahead
description:
**Status:** decision-pending. Several open questions Alex needs to answer before implementation starts. Files separately from the warp / focus-park bead per the navigate-from/to vs search-ingest seam.

# Goal

Make the 123 Local Group objects (LMC, SMC, Sagittarius dSph, classical + ultra-faint dSphs, plus the M31 subgroup + outer-band dIrrs shipped in stellata-1ui) typeable into the existing destination-search typeahead — same UX surface stars use today, so a user can type "M31" and warp.

# What exists

- `src/client/search.ts` / `src/client/typeahead.ts` — the destination-search engine.
- `public/search-index.json` — ~13 MB raw / ~2 MB gzipped index built from the star catalogue, ingested by `scripts/build-catalog.ts`.
- `public/local-group.json` — the rendered LG catalogue with display name, slug id, ICRS centre, distance per object. Already typeable in principle.
- The typeahead today is *star-shaped* — entries carry HIP, GJ, Bayer, etc. designations.

# What's missing

- LG entries in `search-index.json` (or a sibling index).
- A way for the typeahead to disambiguate star vs galaxy results.
- A warp target resolver for galaxy entries (separate bead — see "Companion bead" below).

# Open design questions (Alex decides before any code lands)

1. **Single index vs separate index.** Append LG entries to `search-index.json` as a new entry kind, or ship a `public/lg-search-index.json` alongside? Trade-off: single index is one fetch + one Fuse instance; separate index is cleaner schema but two indexes to merge in UI.

2. **Entry-kind tag.** What discriminator lives on each entry? Options:
   - `kind: 'star' | 'galaxy' | 'cloud' | ...` enum.
   - Implicit via designation pattern (M / NGC / IC prefix → galaxy).
   - The kind drives the warp target resolver and the typeahead row presentation.

3. **What designations to ingest per LG object.**
   - Primary display name (already in local-group.json).
   - Catalogue cross-walks — does NGC 205 also typeable as "M 110"? "Messier 110"? IC 10 / IC 0010? "Andromeda Galaxy" → "M31"? Alex's call.
   - LVDB key (slug) as a fuzzy fallback.

4. **Typeahead row presentation.** Today star rows show name + designation cluster + distance. For galaxies — show distance + type (dSph / spiral) + apparent magnitude? Or stay minimal? Likely related to stellata-lo5 (hover labels) — the same "what info matters for a galaxy" question.

5. **Constellation typeahead interaction.** Today there's a separate `#con-input` for constellations. Galaxies should sit in destination search, not constellation typeahead. Confirm.

6. **Disambiguation collisions.**
   - "Sagittarius" already routes to the Sagittarius constellation. Now also a dSph (existing) AND a dIrr (new at ~1.2 Mpc). Three results — what's the rank order?
   - "M31" doesn't collide with any star designation today, but "M 32" might look like a Messier-cluster shorthand.
   - Decision: ordering rule on collisions (e.g. brightest / closest / most-clicked / alphabetical).

7. **Index size.** Adding 123 entries × ~50 bytes/entry ≈ 6 KB raw / 1 KB gzipped — negligible. No size pressure either way.

# Companion bead

Warping *to* an extended LG object is a separate concern, filed as a sibling bead (focus-park model for extended objects — see stellata-7l5 once filed). Search ingest doesn't need that to land first, but the typeahead row's "warp on enter" hook won't function end-to-end until both beads ship.

# Smoke / acceptance (post-decision)

- Type "M31" into `#go-input` → see M31 in typeahead → enter → camera warps near M31 and parks at a sensible distance (sensible = whatever the warp bead defines).
- Type "M 32" / "M32" / "NGC 205" / "Sextans" — each disambiguates correctly per decisions above.

# Related work

- stellata-nxb — Expand search index with alternate designations (V-numbers, GJ, HIP variants, CCDM, etc.). Same pipeline, different content; the kind-discriminator decision in this bead overlaps with theirs.
- stellata-9hp.4 — Search index integration for RNe illuminators. Same shape — file under a unified scheme if 9hp.4 hasn't started yet.
- stellata-lo5 — Hover labels' design gate also touches "what info matters for a galaxy"; cross-link the field-list outcomes.
- stellata-1ui — The 2 Mpc envelope this builds on.

# Out of scope

- Warp animation / focus-park model — separate bead.
- AT-HYG catalogue cross-walks for LG stellar populations (LMC red supergiants etc.) — stellata-8l0 territory.

title: LG / extragalactic apparent-magnitude filtering — needs visual-vs-representational decision first
description:
**Status:** research / decision-pending. Not implementable yet — blocked by stellata-8hu (Physical vs Representational rendering — three-mode framework) and entwined with stellata-dbl (Calibration workflow).

# Context

The Local Group layer (stellata-38m + stellata-1ui) ships objects out to 2 Mpc as **representational wireframes** — LineLoop ellipsoids / discs that ignore apparent magnitude. The wireframes are visible whenever the MW-fade band reveals them, regardless of how dim the actual object is to a human eye. This is correct for the representational use case: if we didn't draw a wireframe at all, the user would never see most of these dwarfs even with eyes glued to the eyepiece — they're too diffuse, too low-surface-brightness, or too distant.

Stars sit on the other side of that line: they're rendered with a calibrated apparent-magnitude pipeline (the magnitude slider gates which point-sources draw, calibrated to the perceptual-disc shader). Feeding the Local Group through the same slider conflates two different rendering philosophies.

# What we already have

- **LVDB carries `M_V` (absolute V magnitude)** for every row in the snapshot — column 86 of `data/local-group/lvdb-snapshot.csv`. Apparent magnitude from any vantage is `m_V = M_V + 5·log10(d / 10pc)`, computable at build time once distances are committed.
- **M31 / M33 standalones** ship without M_V (the overrides.tsv schema doesn't carry it). Well-cited values: M31 M_V ≈ −21.5, M33 M_V ≈ −18.8. Hand-curate when implementation lands.
- The pure path is already exporting `distance` per object; adding a `appMag` field is a build-script change of ~10 lines.

# Two ways to apply this — both depend on stellata-8hu

## (A) Standard-mode representational filter — independent slider

Add an LG-specific magnitude slider (or a single "deep-field" cutoff knob) that gates wireframe visibility by computed apparent V magnitude at the camera vantage. M31 and the Magellanic Clouds stay visible across the typical range (m_V ≈ 0–5); the long tail of ultra-faints (m_V ≈ 13–17) drops out at moderate settings.

The key argument for a separate slider: stars and wireframe galaxies compete for different screen real estate. A 17th-mag galaxy spanning a degree is perceptually very different from a 17th-mag point source — the slider semantics shouldn't be identical.

## (B) Pure-Visual blob rendering — calibrated against stars + MW background

Replace (or augment) the wireframe with a low-surface-brightness blob shape whose integrated brightness matches `m_V` at the camera vantage, calibrated against the star-field + MW volumetric background so the relative perceptual weight is right. This is the "what would I see from here" view — and the answer is often "not much" once you're not inside the disc.

Blocked by stellata-8hu's three-mode framework — Pure Visual exists as a concept there but no layer renders that way today. Also depends on stellata-dbl's calibration procedure (need a benchmark image / procedure to verify M31 blob brightness lines up with the LMC blob brightness at the same magnitude preset).

# Decisions to make at unblock time

- Whether to ship (A), (B), or both. The current 2 Mpc render is the representational baseline; (A) and (B) are two different paths off it.
- Whether (A)'s slider is independent of the star magnitude slider or unified.
- Whether (B) replaces wireframes in Pure Visual mode, or both render and the user picks.
- Blob shape model for (B) — Gaussian falloff vs single-component Sérsic vs n-body-style point cloud. (B) is essentially the planet-body / star-point physics applied to extended objects.

# Data work to do at implementation time

- Build script (stellata-1ui's `build-local-group.ts`) exports `appMagFromSol` per object (computed from M_V + distance). Trivial extension once M_V plumbs through.
- Add `M_V` column to the override TSV for M31 / M33 (and any other standalone rows). LVDB-merge rows pick up M_V from the LVDB row.
- Calibration spot-checks against published magnitudes (LMC m_V ≈ 0.9, M31 m_V ≈ 3.4, M33 m_V ≈ 5.7, Sextans dSph m_V ≈ 10.4, …) to verify the M+5log10(d/10) formula lands right.

# Out of scope (separate beads if/when they happen)

- Adding the LG to the star magnitude slider semantics (the unified case under decision point above). Defer that decision to 8hu's design gate.
- Stellar-population resolved rendering for LMC / SMC / Sgr dSph — already excluded under stellata-cds-data-sources-and-detail-gradient + stellata-38m's out-of-scope list.

# Refs

- stellata-1ui: the 2 Mpc envelope this builds on.
- stellata-38m: original LG wireframe layer.

title: Lift apparent-size label-ranking into a reusable engine + audit chart-labels overlap
description:
# Goal

Capture the top-N apparent-size label-ranking pattern introduced in
stellata-38m as reusable infrastructure so other catalog families
(future user-option label sets — brightest stars, named stars, nebulae,
the M31 group when stellata-1ui lands) can share the same ranking,
viewport-clipping, and SVG-binding machinery. Audit overlap with the
existing chart-mode label engine (`chart-labels.ts`) before deciding
whether to lift-and-reuse OR unify the two engines.

# Current state

`src/client/local-group.ts` carries the pattern:

- `LabelCandidate` = `{ id, centerAbs, maxAxis }`
- `RankingParams` carries camera matrices + viewport + tunables
  (`topN`, `minPixelSize`, `mwInsideDiscPc`, etc.).
- `computeVisibleLabels(candidates, params) → Set<id>` runs per frame:
  1. Inside-MW guard (extra-MW objects suppressed while camera is
     inside the disc).
  2. Behind-camera filter via `matrixWorldInverse · centerAbs`.
  3. Apparent-size floor via `2·atan(maxAxis / camToObj) × pxPerRad`.
  4. Viewport overlap (centroid ± pxSize/2 vs the screen rect).
  5. Sort descending by apparent size; take top N.
- Tunables exposed via the Deep-field debug panel
  (`local-group-tuning.ts`).
- SVG labels bound through `createDistanceGatedLabel` with predicate
  `() => visibleLabelIds.has(id)`.

The MW-inside guard is the only LG-specific piece. Everything else is
generic.

# Audit deliverable

Read `src/client/chart-labels.ts` and the chart-mode label flow in
`docs/chart-mode.md` and write a short audit (in this bead's notes,
or a new design-gate child) covering:

- Does chart-labels.ts have its own \"which to label\" picking
  (magnitude-based? collision-avoidance? top-N?), and how does it
  relate to the LG pattern?
- Is there a single shared abstraction that fits both, or are the
  domains different enough that two engines is correct?
- What's the right module name + shape for a shared engine?

Then either:

**(a) No meaningful overlap.** Lift the LG ranking — `LabelCandidate`,
`RankingParams`, `computeVisibleLabels`, and the registration +
frame-handler scaffolding — to a new `src/client/label-ranking.ts`.
Move the MW-inside guard to a per-candidate hook so the helper stays
generic. `local-group.ts` becomes a consumer; chart-labels.ts
unchanged.

**(b) Real overlap.** Unify the two: define a single label-engine
interface; reimplement chart-labels.ts and local-group.ts on top of
it. Larger scope; file as an epic with the two consumers as children.

Either way, leave a follow-up bead for the user-option consumer Alex
sketched in the stellata-38m PR thread: \"show labels on the screen as
a user option\" — a settings-panel toggle that opts into labelling for
a configurable set (brightest N stars, named stars, named nebulae,
etc.) reusing whatever shared engine lands here.

# Out of scope

- Any user-facing labelling option (e.g. a 'show labels for named
  stars' toggle). Gates on the audit conclusion; file separately.
- The chart-mode rework itself (m40.x) — this bead only touches
  chart-labels.ts if the audit chooses path (b).

# Acceptance — audit only (this bead)

- Written audit answering the three questions above, stored in this
  bead's notes (\`bd update --notes\`).
- Decision recorded: path (a), path (b), or 'neither — keep two
  engines'.
- If (a) or (b), a follow-up implementation bead filed (P3) with the
  audit linked.

# References

- stellata-38m PR: introduced the LG ranking pattern.
- `src/client/local-group.ts`: current implementation.
- `src/client/chart-labels.ts`, `docs/chart-mode.md`: chart-mode
  engine to audit against.
- `src/client/distance-gated-label.ts`: the SVG-binding layer that
  both engines presumably want to share regardless.

title: perf-hud: tear down realMark/realMeasure/realFrame on panel close
description:
# Problem

`perf-hud.ts` flips `installed = true` and rewires the module-level
`_mark / _measure / _frame` function references to the real
implementations on first `buildPerfSection()` call (lines 130-135).
`dispose()` (lines 245-253) explicitly does NOT reset them — the
in-file comment justifies this:

> realMark/realMeasure/realFrame stay installed — ring buffers keep
> filling between toggles so the histogram has data on re-open.

Result: once the user opens the debug panel (or even the Perf section
specifically) once in a session, every `perfMark / perfMeasure /
perfFrame` call site keeps doing real work — Map lookups, ring writes,
section GC sweeps — for the rest of the page session, even after the
panel is closed.

# Call-site cost

`stellata.ts:animate()` calls perf instrumentation ~12× per frame
(`frame.total`, `controls.update`, `pre-render`, `coreMask`,
`gpu.render`, `frame.handlers`, each mark + measure pair, plus
`frame()`). At 60 Hz that's ~720 Map ops/sec, plus the section-GC
walk in `realFrame`. Additional call sites in `chart-labels.ts`
(chart-mode label engine) and elsewhere stack on top.

Modest in absolute terms but it's continuous, perpetual work that
exists ONLY for the HUD — and runs whether or not the HUD is open.
The session-wide install runs counter to the "debug panel sections
should be dark when hidden" invariant Alex specifically asked about.

# Fix

In `dispose()`, restore the three function references to no-op stubs:

    _mark = () => {};
    _measure = () => {};
    _frame = () => {};
    installed = false;

And clear the data structures so a re-open isn't holding pinned
references:

    sections.clear();
    starts.clear();
    frameCounter = 0;

# Trade-off

Reopening the panel will cold-start the histogram (no recent history
to show). The original comment cites this as the reason for keeping
the install warm. Acceptable cost — the perf HUD is opt-in via the
dev console, not a live monitoring surface; users who want continuous
metrics should keep the panel open.

# Scope clarification — pin/arrow HUDs are NOT in this bead

`pin-debug-hud.ts` and `arrow-fade-debug-hud.ts` also keep their
`onFrame` body running latch comparisons when the section is
collapsed (DOM build is gated, latch updates are not). This is
INTENTIONAL per the in-file comments — latched extremes are meant
to span a collapse/expand window. Different design call from the
perf-hud install leak. Leave alone unless filed separately.

# Acceptance

- `dispose()` resets `_mark / _measure / _frame` to no-op stubs and
  clears `installed`, `sections`, `starts`, `frameCounter`.
- After panel close, a quick Chrome devtools sampling profile during
  a focused-camera-warp shows zero time in `realMark / realMeasure /
  realFrame / ensureSection` (or those frames no longer appear in
  the profile at all).
- Re-opening the panel cold-starts the histogram (empty) and fills
  it over the next ~RING_SIZE frames as expected.
- All existing perf-hud vitest tests still pass.
close_reason: merged via PR #68 (perf-hud: tear down on debug-panel close)

title: poi-overlay.ts: add test file covering syncPool / hideEntry / on-screen vs off-screen branch
description:
T-2 finding from the PR #55 review (637bdf9). poi-overlay.ts has no test file. The module ships state-machine behaviour (syncPool's per-POI Entry pool, on-screen/off-screen projection branch, hideEntry sentinel wipe, idempotent show/hide) and a click affordance contract (on-screen label deselects, arrow label aims-at) — none of it is covered.

The PR #55 review explicitly fixed the hideArrow / hideEntry sentinel wipe (9mm.170). hud-overlay got direct test coverage in the review-response commit (637bdf9) via resetArrowSentinels; poi-overlay's hideEntry equivalent is still untested.

Acceptance — initial coverage:
- poi-overlay.test.ts created.
- syncPool: assert createEntry / destroyEntry happen on togglePoi state changes (mocked Stellata.getPois()).
- hideEntry: assert the sentinel wipe (lastArrowD preserved, numeric sentinels → NaN, string sentinels → '\0').
- on-screen / off-screen branching: parametrise the projection so the helper functions can be tested without standing up the full overlay — likely needs a small refactor to extract the on-screen test condition (`projected !== null && projected[0] >= ON_SCREEN_PULL_IN_PX && ...`) as a pure helper.

Couples with F-4 (the emptyEntryDirtyState() factory introduction).

title: poi-overlay vs hud-overlay shaft-length sibling-symmetry: POI keeps 8 px cutoff that HUD removed
description:
F-5 finding from the PR #55 review (637bdf9). Sibling-symmetry violation:

- hud-overlay.ts deliberately removed `MIN_SHAFT_PIXEL_LENGTH = 8` (the cutoff that snapped Sol/GC arrows to invisible at <8 px shaft length). The PR description and the inline comment block at hud-overlay.ts:29-34 explain why: the disc-coverage fade in navigate mode keys on the longest currently-drawn shaft, and a discontinuity at 8 px broke the fade's smooth alpha behaviour. The arrow now renders at any positive shaft length so the fade reads continuously.

- poi-overlay.ts:36 still defines `const MIN_SHAFT_PIXEL_LENGTH = 8;` and uses it at line 349 to hide the POI chevron when the shaft would be shorter than 8 px.

This is `stellata-consistency-at-the-seam §2` (sibling symmetry): two arrow renderers sharing the same screen-direction cascade + shaft formula, one with a hard cutoff and one without. Question: is the POI cutoff still needed, or is it a leftover from before the fade existed? POI arrows render in OBSERVE mode where the disc-coverage fade is not active (camera is parked at the focal star, no nav-arrow fade alpha), so the cutoff isn't tied to a fade-continuity story. The only justification would be "an 8 px chevron just looks bad" — which would apply equally to the (now-rendering) sub-8-px Sol/GC chevrons.

Acceptance:
- Decide: remove the POI cutoff (match hud behaviour) OR add the cutoff back to hud (matching POI). My read: remove the POI cutoff is the correct call given the architectural direction. The Sol/GC arrows already render at any positive length and the disc-coverage fade story is the more demanding case.
- Implementation: drop MIN_SHAFT_PIXEL_LENGTH from poi-overlay.ts; replace the gate at line 349 with `if (shaftLengthPx <= 0)` mirroring hud-overlay.ts:388.
- Smoke: pin a POI in OBSERVE just barely outside the viewport on a high-FOV camera. Should see the chevron continuously shorten as the POI enters the pull-in margin, not snap to invisible at 8 px.

Pre-existing — the asymmetry pre-dates PR #55, but the PR touched both files and the review surfaced it.

title: DRY hideEntry sentinel-reset against hideArrow's resetArrowSentinels shape
description:
F-4 finding from the PR #55 review (637bdf9). hud-overlay.ts::hideArrow and poi-overlay.ts::hideEntry both do the same "wipe per-attribute sentinels back to poison-init" operation on different state shapes. PR #55's review-response commit (637bdf9) extracted resetArrowSentinels(state: ArrowState) as a free function in hud-overlay.ts so the hud side is testable; poi-overlay.ts still inlines the wipe at line 173-187.

Per stellata-consistency-at-the-seam §1 (lifecycle pairing) and the existing `Object.assign(this.solDebug, emptyArrowDebug())` precedent at hud-overlay.ts:444, the cleanest fix is:

1. Define `emptyEntryDirtyState()` in poi-overlay.ts (returns the sentinels-only slice of the Entry interface).
2. hideEntry becomes: gated `d=''` + `display='none'` writes (as today) then `Object.assign(e, emptyEntryDirtyState())`.
3. Add a vitest for the wipe (mirrors the new hud-overlay resetArrowSentinels test) — pins the 9mm.170-equivalent regression at the POI call site.

Acceptance:
- poi-overlay's hideEntry visibly uses an emptyEntryDirtyState() factory.
- One vitest pins the per-field wipe (lastArrowD preserved, lastRingCx/Cy → NaN, lastOnScreenLabelText → '\0', etc.).
- Existing tests still pass; build clean.

Connects to T-2 (poi-overlay.test.ts doesn't exist yet — this bead creates it).

title: Hoist ARROW_PIXEL_LENGTH + RING_HALO_GAP_PX out of hud/poi overlays
description:
F-3 finding from the PR #55 review (637bdf9). Two calibrated screen-pixel constants are duplicated verbatim between hud-overlay.ts and poi-overlay.ts:

- `ARROW_PIXEL_LENGTH = 110` — hud-overlay.ts:18, poi-overlay.ts:34
- `RING_HALO_GAP_PX = 4` — hud-overlay.ts:23, poi-overlay.ts:35

Both arrow renderers consume these to size the shaft + the ring-rim halo gap. Per stellata-named-constants-and-dry rule 1 ("hoist numeric literals to named consts at first sight of a second usage"), the duplication is review-blocking. The right home is either arrow-path.ts (the shared arrow util that already exports buildArrowSvgPath, ARROW_HEAD_DEPTH_PX, ARROW_LABEL_OFFSET_PX, ARROW_LABEL_PADDING_PX, screenDirToTarget) — so hud-overlay and poi-overlay re-import from that single canonical location.

Acceptance:
- arrow-path.ts exports ARROW_PIXEL_LENGTH + RING_HALO_GAP_PX.
- hud-overlay.ts and poi-overlay.ts import from arrow-path.ts and drop their local consts.
- Existing tests + typecheck + build all pass.

Smoke: focus a star with Sol off-axis, confirm Sol/GC arrows still attach correctly to the focus ring rim (halo gap visually intact); pin a POI off-screen in OBSERVE, confirm the POI chevron still attaches to the HUD ring rim at the same gap.

title: Expand model volume to 1.5–2 Mpc + Andromeda subgroup
description:
**Goal:** complete the Local Group rendering by expanding the model envelope from 250 kpc (stellata-38m's cap) out to the canonical Local Group boundary (~1.5–2 Mpc), and add wireframes + distance-gated labels for M31, M33, the M31 satellite subgroup, and the outer dwarfs that sit beyond 250 kpc but inside Local Group canonical membership.

This bead is **deferred** at creation. Depends on stellata-38m. Unblock when the 250 kpc layer is shipped and Alex wants the further-out context.

# Why deferred

Per [[stellata-prioritization-framework]], adding more layers (especially far-field) is tier 5. The 250 kpc cap in stellata-38m is the immediate "give context for where the MW sits" payoff; the M31 subgroup is a separate value-add that requires bigger camera + UI work. Splitting keeps the first PR's smoke surface manageable.

# Scope

- **Camera envelope bump:** `src/client/stellata.ts` `PerspectiveCamera` far plane → ~3 Mpc, `TrackballControls.maxDistance` → 1.5 Mpc (or 2 Mpc — decide at unblock time based on whether the IC 10 / WLM band beyond canonical Local Group is worth including).
- **Data filter extension:** `scripts/build-local-group.ts` distance cutoff → new cap (1.5 or 2 Mpc).
- **Override rows for the major new objects** in `data/local-group/overrides.tsv`:
  - **M31 / Andromeda** (~780 kpc): inclined disc, i = 77°, PA = 35°, ~30 kpc disc diameter. Cite Conn et al. 2012 (distance) + relevant PAndAS structural papers (e.g. McConnachie et al. 2018) at impl time.
  - **M33 / Triangulum** (~840 kpc): inclined disc, i = 54°, ~17 kpc disc. Cite the Cepheid distance reference (Science 308, 1248 / [10.1126/science.1108342]) at impl time.
  - **NGC 205, M32** (M31 satellites, ~780 kpc): ellipsoids, semi-major ~2.7 / 1.6 kpc.
  - **IC 10, NGC 6822, Leo I (if it landed outside 250 kpc), Leo A, IC 1613, WLM**: ellipsoids from LVDB rows.
- **Labels:** per-object `label_threshold_pc`. M31's label probably wants ~200 kpc threshold so it only appears once the user can see the whole M31 disc as a coherent shape. M33 similar.
- **Distance formatting:** consider extending `src/client/distance-util.ts` (`fmtDistAuto`) to format Mpc explicitly past some threshold. Today's Mpc-scale objects would render in light-years (3.26 Mly per Mpc), which works but is unusual UX-wise.
- **Chart-mode coordination:** M31 specifically deserves chart-mode treatment via `stellata-m40.4` (distant DSO glyphs) once that bead unblocks. File a `relates-to` link.

# Smoke

- Zoom from first-load all the way out. MW disc + 250 kpc wireframes fade in as before. Past ~500 kpc, M31 + M33 outlines appear; labels appear at their respective thresholds.
- Sky-position sanity: M31 in Andromeda (RA 00h 42m, Dec +41°); M33 in Triangulum nearby.

# Out of scope

- Anything outside Local Group canonical boundary (~2 Mpc). Sculptor Group, M83 group, etc. — separate decision.
- Sgr stream + M31 stellar streams — invisible / stellar-scale, not a wireframe primitive.

# Data sources

In addition to those already cited in stellata-38m:
- M31 disc geometry: Conn et al. 2012 + McConnachie et al. 2018 (PAndAS). Look up canonical references at impl time.
- M33 distance: Bonanos et al. 2006, *ApJ* 652, 313 (10.1086/508140) — 840 ± 11 kpc Cepheid distance.

# Reference

Planning session that filed this bead: /Users/alexm/.claude/plans/let-s-start-plan-mode-nested-bentley.md
close_reason: merged via PR #69 (Local Group expansion to 2 Mpc + M31/M33 + Mpc readout)

title: Raise camera bounds to 250 kpc for Local Group rendering
description:
**Goal:** small preparatory PR that widens the model envelope so the Local Group wireframe layer (stellata-38m) has room to render. Status quo: camera far plane = 200 kpc, TrackballControls maxDistance = 100 kpc — far too cramped for LMC (50 kpc), SMC (62 kpc), Sgr dSph (26 kpc) to sit comfortably in frame at zoomed-out views.

# Scope

- `src/client/stellata.ts:662–667` — `PerspectiveCamera` far plane `200_000` → `500_000` (pc). Near stays at `1e-10`. Log-depth math is fine across this range; the absolute span matters more than the ratio and we're well inside the precision budget Stellata already relies on for sub-AU near work.
- `src/client/stellata.ts:681` — `TrackballControls.maxDistance` `100_000` → `250_000`.
- Sanity-check `src/client/scale-bar.ts` + `src/client/distance-util.ts` (`fmtDistAuto`) handle 200 kpc gracefully. Parsec / light-year formatting scales naturally; no Mpc band needed yet. No change expected — just verify.

# Out of scope

- Any rendering changes. This bead only widens the envelope; the rendering work is stellata-38m.
- Anything Mpc-scale. The 1.5–2 Mpc expansion to encompass M31/M33/Andromeda subgroup is a separate deferred bead (the new Bead C, filed alongside this one).

# Smoke (manual; per stellata-stellata-smoke-tests do not start a dev server)

1. `npm run typecheck` + `npm test`.
2. Zoom fully out from first-load. Galactic-disc outline visible at far zoom (already full opacity past 5 kpc from Sol per docs/galactic-overlay.md). Scale bar formats sensibly out to 250 kpc.
3. Focus + warp to a near star. Confirm no precision regression in the close-up regime (near plane unchanged).

# Versioning

Patch bump per RELEASING.md — visible behaviour change in maxDistance even with no new rendering. PR has a Release notes block.

# Blocks

This bead blocks stellata-38m (the Local Group wireframe layer needs the larger envelope).

# Reference

Planning session (this bead's design context): /Users/alexm/.claude/plans/let-s-start-plan-mode-nested-bentley.md
close_reason: merged via PR #67 (Local Group wireframes + MW label) — camera bounds raised to 250 kpc for LG rendering

title: Live debug panel for warp curve tuning
description:
Live tuning surface for the camera-arrival profile, exposed in the unified debug panel (debug.panel()).

# Scope

Per the stellata-2br handoff plan, after the 2br.5 fix lands and the baseline is smoke-validated, expose a debug section that lets Alex iterate live on:

- Curve shape selector — cubic-Hermite (default), quintic smoothstep `6u⁵ − 15u⁴ + 10u³` (sharper decel), asymmetric `f(u^p)` with p > 1 (decel later) or p < 1 (decel earlier), identity-log (`u_eased = u`, sanity-floor case).
- Per-site duration sliders — `FOCUS_LERP_MS`, `WARP_T_MIN_MS`, `WARP_T_MAX_MS`, `OBSERVE_TRANSITION_MS` (the unfocus path).
- Live readouts — peak `dec/s` (`1.5·log(dEnd/d0)/durationMs` for cubic-Hermite), current `u` and `u_eased` for the in-flight arrival, `recenteredToDest` flag.

# Why

If smoke surfaces a divergence between focus-park feel and warp-Fly feel (the two sites use the same helper but the perceptual context differs), iterating via debug HUD is faster than the bake-tune-deploy-test loop.

# Implementation pattern

Follows `src/client/star-tuning.ts` and `src/client/milkyway-tuning.ts` — a section builder that mounts into `debug.panel()`, registers slider/select widgets via the shared helpers, and wires them to setters on `camera-motion.ts` (or to module-level mutable refs the helper reads).

The `ease` function should become an optional param on `NewArrivalOpts` (defaulting to the current cubic-Hermite) so each call site picks its own — focus-park, warp Fly, and unfocus can diverge if desired.

# Acceptance

- Section visible in debug panel under `window.debug.panel()`.
- Curve-shape selector changes the in-flight Fly profile within ~1 frame.
- Per-site duration sliders are independent (focus-park vs warp Fly can have different durations).
- Once dialled in, bake chosen defaults into the helper / call sites and either gate the HUD behind `window.debug` or remove it.

# Out of scope

Permanent UI exposure (this is a tuning aid). Cloud-arrival shape is already kind-agnostic via the helper; no per-kind toggle needed unless smoke surfaces a divergence.

title: Add comment explaining the 'this.bus.emit' regex constraint in stellata-events.test.ts
description:
The structural integration test stellata-events.test.ts scans stellata.ts text for /this\.bus\.emit\(\s*'([a-zA-Z]+)'/g — the regex requires the literal "this.bus.emit" prefix. A future refactor that destructures the bus into a local variable (const bus = this.bus; bus.emit(...)) would pass tsc but make the regex silently miss every emit site, so the test would fail "expected has emit site" with no obvious cause.

# What to add

A one-paragraph comment above the regex explaining:

- Why the literal "this.bus.emit" prefix is required (constrains the search to emit sites inside Stellata; avoids false positives from any other EventBus<...>.emit elsewhere).
- That a refactor destructuring `bus` from `this` will break the test silently and the regex must be updated in lockstep.

# Acceptance

stellata-events.test.ts has an explanatory comment. The type-side coverage (emit sites are still typed against StellataEventMap via tsc) is mentioned so the reader understands the structural test's complementary role.

Why P3: test maintainability, not a correctness bug.

title: Wire HMR accept handler so Stellata.dispose() and bus.clear() actually run
description:
PR #51 (stellata-77m) added bus.clear() to Stellata.dispose() and pitches HMR cleanup as the motivating use case. But Stellata.dispose() is never called in production — main.ts has no import.meta.hot accept handler. The dispose path (and bus.clear() with it) is therefore theoretical defense, not currently exercised.

# What to do

Add an import.meta.hot.accept block in main.ts that:
1. Calls stellata.dispose() on the existing instance.
2. Lets Vite re-import and re-bootstrap.

# Acceptance

- Editing src/client/stellata.ts (or any module that re-creates Stellata wiring) under `npm run dev` tears down the old Stellata cleanly and constructs a new one without page reload.
- Manual verification: open dev tools, watch for "previous instance disposed" log line (or similar marker), confirm no listener accumulation across 5 HMR cycles.
- Coordinate with stellata-9mm.44 (the deferred dispose smoke test): once HMR is wired, the smoke test can also assert clean re-creation.

Why P3: dev ergonomics, not user-visible. Useful but not blocking.

title: Document the bus-subscriber unsubscribe contract in docs/architecture.md
description:
PR #51 (stellata-77m) landed the typed EventBus migration. 15 of the 18 external subscriber files do NOT return an explicit unsubscribe — they rely on Stellata.dispose() calling bus.clear() for teardown. The other 3 (pin-debug-hud, arrow-fade-debug-hud, time-readout) DO return unsubscribers because they have their own mount/unmount lifecycle.

The contract is correct but undocumented anywhere. The next contributor adding a temporary subscriber will read whichever sibling they happen to look at first.

# What to add

One paragraph to the event-bus section of docs/architecture.md spelling out the rule:

  "Subscribers mounted for the lifetime of Stellata may omit the unsub —
  release happens via bus.clear() inside Stellata.dispose(). Subscribers
  with their own mount/unmount lifecycle (debug HUDs, modal-scoped
  overlays, anything that can be torn down without Stellata going away)
  MUST capture the returned unsubscribe and call it during teardown."

# Acceptance

docs/architecture.md event-bus section explicitly states which subscribers need explicit unsubs vs which don't, and why. No code change needed.

Why P3: pure documentation; not user-visible.

title: Stellata core refactors + catalog/picker tests
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm collecting the residual core-refactor and catalog/picker test-gap items that don't fit the other themed epics.

Scope:
- Subsystem extracts: StarPipeline class (disc/glow/coreMask resource ownership), DustParticleLayer subsystem.
- Camera-busy refactor: migrate strict-equivalent sites to isCameraBusy(), audit + document the decision matrix.
- Catalog/binary test gaps: build-catalog→loader round-trip, SearchEntry wire-format pin, readPipeDelimited unit coverage, HEADER_LAYOUT field-type encoding.
- Picker/search: Typeahead<T> unit tests (in-progress, stellata-9mm.98), cloud picker asymmetry (deferred), log-depth precision regression test (deferred).
- Legacy URL-state schema decoder retirement (deferred).

Why a dedicated epic: heterogeneous but all 'core internals' work — neither overlay nor planet nor event-bus. Grouping prevents them from looking like loose ends of the meta umbrella.

Priority: P3 default — internal refactors; individual children can bump if they block in-flight P1/P2.

title: Debug panel & perf-hud cleanup
description:
Themed sub-epic of stellata-9mm covering follow-ups from the debug-panel consolidation (stellata-n1t).

Scope: section builder API split (star/milkyway return HTMLDivElement, perf/pin return {element, dispose, setVisible}) needs unification, diagnostic-readout root styling + reset-link DRY across pin/arrow sections, perf-hud pure-helper extraction + tests (summarize, insertSorted, colourForAvg), debug-panel pure-helper extraction + tests (clampToViewport, rgbToHex, hexToRgb), row-colour dirty-check compares hex against serialized style.color (always mismatches), headline render walks DOM by index instead of captured spans.

Why a dedicated epic: ~7 internal-tooling findings that don't ship to users but slow down every future debug-panel session if left to drift. None individually justify a PR; bundled they're a focused cleanup pass.

Priority: P3 — internal tooling, not user-visible.

title: DRY: diagnostic-readout root styling + reset-link duplicated across pin/arrow sections
description:
Surfaced during PR #65 (stellata-2mi) self-review.

# The duplication

`pin-debug-hud.ts` and `arrow-fade-debug-hud.ts` now build their own root element + reset-link with near-verbatim styling and behaviour:

- Root `style.cssText` — green-on-black ui-monospace readout box, `white-space:pre`, `overflow-x:auto`, `user-select:text`. Arrows adds a `border-left:3px solid #0f0` that the pin version lacks; otherwise identical.
- `[click to reset latches]` link — same text, same CSS (`margin-top:6px;cursor:pointer;color:#999;user-select:none;`), same `Object.assign(latch, emptyLatch())` handler shape.

# Why it matters

Per `stellata-named-constants-and-dry` rule 2: two functions with mostly-identical structure parameterise their differences and share a builder. The duplication existed in pin first (filed before this issue is the right framing — arrows inherited the shape during the 2mi fold). With two live diagnostic sections now using this template, any third will compound the drift.

# Suggested fix

Add a `buildDiagnosticReadoutSection(opts)` helper in `debug-panel.ts`:

```ts
interface DiagnosticReadoutOpts {
  borderColor?: string;        // default '#0f0'; arrows uses this for independent-state alert toggling
  onResetLatches: () => void;
}
interface DiagnosticReadout {
  root: HTMLDivElement;
  body: HTMLDivElement;
  setBorderColor: (c: string) => void;  // for arrows' red/green alert
}
```

Then both `buildPinSection` and `buildArrowSection` become thin builders that call into it and just own the latch object + per-frame `body.textContent` write + onFrame subscription.

# Acceptance

- `buildDiagnosticReadoutSection` exported from `debug-panel.ts` with the shape above.
- Both `pin-debug-hud.ts` and `arrow-fade-debug-hud.ts` use it; no inline `style.cssText` for the green-readout chrome or the reset link remains in either file.
- Arrows' independent-state border toggle keeps working (red on disagreement, green otherwise).
- All existing vitest + typecheck stays green.

# Smoke

- `npm run typecheck && npm test`
- Manual: open the debug panel, expand both Pin and Arrows sections, confirm both look unchanged and reset-latch link still works on each.

# Files touched

- `src/client/debug-panel.ts` (new helper)
- `src/client/pin-debug-hud.ts` (consume helper, drop inline chrome)
- `src/client/arrow-fade-debug-hud.ts` (same)

title: Audit + apply sentinel-init poison across overlay visibility tracking
description:
# Context

PR #64 (stellata-8ee) fixed the heliopause label's first-load visible-by-default bug by changing the visibility sentinel from `let visible = false` to `let visible: boolean | null = null`, so the initial `setVisible(false)` call writes through instead of early-returning on the `if (on === visible) return` guard. Same shape is documented under [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] rule 3.

A peer survey during PR #64 review showed **the same anti-pattern in 4-5 sibling overlays**. They are latent in production today only because their onFrame loops typically reach a write-through branch within a frame or two — masking the bug behind browser paint timing. The shape is identical to the heliopause case; a timing shift (or a new bail branch in onFrame) would surface them.

# Peers carrying the same shape

| File | Pattern | SVG container initial state |
|---|---|---|
| `src/client/planet-labels.ts:61` | `let groupVisible = false; setGroupVisible(false)` | `<g id="planet-labels">` no inline `display:none`; children appended at SVG (0,0) default before first onFrame writes positions |
| `src/client/poi-overlay.ts:147` | `let groupsVisible = false` | three `<g>` (`poi-arrows`, `poi-rings`, `poi-labels`), no inline `display:none` |
| `src/client/distance-vector-overlay.ts:37` | `let visible = false` | container no inline `display:none` |
| `src/client/scale-bar.ts:155` | `let zVisible = false` | SVG sub-element |
| `src/client/constellation-overlay.ts:17` | `let visible = true` | reverse-polarity sentinel — risk is an initial `setVisible(true)` call no-op'ing instead |

Debug-HUD peers (`pin-debug-hud.ts:59`, `perf-hud.ts:55`) carry the same shape but their visibility lifecycle is user-toggled (no auto first-paint), so lower priority — include in the sweep but don't gate on them.

# What to do

For each peer:
1. Change the sentinel type to `boolean | null` and initial value to `null`.
2. Verify the SVG container's effective initial state in `src/client/index.html` and confirm the first init call will land.
3. For `constellation-overlay.ts` (reverse-polarity), invert the logic: `null` poison still works, but trace the first call's direction.
4. Add a regression test per peer following the heliopause pattern (stub `document.getElementById`, assert synchronous `display === 'none'` write on init). Co-locate tests; consider a shared helper if more than two peers want the same scaffolding.

# Smoke step

Hard-reload `/` with no `?v=` fragment in multiple states:
- Sol focused (default first-load) — confirm no planet-label / poi / distance-vector / scale-bar-z indicator artefacts paint at SVG (0,0).
- Switch to OBSERVE mode, then navigate, then back — overlay transitions clean.
- Theme toggle (light↔dark, normal↔chart) — overlays don't flash.

# Acceptance criteria

- Each listed peer's first-paint contract is pinned by a vitest regression test.
- Manual smoke confirms no overlay artefacts at SVG defaults on first-load across modes.
- Grep `let .*[Vv]isible = (false|true)` across `src/client/*.ts` returns zero overlay-layer hits not covered by a poison-sentinel test.

# Why one PR, not five

The fix is mechanically identical across peers and the regression-test scaffolding wants to be shared (or at minimum, copy-pasted with the same shape). One sweep PR documents the pattern coverage explicitly in the PR description (per [[stellata-pattern-coverage-across-peers]]) and prevents drift between sibling files. Bundles cleanly into one commit + one smoke step per the bead-authoring scope rule.

# Related

- [[stellata-consistency-at-the-seam]] (rule 3, sentinel-init for dirty-track)
- [[stellata-pattern-coverage-across-peers]]
- Originating bug: stellata-8ee / PR #64

title: Investigate Gaia DR3 variability catalog as GCVS supplement
description:
Research bead. Output: written decision on whether to add Gaia DR3 variability tables to Stellata's variable-star pipeline (currently GCVS-only).

# Current state

Stellata's variable-star data flows from GCVS (~85 k variables) via gcvs5.txt + crossid.txt → scripts/build-catalog.ts → binary catalog v4. GCVS has authoritative classifications, periods, and amplitudes for traditionally-known variables. Many are referenced by classical designations users recognise.

# What Gaia DR3 adds

Gaia DR3 has its own variability catalog (~10 M classified variable sources) split across multiple tables:
- `vari_summary` — top-level classification per source
- `vari_classifier_result` — per-source classifier output with confidence
- `vari_rrlyrae`, `vari_cepheid`, `vari_long_period_variable`, `vari_eclipsing_binary`, `vari_short_timescale`, etc. — per-class detailed tables with periods, amplitudes, mode classifications

Compared to GCVS: ~100× more sources, more uniform sampling, recent photometric epochs, but lacks the historical depth and classical-designation cross-references GCVS provides.

# Questions to answer

1. **Coverage gap analysis.** For our 313 k AT-HYG subset, how many stars are GCVS-flagged variable, how many are Gaia-DR3-flagged variable, what's the overlap? Stars Gaia flagged but GCVS missed are the supplement candidates.
2. **Quality at the seams.** For stars in both catalogs, do period / amplitude estimates agree? Disagreements need a tie-break rule.
3. **Faint-end policy.** Most Gaia-flagged variables are below AT-HYG's catalog floor entirely. Out of scope for per-object rendering, but could feed a far-field variability statistic (see stellata-36y).
4. **Render impact.** GCVS already drives Stellata's pulsation. Adding Gaia variables to the AT-HYG-visible set means more stars pulsate — a quality-of-data improvement, not a new feature.

# Acceptance

- Coverage stats: |GCVS ∩ AT-HYG|, |Gaia-var ∩ AT-HYG|, |Gaia-var ∩ AT-HYG \ GCVS|.
- Quality check on 20 sample stars in both catalogs.
- Written recommendation: include / defer / skip. If include, file implementation bead (gcvs + gaia merge in scripts/build-catalog.ts).

# Related

- stellata-fe4 (variable pulsation modelling epic) — modelling-side concern, this bead is data-side. If Gaia DR3 surfaces high-amplitude Miras GCVS missed, they'd be subject to the fe4 R-vs-T amplitude decision.
- stellata-zsr.1 (Apsis ingest) — same archive; coordinate fetch infrastructure.
- stellata-36y (far-field epic) — sub-AT-HYG-floor variables could contribute statistically.

title: Investigate Gaia DR3 NSS (non-single stars) as binary-orbit source
description:
Research bead. Output: written decision on whether to add Gaia DR3 NSS tables to the multiples pipeline (stellata-dch).

# Why this is separate from existing dch sources

stellata-dch already plans an ingest of:
- WDS (resolved visual pairs, separation + PA + mags)
- ORB6 (visual-binary Keplerian orbit fits, ~3 k systems)
- Gaia DR3 — but only for **parallax + PM matching of WDS pairs**, not for the dedicated NSS tables.

The Gaia DR3 NSS tables are a **distinct data product**: orbital solutions derived from Gaia astrometry, photometry, and radial velocities. Many NSS systems are NOT in ORB6 — especially astrometric-acceleration binaries and spectroscopic binaries that aren't visually resolvable.

# Tables to evaluate (VizieR / Gaia archive)

- `nss_two_body_orbit` — full Keplerian fits (~169 k systems): P, T_0, e, a, i, ω, Ω. Direct drop-in for our existing Kepler solver.
- `nss_acceleration_astro` — astrometric-acceleration binaries (~339 k); partial info, not full orbits but useful for "this star has an unseen companion" flag.
- `nss_non_linear_spectro` — spectroscopic-binary orbits from RVS (~33 k).
- `nss_two_body_orbit_combined` — combined astrometric+spectroscopic (~6 k high-quality systems).

# Questions to answer

1. **Coverage overlap with WDS/ORB6 already planned.** How many NSS systems would be new vs duplicate? Useful metric: count NSS-only systems with Gaia source_id matching an AT-HYG row.
2. **Orbital element quality.** NSS uncertainties vs ORB6 quality flags — does NSS supplement or supersede ORB6 for the bright stars we care about?
3. **Render impact.** Stellata's binary visualisation today uses inferred or WDS-derived geometry. NSS gives **physical Keplerian orbits** for thousands of systems we currently approximate. How visible is the difference in scene?
4. **Pipeline shape.** If included, NSS becomes a fifth source in `scripts/build-binaries.py`. Frozen-external-data: one-shot fetch, committed under data/. File size budget?

# Acceptance

- Coverage statistics filled in.
- Quality comparison: NSS vs ORB6 on a sample of 20 well-known multiples (α Cen, Sirius, Procyon, Capella, etc.).
- Written recommendation: include / defer / skip. If include, file an implementation bead as a child of stellata-dch.

# Related

- stellata-dch (multiples pipeline epic) — natural integration point.
- stellata-zsr.1 — separate Apsis ingest; if both happen, coordinate scripts/refresh-* helpers to share fetch infrastructure.

title: Refresh AT-HYG (or replace) when Gaia DR4 releases
description:
Refresh Stellata's per-star catalog against Gaia DR4 once it's released.

# Expected release

Gaia DR4 was scheduled by ESA for late 2026 (Q4 2026 target as of my last-known schedule). Verify against https://www.cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia/release before un-deferring — the schedule has slipped multiple times historically and the exact date in this bead is a placeholder.

# Scope

- Wait for an AT-HYG release that incorporates DR4 (David Nash's HYG project upstream typically tracks Gaia releases with a lag of a few months), OR
- Decide whether to re-derive Stellata's per-star catalog directly from gaia_source instead of via AT-HYG (now that we understand the trade-offs — AT-HYG is mag-limited to classic-IDed stars, leaving Gaia's depth on the floor).

# What DR4 brings vs DR3

Per ESA: full astrometry for all sources (vs DR3's subset), BP/RP and RVS spectra for hundreds of millions more sources, refined astrophysical parameters, longer baseline → tighter parallaxes and proper motions, expanded NSS (non-single stars) tables.

# Why deferred, not closed

External-data dependency. Bd-defer keeps it out of 'bd ready' until the data exists. Reopen and re-prioritise when DR4 lands.

# Related

- stellata-36y (far-field HiPS layer) benefits from a DR4-based HiPS map but doesn't strictly depend on it.
- Any in-flight per-star pipeline changes (catalog format v5, etc.) should be DR4-aware so the refresh is a data swap, not a schema rewrite.

title: FAQ: how Stellata distinguishes physical binaries from optical line-of-sight pairs
description:
Write a public-facing FAQ entry (sibling of stellata-04y / stellata-e64) walking through the optical-vs-physical binary filter in the multiples pipeline. The maths × multi-source-observational story is genuinely interesting and worth a piece of the public docs — currently the rationale only lives in the parent epic stellata-dch's design block and in build-binaries.py comments.

# Why this is worth documenting

Most "double stars" in classical catalogues (WDS) are *optical* — two stars that happen to land in nearly the same line of sight from Earth but sit at very different distances and are unrelated. Filtering those out (so the renderer only animates orbits for genuinely bound systems) is a non-trivial cross-match across three independent observational datasets. The reader gets:

- A nice example of why the same visual phenomenon (two stars at similar sky coords) can have wildly different physical interpretations.
- A worked example: Betelgeuse's WDS system 05552+0724 lists components A–J. The pipeline injects bound ones (Aa, Ab, Ac, Ad — interferometric companions all at xyz ≈ (3.19, 151.36, 19.68) pc, within 1 AU of Betelgeuse) AND the wide visual ones (D, F, G, H, I, J at similar tangent-plane positions but with sky offsets up to ~4'). Meanwhile unrelated AT-HYG stars in the same sky direction (e.g. HIP 27919 at 223 pc, 0.67° from Betel; HIP 26992 at 309 pc, 2.9° away) crowd the viewport when looking at Betel from Sol — they look "near" Betel on screen but sit ~70 pc / ~157 pc behind it in 3D.

# What the FAQ should cover

1. **Data sources** (frozen externals committed under data/):
   - `wds_summ.txt` — Washington Double Star Catalog: ~150k visually-observed pairs with separation ρ, position angle θ, magnitudes. Catalog is observational; it does NOT distinguish optical from physical.
   - `orb6_orbits.txt` — Sixth Catalog of Orbits of Visual Binary Stars: ~3k systems where an orbital fit (P, T, e, a, i, ω, Ω) has been published, i.e. confirmed bound.
   - Gaia DR3 (queried via ADQL, results cached in data/) — parallax + proper motion for both components when both have Gaia solutions. The two independent astrometric measurements per star let us test bound-ness empirically.

2. **The filter chain** (scripts/build-binaries.py):
   - **WDS notes flag** — when wds_notes.txt explicitly says "physical" or "optical", trust it.
   - **Gaia parallax 3σ check** — if both components have Gaia parallaxes and the values differ by >3σ, the two stars are at incompatibly different distances → optical pair, drop.
   - **Gaia common proper motion** — physically bound stars share Galactic velocity; if PM vectors disagree by more than ~5 mas/yr in either RA or Dec, → optical pair, drop.
   - **Spectral / magnitude sanity fallback** — when Gaia data is missing for one component, fall back to comparing spectral type + apparent magnitude against the WDS-quoted separation to flag implausible pairings.

3. **The three rendering regimes** (per surviving pair):
   - **Regime 1 — visually resolved** (default): tangent-plane projection of WDS ρ + θ at the most recent epoch → xyz offset of B from A. No orbital animation; positions are a snapshot.
   - **Regime 2 — interferometric with ORB6 orbit**: solve Kepler at J2000 using the published elements → project to (ρ, θ) → xyz offset. Live orbital evolution per frame (e.g. α Cen's 79.9-yr period, Algol's 2.87-day eclipse cycle).
   - **Regime 3 — spectroscopic-only**: a·sin i for separation magnitude, conventional PA=0 (documented limitation). Rare; only emerges when WDS can't resolve and ORB6 can't fit.

4. **Why some "Betel-X" labels still get visually crowded by unrelated stars**: even with the filter, the rendered scene from Sol projects stars at different distances onto similar sky directions. The pipeline correctly identifies these AS unrelated (no FLAG_BINARY_PRIMARY links them); the visual crowding is a perspective artefact, not a data error.

# Smoke / acceptance

- New section in the FAQ destination (likely a top-level `docs/faq-multiple-stars.md` page or a sub-section of the eventual public site under stellata-e64's umbrella).
- Worked Betelgeuse example with the actual numbers (the parent epic and build-binaries.py have them — re-quote rather than re-derive).
- Cross-references stellata-dch (implementation) and the relevant sections of SCIENCE.md (data-source citations) without duplicating their content.

# Related

- stellata-dch (multiple-star matching epic — the implementation this FAQ documents)
- stellata-04y (sibling FAQ: catalogue provenance for HIP/HD/HR/Bayer/Flamsteed/Gl/TYC)
- stellata-e64 (sibling FAQ: rendering-decisions page about size/brightness/perception)

title: Render multi-star system orbits / connections as representational layer
description:
# Goal

Under the Physical/Standard/Advanced framework (parent epic
`stellata-8hu`), add a representational layer that visualises the
*relationships* in multi-star systems — so a user can see at a glance
that Sirius A and Sirius B are bound, even when both are tiny discs
at the camera's current scale.

Two natural sub-modes (decide at design time):

1. **Wireframe binary connector.** Simple 2-vertex line between
   mutually-paired primary↔companion (when `FLAG_BINARY_PRIMARY` is
   set on one side and `companionIdx` resolves). Cheap; works for all
   ~14,836 mutual pairs in the catalog.
2. **ORB6 elliptical orbit projection.** Requires the `binary-orbit-pure.ts`
   Kepler infrastructure landing in `stellata-dch.9` and the v5
   orbital-elements table from `stellata-dch.8`. Draws the actual
   2-body ellipse projected onto the camera plane, anchored on the
   primary, with the companion sitting at its current J2000+t phase.

Likely v1 = wireframe-only. v2 = pick up orbit ellipses for regime-2
entries once dch.8 + dch.9 are merged.

# Scope

v1 scope (this bead):

- New module e.g. `src/client/binary-link-layer.ts`. Mirrors
  `orbit-rings-layer.ts` architecturally — instanced line geometry,
  toggled by an Advanced-mode switch in the panel.
- Toggle defaults: off in Pure Visual; on in Standard; user-set in
  Advanced.
- Render-order placement per `docs/rendering.md` — line goes UNDER
  star discs but OVER constellation lines (line is informational
  about the foreground stars; constellations are background).
- Style: thin dashed line in the focused star's complementary colour,
  fade by camera distance like the existing focus ring.

# Smoke

- Default Standard mode: open α Cen, see the dashed line between
  A and B at the AT-HYG-resolved positions.
- Sirius: line between A and B; B is a faint distant disc — line
  helps locate it.
- Toggle off in Advanced mode — line disappears, stars stay.
- Pure Visual: line never appears regardless of toggle state.

# Dependencies

- Decision on whether to ship the wireframe v1 standalone or wait
  for dch.8 + dch.9 to land first and ship orbit-ellipse v2 directly.
- Coupling with the chart-mode wings glyph (`chart-labels.ts`) —
  glyph + line should not overlap visually; the glyph is the chart-
  mode answer to the same question.

title: Investigate AT-HYG-native sub-threshold duplicate at (-8.7310, 1.6110, 5.4470)
description:
# Symptom

Clean `npm run build:catalog` after `stellata-dch.6` lands logs exactly one
`sub-threshold mutual pair below MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC` warning, with
both stars HIP-less and at literally identical coordinates:

```
sub-threshold mutual pair below MIN_RENDER_SEPARATION_PC (0.00e+0 pc):
  dropping fainter (no HIP, xyz=-8.7310,1.6110,5.4470 absmag=4.71)
  (kept (no HIP, xyz=-8.7310,1.6110,5.4470 absmag=4.24))
```

# Why this is worth chasing

The safety-net in `inferBinaries` is handling it correctly (drops fainter,
keeps brighter), but the existence of two AT-HYG rows with zero positional
separation and no HIP suggests either:

1. A literal duplicate row in `athyg_33_classic_ids.csv` (data hygiene
   bug we should ticket upstream / patch around).
2. A genuine binary that AT-HYG modelled by giving both components the
   primary's astrometric solution without offset (the same pathology α
   Cen has, but without the HIPs needed to participate in the multiples
   pipeline).
3. A non-Hipparcos data source merged into AT-HYG that fed two rows
   sharing a Gaia/Tycho-2 solution.

Identifying which lets us decide whether (a) to file an AT-HYG upstream
issue, (b) extend `applyMultipleOverridesPure` to cover HIP-less famous
companions via name match, or (c) leave the safety net as the
permanent fix.

# Scope

Read-only investigation. No code changes expected unless the conclusion
is "we should bake in an override".

# Procedure

1. `awk -F, 'NR>1' data/athyg_33_classic_ids.csv | awk '{ if (sqrt((-8.7310-x0)^2 + (1.6110-y0)^2 + (5.4470-z0)^2) < 0.001) print }'` (adjust column names — `x0,y0,z0` in AT-HYG schema).
2. Cross-check `proper`, `hip`, `hd`, `hr`, `flam`, `bayer` for both
